boardgame: Hit Send: The High Risk Game of Text Roulette
description:
This twistedly funny, ridiculous card game will bring tears of laughter to game night. Hit Send is causing message mayhem across social media and Youtube after it was co-created and played by The Sidemen: JJ (KSI), Harry (W2S), Ethan (Behzinga), Tobi (Tobjizzle), Simon (Miniminter) and Josh (Zerkaa). It's easy. Take a letter and number card, e.g. M & 3. Scroll to your 3rd contact under M in your phone (your best friend’s mom). Other players put down their most outrageous text card. The DM Don chooses which message you should send. If you send the chosen text, such as ‘Do you ever fantasise about me?’, then you win a point. If you back out, the person who put the card down wins it. Get a reply to win another point. Turn your game night into contact chaos with this high risk game of text roulette.

—description from the publisher

This is a Monopoly clone from Masterpieces Inc. All the deeds, spaces on the board and cards feature game animals and Realtree products.

Players roll dice to determine how far they move. Landing on a space gives the player the option of buying the property. When a player collects all the properties in one color group, the properties can be improved by building. When players land on a space owned by another player they must pay rent. Rent is determined by the location of the property and the number of improvements on the space. Some spaces instruct a player to draw a card which can be beneficial or harmful to the player. Players must avoid going bankrupt to stay in the game. The winner is the last player left in the game.

In Podium, the serious flirts with the absurd to deliver unforgettable moments of laughter. With each map, you'll encounter two types of rankings: unusual rankings derived from official studies, and more comical rankings compiled from the responses of 100 people in a survey. To put it simply, you might find yourself trying to guess the TOP3 "countries with the most red hair" and, just after that, the TOP3 "objects that can be sucked".

Dynamic and hilarious, all players play at the same time. If you don't have the answers, try anyway! A little misunderstanding can go a long way. Bonus rounds add spice to the game and help you decide between the two.

At the end of the 8 rounds, it's time to add up. Who will be the grand champion of the rankings? At Zenagame, we're willing to bet that this title will soon be up for grabs.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Organize your own rock festival with your buddies and meager resources. Select the best bands of the moment for your program. Recruit technicians, organize refreshments, security, set up the stage, handle communications and find funding. Avoid the hassle and enjoy the festival!

Rock festival: Crescendo is a cooperative game. Players start the game with 20 resources and a motivated team of 15 volunteers.
Depending on the number of players, you have 8, 10 or 12 rounds to recruit 10 of the most popular artists from around the world.
During his turn, each player must select a card from among 6 cards (the river, which will be reconstituted at the end of each turn), paying for the necessary resources or volunteers. Each type of card allows you to gain either fame (by recruiting artists), or additional resources or volunteers. Additionally, by collecting cards of the same type you'll get bonus points at the end of the game. But beware, some cards (galleys) will get in your way, and you'll have to spend your resources and volunteers to discard them as soon as possible: accumulating 4 galley cards in the river will make you lose the game.

The game is won if, at the end of your festival, you have at least as many Resources and Volunteers as at the start, i.e. 20 Resources and 15 Volunteers available to organize the next edition of the festival.

The artists you've recruited during your festival will determine your degree of victory.

Break the (d)ice from the floe! Try to score it before it melts away!

Will you keep a cool head or will your points run between your fingers?

In this quick dice game mass isn't everything. Because even if you gain the most ice in the first phase (breaking), you might lose it all in the second phase of the game (scoring).

At the beginning a 4x4 (d)ice floe floats between you. With the fire dice you break ice from the floe and gain it. And if you separate single dice from the rest you also gain those!

Once all the ice has been collected, you continue to the scoring phase. In this you try to turn your ice into points. But if you risk too much, it might melt instead. And even the other players threaten your ice. If they roll doubles, some of your ice might melt! Only a shield token can save you ... hopefully you've gained one in the breaking phase!

Eisbrecher can be played by families but it certainly rewards forward thinking and thoughtful risk taking.
A three-in-one: A wine trivia game, a 64-page booklet of wine trivia, and a wine tasting notebook.

A fun, 2-player, head to head game. With 2 ways to play: choose a pre-made team (8 fictional teams) and the roster that comes with them OR if you have more time, I recommend the draft mode. During the draft, you draft a total of 12 players and 1 Coach. After you have your team, it’s football in a card game(with dice). Offense cards, Defense cards, and “Extra” cards, which involve penalties, injuries, and add-ons to make your players better, which are able to be bought with a shop system with the in-game currency that you acquire throughout the game. Many other interesting things that affect real-life football is also implemented, such as Status Affects(Rain,Snow,etc.) , among other things like Homefieled Advantage, or Team Chemistry can affect how you choose to draft your team.

—description from the designer

A game where the players each choose one of their cards to be the caption for the picture chosen by the judge. The judge then decides which caption is the funniest, and the player who played that card gets a point. In the next round, the player to the judge's right becomes the new judge. Play until you want to stop, and the player with most points wins.

The Devourers are a nightmarish cosmic plague that sweeps through the galaxy, leaving only desolation in their wake as they consume every civilization they encounter.

Includes:
1 new underboss card
2 new boss cards
1 new event card

boardgame: Warp-Lancer: Promo Christmas 2023 – A Kind Universe
description:
Amidst cosmic threats and adversities, beacons of hope shine across the galaxy. Generous alien races, driven by a spirit of unity, offer their belongings to spread peace. Diverse beings unite against the darkness, embodying the enduring force of kindness in the face of lethal danger.

Includes:
2 new events
1 new pilot
1 new Augment

Gee-Haw is a solitaire game that uses a standard poker deck (with jokers) and the Gee-Haw score board and pegs. The challenge is to get all the pegs to either the red or the black side of the score board, based on the suit color of the numbered cards and the "wild card" powers of the face cards. You decide how to utilize most of the card values, but a few have unavoidable consequences. Can you accomplish the goal before the deck runs out?

—description from the designer

Bourgeois and Benefactor promo cards for Coup.

The Bourgeois: Take 4 coins. Other players can also declare to influence the Bourgeois. After all declarations are made, disputes (if any) are resolved clockwise from the active player. The active player gives 1 coin for each declarer who was not successfully challenged.

The Benefactor: Take 3 coins from the player with the most coins and give them away for the player with the fewest coins. In case of a tie, the active player decides who the target is.

Rock Paper Scissors Royale is a 1vs1 competitive card game where your team takes over 12 territories by winning rounds of classic Rock Paper Scissors.

Shuffle the cards and distribute them equally, place the territories face down, turning over one territory each round.
The territories have different colors to decide draws.

Each turn players will play a card from their hand at the same time. If both players play the same card, it is a draw and the cards are discarded. The player that wins takes their opponent's card and adds both cards to their win pile.

After all cards for the round have been played, the player with the most cards in the win pile, wins the round a can place their team token on the territory. Once all territories have been taken, players can challenge for the lands until 1 player occupies the whole map.

—description from the publisher

Our first ever Boba Shop Collaboration - with none other than Tastea - the popular California Franchise that's won many hearts with their delectable drinks, punny names, and cute mascot!

Tastea friends join the Sabobatage universe in this brand new expansion pack introducing new mechanics that enhance gameplay. A new draw pile called the Employee Roster - allows the hiring of employees across the board allowing their special abilities to help you in your goal of building 5 boba drinks!

This add-on includes a new playable faction: Portugal. This add-on contains the following components:
Portugal:
- 17 battle cards.
- 1 unique Tactic card of the faction.
- 7 cards of Portuguese colonies.
- 15 control markers.
- 15 coins.
- 1 Portuguese player board.

The add-ons are not autoplayable. You need a copy of the base game Colonial 1700-1800 to play.

This add-on contains a total of 20 new politicians, which allow players to customize their political board, adding almost infinite replayability: games focused on naval strategy, games focused on economics (less confrontation), games focused on weakened defenses (more confrontation), etc.

This add-on allow players to set up boundary conditions of the game looking for a positive strategy (politicians who boost you) or a negative strategy (politicans who harm the rivals).

The Carnatic Wars scenario takes you to the struggle for hegemony in India between 1746 and 1763.

It includes a new scenario involving Great Britain and France (playable with just Colonial 1700-1800 base game, no need of Great Battles add-on).

The content of this scenario is listed below:
- 8 New colonies
- 2 Scenario cards
- 3 Battle cards
- 3 Standard cards for the Political Board.

Free for followers of the campaign.

The follower gift is made up of two (2) promo cards that will increase players' tactical options.

The early bird will allow you to burn down a colony's production buildings, causing great damage to the opponent's economy. 
Catches the worm allows you to do the opposite: cancel the effects of that incursion or garrison the colony with a levy of militiamen to be better prepared for attacks.

Memorfe is a board game with universal accessibility that seeks to enhance your memory through the use of strategies. In this Memorfe, having the memory of an elephant is not necessary to win, but using you memory strategically is.

And the best thing... is that there are three games in one!


     Kermorfe: a memory race where not everything is speed.
     Domemorfe: Connect the cards with a mechanic similar to dominoes.
     Dismorfe: A strategic auction of shapes and textures.


Do you dare to play it?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bob Moog's Dad Jokes Card Game

A card game based on the collection of "Dad" jokes compiled by Bob Moog.

The game ends when a player has made 8 Completed Jokes and that player is the winner.

Contents: 110 Cards (55 Jokes, 55 Punch Lines), Instructions.

boardgame: Bob Moog's Dad Jokes: Holiday Edition

The game ends when a player has made 8 Completed Jokes and that player is the winner.

This add-on includes a new game mode (scenario mode) and 5 historical scenarios where you can recreate the greatest colonial wars of 18th century, as well as a new playable faction: United States. This add-on contains the following components:

Historical scenarios:

Pike & Piquet: The 30 Years War is a print and play game using the ancient French card game of Piquet to power a low complexity, sandbox-style wargame on the 30 years war. Heavily inspired by Fred Serval's A Very Civil Whist, the game utilizes trick taking to manipulate forces on the map and diplomatic maneuvering.

WEIGHT: 6/10
PLAYERS: 2
SOLITAIRE: 4/10

—description from the designer

description:
Duel Shogi is a mini version of Shogi. Traditional Shogi is a 9x9 spaced board and we use 20 pieces per player, playing time usually takes one hour or so.  It is very difficult for beginners and those who are unfamiliar with Japanese culture and Shogi rules.
I wanted to create a shortened version of Shogi for beginners.  Duel Shogi will enable anyone to learn the basic rules by simple, repeat play.  Each player starts with eight pieces, each of a different type. Thus the minimum board must be composed with 4x5 spaces.

—description from the publisher

Embark on an epic quest right from your pocket with Dudes & Dungeons, the compact and thrilling dungeon crawler that's ready to accompany you on all your adventures, big or small!

In Dudes & Dungeons you choose your from a pack of heroes, all having different abilities and effects that will help you to fight the monsters that you encounter. 
Roll some dice, assign the numbers in a clever way and cast your hero's effects to outsmart skeletons, goblins and much more on your way through 6 levels of dungeon madness.

Will you become the toughest dude in the dungeon and survive?

—description from the publisher

Square City, the compact pocket-sized board game of strategic urban planning! In this thrilling head-to-head challenge, players collaboratively construct a bustling cityscape and try to mark the bigger areas in their respective color.

Square City is played with a deck of squared cards, which are alternately played to build a city. Once the city grows the players can place their colored cubes in certain areas to mark their territory. Once all cards are played and the city is complete, the player with the biggest area wins. Easy, isn´t it?

Are you ready to shape the skyline and outwit your rival in Square City?

—description from the designer

After rolling a D12, Players draw shapes on their maps to represent terrain and score points.
Each of the 8 different terrain types score differently at the end of the game.

Each Round has 4 Phases:

     Select Terrain and Polyomino Cards.
     Roll the D12 Die to determine where shapes can be drawn.
     Draw the selected Polyomino shape onto the 12x12 map grid.
     Clean up.


—description from the designer

boardgame: The Wars of the Sun King 1648-1713
description:
This the Deluxe and augmented version of Les Guerres du Roi Soleil 1667-1713 (The Wars of the Sun King 1667-1713) published in 2019 by Cerigo Edition.
This the first opportunity to play, with a single set of rules, the five conflicts led by France under the reign of Louis XIV the Great and the great rebellion called “La Fronde”.

La Fronde Rebellion (1648-1653) – taking advantage of the weakness of royal power while Louis, the future Louis XIV, was still a minor, the parliament of Paris and the nobility revolted at different periods and to different degrees against the regency ensured by Anne of Austria (widow of Louis XIII) and Cardinal Mazarin, hated by all. This weakening of France is a boon for Spain, still at war with France, to recover territories (Catalonia, Roussillon, the North of France).

The War of Devolution (1667-1668) - Spain’s non-payment of the Infanta of Spain’s dowry at her marriage to Louis XIV was used as a pretext for a war of conquest. This was the first conflict initiated by Louis XIV against a militarily and economically weakened Spain.

The Franco-Dutch War (1672-1678) - France wants to bring the Dutch Republic to its knees. This fierce commercial competitor, too tolerant as a state, opposes France’s expansion toward the Spanish Netherlands. This war for glory pitches against each other living legends of the Thirty Years War like Turenne, Condé et Montecuccoli. As a result of this conflict, the King of France earns the nickname Louis the Great.

The War of the Reunions (1683-1684) - Spain does not accept the annexation by France of enclaves and surrounding areas in the Spanish Netherlands, stating their boundaries were poorly defined by the international treaties ending the War of Devolution and the Franco-Dutch War. Vauban recommends limiting the territorial enclaves, leaning against the mountainous area and securing the enclaves by constructing strongholds; it’s a matter of turf.

The War of the League of Augsburg (1688-1697) - The Habsburg’s victory against the Ottomans diverted the Empire’s attention on the French territorial gains following the War of the Reunions. In 1687, Louis XIV wanted to transform the Truce of Ratisbon into a permanent accord and send an ultimatum to the emperor who refused. The German princes and the great European powers form an alliance to counter France’s politics of expansionism and religious persecution. France finds itself diplomatically isolated.

The War of Spanish Succession (1701-1713) - King Charles II of Spain dies with no descendants. The two principal reigning families of Europe, the Bourbons (France) and the Habsburgs (Austria), are both related to Charles II and claim the throne. The principal stake is the domination of Europe by seizing the enormous heritage of the Spanish Habsburgs. France is confronted with its fiercest military enemies: Marlborough and Prince Eugene of Savoy.
Maximise your resource points to recruit armies, build strongholds, lead sieges and battles and win glory points.
Will you be able to vanquish Montecuccoli, Eugene of Savoy or Marlborough with Turenne, Condé, Villars and impose the Sun King’s will? Or Vauban will be the nemesis of all enemies ‘citadels?

The game includes :
• One 59 x 85 cm MOUNTED (!) map
• 432 double-sided, pre-cut counters and markers
• A scenario booklet and a rulebook 
• Many player aids

-description from designer

description: Dragonvault: Evernight Vale is a standalone game in the Dragonvault line and can be played alone or combined with its sister games Dragonvault and Dragonvault: Voyage of Peril.  In this iteration, game play is the same as Dragonvault, though with the addition of the new freeze mechanic. eight new champions, and the dual treasure/action rune cards.

Dragonvault: Voyage of Peril is a standalone game in the Dragonvault line and can be played alone or combined with its sister games Dragonvault and Dragonvault: Evernight Vale.  In this iteration, game play is the same as Dragonvault, though with the addition of an alternate win condition, eight new champions, and a new recruitable companion.

boardgame: 西游斗妖行 (Journey to the West)
description:
Journey to the West is a cooperative adventure game designed for 2-4 players. Players can choose to embody any of the four characters from Tang Monk's entourage, collectively immersing themselves in the rich mythological narrative of the Westward journey. Utilize strategic thinking, teamwork, and individual abilities to defeat a multitude of supernatural foes and overcome the predetermined trials along the perilous pilgrimage.

Gameplay

1. Explore

Draw a terrain card and place it on the game board, then resolve the event depicted on the card. This represents the various encounters and challenges faced while exploring the path ahead.

2. Player Actions

Players can choose from six different actions, each potentially consuming action points:

     Meditate: Spend one action point to randomly draw a Five Elements magic.
     Acquire Skill Cards: Spend one action point and 1-6 magic to obtain powerful skill cards.
     Move: Spend one action point and 1-6 magic to move to an adjacent location displaying a terrain with the same magic cost.
     Attack: Spend one action point to roll combat dice and attack a local demon.
     Activate Skill: Execute the effect of a skill card, discarding it afterward.
     Special Chapter Action: Each chapter features a unique action.


3. Player Action Resolution

Resolve the chosen actions of all players.

4. Demon Actions

The next player takes on the role of controlling all demons, initiating attacks on the players according to specific rules.

5. Demon Resolution

Resolve demon attacks or skill effects based on dice results.

description:
Description:

"Lucky Dog" is a charming tabletop game where players join forces to construct shops and welcome the arrival of auditors bringing fortune! This engaging board game set in the Year of the Rabbit features playful elements, including jingling bells, in a rich Chinese cultural theme. Players embark on a journey similar to Monopoly, rolling dice, building houses, and accumulating wealth. The introduction of "purchase card" actions adds an element of surprise, making the game accessible to all ages and perfect for family play.

Key Learning Objectives:

Spatial Imagination
Logical Reasoning
Planning Skills
Flexible Thinking
Concentration
Creativity

Game Components:

Game Board x1
Player Aid Cards x4
Action Cards x62
Wealth Deity Figure x1
Card Stands x4
Dice x1
Copper Coins (1 Yuan denomination) x30
Silver Bills (5 Yuan denomination) x8
Silver Bills (10 Yuan denomination) x16
House Models x40

Gameplay Overview:

Players start with 3 copper coins each and a set of 10 houses. The objective is to accumulate wealth by buying and building houses, with the Wealth Deity's progress determining the income. Players take turns rolling the dice, moving the Wealth Deity, and earning income based on the street where the deity stops. The game involves strategic choices, including buying cards and houses, and utilizing special card effects.

Victory Condition:

The first player to accumulate 50 units of currency emerges as the lucky winner in "Lucky Dog."

Additional Notes:

The game features a thematic Chinese design with elements like copper coins and a national-style audit figurine.
Gameplay involves a balance of dice rolling, resource management, and strategic decision-making.
Suitable for players of all ages, with simple rules and engaging components.

Two modules to add to your Rauha universe:

- Discover the Shaman cards that open up new possibilities.  Vary your games and your strategies with 7 new Divine Entity tiles.

- Tiny Creatures lurking in the shadows or at the bottom of lakes are appearing.  New cults are emerging, related to the Biomes and the recently occurring fauna.

boardgame: H.D.P.: Lo mejor del H.D.P. – Dibujame
description: Promotional micro expansion with new cards, taken from the standalone game H.D.P.: Hasta Donde Puedas – Dibujame.
Promotional micro expansion with new food-related cards.

Sangrevores are a new type of enemy race for Nemesis: Retaliation. There is no Queen this time - but a mighty "King", an ancient being awakened from a long slumber. Mining scavengers found a mysterious-looking "capsule" created out of non-human bones stacked together as if multiple beings had created this tomb from their own bodies. It was quickly transported to the closest human base for further investigation. This is how it all started. 
 This expansion introduces a totally new mechanic that can drastically change the semi-co-op experience. During the contact with the King or Blood Spectres, your character might become converted! This can entirely change your secret objective mid-game without other players not being sure about it. 
 With some of the attacks, you will get special infection cards (added next to your special objective face down) that only you can see. Most of the time, they will be neutral - but sometimes, they can transform you into Hemodyne yourself and shift your objective. Contamination cards are changing. Instead of a standard contamination card that will take a slot - you will actually get some unique ability representing your character getting mutated. This will happen only with contact with Crimson Despot or Blood Spectres.

Those cards will give a toolset for players to increase negative interactions - only if they wish for it. But combined with the possibility of them being turned, it will add new layers of paranoia to the game.

—description from the Gamefound campaign

Take on the role of legendary leaders as you fight to defend your territories and eliminate your foes in this chaotic card game of conquering. With a unique blend of action cards, attacks, and ambushes, every turn is filled with suspense and intrigue.

But beware - losing your last territory draws a dreaded

Outwit your opponents, defend your lands, and seize victory in this fast-paced game of skill, cunning, and alliance.

Victorus  in Latin means conqueror and now springs to life as a highly tactical medieval abstract game for up to 6 players. Each player controls a chess-style army consisting of a king, a queen, and a variety of supporting knights. The object of the game is to maneuver one of your royalty around the hexagonal board then reach the center space.

The hex board offers a variety of movement options for each type of piece and, combined with the rule that capturing an opponent's piece awards an extra turn, creates an ever expanding web of possibility. Knights are themselves promoted to royalty after their second capture and gain increased mobility with a second move every turn.
The drama increases when the royal families embark on their journeys of destiny to conquer the central territory and become MAGNA VICTORUS.

Cobble is a hidden words game for one or more players. Similar to Boggle, the game is about cobbling letters into words. It is language-independent.

There are two versions: Standalone and Scrabble add-on. The Scrabble version uses your own Scrabble tiles.

COMPONENTS: 

     6-inch lasercut game board with inset grid
     10 arrow tiles
     4 dry erase game boards
     4 dry erase markers (with extra addon)
     26 letter tiles or 26 of your own Scrabble tiles.


GOAL: 
Make the highest scoring word each round. Have the best score after ten rounds.

 
SETUP:  

     Randomly layout 25 tiles face up in the grid.
     Scatter the arrows face down
     Pick up at least three arrows and place in their corresponding locations in the grid.
     Give each player a dry erase marker and dry erase board.


GAME PLAY:  
Playing simultaneously, players have one to several minutes to each “cobble” together a word. After the time limit, each player calls out their word and their score. 
Mark your score on your dry erase board.
A word consists of one letter from each of the marked vertical columns and/or horizontal rows.

FORMING WORDS:
To form a word, collect one letter from each line marked with an arrow. See the examples in the images.

PLAY IN ROUNDS:  
After each round, shuffle and swap at least six letter tiles. Remove the arrow tiles. Decide upon 3-6 arrows for this round and randomly select and place a new set of arrows. 
Ensure that one of the lines contains a vowel.
 
ENDING THE GAME:
The game is over after ten rounds.

WINNING THE GAME:
The winner will have the most points. If there is a tie, play another round.

TIPS:
Use the space at the bottom of your dry erase board to write eligible letters. You may need to unscramble the letters.

FAQS:

     Do the letters need to be in a specific order or follow the numbers 1-10? NO


—description from the publisher

GAME 9AM is move-oriented social game for 3-5 players. It is derived by extending of popular social game 'mills'
Each player gets 9 tokens of corresponding color
Game can be divided into two phases: phase 1 - putting tokens and phase 2 - moving tokens
PHASE 1 - PUTTING TOKENS
In this phase players put tokens on the table. Token can be put on any free field
If you make a mill by putting token, a player removes one of the tokens of any other player - see 'MILL MAKING AND REMOVING OF TOKENS
PHASE 2 - MOVING TOKENS
In this phase players move their tokens from current to neighbour free field. It is allowed to move any of the tokens
If the player is left with 3 tokens, he can move any of the left tokens to any free field - 'jumping'
If no token can be moved, no moved is made, a player is stepped over and next player is to move
If a mill is made by moving a token, a player will remove one token of any other player - see paragraph below
MILL MAKING AND REMOVING OF TOKENS
A mill is made by putting or moving token and making a line of three tokens of the same color. (in 5 players game, a mill is for example 1-4-7)
Player who made a mill will obtained number of cards equivalent to number of 'neighbouring' mills
(for ex. if a player in 5 players game has tokens on fields 1,4,7,10,11,52 and 57 he will get 3 cards - because of mills 1-4-7, 7-10-57 and 52-11-57)
Also, if by removing of tokens a player is left with less then 3 tokens, a player who removed his last token is rewarded with 2 additional cards
Player that is left with 2 tokens is expelled from game (and these 2 tokens are removed, too)
After making a mill, a player has the right to remove one of the opponents' tokens
Token that is about to be removed must not be in 'mill'. If all tokens are 'in mill' no token will be removed and next player is to move
POINTS
Every player starts game with 10/15/20 points depending of game type (for 3/4/5 players respectively) - 'basic points'
Besides basic points, points are calculated as difference from average number of cards obtained
(for ex. for 5 players game and for 30 total cards obtained and 14 cards obtained for this player. Then, points number is B = 10 x (14 - 30:5) = 80 points)

Arkham Asylum is an expansion that brings Batman’s legendary rogues gallery to the game, in the form of both Super Heroes and Zombie Heroes. These Super-Villains will have to make use of their most devious tactics in the fight against Anti-Life, especially since it’s not only their former accomplices that are now undead, but also the hulking Titan-infused inmates.

A cooperative game of animal deduction. One player has a clue card specifying three spots in a nine card grid of animals. They draw three animal cards and place each as "similar" or "dissimilar". Then all the other players discuss and agree on an animal they think fits.

If they get it wrong, they flip a guess token and turn the animal sideways to indicate it's the wrong one - then the clue-giver draws three more cards to use as clues.

If they get it right, the clue-giver resets the guess tokens and clue cards and moves on to animal two.

Find all three animals without running out of guesses to win the game!

Crossword Rummy is a word card game that appeared in 1942 or earlier. Its deck of solitaire-sized cards (2.5" x 1.75") include 55 letter cards and 3 wild cards.  The rules book has roles for these games:

CROSSWORD RUMMY 
During your turn you play cards that add a word to a growing crossword on the table, or draw a card. The first player to play all cards from their hand gets the points on all the cards of the other players. First to 250 points wins.

MEMORY WORDGAME
A version of Concentration. All the cards are spread out face down on the table. During your turn you reveal 4 cards, and if you can spell a 3- or 4-letter word you keep its cards and turn the rest face down. When all cards are used or no other words can be made, the player with the most cards wins.

WORD CASINO
Like Logomachy or Casino. Four cards are played face up, and 4 cards dealt to each player. On your turn you take a card from your hand and either make a word with it and cards on the table, or leave it face up on the table. When all hands are played out, deal 4 more to each player. When the deck is gone and all hands emptied, the player with the most cards wins.

WORD POKER
Five cards are dealt to each player, who may then exchange up to three of them at a cost of -10 points per card. The player who can make the longest, most-points word gets 10 points for each unused card of the other players. First to 250 wins.

WORD SOLITAIRE
Like Canfield solitaire, with 28 cards in 7 piles, and then revealing the rest one at a time. From the visible cards you can make new words and add to previous words.

Come Game Time it all comes down to the Final 5 plays of the game and winning depends on more than just luck! You'll need a combination of strong roster stats, reliable playbook strategic options as well as good ol human player-on-player bluff and anticipation.

Final 5 let's you tailor your team. Build a 2 point scoring powerhouse, a 3 point sniper squad, a defensive juggernaut or a balanced squad of all three. A middleing team roster with great coaching and great player decision making can still win Championships.

At the end of the season, the higher your team standing, the easier your playoff progression will be. On the flip side, you are always in with a chance to have a late surge to glory!

A war strategy-based board game for 3-4 players set in the fantasy realm. Players must outwit their rivals to seize coveted territories, all while fending off the relentless advance of the Undead. One wrong decision could plunge your ambition into the chilling realm of the undead.

Embark on an epic journey in this dynamic war strategy board game, where cunning cooperation meets fierce competition. As a determined prince in a chilling realm plagued by undead, joins forces with an ally to claim strategic regions and outsmart both rivals and the relentless undead. Every move becomes a strategic dance, testing loyalty and tactical wit.

In the icy embrace of winter, unity is your strength against the dual challenges of betrayal and undead forces. The throne awaits the shrewdest tactician in this riveting battle of minds, where every decision shapes your destiny. Will you emerge victorious through clever cooperation or cunning competition?

Game Highlight

Liquor Ceremony 
In an intriguing twist, players also have the option to forge alliances through a liquor ceremony, fostering mutual benefits throughout the game, which takes place during spring.

Summer Explore
The game then commences in summer, granting each player the opportunity to select an action phase. Players might also inspect all the cities to explore skill cards and relics.

The War Begin
As autumn arrives, the battleground ignites with the clash of rival princes.  Get ready for the excitement to unfold, as players engage in negotiations, deploy their skills, and unleash powerful relics, all in the pursuit of winning this war.

Undead Rising
The war ends and the snow has falls, it’s cold embrace serves as a poignant reminder that victory comes at a price. During winter, players must prepare for the imminent awakening of the undead, who will now launch attacks against all the players. Can you take the advantage in this chaotic season?

—description from the designer
A carnivorous plant becomes a playable character, equipped with vines, vines, lots of vines and a giant man-eating maw.
A golden golem is added as a playable character, having no need for weapons with its indestructible exterior, which must be peeled away to reveal the soft coppery center to be defeated.
An immortal vampire becomes a playable character, equipped with various antique weapons-but a master of each.
A horde of angry rats is added as a playable character. They don't have any weapons, but they attack in such numbers they can reduce a man to a skeleton in a minute.

boardgame: Star Blazers: A Solitaire Game of the Quest for Iscandar
description:
This game is based on the highly successful anime titled "Space Battleship Yamato", which was initially broadcast in 1974. At the time of its first broadcast, the anime did not gain much popularity for various reasons, but later it became a huge hit thanks to television reruns and film screenings. This game is inspired by "Space Battleship Yamato" and puts you in the shoes of Captain Okita, commanding the Yamato. Your goal is to guide the ship to Iscandar, obtain a radiation removal device, and return to Earth before humanity is exterminated.

The original game was created in 1983 during the summer vacation of the third year of middle school as a solitaire game. This version was reposted based on my memory. At the time I had no intention of letting anyone else play it or releasing it anywhere, and the rules varied every time I played it. I hope you can face it with a great spirit.

MadMars offers a seamless experience for players, ensuring newcomers can easily grasp the mechanics and have plenty of fun, while seasoned gamers can make the game as intricate and complex as they wish. Notably, MadMars is inclusively designed to accommodate colorblind players by incorporating ColorADD.

Welcome to the legendary world of Aventuria, a realm where every dice roll plunges you deeper into an epic adventure filled with magic and mysteries. Adventures in Aventuria is not just a game, but a gateway into a world where you, as a brave leader, assemble a group of heroes to conquer fabulous adventures and gain glory.

Suitable for 2 to 4 players aged 8 and up, this game is a journey through fantastic landscapes and adventures beyond imagination. Each game, lasting about 30 minutes, is a unique experience full of strategy and excitement.

The Saga of the Game:

     The Path of Discovery: Each turn begins with a roll of the fate dice, determining how far your heroes can travel through the mysterious regions of Aventuria. Choose your path wisely, as every decision can alter the fate of your heroes.
     The Alliance of Heroes: In the recruitment regions of Aventuria, you meet brave companions. Win these heroes for your cause and form an unbeatable team.
     The Challenge of Adventures: Face the adventures lurking in the mysterious lands in each round. Skillfully use your heroes to collect victory points and pave the way to triumph.
     Rewriting Fate: If you find yourself in a region where neither recruitment nor adventure is possible, seize the opportunity to rewrite fate by wisely exchanging your hero cards.


Dive into Adventures in Aventuria and experience a world where every game writes a new chapter in an unforgettable legend. Gather your heroes, master the adventures, and write your own story in the world of Aventuria!

—description by publisher

Willkommen in der legendären Welt von Aventurien, einem Reich, in dem jeder Würfelwurf dich tiefer in ein episches Abenteuer voller Magie und Mysterien führt. Abenteuer in Aventurien ist nicht nur ein Spiel, sondern ein Tor in eine Welt, in der du als mutiger Anführer eine Gruppe von Helden zusammenstellst, um sagenhafte Abenteuer zu bestehen und Ruhm zu erlangen.

Für 2 bis 4 Spieler ab 8 Jahren ist dieses Spiel eine Reise durch fantastische Landschaften und Abenteuern jenseits der Vorstellungskraft. Jede Partie, die etwa 30 Minuten dauert, ist ein einzigartiges Erlebnis voller Strategie und Spannung.

Die Saga des Spiels:

     Der Pfad der Entdeckung: Jeder Zug beginnt mit einem Wurf des Schicksalswürfels, der bestimmt, wie weit deine Helden durch die geheimnisvollen Regionen Aventuriens reisen können. Wähle deinen Pfad weise, denn jede Entscheidung kann das Schicksal deiner Helden ändern.
     Das Bündnis der Helden: In den Rekrutierungsregionen Aventuriens triffst du auf mutige Gefährten. Gewinne diese Helden für deine Sache und bilde ein unschlagbares Team.
     Die Herausforderung der Abenteuer: Stelle dich in jeder Runde den Abenteuern, die in den geheimnisvollen Ländern lauern. Nutze deine Helden geschickt, um Siegpunkte zu sammeln und den Weg zum Triumph zu ebnen.
     Das Schicksal neu schreiben: Solltest du dich in einer Region befinden, in der weder Rekrutierung noch Abenteuer möglich sind, nutze die Gelegenheit, das Schicksal neu zu schreiben, indem du deine Heldenkarten weise austauschst.


Tauche ein in Abenteuer in Aventurien und erlebe eine Welt, in der jedes Spiel ein neues Kapitel in einer unvergesslichen Legende schreibt. Sammle deine Helden, meistere die Abenteuer und schreibe deine eigene Geschichte in der Welt von Aventurien!

—description from the publisher

Mr. MARK is a card game that offers numerous play options in a single deck. A playing card with no numbers made of 64 cards with 4 types of eyes, shapes, and colors providing various enjoyable activities.

Mr. MARK cards lack numerical values allowing for gameplay centered around recognizing "same" and "different." This feature makes it accessible for young children who may not yet grasp numbers!

Embark on this asteroid mining adventure, a groundbreaking fusion of trick-taking and map exploration. Your goal: play drill cards matching the target tile's color, snatch tiles and crystals, and strategize with six dynamic actions during your turn.

Enhance your mining prowess with gadgets, offering wild hand colors, double card plays, expanded hand size, and more. Brace yourself for incidents triggered by deck depletion and unexpected monster appearances on the map. Success could mean a windfall, but survival is the key.

Salad Master Smoothie is a board game that allows modern individuals facing a vegetable deficiency to easily consume vegetables. Building on the previous game, "Sarada Master," it includes carefully selected vegetables.

The rules involve deducing and pointing to vegetables with the correct quantity according to a given theme. However, some vegetables are only visible by name, requiring a bit of attention.

The nutritional content of the vegetables on the cards is based on the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology's Japanese Food Standard Composition Table 2020 (8th edition).

Shareholders is a cooperative 4-player card game. It is played with a deck of standard playing cards and 12 additional tokens(3 'shares' per suit). Players form 2 teams of 2-players, and compete for higher scores by owning shares of more influential suits.

While proceeding with typical trick-taking format, each player has an option of taking a share from one of the suits, instead of playing a card from one's hand. Each suit is 'completed' as soon as all 4 cards with alphabets (J, Q, K, A) are played during the game, and gives 3, 4, 5 points per share to shareholders by being completed 1st, 2nd and 3rd respectively. The game ends as soon as 3rd completion of a suit is made. The team with higher sum of scores wins.

It’s time to play We're All Gonna Die - the game of dicing with death. You and your friends will need to work together in this strategic dice-rolling game of laugh-out-loud hilarity for grown-ups. Flip a card, roll the dice, and try to get the death-defying combination to cheat your untimely death. Work through your death cards and make it through all 20 cards to survive and live to see another day!

A set of four promo cards for the game "Smoki". Atak kruka(Raven attack)
The player that uses the card can put it in stack face up or use its special action: the player put it in theirs dream and  exchange any of cards among his/her dream with any of others player's cards among their dream.

Space FTR is the second print at home game from Moonbound Games, following the success of Skull Searchers. You are quested to gather as many Auras as possible (the primary source item that earth now desires).

You start with a fixed Energy amount and must survive from Mission 1 all the way to Mission 8. Missions have levels that you must get through without falling to 0 HP. There are explosions, hazards, traps, enemies, and bosses to fight BUT don't worry as you can collect energy to gain HP scattered throughout along with auras and rockets. As you journey through your missions you will gain experience (XP) that will allow you to learn skills and add more power & speed to your character. There is a Final Score at the end of the campaign where you can calculate your score. Replay again or compare with friends to see who can get the highest score.

"The Only Game Where Nothing Is Fair"

You'll get an awesome set of cards and an artisanally designed board. You'll travel up and down the 101, earning money and investors, pitching companies, and networking with other famous VCs and founders.

You'll stop in places like Marin County, where you'll smoke pot with someone famous and collect 5 Millions Dollars! Your ultimate goal is to get to Burning Man to host a transformational VIP Disruption Camp and collect 6 Investors and 10 Millions Dollars!

When you pick up a pitch card, or land on a pitch space, you and other 6 other VC players will pitch companies based on 2 cards that you pick up.

It’s your job to make your pitch convincing to the other players because they will all be investing a certain amount of their investment cards into your company, and if you don't sound like you know what you're talking about, it'll just be ...awkward. As you move around the board, you’ll be instructed to take certain actions, according to the networking cards you pick up every turn.

You can declare you are the Winner(!) when you get your portfolio of startups to IPO, or you earn 50 Millions Dollars and declare that the bubble has popped.

boardgame: Битвы Fantasy: Штурм каземата  (Storming the Casemate)
description:
This is an action game where both sides are trying to eliminate each other's soldiers. The game uses actual firing devices, similar to Crossbows and Catapults.

Players roll a die for actions and use them to either move pieces or fire. Any soldier that is knocked down is eliminated from the game. Players can also move their soldiers into contact with the enemy and fight melees. Players can also use magic cards to assist them.

Several versions of this game were made over the years.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Starter Commander Deck: First Flight
description:
Discover Magic's most popular format with the Starter Commander decks! This preconstructed deck of 100 unique cards is the best entry point to start playing Commander, a fun and epic multiplayer format! This deck is ready-to-play and will help you get familiar with the mechanics of Commander before you try customizing it!

This deck comes with several inserts allowing you to discover the strategy for your deck and to guide you through your first games in Commander.

First Flight is a White - Blue Deck with Isperia, Supreme Judge for Commander. Rule over the battlefield with your flying creatures and keep the game under control thanks to the many cards you draw!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Starter Commander Deck – Token Triumph
description:
Discover Magic's most popular format with the Starter Commander decks! This preconstructed deck of 100 unique cards is the best entry point to start playing Commander, a fun and epic multiplayer format! This deck is ready-to-play and will help you get familiar with the mechanics of Commander before you try customizing it!

This deck comes with several inserts allowing you to discover the strategy for your deck and to guide you through your first games in Commander.

Token Triumph is a White - Green deck with Emmara, Soul of the Accord as Commander. Creature a maximum of creature tokens and boost them to overwhelm your opponents

Motór GT is not just a card and board social game. It's a nostalgic Grand Tour to the golden days of motorsport, the primacy of mechanics over electronics, and design that rubs up against art. It's a way to compete with a group of petrolhead friends and help pass on the passion to your child.

Motór GT includes:

     100 cards of iconic cars
     race track
     wooden pawns


Such a set allows 2 modes of gameplay:
1. MOTO QUIZ
Automotive quiz will test your knowledge and allow you to learn new, unknown facts about the legendary models.
2. RACE
Classic race on an oval track will select the driver who best controls the speed, grip and condition of the car.

—description from the publisher

The election of a new manager of the farmstead is imminent. Animals have begun preparing programs. It won't be long before spectacular speeches, election spots, fierce discussions, slander and even sabotage actions will begin!

Will you manage to take part in this brutal game and survive until election day?

Here is a game for 2 to 4 players, where you will finally be able to prove to your rivals that they have no idea about politics! During the gameplay you will create your own decks of cards, full of sharp and clever plays. The winner will be the one who most effectively reaches out to his constituents and accumulates the most Votes....

Or simply devastate the image of your competitors!

—description from the publisher

Set of four extra family cards and 1 extra border card, in as well English as Spanish (so 10 cards in total).

The four family cards are: Ocateph, Garridephen, Tor-reshem & Manu-martop.

The border card has the Spiel-logo on it and abducts servants.

A must-follow trick-taking game themed around the Sengoku period. The lead player is called the "general" and the following players are called "vassals" and they aim to acquire territory by playing cards and going into battle.
publishers: 静岡テーブルゲーム工房 (Shizuoka Table Game Studio）

description:
Postrånet is a standalone game that picks up where Afrikan tähti (Den försvunna diamanten) leaves off. The game is inspired by the true story of the Star of Africa's journey to King Edward VII.

As a mail carrier, your mission is to deliver the diamond to the king without any robbers catching up with you. The winner is the player who manages to deliver diamonds on the king's birthday.

Each player gets 10 dice.

The main objective is to get all 10 of your dice to show the same number before any other player.

The set of 40 dice can be played with other other variants of dice rolling games.

World of Snooker allows you to stage matches between the sixteen All-Time-Greats or current players who are World-ranked using the provided Ranking to Ratings Chart.

World of Snooker is an attempt to re-create the excitement of a world class snooker match. It is designed, in the usual replay game fashion, to be played solo but can equally well be played head-to-head. Whilst dice rolls clearly control the outcome there is room for some tactical play.

The simulation uses just three basic Player Ratings, Potting, Cue Ball Control and Break Building. A frame is played out shot-by-shot but the result of that frame can then be used to determine the score in frames for any session (from 3 to 10 frames).

With a core design of making your table top group burst into a fit of giggles, the game of Merch'Entice has players take turns role-playing as the Hero on a quest while the remaining players become Merchants!

Each round starts with the Hero beginning their random or ridiculous quest through drawing a Quest Card; meanwhile, the Merchants, with their hands full of cards, will carefully craft an enticing item by combining two types of cards, the Enchantment Card and Item Card!

When the Merchants have finished forging their merchandise, the market opens and the Hero will be tasked with visiting each Merchant individually; browsing the merchants' wares and conversing with them to find the item that will most suit them on their Quest! Each Merchant will have a chance to entice the Hero with their goods and earn that precious coin!

Inside Merch'Entice, the three unique cards - Quests, Enchantments, and Items - will provide the means for all sorts of market themed role-playing mayhem!

—description from the publisher

Can Maths be funny? Yes!

In this fast cards game players compete for uniting colors and identify the signs, trying to be the first to solve the problems and getting the right answer.

Secure The Loot is a 2-8 player point based card game where each player aims to get 50 points on the play field to obtain a loot. There is a draw pile, discard pile, and character pile. At the beginning each player chooses one of four suits: Swords, wands, coins, and cups. Each of the four have different playstyles. They then draw a character and keep it hidden until they choose to use the characters special ability. They then draw 5 cards and draw one each turn after every first turn has been completed.

One can play a single point card and action card each turn unless bypassed by another ability. There are 5 action cards within the draw pile that can be played by everyone and there are suit specific action cards such as the ace of swords, that can only be played if one chose swords as their suit at the beginning of the game.

The character you have determines how many loot you need to win the game, 2 loot win characters have weaker abilities than 3 loot win characters. But once a loot is obtained one can choose to draw a new character and shuffle their old character back into the character pile.

Instead of there being one winner everyone continues to play for placement.

—description from the designer

Agents of the History Correction Syndicate, we need you! There has been a disruption in the timeline and Mr. William Shakespeare is suffering the consequences. While we're sure schoolchildren around the world would prefer it this way, we simply cannot allow it. You must rescue the barred Bard before it's too late!

Step back in time and immerse yourselves in the world of Elizabethan England. You and your fellow agents must work together to restore the reputation of William Shakespeare who has been accused of-gasp!-larceny and plagiarism! Use your combined powers of logic, wit, and perseverance to solve the puzzles and prove his innocence. If you fail, history will be rewritten. But succeed, and the true timeline will be restored!

Perfect for friends, families, puzzle solvers, and Escape Room fanatics, it's suitable for ages 12+, two or more players, and has an average 60-minute gameplay. Plus, unlike most escape puzzle games, Timescape: Save Shakespeare is entirely reusable and re-giftable as the content is not damaged during play . . . as long as you use a pencil!

—description from publisher

A monkey-themed observational card game.
Match the turned card in the middle with the position (of the monkey) on another visible card and the colour (of the background) on another visible card. 
Be the first to make the match and you lose one of your cards, make a mistake and you gain a card.
First player to get rid of all their cards, wins.

Hack Comics! is a solo game where you experience a year in the life of a comic book company, inventing new series, drawing comic covers, writing crazy synopses, pursuing subsequent issues, and finding crossover and spinoff opportunities. (Game does not include art: you're the artist. Sorry.)

This is conceived as a free “Make-as-You-Play” game, which means that the game starts with a deck of 50 blank index cards, which you will modify during the game. A 10 game campaign will finish out the deck, but you can always add new stacks of 50 for subsequent years!

At the end of the game, you score points based on the length of comic series, the number of crossovers, and the number of spinoffs.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tokyo vs Burrito: Taco vs Burrito Expansion Pack
description:
Tokyo vs Burrito Expansion Pack is an expansion pack for the popular Taco vs Burrito card game. It requires the core game to play, adding new dimensions and fun twists to your game nights.
More Players - This expansion pack allows for up to 8 players, making it a great choice for family game nights, gatherings with friends, or parties.

Additional Cards - The pack comes with 30 unique cards, providing more variety and unpredictability to your games.
Card Holders - Included are 4 additional card holders, providing convenience and organization during play.

—description from hte publisher

1v1 family orientated language-independent battle card game. With an overarching focus on inclusion and accessibility. Game play is between 2 players.

The two players set their avatars on one of the four grids on the battlefield. Two 15 card battle decks are set next to the battlefield. Three win cards aset to the left of set up. The youngest player starts the battle by flipping their first card. The action card has a picture that indicates the type of attack. Their is a black square on a four section grid that mimics the battlefield.

If the opponents avatar is located in the pictured section, the opponent takes one damage and they remove one of four paper clips. If the opponent is not located in the section pictured on the attack card the attack is a miss.The opponent successfully doges the attack. If the player pulling the action card draws a dodge card. Indicated by the card picture, they have the option of moving to the grid square pictured on the grid or they may stay in same position.

Game play continues with each player taking a turn and pulling an action card, shuffling when needed until one player removes all four paperclips. The winning player takes and retains one win card for the next battle round. The defeated player removes one win card from the board. The win card has an automatic hit or automatic dodge that can be used by interrupting game play at any time. The win card is set aside and retained to show the win of a previous round. Game play continues for three rounds or when one player collects two win cards.

-description from designer

A Kickstarter Promo Pack released with the Wizards & Relics Awakening Kickstarter in 2023. It contains the following:

21 Cards:


     3 Rules/Clarification Cards



     9 Wizards:
         Frank Unleashed
         Fred, Finder of Fair Things
         Edvard, Ancient Researcher
         Professor Hans
         Mr. Gryff, the Beastslayer
         Mola, the Moon Mage
         Undead Ed
         Gourdie
         Manny, the Absent Bodied Magician
    



     9 Relics:
         Staff of Silencing
         Hat of Illusion
         Peacemaker's Pipe
         Sapphire Saber
         Emerald Edge
         Ruby Reckoner
         Zombie Hand
         Dagger of Rebirth
         Brandywood Shrrom

A small 18 card mini-expansion for Wizards & Relics that was released during the Awakening Kickstarter campaign. The Merchant's Market cards can be used with either the original base set or with Awakening. It contains the following:

21 Cards:


     3 Rules/Clarification Cards



     9 Wizards:
         Hof, the Masterful Merchant
         Wes, the World Shaper
         Holli Rose, the Artisan
         Illustrious Illusionist
         The Juggler
         Slimy Swindler
         Merchant of Many Hats
         Pipe Peddler
         Merchant of Staves
    



     9 Relics:
         Lars' Staff
         Hat of Many Colors
         Queen's Pipe
         Goblin Finger Trap
         Tinkerer's Puzzle
         Trickster's Trinket
         Annor & The Twelve
         The Leaf of Divinity
         Chasing of the Wind

In the 16th century, Russians ventured beyond the Ural Mountains and initiated the conquest of Siberia. What attracted them to Siberia was the furs from small animals, that were crucial exports for Russia. In pursuit of these valuable pelts, Russians expanded  their territories eastward.

China, a prosperous land in the east, was also important, with its highly valued products. Moreover, when fur exports to Western Europe declined, China emerged as a major alternative market.

In this game, players aim to increase their wealth by skillfully utilized, their subordinate workers. Try your best to become the most renowned merchant in Siberia!

boardgame: Stranger Things: Upside Down – Eleven Dashboard Promo
description:
Originally available at 2023 Comic-Con International: San Diego as well as a print and play on the publisher's website.

Starting Location: Hawkings Laboratory
Movement: 1

At the start of your turn, you may move 1 foe to any location.
Help Eleven abilities can not be used by any character thoughout the game.

Chronicles of War is a compilation of some of the most sought after Warhammer articles from White Dwarf magazine including "Flying Monsters", "Lord of Dragons" and "War Machines". The book contains six battle reports featuring the massive four part studio campaign, and tactics articles from the White Dwarf team which will help you make the most of your army. Also included are three ready to assemble card buildings and new magic item cards, ideal for use in Warhammer battles, plus guidelines for running your own narrative campaign in the Warhammer World and much more!

スカイプロジェクト (Sky project) is a engine building card game from Japan.

The publisher says: 
The theme is a competition for the development of neutral balloons. Super exhilarating! High-speed engine-building card game!

Simple rules: play or draw cards.
All cards of the same colour as the card you play activate and draw a lot of cards! Massive scoring!
The engine-building experience is concentrated and can be played in a short time.
Also, if you play cards of all colours, you get the bonus of scoring a lot of points!
The dilemma is whether to play cards of the same colour to turn the engine or play cards of a different colour to score bonuses.

中性の気球開発競争がテーマ。
超爽快！ハイスピードエンジンビルドカードゲーム！

カードをプレイするか引くかの簡単ルール。
プレイしたカードと同じ色のカードは全て発動し大量ドロー！大量得点！
エンジンビルドの体験を濃縮し短時間で遊べます。
また、すべての色のカードをプレイすると大量得点のボーナス獲得！
同じ色のカードをプレイしてエンジンを回すか、別の色のカードをプレイしてボーナスを獲得するかのジレンマがあります。
--description by publisher

boardgame: Wandering Towers: Draw Two Cards Promo Card
description:
PROMO 2022
Also included in Wandering Towers: Mini Spell Expansion 2.

Effect:
If you use this magic spell, you may draw the top two cards from the draw pile. You may not draw cards again until you have less than 3 cards in your hand at the end of your turn.

This promo has printed German and English rules, but the tile itself has no text.

boardgame: 僕の考えたラノベのタイトルは何かがおかしい (Boku no kangaeta ranobe no taitoru ha nanika ga okasii)
description:
僕の考えたラノベのタイトルは何かがおかしい (Boku no kangaeta ranobe no taitoru ha nanika ga okasii)is a word game from Japan. Players are compiling a long and somewhat rude title of their light novel, and the designer gives the English title as  "There's something wrong with the title of the light novel I came up with".

In this game, players take turns turning up word cards. They must turn up one card, and may choose to turn up more or stop. If any card they turn up has an ▲ icon, they must turn up another. Once they stop, they can collect whichever cards they like into their hand. They must take one if a ▲ icon was turned up, but otherwise can choose to not take any that turn.

Once a player feels they have enough cards in hand to make their book title, they can declare Complete! and stop for the round. When every player has stopped, they present their titles formed from all the cards in their hands and score points if possible.

The word cards show two words, top and bottom and the player can select which word to use. The cards may also show a Restricted Number (R2, R3 and so on) because those words are a bit rude in Japan. If a player's title totals R18, they are eliminated because they made it too rude for a light novel.

Before the round starts, three theme cards are turned up and players are trying to build a title matching the theme. This includes an R number, and bonus points for matching the theme. Players score points for the total of the R numbers in their title and can also score bonus points for using tricky words (marked with stars).

At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins, being able to craft the best long titles to suit the market.
description by user

Word-based party game about the long names of Japanese literary works called light novels.

Players combine different word cards, such as "different world" and "reincarnation", to create their own light novel title that fits the game-specific objectives.
Each word card has a special value called R-points. If that number is over 18 in the end, you are disqualified, but you also get extra points for the final score.

ライトノベルという日本の文学作品の名前が長いことをテーマしたワード系パーティーゲーム。

「異世界」や「転生」といったさまざまなワードカードを組み合わせて、ゲームごとの目標に合うような独自のライトノベルのタイトルを作ります。
各ワードカードにはRポイントという特殊な数値があります。最終的にその数値が18以上だと失格になるけども、最終的な追加得点にもなります。
--description by publisher

A mid-1950's crossword game. Each player had their own board and choose a group of alphabet letters to use which they try to create as many "3, 4, or 5-letter words" as possible. 3 letter words are worth 30 points, 4 letter words are 40 points, and 5 letter words are 50 points.

-User Summary

The game wants to simulate an alternative economy proclaimed by a community.  
Roll the dice. Get income according your cards. Buy a card, if you want to and if you have enough money. Optionally invest money in the bank called Bürgschaftsbank. The more cards you have, the better the chance to get return on your investment.
The winner is the player who first collected 4 specific cards.
A burglar becomes a playable character, stealthily sneaking behind opponents to shiv them in the back.
A Texas billionaire is added as a character, using his vast wealth to take control of other characters for one turn-if not, then he'll probably just shoot ya.

A cyborg sent back from the future to assassinate a pastry chef before he publishes a brioche recipe that will cause bread machines to take over the world, nothing can escape her laser-targeting vision, or her sig sauer machine guns.
A knight becomes a playable character, using lots of deadly medieval weaponry, he's an expert in slaying monsters.

In this city, there have been numerous unsolved cases dating back in time, and in order to unravel their truths, top-notch detectives from around the world have been summoned. You are one of the summoned detectives and will gather clues about the incidents. By exploring the city, collecting necessary information and evidence, and utilizing your unique investigative method called "Esoteric Profiling," you will get a glimpse into the heart of the events. Furthermore, by hiring assistants and utilizing familiar items, you will approach the truth behind the cases. Your challenge to unravel the mysteries of the unsolved incidents that remain in the city begins now.
- from publisher

Esoteric is a roll and write game where detectives draft from five rolled dice to gain resources in polymino shapes on their player sheet in the exploration phase, then use those resources to gather evidence with polymino shapes in another grid on their player sheet. These collected pieces of evidence  are used to score in different ways at the end of the game.

A set-collecting party game where the goal is to find all a farmer's lost donkeys. Players are dealt three cards and must play one and discard one card per turn. If at any point you have less than 3 cards you are eliminated. Each card has unique abilities that allow one to draw from the discard pile, steal cards from another player's hand, or take donkeys from another farm, for example. The first player to get three donkeys wins.

A set-collecting party game where the goal is to rescue birds before poachers get them. Each player is dealt three cards and must play one, and discard one, per turn. Each card has unique abilities, such as stealing a card from another player's hand or drawing from the discard pile. If at any point you have less than 3 cards you are eliminated. The first player to collect a full set of birds wins.

A set-collecting party game where one must protect one's family tomb from raiders and retrieve stolen gems. Each player is dealt three cards and must play one, and discard one, per turn. If you ever have less than three cards, draw. If you cannot draw you are eliminated. Cards have unique abilities that allow one to block another player from stealing your cards, or let you take gems from another tomb. Some cards will force to discard Jewels or steal 1 from you. The first player to get five jewels wins.

The third volume out of 6 of the Mickey Mouse Library Games collection. The cards are designed to be dominoes with numbers ranging from 1 to 7, and these characters:

     Mickey Mouse
     Minnie Mouse
     Pluto
     Big Bad Wolf
     Three Little Pigs
     Donald Duck
     Ferdinand the Bull
     Clara Cluck


The game plays like dominoes where only a 1-1 card can be played first, and then players can either match on the end of cards already down on the table, or draw a card if they don't have one to match. If the drawn card is playable, play the card, if not then your turn passes.
The round is over whenever someone runs out of cards, and score is the sum of all the opponent's hands.
The first player to reach 50 wins the game.

The rules of the game are as states:
"The game may be played by three or more players. Shuffle and deal all the cards one at a time. The first player to the left of the dealer who has a card with number 1 starts by placing it on the table in front of himself. The player who has number 2, of the same suit places it in front of himself. Then the player with number 3, etc. until all twelve cards in that suit have been played. The cards are played end to end toward the market in the center of the table. The player who plays number 12 then starts a new suit with number 1 if he has it, if not the next player to his left who has number 1 starts by placing it beside the first card which he played. Then the player with number 2 of this suit plays etc., until some player gets rid of all of his cards.
The player who gets rids of his cards adds the numbers on the cards remaining in the other players hands for his score."

It's a part of the Mickey Mouse Library of Games collection. The spine shows it's volume 5 out of 6.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
A brand new trading card game experience with a rich story.

In Argent Saga, players take on the role of a Champion who leads an army of Units and casts powerful Spells to destroy their opponent’s five Towers of Power and ultimately taking over the sixth tower, the Argent Tower.

We created Argent Saga to give the power back to the community by engaging with our players on an intimate level. Between our unprecedented level of community involvement and our team’s combined experience of over 100 years in the game business, we aim to avoid the pitfalls of our predecessors and create the next big thing in card gaming.

Argent Saga: Trading Card Game is the result of countless hours of playtesting and devotion to one central idea: Let’s make a game that’s fun, easy to pick up, and that can react quickly to player feedback. We’re not a huge company with corporate overlords to please; we started small and have grown because of the community. In many ways, Argent Saga is a love letter to that community.

—description from the publisher

An UNO variation different from the other Disney Princess editions. The images on these cards are from multiple Disney princess movies all the way up to the year 2020.

In this variation, a new card with the Power of Friendship Rule. When this wild card is played, the player will choose a color from their hand and place it face down in front of them. Going clockwise, every other player must choose a color they THINK the active player placed down, face down. Once all players have done so, everyone reveals. Players who colors matched with the active player get to discard their card, while everyone else just places it back in their hand.

boardgame: Marvel Zombies: A Zombicide Game – Batman Promo Card
description:
Promo card to use Batman from Dark Nights Metal in Marvel Zombies.
First edition has the illustration on the ID card from Dark Nights Metal expansion.
Second version has DCeased Batman art. (Available at Essen 2023)

The figure of Batman from Dark Nights Metal for Zombicide 2nd edition may be used with this character card in Marvel Zombies. Batman is a melee character who can use his gadgets and talents in the game. You can use Power to eliminate a Walker/Runner within Range 1. Because in the Heros version, there are no preset turns, you may have an advantage being last, prep time is the key. Lastly, the World's greatest detective skill allows you to pull an extra spawn card and decide which will be more suitable for next turn.

A CARD GAME FOR THE WHOLE FAMILY: Put your memory to the ultimate test at at your next game night with this entertaining Elf card game featuring designs from the hit holiday film the whole family can enjoy. Could you be having any more fun?

MEMORIZE, SWITCH, MATCH AND WIN! Partner up and compete in a game of memory at home or on the go! - 5 minutes to learn the game - 30 minutes to play - 4 players (2 teams) - ages 6 and up - do you have what it takes to be crowned the Memory Master?

Lacrosse Blast Express uses the same cards and concepts as the full-play version (Lacrosse Blast) but in about ten minutes of game time.

This is primarily meant as a solitaire game, but has options for 2 players. The game plays over 24 segments of game-time with six segments per period. A roll of three dice dictates what happens in each segment and whether there is a scoring chance, a player quality check or a penalty check. Triples can also bring in player injuries, critical face-offs and fights.

Fractal: Beyond the Void is redefining the 4X genre with an expansive and dynamic strategy game in an ever-changing, story-driven legacy setting. Millenia after the collapse of the Federation, the greatest empire to ever rule the galaxy, a few species are taking up to the stars again to escape their dying worlds. They all have the same goal: to rebuild their civilizations and reclaim their former glory. But there can only be one ruler.

This Collector Edition of Fractal includes the core game and the first campaign, “Beyond the Void”, along with Kickstarter-exclusive acrylic colony tokens, metal first-player token, and the solo/cooperative Alliance expansion.

Beyond the Void is the first campaign of Fractal, using a custom legacy framework: the Aftermath. It offers a full narrative experience where every action has consequences, and where the fate of the universe will be decided by the victories and defeats and every player around the table. Amongst other components, factions themselves can be expanded by unlocking new governments and masteries, pushing always further their natural asymmetry. 5 new factions can also be discovered, all with unique mechanics and gameplay, and all freely usable in the core experience once encountered during the campaign.
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
4917 is a card game for teaching kids how to read and write machine code, using a simple 4 bit computer with very simple instruction set, 2 general purpose registers and only 16 cells of addressable memory.

While the deck supports many play variations, the Hacking variant requires two people, with the goal to HALT the other player's computer without using the HALT instruction, you can modifying one memory address per turn.

The game is released under a Creative Commons license and can be downloaded and printed for free from the game website, while a professionally printed card deck can also be purchased from the site too.

"...Winters, lacking a demolition kit, aimed to disable the gun. Lipton had one in his musette bag back where the attack started. Winters instructed him to retrieve it. Leaving three men to guard the first gun, Winters led five others in a charge down the trench, throwing grenades, firing rifles. They captured two wounded Germans at the first gun. The second gun's crew retreated; Easy took it with minimal casualties..."

"Assault on the 105s!" is a tiny solitaire wargame published by Joyance Games and designed by Hans Gem. The game depicts a remarkable assault carried out by soldiers of Easy Company, 2nd Battalion, 506th Parachute Infantry Regiment of the U.S. 101st Airborne Division during World War II, the assault on Brécourt Manor on June 6, 1944. This historic engagement is upheld by the U.S. Military Academy at West Point as a classic example of command artistry and assaulting fixed objectives, and it is also portrayed in the book "Band of Brothers" and its namesake TV series.

The game has a duration of approximately 30 minutes, with AI controlling the German forces and players assuming the role of U.S. Airborne. Players must strategically plan actions and equipment usage, aiming to minimize their own casualties while swiftly demolishing four 105mm LeFh howitzers in the German stronghold. The objective is to ensure these artillery pieces have minimal impact on the attacks on Utah Beach. After neutralizing the cannons, players must carefully evacuate their paratroopers from the battlefield to secure victory.

In the game, event cards influence the actions of the German soldiers and may bring positive or negative effects to the U.S. paratroopers, such as ammunition shortages, intelligence gains, or reinforcements, among others. Additionally, the MG42 machine gun position situated behind the artillery emplacement poses a significant challenge. Players face the decision of prioritizing soldier safety, gradually suppressing the German firepower, or focusing on overpowering the artillery position to expedite victory.

The power of choice lies in your hands!

The game includes the following components:
1 rulebook (with 2 Player Aid)
1 game board
1 countersheet (56 counters)
30 event cards (10 cards per event stage)
1 Vassal Module

In this "Roll and Write" Game, in 12 rounds, players will plant Trees and to do so they must roll 4 Dice with colored faces and make marks in different locations on their sheet. For each Tree marked or earned with Bonuses present on the Sheet, your "Planted Tree Control" will advance.

     Active player rolls 4 dice and chooses 2
     Other players choose 1 dice


At the end of the round, regardless of the number of players, each player will have chosen 3 Dice and marked them on their sheet.
There is the possibility of combos, since there are several bonuses that the player can earn throughout the match, depending on where they made the mark.
Game aimed at children and school students, where we seek to highlight the importance of planting trees, which on a large scale will end up improving conditions in the medium and long term.

—description from the designer

Monstros & Tesouros is a Brazilian, independent game, born from decades of fantasy RPG campaigns and the author’s desire to simplify the tabletop RPG experience just with cards and a pair of dice.
 In the “Adventure” mode, each player assembles a party of three unique, preconstructed heroes, each with a different set of abilities, from a pool of 35. Players take turns revealing Terrain cards (from a total of 65), which pit a different set of enemies against the heroes. During their turn, the player controls their heroes, while the monsters are controlled by another player, reducing downtime. In a two-player game, you will never be idle, as you’re either playing with your heroes or controlling the monsters against your opponent.

The game features a total of 100 unique monsters to face. During combat, players use their action cards (from a pool of 100), which have stat prerequisites that correlate to the heroes/monster stats: hand management is a key aspect of the game. Dice, in combination with various stats, are used to determine the result of some actions and the winner of successive rounds. Once monsters are defeated, the heroes gain treasure (from a total of 100 unique treasure cards) and experience, becoming stronger and able to use more powerful action cards. The game continues until one player reaches a predefined goal.
