There are other gameplay modes, including “Arena” - a free-for-all single combat between two parties, “Bestial Arena” - where monsters face off against each other in quick combat, and solo play. The game is highly customizable with many optional rules and is open to house rules as well. One box contains 400 cards, a pair of dice and the rulebook.

-description from designer
110 cards that make up a number of stories. These are short scenarios that can be played between the main scenarios that make up the campaign. Expansion for Zerywia the Board Game.

C&B is an abstract assymetrical game for two players. The Republic, in center of the board, is defended by the Oligarchy, while the People's insurrection arises with its militants from the Conurbans, on the periphery. The capabilities and mobility of the pieces of the Oligarchy far exceed those of the People, who on the other hand tend to be very numerous as the insurrection grows.

KOAN Juegos de Mesa, a young Argentine game developer and publisher, has launched its first game C&B - Civilization and Barbarity.

It is a strategy game that is defined by the absence of chance and where each of the opposing parties seeks to impose its hegemony. To prevail, the People must block and paralyze each of the oligarchs, or take the Republic by storm through an irruption of their Leader, making the Revolution possible. The Oligarchy, on the other hand, must seek the obstruction of the Conurbans to stop the insurrection, or, using its ability to capture People's pieces, remove the Leader from the board twice.

It is a game where the strategies are completely asymmetrical, and yet a balance is achieved between the two fields allowing for the development of very different situations from game to game, in which apparent advantages often crumble quickly leading to surprising setbacks.

—description from the publisher

Desde el principio de la historia la humanidad se ha debatido entre la civilización y la barbarie. En este juego asimétrico en el que las estrategias del pueblo y de la oligarquía son netamente diferentes, para conseguir la victoria la oligarquía buscará ahogar el levantamiento popular o suprimir su liderazgo con un magnicidio, mientras que el pueblo deberá crecer en número para bloquear a la oligarquía o irrumpir en la sagrada república y hacer la revolución.

—description from the back of the box (original)

Since the beginning of history, humanity has debated between civilization and barbarity. In this asymmetric game in which the strategies of the people and the oligarchy are completely different, to achieve victory the oligarchy will seek to drown out the popular insurrection or suppress its leadership with a magnicide, while the people must grow in numbers to block the oligarchy or break into the holy republic and make the revolution.

—description from the back of the jîbox (translated)
100 cards. This is the perfect pack to deliver even more Exploration cards (56), Creatures (8), Items (8) and Sagas (28) For Zerywia the Board Game or Zerywia Quest.

factovillage is a two-dimensional card placement engine-building game based on your own village building.

Cut trees, process them into boards, cut out stones, sharpen them into blades, and make axes.
Chop more trees with the axe and start mass papermaking.
Distribute leaflets with paper. More workers.
Bind books from the paper produced in large quantities by the increased number of workers, and build a library!

It is a game like this, but it is not so easy.

In short, all you have to do is to place a few cards each round and resolve card effects in any order you like, but the cards in the field can only interfere with each other if they are adjacent to each other, up, down, left, or right.

Therefore, it is necessary to imagine the final shape of the game to some extent from the beginning and to place cards systematically.

This is not a game where you can do some things at random, or where you have few options in the game because of the small number of engine parts you can obtain. Therefore, the pleasure you feel when you get it right is supreme.

This is the best 2D placement engine build that asuno-labo brings to you so that you can enjoy the process of planning → execution.

╠description from the publisher (translated)

Gameplay Expansion with two new Heroes: Asuba and Otok includes 2 resin miniatures, personal Saga sets for both Heroes and new Exploration, enemy and Relic cards!

Into the Wild components:
    2 miniatures of Zerywia Heroes (Otok and Asuba)
    30 Saga and Hero cards
    25 Exploration cards
    Expansion Introduction
4 miniatures for Scorned Renegdes (Zagra, Kost,Vidnur, Asir) 53 Saga, Hero and Enemy cards For Zerywia the Board Game - Miniatures are unpainted, do not require assembly.

Pochara, the Monstrosity (huge scale model) with 6 Pochara Saprolings minis & 6 Garrisons minis. 30 Cards (Scenario and Enemy).

Pochara Expansion Components:
    1 Pochara Monstrosity miniature (resin, pre-assembled, unpainted)
    6 Pochara's Offspring miniatures
    6 Garrison miniatures
    30 Cards

boardgame: Zerywia: Nix – The Lady of the Void
description:
High quality resin miniature and a “The Dark Age” deck.

Goddess Nix with her deck of dark ages. Based on the Thymor Gods mechanic, we will be given the opportunity to play alongside Nix on the Zerywia board itself. Her deck will provide new exploration cards, items, relics, rituals and allies (50 Cards).

A huge model of the Herald, the Primeval Titan (70 x 100mm) that will roam the world of Zerywia and devour the land piece by piece.
107 cards:
- Two campaigns consisting of 3 scenarios each. (20 cards)
- The Herald and its attack cards. (7 cards)
- A set of  Nix cards to increase replayability in the repetitive scenario. (
6 cards)
- New Personal Sagas for Svara, Veda, Goryv, Dalbor and Hexer. 
(50 cards)
- A set of exploration and creature cards. (24 cards)

Every game is different. Play over and over again!

A unique concept in murder mystery games that allows the game to be played many times. The game plays equally well with any number of players between 4 & 12. There are no "add-on" characters. The game can be played either as an informal after dinner game or as an organized murder mystery event.

-description from the back of the box

boardgame: The Floor is Lava: Family Edition
description: You better watch out because the floor is lavaaa! Keep the couch cushions where they belong; this kid-favorite just got a BIG upgrade! With more tiles than ever, new challenges, and 10 different ways to play, the fun never has to stop! Kids and parents will have a blast stretching their legs and their imaginations while avoiding obstacles and completing challenges designed to knock players off their feet. Can you kick an imaginary soccer ball or act like you’re paddling a canoe without tipping over? Put your balance and problem-solving skills to the test, but don’t fall in because remember, THE FLOOR IS LAVAAA!

Named after the comic strips presented in newspapers during the first half of the 20th century, Funnies is a set of Legendary Units inspired by the lighter side of life, including older versions of D-Day Dice, gaming community staples and other silly things. It also includes a very serious Unit, "Hobart's Funnies".

Each of these 10 Legendary Units offers a unique RWB table that replaces the standard one and a set of 5 strongly-themed legendary cards. These will help players beat the Bunker with a smile.

-description from designer

boardgame: D-Day Dice: To Do or Die
description:
To celebrate 15 years of beach-conquering mayhem, every rule, optional rule, and scenario ever published can now be found between the same covers. Along with additional goodies.

If you only have the old 1st Edition of the game, you can use this book to get you up-to-date with the current rules while still using your vintage components.

A variation of UNO based on the toy company FAO Schwarz.

Celebrating 160 years of toys, F.A.O. Schwarz continues to inspire awe and discovery for all children and the child inside all of us.
Force another player to draw 3 cards with the Special Delivery card!
And when you're down to your last card, don't forget to yell 'UNO!'

-description from the back of the box

The Deep Sea Expedition expansion for Hidden Ark introduces a new expansion board, deep sea species, submarines, new opportunity tokens, a pink player color, and components to support five-player games!

—description from the publisher

Cooperative game in which players protect Ukrainian houses from invaders.

The basis of the game consists of three decks of cards: the infantry deck, the tank deck and the aircraft deck.
The back of each of the cards in these decks shows an enemy (infantry, tank or plane). The front side of the map represents the Ukrainian forces and is divided into two zones that are played separately.

Before the start of the game, players hide one enemy's bunker card under each of the enemy's decks. The goal of the game is to find the bunker that will lead to victory.

Players also take cards of Ukrainian houses, the protection of which will be the goal of the game.

The game consists of two cyclically repeating stages:
draw phase and battle phase.
In draw phase player draws two cards, then put one card face down, thus forming enemy forces, and keep one for themselves.
In battle phase all players open all the cards and jointly decide which properties of cards and in what order to use them in order to defeat all the enemy cards. If some enemy cards cannot be destroyed, they capture or destroy Ukrainian houses.

Phases repeat until either the "Victory" bunker is found or all the houses are captured or destroyed.

There are 2 modes in the game:
Open: in all phases of the game, players are not limited in communication and can decide together which of the two cards just taken from the deck to keep in their hands.

Closed: the decision of which deck to take cards from and which card to keep is made by each player independently without knowing which cards his partners have left for himself.

-description from designer

An UNO variation featuring Buzz Lightyear, promoting the 2022 Disney movie Lightyear.

Players still race to get rid of all their cards by matching a card in their hand with the current card shown on top of the discard pile, and the special Hyperspeed rule can result in multiple players being skipped one after another.

A Bible-themed Bingo game published by Chuck Taylor Enterprises. No date; the box says "Copyright Applied For." Looks like it was made in 50's or 60's.

Comes with sheets of chits, used to call out what square players may mark off. Each chit has a book of the Bible, which corresponds to squares on the cards. The top row of each card has the game's name written on it, one letter per column. Different columns have different possible books; a list in the instructions tells you valid books for each column.

—user summary

All the cards are placed on the table with the yellow and blue side of the "Rivet" facing up, mixed thoroughly and laid out in 10 rows with 10 cards in each row.

Any other number of rows and number of cards in each row is allowed at your discretion. The main thing is that the cards on the table must resemble straight rows of beets in the garden....

We recommend that you give the right to make the first move to the youngest player at the table, but age is a very conditional thing, so players have the right to determine in a convenient way (but within the framework of the current legislation) who will go first, second, third... and who will go for pizza.

Each player has the right to turn over any two cards in his turn. If the images on them match, the player takes them for himself and has the right to take another turn.

But as soon as the images on the two open cards do not match, the player turns them back over face up and places them in the same places where they were.

The other players try to memorize the location of the shorts they saw. The turn passes to the next player.

The game ends when there are no cards left on the table. The winner is the one who has collected the largest number of cards or the one who has recognized all the symbols of Ukraine depicted on all the cards of the game.

-description from publisher

Board game "Remember Everything! Football" will finally unite ardent fans with those who only know that the ball is round (but that's not accurate!).  And it's still unclear who will assemble a cooler team - a fan with experience or his grandmother.

88 almost collectible cards with the top players of the season and football legends, 2 yellow cards, 1 red card, VAR cards, "play the match" action, quick entry into the rules - a fun and exciting evening is guaranteed. Even those who have never been interested in football before will not stay away.

We advise granting the right of first move to whoever was the last person to watch a soccer game in the stadium, but players may otherwise legally determine who goes first.

All cards are placed on the table with the lawn labeled "Rivet" facing up, mixed thoroughly and laid out in 10 rows of 10 cards in each row. Any other number of rows and cards in each row is allowed at your discretion.

The main thing is that the cards on the table should resemble even rows of spectator seats at a soccer stadium.

Each player has the right to turn over any two cards in one turn. Depending on what is shown on these two cards - the player acts as follows:

If two open cards show the same player, the player takes the cards and has the right to another turn.
But as soon as the images on two open cards are NOT the same - the player acts as follows:

if the card has a picture of a player - the player turns it back over with the turf up and puts it exactly where it was. The other players try to memorize its location.

If the card is yellow, red, VAR or PLAY GAME - the player takes the card as a prize card and takes it for himself.

The game ends when there are no cards left on the table. The winner is the player whose players have the highest sum of points. If two players have the same sum, the winner is the one who has collected the greatest number of all cards, including unused prize cards that did not go into the rebound.

-description from publisher

Each player's turn consists of two actions:

The first action of the turn is the right to compose from their already collected cards any words that exist in the Ukrainian language. Each compiled word the player writes down on the sheet with the sum of points of used letters and this word. The player's cards that were used to make words are discarded and are not used anymore. When a player can no longer make words from his cards or has no cards at all (as at the beginning of the game), he proceeds to the second action of his turn.

The second action of the turn is the right to turn over any two pairs of cards laid out on the table. The number of flipped cards must be one more than the player made words in the first action of his turn. For example: during the first action of his turn the player made 1 word, then accordingly the player turns over 2 pairs of cards (i.e. 4 cards at the same time).

The game ends when there are no cards left on the table and no player can make a word from his cards. The winner is the player who has the highest sum of points for the words made during the whole game. If players still have unused cards that have not been discarded, each card counts as 1 point regardless of whether they are prize or letter cards and how many points are indicated on them.

-description from publisher

Each player turns over any TWO cards in one turn. Depending on what is shown on these two cards, the player acts as follows:

If there is a match of a WORD and its corresponding PICTURE on the two cards opened in turn, the player collects these two cards for himself and has the right to turn over any two cards on the table again.

But if there is no match between the WORD and its corresponding PICTURE on the cards opened in turn, the player acts as follows:

If the first card revealed was a PICTURE and the second card revealed was a WORD that does not match the picture, the player turns both cards back over with the word "Klepka" facing up and places them on the table exactly where they were. The other players try to memorize their locations.

If the first card revealed was a WORD and the second card revealed was a PICTURE that does not match the image, the player flips both cards back "Klepka" up and places them on the table exactly where they were. The other players try to memorize their locations.
if the first or second card revealed is a Syncopus prize card - the player takes the Syncopus card for himself, and the other card, regardless of what was depicted on it, turns it back "Klepka" up and puts it on the table exactly where it was. The other players try to memorize their locations.

If the first card and the second card opened were PICTURES, the player turns the second card back over with "Klepka" facing up and places it on the table exactly where it was and opens any other card instead, trying to find the corresponding WORD to the first card (which is still lying open on the table). This is repeated until the player finds any WORD.

Once there are no face-down cards left on the table, each player must make a chain using only their word cards. The chain is made by matching the last letter of the previous word with the first letter of the next word.

The winner is the one who builds the longest continuous chain of word cards. The length of the chain is determined as the sum of letters of all the words in the chain.

-description from publisher

Return to the Neververse with Battlecrest: Deadrock, our third year base set. Only the toughest and most brutal fighters can survive this desolate landscape. As with Fellwoods and Metronn, Deadrock comes with a new 6-card map and two new heroes. More heroes will be added throughout the year to test their might against each other and their environment.


     Deadrock Map Set: 6 Landmark cards


The all-new Deadrock map is as dangerous a place as the Neververse has ever seen. This rocky desert offers plenty of opportunities for clever heroes to rain damage on their foes, but the twin storms at its center can bring down even the toughest warrior all by themselves. Don’t get caught in their path or you might not ever get out.


     Cirrus Hero set.
     Cirrus AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.



     Noster Hero set.
     Noster AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.



What is the mark of a true champion? Legendary heroes clash across the Neververse to find out.

Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Characters are composed of 6-card sets featuring a hero card, a health tracker, and action cards. Occasionally a minion or item will replace an action card.

The map is built of 6 cards as well, with the cards randomly placed in a set pattern.

Players take turns maneuvering their characters around the dynamic, and often dangerous map while unleashing powerful attacks. As action cards are used they open up new possibilities and empower the character’s battlecrests. Build up to decisive strikes or refocus to get powers back. Every character has a unique set of actions and preferred tactics, so no two battles play the same.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play
Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
GAME SYSTEM

    
    
         This entry is to allow for discussion/rating of the game system as a whole, and the foundation deck in general. It is not for a specific game.

    



Living elements interweave throughout the realm. A water guardian extinguishes a roaring wildfire that leaves behind unrecognizably scorched earth. The seared avatar of that earth rises and consumes the water of a dozen seas. With infinite variations on their eternal cycle, no element can ever reign supreme.Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

The base deck includes rules for three core games:

     Nine Perils: a quick but puzzly solo game of arranging a sequence of events that will avert nine disasters.
     Sorcerers' Showdown: a game of outplaying your opponent, calculated risks, shrinking hands, and rising chaos.
     The Path Ahead: a light game where players take turns guessing whether what lies ahead on the path is stronger or weaker than what they’ve already encountered.



Nine Perils

The prophecy foretells nine great perils, warnings of coming doom. As a seer, you must peer into the future and find a path to avoid these perils.

A solo-only game of limited information and anticipating the future to avert nine disasters using the Mysticana deck. Using your hand of cards (based on the difficulty chosen) play against the central timeline of 9 peril cards. Each turn you’ll flip one of the peril cards in the timeline face-up, draw a new card into your hand, then play a single from your hand into a free space in a separate row below any peril card. After 9 turns, compare each peril’s rank and element against the adajcent card’s rank and element. Each of the perils must be successfully outmatched to win!

Sorcerers' Showdown

Two sorcerers clash in battle, each spell and counterspell growing less predictable as they wear down.

A dueling-like game for 2 players using the Mysticana deck. In the first round, players are dealt 8 cards and choose 6 to battle with, setting aside 2 to hold onto for the next round. The first player then plays a card to start a clash. The other player responds with a card of their own, matching the element if possible. The clash is evaluated by rank and element, with the winner capturing both cards. Should an Avatar (rank ⟐) win a clash, the winner steals a pair of previously captured cards from their opponent. Clashes are played and resolved until both players are out of cards. Should one player have all of the captured cards, they win! Otherwise, players take the 2 cards set aside at the beginning of the round and draw a hand size that is 1 card less than the previous round to continue the duel.

The Path Ahead

In every corner of the realm, travelers engage in a timeless game of deduction and chance.

A guessing game for 2-4 players that uses the Mysticana deck. Each round, the active player guesses whether the next card drawn will be stronger or weaker than the currently showing card (accounting for rank and suit). A correct guess moves play to the next player; an incorrect guess earns a consequence card. These may count against the player’s score if not used to make a set of matching consequences. At the end of each round, players add up any consequence cards they still hold and add it to their running score from previous rounds. The game is over when any player hits 20 points, with the lowest-scoring player the ultimate winner.

boardgame: Skulls of Sedlec: Expansion Collection III
description:
A wallet compilation of 3 previously released expansions for Skulls of Sedlec.


     Archers
     Farmers
     Troubadours

Ancient Realm: Leaders introduces 18 renowned leaders from major eras in history.


     Ancient Realm: Leaders – Set I
     Ancient Realm: Leaders – Set II
     Ancient Realm: Leaders – Set III

A wallet compilation of expansions for Super Slopes.


     Super Slopes: Snow Report
     Super Slopes: Black Diamond
     Super Slopes: Park Rat


Park Rat adds a terrain park that you get points for if you follow the right direction as you score. Snow Report adds weather cards to mess with things. And Black Diamond brings cards 19-24 into the draft with a new ability that lets you reserve cards.

This expansion for 18Chesapeake has the following key differences:

There are seven railroad corporations are in the game. In a two or three player game, one of them is removed at the beginning of the game. The Yokohama-Shinbashi Line is always in the game. Private companies can only be bought into corporations at face value.

No auction, privates are available for purchase by players starting in the first stock round; purchasing a private counts as a buy action.

Trains export after every set of ORs up to and including the first D train.

No bank; game ends either in bankruptcy or 3 ORs after first D train comes out. If the D train is exported, game ends after next set of ORs. If the D train is purchased by a corporation, a stock round will happen first, then one last set of ORs.

—description from publisher

Each player has the right to turn over any two cards in one turn. Depending on what is shown on these two cards - the player acts as follows:

If the images on both open cards match - the player takes these two cards and has the right to turn over any two cards on the table again.

But as soon as the images on the two open cards do NOT match - the player acts as follows:

if a card has a number image - a player turns it back over with "Klepka" written on it upwards and puts it on the table exactly where it was. The other players try to memorize its location.
If the card is a mathematical sign, or a variable, or chaos - the player takes such a card as a prize and immediately takes it for himself.

The turn passes to the next player.

Once there are no face-down cards left on the salt, each player must make an arithmetic expression using only their cards obtained during the game to get as large a number as possible.

The winner is the one who has the largest number of complex arithmetic expression from all his cards.

-description from publisher
A collection of expansions for Naturopolis.
A collection of expansions for Agropolis.

All square cards with the elements of the New Year's puzzle are placed face down on the table, thoroughly mixed and laid out in even rows of eight cards in each row tightly together so that a large square of the hidden picture is formed. All rectangular cards are thoroughly mixed and laid out back side up on the table in a stack.
Each player in one turn has the right to turn over any one unopened square card from the hidden drawing laid out in rows and looks for exactly where that card should be.

If a card revealed by a player should be on a spot that does NOT share a border with any already revealed card on the table, that move is considered unsuccessful. The player returns the card back to its place face up. All players try to memorize its location. The turn passes to the next player.

Attention! Only the cards that are touching by the sides are considered to be cards that have a common border. Touching cards with corners is not considered to have a common border!
If a card opened by a player should be on a place having a common border with some already opened card on the table, such a move is considered successful. The player puts it on this necessary place on the principle of a puzzle according to the pattern instead of the unopened card on this place, which in its turn without opening moves to the empty place where the card opened by the player was earlier. For each successful move the player gets three privileges:
a) to take from the stack one top prize card with accrued points;
b) the opportunity to increase the points accrued (if it is provided in the text of the prize card received);
c) make another move (if it is not prohibited in the text of the received prize card).

Use of Prize Cards:
A prize card contains a number of points gained or lost by a player during a turn.
A prize card may contain a prohibition on taking another turn, even if the turn was successful.
A prize card may contain additional conditions that a player must read and, if possible, fulfill in order to increase the number of points he has gained or decrease the number of points of other players.
The game ends when the hidden picture puzzle is completely assembled on the table. The winner is the one who has collected the most points in their prize cards including extra points for fulfilling the condition that are indicated on them.

-description from publisher
Lucky Numbers - 5th Player Expansion contains a board and 20 blue tokens.

boardgame: ふわふわのクリスマス (Fluffy Christmas)
description: A set collection game using the patchwork system in which you can only move up to 3 spots in front of you. Collect presents, reindeer, dice, and +7 point cards to get the most points possible.

Players compete in a somewhat traditional cube rails game set in a fantasy, steam punk world. In addition to the open information game of a traditional cube rails game, this game includes two modules intended to alter the gameplay significantly. The first module is the Peoples of the Dwarven Kingdom module which adds asymmetrical abilities to every player. The second module is Events. Players can choose to play with neither, one, or both modules for three different types of games.

The game borrows some mechanics and is inspired by Robin David's Luzon Rails, while departing significantly.

—description from the designer

Players in the game take the part of one of the title heroine's many suitors. One spins the dial but all advance along individual paths marked with the ups and downs of pursuing a lover. Several clever twists allow for advances or retreats, and the winner is the player who first reaches "Acceptation."

The Coquette or, “The History of Eliza Wharton" is an epistolary novel by Hannah Webster Foster, published anonymously in 1797. The novel was one of the best-selling works of its time and it was reprinted eight times between 1824 and 1828. Still, it was not credited to Foster until 16 years after her death in 1856. A story based on real-life events, it involves a single woman who chooses not to marry immediately and is eventually scorned and ruined for her choices. The book was still recognized in 1858, when a Boston publisher brought out a board game loosely based on it.


     Description above taken from the Strong Museum of Play website.

Two players compete to bring the most visitors into the theme park while drafting rides, action points, and bonuses.  Additional points can be scored for matching riders to their preferred seating placement and gaining Mascot tokens. At the end of the game, players will score for their parks and also for the visitors who stuck around to watch the Fireworks Show.

—description from the publisher

In One Finger you must compete to get the most points by typing the color requested by the dice as soon as possible with a single finger.

The objective of the game is to use a single finger and get 10 or more points by typing the colors that appeared on the dice as quickly as possible.

Competitor in “2022 1-Card PnP Design contest”.

—description from the designer

Blackbeard's Treasure Hunt game helps kids learn colors, pattern recognition, and develop fine motor skills. Play as a pirate racing to unlock Blackbeard's treasure. On your turn try and match color cards in the correct order to follow your treasure map. If you match the correct color, fill your key by pushing it down onto the color's matching cube. Once you have filled your key in the correct order to match your map, use the key to unlock a 3-d treasure chest. You've won the game when the coins spill out of the chest to reveal Blackbeard's Treasure!

A new form of Matching Card Game with higher challenge your memory. There are 2 Mode for play

The First Mode is "Matching"
Player has to matching the Couple of item card (Flip 1 by 1)
If the Player can matching the First Couple 
You got the First Mission and then go the Mission 2

Before go to the Mission 2
take that couple & other 4 cards shuffle and lay down (Face down) like the same.

Mission 2 
Player has to match the both couple in the straight 
(ex. A - A then B - B .. if A-A then B-C .. It's over)
Pass turn to Opponent

The Player that finish Mission 4 First is win

The Other Mode is Put in Order
Player has to order Item follow the Mission
The one who can put in order follow the Mission First is win

—description from the designer

The story continues from 'Meow! The Cat Cafe' 
This game is the second game of 'around the park series' which you act as a staff in cactus garden try to take cactus and item to service the customer by moving your staff token to the row that you can do actions

In a turn you can do 4 action points by doing any these actions
- take a special coin
- Move 1 Space
- Take a Cactus Card in a row
- Complete (Customer) Order or a cat

When the player encounter each other and do action in the same row. that player must pay special coin to that one which make the game must think about worthiness.

The game end when player has 9 Completed order and Cat and then scoring by the point of Set of Customer, The Majority of each type of customer, Mission Card and Cat Card.

-description from designer

Racketeers is a strategic game that blends worker placement, area majority, negotiation, and push your luck mechanics to create an exciting criminal experience. Players will step into the shoes of cunning Racketeers and lead a gang of goons in taking control of illicit rackets, gaining cash, booze, guns, and notoriety!

In every round, players strategically position control markers to secure their dominance over rackets, enabling them to obtain the highest and most lucrative rewards by gaining majority control over their businesses. Alternatively, players may decide to partake in clever negotiations with fellow players, creating temporary alliances that can be mutually beneficial and profitable. Players should also be cautious about attracting unwanted attention, as excessive heat could lead to a raid and the potential loss of both stock and goons.

The player who gains the most notoriety at the end of the game wins!

-description from designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Scramble to collect and krush your opponents in Kawaii Krush, a rapid-paced party game. Everyone will be racing - be quick or your opponents might grab your hand first

—description from the back of the box

To win player must have 8 of the same Kawaii cards, then reveal their hand and shout a final Kawaii Krush to win the game.

Krabby Krush is the same game but with SpongeBob SquarePants characters instead of kawaii foods.

boardgame: Friends?!: Das Rad der Geheimnisse – Wie gut kennst du deine Freunde?
description:
Fun for friends!

On a scale of 1-6 ... Funny, cheeky and sometimes a bit mean, 150 questions are guaranteed to provide fun and variety at any party. You set the answers on your turntable - first you have to answer the question for yourself, then you rate your friends: which answer is the most common and who has set the same answer as you?

With 150 questions on character traits, bizarre situations and hobbies and extra cards for Harry Potter fans
On a scale of 1-6 - with 8 turntable cards for the answers

How hot do you think you are? From 1= iceberg to 6= fireball. Someone has lied to you badly. How do you react? From 1= oh, it can happen to 6= I curse the day he/she was born. Ask yourselves the exciting questions and assess your friends correctly. How well do you really know each other?

—description from the publisher

Exclusive Kickstarter Big Box with the exclusive Redwood Miniature that is only available in the Big Box.

Contains the Base game and all expansions.
Extras:
- Big Box with plastic inserts
- Bonus Constraint cards
- Exclusive Redwood Miniature Add-on: adds a possibility to get extra VP during the game, when moving over the tree base.

The ultimate quiz for all Potterheads!

Become part of the magical universe and feel like a real sorcerer's apprentice in the world's most famous school of magic! Test your magical knowledge with 300 exciting questions and find out if you are a true Potterhead. Work your way through all seven school years alone or together with other sorcerer's apprentices. The practical game templates for the joint quiz are easy to download via QR code. With the magic house test, you can also find out which house the talking hat assigns you to. Are you ready for your test?

300 magical questions in three levels of difficulty: for young sorcerer's apprentices, advanced and professionals
Includes house test, ZAG and UTZ tests
Can be played alone and together
With decoder foil to decode the solutions
Extra: magical templates to download via QR code

Take on the ultimate quiz and puzzle your way through the various levels of difficulty. Take your ZAG and UTZ exams and prove that you are a true wizard! All solutions are encrypted and can only be revealed with the decoder foil. So cheating is out of the question! Magical puzzle fun for Potterheads and all those who want to become one.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Into the Godsgrave: The Forgotten Song
description:
Experience the other side of the story. Travel to the land of the Triarchy and take on the role of the followers of the Triad, the believers of the new gods. Venture into the unknown in search of lost pieces of an ancient ritual text that can bring a god back to life.

Favor of the Gods Mechanic
The Triad takes great care of their followers, and grants them numerous blessings. Throughout the story, you will be able to manipulate the world using Favor Points, which are a divine reward for those who have proven themselves worthy to the new gods.

Take On the Role of a Mysterious Executor
Explore the world as a Triarchy Executor - a mysterious emissary favored by the Triad. He is the vessel of the new gods, blessed with talents and tasked with goals beyond our understanding.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Into the Godsgrave: The Ultimate Trial
description:
In this expansion you will  face an ancient deity that was killed centuries ago, now brought back to life and filled with hatred towards its former servants - the mortals. But a lot has changed in the intervening centuries, and the so-called “slaves” are ready to make a stand.

Challenge yourself and your team to defeat a single enemy in an arena-like game mode. A map will be generated around a “godsgrave” where unimaginable perils await those who would challenge a god.

You will need all the skills you have learned while playing the base game to survive in the free-for-all mode.  You will craft your starting deck using all the cards the game provides. There is no time for leveling up; you will need all your energy and focus to find the perfect strategy to defeat the awakened god and prevent the world from the return of divine tyranny.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Into the Godsgrave: Trickster Class Pack
description:
Are game rules not for you? Are luck and deception your main tools of play?

If you choose the Trickster Class, your life motto will be “all or nothing”. During tests, the Trickster bets on the results. If your bets are correct, you will get extra benefits. If not - well, quite the opposite. You will have the means to manipulate the dice to some extent, but how long before your luck runs out?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Into the Godsgrave: Noble Class Pack
description:
Very few mortals possess a relic of the gods, but you are one of them.

That puts you in a unique position - will you use your relic for the greater good, or hide it away from prying eyes? Some Nobles are helpful, while others are as insidious as the old gods used to be. The bigger question is, what are you doing with a bunch of swords for hire? What is your true goal?

—description from the publisher

In 103 AD, Rome's important port, Ostia Portus, received large quantities of supplies from all over the empire. This port played an important role in transporting food to inland Rome. As the owner of a large fleet, players lend ships to merchants and aristocrats to expand their business.

In Ostia Pirates, powerful pirates await players when they step outside the safety of the Mediterranean Sea. Players must collect weapons and soldiers and eliminate obstacles that get in the way of their business. If you can successfully develop a trade route, you can expect to earn enough income to be worth the risk.

boardgame: 커피 러시 프로모: 크리스마스 (Coffee Rush: Christmas Promo Cards)
description:
Coffee Rush has released two Christmas season promo cards in December 2023.

When setting up the game, return two random order cards to the box, then shuffle the promo cards together with the order card deck for the base game.

Have a warmer and sweeter Christmas with Christmas season promo cards of Coffee Rush!

Buy 1 of these shiny holofoil Bhairav cards with your leftover coin to gain extra bonus points at the end of the game.

The expansion includes:

     5 holofoil Bhairav cards


—description from the designer

Give a nice boost to your game experience with these unique wild cards by simply adding them to the Red deck.

The expansion includes:

     10 unique wild cards


—description from the designer

These shiny holofoil Buddha cards give an exciting twist to the way players can collect points for building Buddhas during the game.

The expansion includes:

     5 holofoil Buddha cards


—description from the designer

In a post-apocalyptic wasteland, players take on the role of "Overseers" responsible for their faction.

Magnetic hex tile holders for customisable map designs and player-chosen placements for resource tiles give a fresh dynamic to every game.

Players must also battle against the Ecolapse, which flips hex tiles and renders any resources on them (Soylent or Desolene) as completely useless. As the game progresses, the usable land on the board dwindles.

The savage tech tree and a unique battle system of converting enemy troops, or sacrificing your own troops, means that a calculated defeat can be more important than a victory.

'—description from the designer'

Állati Árverés ("Animal Auction") is a strategic boardgame. Players have to bid on different animals illustrated on cards. The bidding is enhanced by animals with special abilities and bonus cards.
Depending on the number of players, the challenge is always different, and novice players have the opportunity to try the game in practice mode.

—description from the designer

What's the Sum? is an abstract summation card game for 2-6 players. The box contains 3 games with unique gameplay that makes each round of the game different from previous rounds.

In each What's the Sum? game, the players will try to zero the sum. Sounds easy? Let's try. If you succeed you can win, but the game can always change in favor of another player.

What's the Sum? games are fun, interesting and addictive, with clever gameplay. You will not want to stop playing and it will never get boring.

-description from back of the box

The three different variations inside the box all use the same 89 cards and Table Sum printed on the back of the front cover.

     Classic Game: Players try to get rid of all their cards by adding and subtracting numbers in between -5 and 5, without going past the boundaries.



     Advance Game: Players strive to reach one card in their hand as they lay a series of cards down to "zero" out the top-most visible card in the deck.



     Fast Pace Game: Played just like the Advance game, with the exception of no turns. Players need to play their series of cards as soon as they can before the others.

The cheeky little goblin "April April" has had some fun and mixed up the seasons.

Suddenly, snowflakes fall in summer, colorful foliage sails from the trees in spring, pears ripen in winter and the first buds and flowers sprout in autumn.

Now it's time to master the chaos and be quicker than the little goblin.

The game is played directly in the packaging: the four seasons are depicted in the bottom part of the box and separated from each other by fixed hurdles. All the seasons shown are equipped with season balls, although these are mixed up and therefore not always in the right place. There are matching season chips on the table, among which four with the cheeky goblin are hidden.

Now it's time to reveal the first chip and bring order to the balls of seasons by blowing. With one breath at a time, incorrectly placed balls should be moved to where they belong. The game is played as a team against the cheeky goblin. If you uncover all four season chips with the goblin before order is restored, the game ends and "April, April" has won.

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
This is an adaptation of TSATF to cover the various skirmishes and small wars of the early 20th century up through the 1930's. Most forces are armed with bolt action rifles with smokeless powder. Native forces may still be armed with black powder, single shot breech loaders and even muzzle loaders. The fierce native charge may still occur, but machine guns rule the battlefield, and light automatic weapons and grenades are making their appearance. These rules can be easily used for skirmishes as varied as East Africa WWI, RCW, NWF 1919-1938, SCW, China Between the Wars and the Banana Wars, to name a few.

Being an adaptation of Larry Brom's The Sword and The Flame Colonial rules set, for use in more modern times, on many frontiers, from Afghanistan, to Central America. The Jackson Gamers assume that you already know how to play "TSA TF". The charts below are set up as those in the "20th Anniversary Edition" of The Sword and The Flame. A copy of those rules will be necessary as the rules below are in semi-outline form.

—description from the designer
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

description:
In 1953 the US Air Force went toe-to-toe with the Czechs, resulting in the loss of an F-84E jet. Both sides, predictably, blamed each other and frantic diplomacy sought to cool the ardour of the two air forces.
 In this free two-game mini-campaign for Wings at War: MiG Alley, players can recreate the tense engagement over the West German – Czechoslovak border and a hypothetical rematch six months later.

—description from the designer

Dealership is a cool fun facts board game for 2 -6 players as you interact with car minded people. Anyone can play from the new driver to the auto expert. Its a game of chance with humor filled obstacles along the way from "The Lot" to the "Winners Circle". Start up your competitive spirit with 500 challenging questions and answers on automotive pop culture, the dealership retail experience, the factory, buying, selling, finance, service, parts, along with the history and future of the car business.

Fred’s Crazy Cat Lady is the go-to game for those with an affinity for felines. Your goal is to stack and balance the most cats on the wobbling lady without them toppling over.

Challenge yourself or a friend in Crazy Cat Lady! The game can be played alone while you try to stack and balance as many cats as possible, or you can alternate turns with an opponent.

Each Crazy Cat Lady set is made of solid beechwood and includes 1 crazy cat lady rocking base, 15 cats, and a muslin storage bag.

There is no age limit to play this creative wooden balance game, it is fit for both adults and kids. Fred's Crazy Cat Lady is a great activity for enhancing focus and problem solving abilities for all.

Fred's Crazy Cat Lady stacking game is packed in a quality kraft display box with a full color band. This wooden stacking game makes a great gift for all occasions, and all cat lovers!

—description from the publisher

An exciting action racing game inspired by the world of Mario Kart!

Use the buttons and levers to maneuver through 6 obstacles and race to the finish line!

Be the first to complete 5 laps and become the winner! Practice your racing skills and compete for the fastest lap!
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

boardgame: Powers & Bananas: A Game of Balance
description:
Welcome to the Peoples Banana Republic, where diplomacy is a fine art, and the balance between the great powers, Uncle Mishka and Uncle Sugar, is the key to maintaining independence. In "Powers and Bananas," you're not just a player; you're the guardian of this delicate equilibrium. The fate of the republic is in your hands!

Can you keep the balance between Powers and Bananas?

Powers and Bananas is a fast-paced card game for 2 to 6 players. The game combines elements of bidding, strategy, and a touch of luck, making every round a thrilling diplomatic dance. Players select bidding cards from their hand, indicating their diplomatic stance. Are you leaning towards Uncle Sugar, Uncle Mishka, or attempting a delicate balance? Tokens are collected based on the bids. Be cautious—too many tokens of one color indicate a tilt in favor of that superpower. Players can strategically play cards to avoid or counteract tokens, aiming for equilibrium. At the end of the game, the player with the lowest total of tokens, of either color, emerges as the hero of the Peoples Banana Republic. You've successfully navigated the balancing act of third world independence.

Diplomacy, strategy, and a dash of humor await you in Powers and Bananas: A Game of Balance. Will you be the diplomat who guides the republic through these challenging times, or will you succumb to the influence of the superpowers? The choice is yours. Can you keep the balance?

—description from the publisher

Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine brings you more hard-hitting BattleMech action from the heavy-metal wars of the 31st century and beyond—but don’t let your guard down!

No matter the situation, you can stay out of harm’s way by reviewing a most-wanted list of criminals and assassins, a roster of the Marian Hegemony’s Legion of the Dead, technical readouts, unit and planet digests, playable scenarios, a campaign set in the resource-poor wilds of the Third Succession War, and more—all by battle-hardened BattleTech authors and some new boot-camp graduates.

Giant Double-sized issue!

-description from publisher's website

This is an expansion for Holotype: Mesozoic North America. Game play does not change, but new cards are added to the base game, including:

8 unique Crurotarsi specimen cards
6 new Field Expedition cards
12 new Bone Bed cards
17 new Global Objectives cards - includes new 2-3 player cards
2 new Personal Objectives
1 new Milestone Achievement

Sheep Shuffle is a 2-4 player tabletop game about collecting sheep tokens to build up your flock and level up your sheep. 12 sheep types are separated into 3 tiers with increasing ability power: Starter Sheep, Special Sheep, and Shiny Sheep.

During your turn, you may do 2 of 3 possible actions, or repeat an action: Draw a starter sheep, Mix 2 sheep to increase their sheep tier, or shear a sheep (activating its special ability).

The first player to complete 1 of each objective type (Easy, Medium, & Hard) wins! Objectives requirements vary based on type, such as: collection, leveling, or stealing sheep.

—description from the publisher

This add on increases player count to 5, adding a new player mat and tokens to support an extra player!

One additional playable hero, Gotzo, the conspiracy theorist and 2 new Guardians, with their miniatures, cards and mats.


     1 Extra hero, Gotzo the conspiracy theorist, including 5 cards, 1 miniature and tokens.
     Player Mat and tokens to bring player count to 5.
     Double sided mat for 2 extra guardians, Shaggy the rabbit and Bibi&Bobu the tomb rider siblings. Each Guardian comes with 10 cards, tokens and detailed miniature.


Extra content to add to the core game (cannot be used on its own).

—description from the publisher

You know all that content that you wanted to be in the game but our grandmas forbid to put in the core box?

It's all here.

25 Encounter cards (poker size)
5 Dungeon cards (poker size)
24 Item cards (mini size)

Additional content to add to the core game (cannot be used on its own). Some of the cards contain mature content, this expansion is recommended for 18+.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gods of War: Robert E. Lee
description:
Gods of War - Robert E. Lee is a historical wargame set in the time of the American Civil War and the second half of the 19th century. You can play battles from Bull Run to Gettysburg and from Solferino to Sedan. Thanks to our game you will become the commander in chief of an entire huge army. Do you want to equal Robert E. Lee, Ulysses S. Grant, Helmuth von Moltke or Giuseppe Garibaldi? It is possible now!

Forget about the tactics of regiments or individual brigades - you will lead entire corps and divisions. Prepare your battleplan, manage your subordinate generals, and - with a bit of luck - you will win. But be careful - fog of war is extremely important, so you will have to guess your opponent's plans and movements. The fate of the entire country is in your hands. Win and you you will become a real hero, a real strategist, a real commander-in-chief!

Are you ready to command an entire army?

—description from publisher

Jurassic Park The Spy Game is a hidden role game in which players and their team will try to accomplish their mission to beat the rival team. Discover who deserves your trust in a battle of deceit, betrayal, and strategy. Will you be a daring spy, ready to steal valuable dinosaur embryos, or will you join the Jurassic Park staff team, trying to prevent a dire outcome? The excitement and suspense build as the game progresses! Will you achieve your goal?

In this game, you capture cities by throwing dice and moving generals, and acquire generals and strategy cards from the captured cities. You can battle with your opponent using the acquired generals and strategy cards, and then use various strategy cards to attack and defend. It's a thrilling game where you can't let down your guard until the end and can turn the tables.

—description from the designer

Kotobani Naranai Mojiha Iranai Game roughly translates to "I can't put it into words; I don't need letters", and that conveys the essence of the game: Try to avoid taking letters, especially when you can't put them into words.

In the game, each player has a hand of cards and four cards are placed into a column on the table. Each card features a number and one of the 48 characters in the Hiragana syllabary.

On a turn, each player chooses and reveals a card from their hand. In numerical order, each player places their card at the end of any row in which the character on their card comes later in the Hiragana syllabary than the character at the end of the row.

If you can't place your card in any row, then you must take all of the cards in a row, making your played card the first card in a new row. The cards you take are worth negative points equal to the number of red Xs on them — but if you can create a word from some or all of these cards, you discard the used cards instead of placing them in your score pile.

The player with the lowest score at the end of the game wins.

Alliance Correspondence: Battle Swayers bears the Japanese title 決戦前の書状 ("Letter Before the Battle"), and by sending letters, you can invite or prevent betrayal to affect the course of battle.

Players are divided into eastern and western armies and aim to win the battle at Sekigahara, a strategic location where the Tokaido and Hokkoku side roads intersect. Each turn, you take three of the six rolled dice, then use their numbers to check your faction sheet and arrange the military commanders.

The game ends when a certain number of military commanders have lined up in their formations, and you earn victory points by comparing the strength values of each area, but you must also send letters in advance and be on the lookout for betrayal by your friends and enemies that can reverse an outcome you thought was certain.

"The shattered star will redeem us all", they chant in unison. Most of us don’t share their beliefs, or even really understand them. They seem to be looking for a mythical star that is rumored to have splintered eons ago and is now spread across the system. While they won’t tell us more, we reluctantly invite them to our cause. They are erratic and unpredictable, but the relics they carry seem to more than make up for it.

This Moonrakers 25-card  mini-expansion launched with Fractured Sky and features some of its characters.

In Zombie Sniper / ゾンビスナイパー, a crowd of dead people approach through the fog. You have only five bullets on hand to protect yourself. Will the zombies or humans survive?

In this game, players are divided into zombies and snipers. Each turn, all but one of the zombies approach the sniper. The sniper and the crowd must work together to identify the stationary zombies and shoot them with bullets.

If an advancing zombie reaches the final line, the zombie side wins; if the sniper can defeat three zombies before then, the sniper wins. Unfortunately, some among the crowd might be on the side of the zombies, so as a sniper, you need to think carefully about whose opinion you should listen to before acting. Your last shot can also be fired into the crowd. Will the outcome be tragedy or victory?

MOMOJAN is a card game that combines elements of Mahjong and Hanafuda and that challenges the two players to collect sets. The more sets you play, the more your hand is revealed — and the more your sets overlap, the more points you'll score if you win.

The opponent can steal your point sticks, however, and whoever holds the most point sticks at game's end wins.

It’s Christmas, but Santa’s presents were stolen! What a disaster it would be if it wasn’t for the brave bunnies’ team who decided to investigate this unacceptable crime.

Now the Bunnies Patrol has to find all the presents and give them back to Santa before the sly thief Fox gets to Santa’s house.
On their way to Santa, Bunnies will find Lost Presents, use Burrows to get from one place to another quickly, and face different obstacles like Ice Holes that they’ll have to jump over.

Every game can be unique as you can rearrange the game board each time you play! Remember that you play as a team, so no one must be left behind — everyone must get to Santa’s house in time to win.
Hurry up, Bunnies Patrol, to save Christmas for the whole world!

—description from the publisher

Now that Lord Davicus has led his army back across the Veil, two factions have emerged as threats to Strahk’s plans for the galaxy. The steadfast Immari, dwarves who have survived tragedy without measure, must persevere against insurmountable threats. And the Gorkog, nightmarish monsters whose savage assaults are carried out with cruel precision, return once again from their long slumber. Though no allies to each other, the fate of the galaxy rests with the strengths and whims of these mighty armies.

Ravaged Star is a fast-paced, strategic, science fiction miniatures tabletop wargame. It uses ten sided dice in a system that is meant to be streamlined and quick.


     Units have target numbers for attack and defense. Things such as cover and other effects add or subtract from the number needed to be rolled and rolling higher can add extra effects. 
     A chit pull system is used for activation of units including a number of Boost Tokens for each side that can activate units with extra abilities to be used when boosted.
     Miniatures in the line are fully or mostly prebuilt, with some having options for building that require a final push-fit assembly.
     Movement is done by measuring and moving the leader of a unit and moving the rest near the leader.


Gameplay is done through scenarios which may have special rules for play and may accommodate 2 players, teams, or 3 players battling it out.

In Patrix, the aim is to be the first to stack colored rings in a precise order on the last of 4 wooden rods.

Of course, you can only move one ring at a time, and the first to get it right wins the round!

Kugelzoff am Weihnachtsbaum (German for "Ornament Quarrel at the Christmas Tree") is a microgame for 2 players in postcard format.

The game is played with brass and copper coins (e.g. 10x €0.10 and 10x €0.02) on a grid of dots and cones arranged in diagonal lines across a Christmas tree.

The coins represent Christmas ornaments and players take turns placing a coin on the tree. The first coin must always be placed on a cone. Each new coin must be placed in a diagonal line with the last coin. Two coins of the same color can never be placed next to each other.

The game ends when no more coins can be placed. Players score 1 point for each of their coins on diagonal lines with 2 or more of their coins. They also score 1 point for each of their own coins next to a light bulb of their preferred color (the color is determined by the side of the postcard they're sitting on).

The player with the most points wins the game. In case of a tie, the player who placed the last coin wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 奔跑吧！红细胞 (Run, Red Blood Cells)
description:
Explore the mysteries of the human body with Run, Red Blood Cells!
This game assists young children in recognizing the structure of the human body. The game board and event cards offer extensive knowledge expansion, helping kids understand their bodies and instill a sense of self-protection from an early age. Learning is seamlessly blended with enjoyment in this accessible and engaging game.

Features


     Simple and easy-to-learn gameplay
     Suitable for ages 4 and above
     Red Blood Cells move along 5 routes to transport oxygen
     Roll the dice to determine the number of steps to move
     Victory is achieved by filling specified body parts on your card with nutrients


Game Components


     Game board
     Event cards
     Dice
     Target cards


Gameplay Mechanics


     Players draw a target card and roll the dice to determine movement.
     Transport nutrients to the target positions on the card.
     The first player to successfully fill all specified body parts wins.

ควงพลิ้ว ("Meatspin") is the bluffing and negotiation card game. The game show about "Pants Tugging", The great playing and pranking in Thai childhood.

While playing, Each players have to find the pants and don't be ripped off from another players. If you show the "Weenie". You will be eliminate from the round. You have to be the last one for get the Point.

This game contains the "Weenie" "Penis" "Dick" "Cock" and "Meatspin".

—description from the designer

Hell under the sea is a solo war game where the player will command a German submarine from World War II. A single decision made can change the course of the patrol and the mission.

You can purchase it at https://www.wargamevault.com/product/464413/HELL-UNDER-THE-SEA
​

Objectives

     Complete as many waypoints as possible until turn 20.
     Sink the greatest amount of tonnage.
     Do not be destroyed while patrolling.


Preparation

     Write the name of the submarine.
     Mark the number of the mission to be played in the log.
     Mark the difficulty of the mission in the log.
     Mark on the turn counter at number 1


—description from the designer

boardgame: Kamigami Battles: Rani of Jhansi Promo
description:
Promo # ACFP002

Promo warrior card released for Kamigami Battles: Avatars of Cosmic Fire.

"ACT: Remove 1 Warrior from the current Chain, then add it to the Chain after Rani of Jhansi and use one of its ACT Abilities. You may only use this Ability if the chosen Warrior could be legally played into the Chain."

Pitstop is a racing game where you go around the course in one turn.
Earn points by taking the lead or overtaking the car in front of you, and the player with the most points after 20 laps wins.
In order to complete the race, you must replenish your deck by "pitting" in the middle of the game.

—description from the designer

Hot Sauce Poker is a push-your-luck, bluffing card game where you make spicy decisions! How well can you read your family and friends? Can you outbid them? There's only one way to find out!

Hot Sauce Poker takes place over a series of rounds where players compete in the Hot Sauce Challenge. The first player to gain two victories is the winner.

In each round players start with five sauce cards from which they choose one to keep and pass the rest to the player on their left and repeat this process five times. Then each player chooses three cards they wish to use and places them face down in front of them (the two cards remaining in their hand will be used for their special abilities later). Players in turn state higher and higher challenges (how much heat they can consume) or pass. The player with the highest challenge then has to turn over five face-down cards belonging to any players. If the total heat level of these cards matches or exceeds their challenge value, they take a victory card, otherwise they failed and gain a penalty card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The expansion pack “STORY MODE” holds several scenarios (～ 20) offering narrative gameplay options and 10 modifiers. Spice up your game and dive into the world of SURVIVE UNTIL DAYLIGHT!

A few mod examples:

Crit! (-) Remove “1 damage” cards from the combat deck.

Collateral (-) All enemies inflict 1 splash damage.

At War (+) Your “first player perk” is always activated.

Epic baby! (+)  Add the “epic” ammo and weapon cards to the rubble deck.

A few stories examples:

PVP: (2P+) Switch from a co-op game to a competitive game. Eliminate the players at the table to win the game.

Collector: (2P+) Keep all enemies you killed as trophies. At the end of the game, the player who has racked up the most kills win the game.

Secret raider: (4P+) Before you begin a game, shuffle the “secret raider” deck. Each player draws a card randomly. One of them is the secret raider. Their goal: ensure the downfall of the group. Uncover who this secret betrayer is to win the game. But guess wisely!

—description from the publisher

El Camino de Santiago is the first board game dedicated to the Camino de Santiago; it's an inner journey and a sharing experience. From 1 to 5 players embark on a path composed of 31 stages, the evocative stops where various events, the unpredictable occurrences of the journey, must be faced.

The pilgrims progress by balancing mental strength and physical resources in an innovative path game, where the winner isn’t the one who arrives first but the one who has gained the most spirituality points.

As it happens in reality, you'll discover that reaching the goal isn’t enough; it's fundamental to get there in the best way possible!

The Path
The game includes all the dynamics of the Camino de Santiago, the route that connects, in nearly eight hundred kilometers, the French town of Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port to Santiago de Compostela, where the relics of the Apostle Saint James the Greater are venerated.

The route known as the Camino Francés is the most famous among the many paths that cross a large part of Europe. This itinerary, which from the Middle Ages to today challenges pilgrims from all over the world, has been declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO.

The locations along the Camino Francés are represented on 32 evocative large-format cards. Players can manage the length of the game from a minimum of 5 stages up to a maximum of 31. Additionally, there is the "catedral de Santiago" card, essential to complete the journey and receive the famous "compostela."

—description from the designer

El Camino de Santiago è il primo gioco in scatola dedicato al Cammino di Santiago; è un viaggio interiore e un’esperienza di condivisione. Da 1 a 5 giocatori intraprendono un percorso composto da 31 etapas, le suggestive tappe in cui si devono affrontare svariati eventos, gli avvenimenti imprevedibili del cammino.

I peregrinos avanzano bilanciando forza mentale e risorse fisiche, in un gioco di percorso innovativo, dove non vince chi arriva primo ma chi ha guadagnato più punti spiritualità.

Come accade nella realtà, scoprirete che non basta raggiungere il traguardo, ma è fondamentale arrivarci nel modo migliore!

Il Percorso
L’opera propone tutte le dinamiche del Cammino di Santiago, l’itinerario che collega, in quasi ottocento chilometri, la cittadina francese di Saint-Jean- Pied-de-Port a Santiago de Compostela, dove si venerano le reliquie dell’Apostolo san Giacomo il Maggiore.

Il percorso denominato Camino Francés è il più famoso dei tanti percorsi che attraversano gran parte dell’Europa. Questo itinerario, che dal Medioevo a oggi mette alla prova i peregrinos di tutto il mondo, è stato dichiarato patrimonio dell’umanità dall’Unesco.

Le località del Camino Francés sono state rappresentate in 32  suggestive carte di grande formato. I giocatori possono gestire la lunghezza della partita da un minimo di 5  tappe fino a un massimo di 31. In aggiunta, la carta de la catedral de Santiago, indispensabile per completare il viaggio e ricevere la famosa compostela.

—description from the designer (Italian)

The popular book series "Wheel Tales" is known to many and is a favorite of many young children, as the wonderful world of Anna Mechler has been expanded with countless vehicles, and almost every character has a small story attached to it.

The board game Jonatán delivers is based on the fairy tale series.  In this exciting game, children can meet Jonathan the truck and his friends, who have been given an urgent delivery order.  The goods are eagerly awaited in stores, so players should try.  Whoever gets there first with the vehicle wins.  But just be careful with your speeding, because sometimes an ambulance or a tow truck blocks the way.

Survive the Night or Become the Shadow.

A city is being terrorized by a monstrous threat that attacks, kills, and, worse, infects its citizens, turning them into grotesque beings.
A traditional family of extraordinary hunters arrives on the scene to try to stop the menace. They can only rely on each other and a small arsenal of special items to save the inhabitants before the day ends and it's too late.
A monstrous challenge for these supernatural hunters that can easily turn into a dog's day.
O Dia da Caça takes players into an epic confrontation between hunters and monsters, with strategic mechanics and unpredictable twists. Choose your fate, fight for survival, or plunge into the darkness as the monster haunting the city.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wage Workers PAX UNPLUGGED 2023 Promo Card (fan expansion for Android: Netrunner)
description:
The Wage Workers is a promo card for Null Signal Games (NISEI) fan expansion for Android: Netrunner.

It is a Corporation card for the Haas-Bioroid (HB) megacorporation of type Asset. It has a install cost of 2 and a trash value of 4.

Available to any attendees who stopped by the Null Signal Games booth at PAX UNPLUGGED 2023 in Philadelphia, PA. There were 3 copies of the card given out to each visitor to the booth.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This pack contains 6 promo cards with 4 brand new words to draw on each card. That’s 24 new possible ways to make your friends look ridiculous! Each card includes a Challenge Word like the rest of the cards in the game, so get ready for even more 2-Player, Forbidden Word, and Don’t Look challenges as well. These promo cards all feature gaming-themed words to tickle your funny bone while they up the challenge.

The backs of these promo cards feature all four numbers (1,2,3,4) so when they are revealed as the top card of your deck they will allow you to choose any word from your current card.

This item contains six (6) promo cards in a zip-lock bag.

—description from the publisher

Choose your avatar, and race to find the Essence of Eternity, the secret to eternal life. Journey through the forest wilderness and majestic mountains before risking the perils of the underworld realm to find the precious runes needed to become immortal. Battle fearsome monsters and cast powerful spells. But beware! Your opponents have powers and control monsters of their own. Are you cunning enough to outwit them? Immortality is within your grasp, but at what cost? A very uncooperative fantasy card game for 2-6 players.

motoGP the BoardGame is a motorbike racing game in which as the riders of the 2023 world championship and some legends of this sport you can challenge each other to the last corner.

Roll the die, use vectors, accumulate energy, buy cards and use them at the right time. The aim is only to cross the finish line before the others.

Game box comes with instructions booklet and a set of 204 cards. Designed for up to 4 players. Game matches range from 20 minutes to 60 minutes.

The goal of the game is to build your own game board, and the first player to complete their game board, wins! Players do this by taking turns drawing Dice cards and playing them along with Assist cards. Assist cards range from as simple as adding +2 to your "roll" to as strategic as making an opponent go back the number you "rolled" instead of you going forward on your turn. Once the player has "rolled" their dice, they draw that number of Tile cards and lay them out in front of them in a grid pattern. Tile cards are square cards that resemble the spaces of a board game.  Your goal is to build a 5x5 grid with Tiles. If you are the first to do this, you win!

Tiles come in three colors: Blue, Green, and Red. The last Tile you draw and place on your turn, adding it to your board, is considered the space you've "landed" on. Blue Tiles don't do anything, they are a neutral space to land on. Green and Red Tiles, when landed on, result in that player drawing a Good or Bad card. As the names suggest, Good cards help you while Bad cards hurt you. Think of them like the chance cards in traditional board games. If a player is made to move backwards, they'll discard the most recently placed Tiles on their board to represent them moving back, and when they go forward again on their next turn, it'll be all new Tile cards they'll draw, permanently changing their board from what it once was, creating endless replayability. Every time you play, it'll be a different board you're playing on and the board will constantly be changing while you play.

With over 20 unique ability cards, some powerful enough to knock the leader down to last place or position the player in the back of the pack for a quick victory, it's anyone's game until the very last second.

This tactical family game takes you into the African savannahs to save 8 endangered animal species. Players take on the role of Maasai guides. They must each choose two animal species and lead them to parks where they will be protected. Tourists and poachers can sometimes interfere with their movements, but a few watering holes will make it easier for the guides to lead them to havens where they can reproduce.

On Christmas Day, 1985, Mali invaded Burkina Faso over a dispute in the contested Agacher Strip. In what became known as the Agacher Strip War, two of the poorest nations on earth fought over a strip of the southern Sahara. Air operations featured prominently, but there were no air-to-air combats. By the time of the Agacher Strip War, Burkina Faso had just one MiG-17, but until a few months before the Force Aérienne de Burkina Faso (FABF) had a force of eight Libyan supplied and manned MiG-21s. Merry Christmas, Mr Sankara imagines if these had still be available during the conflict.

Merry Christmas, Mr Sankara has a background of the war and the forces involved, a full campaign system, maps and a painting guide. Players have to balance offensive and defensive operations, keep an eye on public and world opinion and avoid attrition that they can ill afford.

In a short war players will use Soviet Cold War era jets – MiG-17s and MiG-21s – for air strikes, ground attack, escort and fighter missions and Mi-4 helicopters to transport troops. In the vastness of the Sahara players will need to form a strategy to seize key road junctions. For the Burkinabe player it will be a tough proposition; the Malian forces are more numerous but the Libyan pilots are more experienced.

The campaign should provide between 5 and 8 tactical games. Though designed with Wings at War: Thud Ridge in mind, it can be used with your favourite jet-age rules.

—description from the publisher

The box actually contains "Tel est pris" and "Le Joker baladeur".

The former is a a checkers game in which you have to capture your opponent's letters in order to be the first to form a five-letter word.

In the second, a piece moves across the board, where the pieces have been placed face-up. Staying on the same row or column, the joker picks up the flipped pieces and brings them back to the player, who assembles them into a crossword puzzle.

In KALLAX, your goal will be to fill your shelf with game boxes, following the placement instructions: the right game in the right place for the best collection ever.

A game round is composed of the following 3 phases:

1. Replenish up to two retail slot (facultative)
At the start of your turn, add game boxes drawn from the bag in up to two of the five retail slot. A retail slot cannot accommodate more than 4 Game Boxes.

2. Buy games
Buying consists of pick up game boxes from the retail slots in the center of the play area. You can only purchase one Game Box per retail slot. The purchased game boxes are placed in your shopping cart. The total volume of your purchases during your turn must not exceed the capacity of your shopping cart.

3. Storing your games and organizing your shelf
All the game boxes purchased during this round + one and only one from the unplayed pile are stored in your shelves.

A complete shelf compartment (fully filled) allows you to instantly validate a storage ojective. You score 1 point per game box in the compartment you just completed. You advance your pawn on the score track of the total points thus obtained.

Extra points are scored at the end of the game for objectives and contracts achieved. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.
family: Theme: Rooms: Interiour Design, Home Decoration, Tidying

description: Welcome to the world of ancient Cossack democracy! Immerse yourself in the rich history and vibrant culture of the Cossacks with Rada: A Cossack Council, a quick and engaging card game designed for 3 to 6 players. Experience the thrill of vying for the prestigious Hetman title in the Cossack army. Will you emerge victorious as the supreme leader, or will your rivals outmaneuver you in the quest for power?

Monopoly fans can explore locations from movies such as Disney and Pixar's Up, The Incredibles, Toy Story, Finding Nemo, and more. 
Monopoly tokens including Hector's guitar from Coco, the Boot from Wall-E, Carl's House from Up, the Pizza Planet Truck from Toy Story, and the iconic Pixar Ball, and Lamp.
Players can collect rent as they add Cozy Cone motels and Al's Toy Barns to owned property sets.

The players take turns to roll and move all their pieces. Each has cars and trucks, which have to accomplish certain tasks to record on the control board.
