While moving the players have to mind the traffic rules. The first player to fulfill all tasks and returns all his cars and trucks to their starting space wins the game.

—user summary

Musket & Pike Battles is a free Print & Play Tactical level wargame simulating the art of warfare in Europe in the Seventeenth Century. The game presents the tactics and possible results of many of the battles during this period. These battles are shown as modules giving a mix of units which are moved and engage in combat on the mapsheet. It is an evolution of the Medieval & Renaissance battles rules to Seventeenth Century (https://boardgamegeek.com/filepage/179616/medieval-renaissance-battles).

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Dungeon Scrawlers – Heroes of Waterdeep: Mini-Dungeons Promo Pack
description:
Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 11 Kickstarter campaign.

Set of four promotional mini-dungeons cards. They should be shuffled into Mini-Dungeons deck, expanding the pool of available cards.

Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 11 Kickstarter campaign.

One of the 8th Circle Realms.

Power:
⛧ (Favor) times, each in a different Realm:
Take one Minion belonging to each Player present in that Realm. Mix them up in one hand then blindly choose one to be Destroyed. Return the others to that Realm.

In Museum of Treasures, 2 or 3 players compete to be the first to set up all 5 different types of exhibits (dinosaurs, space, transport, Egypt and art) in their museum. To achieve this they have to, on their turn, roll their 6 dice and place them on of the available exhibit-cards which displays the same value. The first player to put all the necessary dice on the exhibit-card, claims it and gets to decide which is the next type of exhibit that also becomes available. Furthermore there are also "admin"-cards to be claimed, which provide a one-off special ability.
For 4 players there is a team mode.
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Federation Faction Pack: Lost in the Delta Quadrant
description:
Explore the unknown Delta Quadrant with the newest Federation faction pack in Star Trek: Attack Wing! Add brand new versions of the Intrepid-class, Nova-Class, Aerie-Class, and Delta Flyer-Class to your fleet captained by Kathryn Janeway, Rudolph Ransom, Magnus Hansen, and Tom Paris. Add the crews of the U.S.S. Voyager and U.S.S. Equinox to your ships as you try to find your way home through the Delta Quadrant Campaign. Equip new weapons and tech such as the Gravimetric Torpedoes and Temporal Shielding to assure victory. 70,000 light years seems a lot closer with a little luck, some coffee, and help from Neelix.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Independents Faction Pack: Adversaries of the Delta Quadrant
description:
With the Adversaries of the Delta Quadrant, pillage and hunt through the stars with 4 different independent ships: a Hirogen Warship, Nerada-Type, Numiri Patrol Ship, and a Vidiian Battlecruiser. Place a bounty on your opponent or even affect the reputation of their favorite starship with characters such as Kurros, Y’Sek, and Dala. Use new upgrades such as the Isokinetic Cannon and Refractive Shielding to provide a challenge for your opponent in the Delta Quadrant Campaign.

—description from the publihser

boardgame: The War Chronicles of Ganymede Zero PLUS
description:
ガニメデ戦記Zero+ (The War Chronicles of Ganymede Zero+) is a two to four player card game from Japan in which Mechs are enhanced with transparent cards.

In 209X, Nigaleos, who was contracted for development by the Veil Army, was developing two types of Mechs, one specialized in attack and one specialized in defense. The airframe was built at both the research base and the production base.
The introduction of this 'Mech to the front line should have brought the battle on Ganymede to an end quickly. MEC engineers believed so and continued development. But Nigaleos executives had a different idea.

Ganymede Senki Zero+ contains both ガニメデ戦記Zero and ガニメデ戦記Zero 2 and play with up to 4 people.
publishers: さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie)

boardgame: Exploit Zero: Snitches, Glitches, and DAF Augs
description:
Snitches, Glitches, and DAF Augs is a supplement for the solo/cooperative game, Exploit Zero, Cyberpunk Espionage and Mayhem. You need that core rulebook to get the most out of this material. This is not a stand-alone game.

Inside, you'll find mission prompts, tables for generating back stories, ways to add complications to your cyberpunk dark ops, and most of all, new, bleeding edge cyber-limbs for your Agents to field test on the table top.

Of course none of this is so proprietary you can't tweak it and slot it into your favorite mirror shade future. After all, the dark chrome heart of cyberpunk is open source.

Just remember that mega-corps don't do sympathy and they definitely don't tolerate failure; you're deniable, so don't expect backup if your shiny new black market gear locks up in the middle of a mission.

Which it might.

Good luck.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Der Herr der Ringe: Der Ringträger
description:
You and your companions are on the dangerous path to Mount Doom. If you manage to destroy the ring before the Nazgûl catch you, you win together. But the ring bearer is constantly exposed to Sauron's power. He can also surrender to it. Prevent this by coordinating well and using the right cards at the right time. Are you able to handle the power?

—description from the publisher

"A ring to enslave them, to find them all. To drift into the dark and bind forever."

This dark prophecy is borne by the one ring that players will handle in Der Herr der Ringe: Der Ringträger as they take the roles of the ring-bearer Frodo and his companions and make their way to Mordor to destroy the ring and save Middle-earth from the evil of Sauron.

In more detail, the players move across the different landscape tiles, starting in the Shire and fleeing from the constant threat of the Black Riders, the Nazgûl.

One player starts the game as the ring bearer, wearing an actual ring. The game is played co-operatively, with the cards in your hand being partly visible to yourself and partly visible only to the other players; players can play cards from any hand.

Each round, Sauron's power is strengthened and the Black Riders move forward. If they catch the companions, the game is lost. If a fifth card of Sauron's is placed in front of the ring bearer, they fall under the dark power and becomes Sauron's assistant; in the future, the ring bearer plays against the companions, with the "Dark Deck" at their disposal.

After this dark turn, helper cards are laid out and can provide necessary support for the companions. The active player carries out two actions, preparing the journey or discovering the next landscape, before sending the companions on their journey and ensuring them as much of a lead as possible over the pursuers.

If the companions reach Mount Doom without being overtaken by the Black Riders, they win together. If a person becomes Sauron's assistant, they rely on the Nazgûl to enable themselves and their master to win, with the remaining companions trying to prevent this from happening.

boardgame: Manhattan 1929 (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
Manhattan 1929 is a fan-made Print & Play Age of Steam expansion map for 3-5 players, designed by Clef Haworth.

To simulate the stock-market crash of 1929, the game features "crashing" cities, which effectively become colorless Cities devoid of cubes once its empty column is rolled during the Goods Growth phase. Each time this occurs, all players immediately lose Income equal to half that of their current Income Reduction tier. Once a number of Cities equal to the starting player count, Wall Street will also crash, doubling all Income Reduction amounts going forward and preventing further issuing of Shares.

Catharsis Sagas are true solo game boxes that include one character & one boss. If you own the base game, the boss plays up to five players. The game requires strategy, cooperation, and a touch of luck to succeed as the party fights through traps, monsters, and more. You will hunt down the boss while going through an epic narrative. Roll your dice to activate your powers, but be warned, your powers are also your health. You must carefully decide when it's wise to activate a power and how to resolve damage. Spend your morale wisely, or else you and your party will fall to the corruption of Verity.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Chicago 1893: The City Beautiful Tile Game
description:
In Chicago 1893: The City Beautiful, you will compete with your rivals to help rebuild the city of Chicago in preparation for the World's Exposition in 1893! Place Workers to build Neighborhoods, Rails, Parks, Boulevards and Monuments, and try to install your Alderman in the most influential Ward in the City. Whoever can build the most impressive modern amenities and conveniences for a vibrant city will bask in the glory of establishing Chicago as the model for "The City Beautiful!" 
 In October of 1871, the Great Chicago Fire burned a path nearly four miles long through the heart of the City. The destruction was extensive, but it created an opportunity to rebuild Chicago as a modern city. When Chicago was chosen as the location for The World's Columbian Exposition to be held there was a surge of effort to beautify the city and improve its infrastructure in anticipation of hosting travelers from all over the world.

—description from the publisher

Similar to dominoes with a few twists. There are 92 tiles, each with 3 images in a combination from among 6 characters (themed versions include SpongeBob SquarePants, Peanuts, and Space Invaders).  In the center of the table, 3 tiles are placed to form a square. You are given a hand of 3 tiles. On your turn you may either place a tile so at least one character matches an adjacent one, or stack a tile fully on top of an existing one. Score 1 point for each match to your newly placed tile or 2 points for each matching overlapping character. Play until a player has 25 or 50 points, or until the tile stack is depleted.

Leap of Faith is a 2-player cooperative card game. Without having information about the partner's hand, players cooperate to achieve the highest total score possible after the game is finished. It consists of 3 rounds, each of which is again divided into 2 phases - declaration phase and realization phase. In the declaration phase, players take turns playing one of the cards from one's hand to set the 'goal' for the round. In the realization phase, players again take turns playing one card at a time to get as close as possible to the declared goal number. The score for the round is the number of cards used for declaration minus the gap between the goal number and the realized number.

—description from the publisher

Роботех ("Robotech") was Tehnolog's 2003 release for a science-fiction miniatures-based combat game based on robots.

The game mechanics involve fights between piloted and robotic machines.  The rules of the game were typical of many other miniatures games, with movement and range done by measuring distances, and results rolled for by dice.

The rulebook included many different chassis, each with different pros and cons to them.  Weapons were placed on the machines but were limited to the weight capacity of the chassis.

Robots were divided into heavy and light. Robots had the ability to ram, jump, crouch, fight in hand-to-hand combat, and shoot. During the course of the battle their ammunition could run out or their weapons could be destroyed.

The size of the armies varied in size from 3-5 vehicles per side.

The game did not last long, and was phased out by 2006, taking on the form of Robogear.

—user summary

The "Tank destruction badge" is awarded to infantrymen who destroy a tank with a weapon they can carry.
Players carry "empty cans," "rifles," "grenades" and "Panzerfausts" to attack enemy tanks ranging from "Type 97 Medium Tank" to "Tiger I" tanks.

If the armor value of the enemy tank and the attack power of the carried weapons are equal or more, the enemy tank will be destroyed.

To prevent other players from taking the battle results, you can play a card that is actually different from the card you declare to use. Players can play different cards from the ones they declare to be used in order to prevent other players from taking their tanks.

Each player is given an order card, and if he or she achieves the order, he or she will receive a bonus.

The player who destroys the most targets wins.

The game starts with a collective energy consumption of 500 units and a collective generation of energy of 200 units. On your turn, move your pawn on a map showing districts of Baden-Württemberg to another district according to the roll of the die. You may either build a power plant or place a detective looking for energy thieves there. Then you roll another die determining if you have to draw a question or an event card. You get income from your power plants at the end of your turn. Each power plant rises the generation of energy each turn. A detective reduces the energy consumption. At the end of each 5th round, all players must pay for the difference of consumption and generation. The game ends if consumption is not higher than generation anymore. The player with the most money wins. If this is not the case at the end of the 30th round, all players lose.
publishers: Ministerium für Umwelt, Klima und Energiewirtschaft Baden-Württemberg

description:
It is 1340, and master-illuminator Orimina is asked to illustrate a magnificent bible. As his assistants the players try to impress him by placing the most beautiful images next to the text. Complex illustrations are awarded more points than simple ones, and bonus points can be earned by making the right combinations. Will you design the most amazing manuscript?

Anjou offers a unique playing experience because of its closeness to history. Every single image in the game, including the backgrounds to the cards are derived from the actual historical manuscript. Players are not only imagining to be member's of master Orimina's workshop, they are literally playing with an exceptionally beautiful piece of medieval history!

—description from the publisher

Monte Perdido is a lost town but one that everyone has heard of at some point. Since the discovery of some mysterious stones and every ten years, in this humble and prosperous town they hold a contest among their countrymen to promote livestock and agriculture on their lands. To win the contest, participants will have to be able to combine the symbols of the mysterious stones in the most efficient way to be the first to build their farm, raise livestock, cultivate the garden, get a tractor to work on the land and some farmers who are in charge of maintaining and managing the farm. Operación Granja is a very fun and addictive roll and write game in which players combine two mystery stones (ten-sided dice) to obtain money or resources to build their farm. It has two game modes: an "easy mode" in which players perform addition and subtraction to obtain coins and an "advanced mode" where players perform multiplications with the dice to obtain the money and resources necessary to buy the different farm elements.

This satirical game was inspired by the former Czech prime minister, Andrej Babiš, who was very controversial and got involved in many questionable adventures, often resulting in police investigations and corruption accusations. 'Bureš' is the codename he assumed when he collaborated with the former communist secret police.

Bureš is an "honest" politician who "occasionally" slips. Your goal is to gather as much evidence as you can and get him behind bars. However, Bureš is fully aware that you're after him and will do all he can to thwart your efforts. Moreover, you're not the only one trying to get him.

Nalle-Maja has lost her pearls from her necklace, and Bamse and his friends promise to help her find them.
But during the excursion, they happen to drink Skalman's XYZ juice and shrink to tiny sizes.
Among leaves, grass, flowers and mushrooms, they continue their search, helped by small insects and a friendly frog.
Your goal is to help them find all the gems and then drink ZYX juice to return to normal size.
Who will drink the juice first and win the game, proving that even small heroes can make big changes?

Une Histoire Extraordinaire is a hilarious family game, combining aspects of memorization, observation and cooperation. A game that can help younger children with reading and grammatical construction, but which is above all a fun, atmospheric game for all ages.

Players take it in turns to play cards (noun, adjective or verb) that will make up the story to be told. Before laying down the card that will continue the story, the active player must, from memory, tell the whole story from the beginning. The other players can help, but only by miming. If he makes a mistake in the word order, the game ends immediately. The aim is to lay down as many cards as possible.

boardgame: Star Realms: Blob Turkey Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the Star Realms Deluxe Colonial Collection Kickstarter Campaign as well as available at PAX Unplugged 2023.

Card states: Draw a card

Security Craft
Faction: Blob Ship
Cost: 3
Primary Ability: Combat (1) Draw a card.
Ally Ability: Destroy target base.

The game is simple: Players will take turns being the judge, reading a prompt off of a card. All other players will then write down their response to the prompt, but with a twist: All answers must be wrong!

The judge will read aloud the responses and choose their favorite. If you wrote the judge's favorite response, you win the round. Do this five times, and you win the game!

With ninety-four carefully crafted prompt cards (and two blanks so you can create your own), Wrong Answers Only is sure to bring you hours of hilarity and surprisingly deep conversations about things like "why Danny Devito should be added to Mount Rushmore."

We hope you have as much fun playing this game as we did making it! Get your copy today and add Wrong Answers Only to your next game night!

—description from the designer

It's eat-or-be-eaten in this twist on Go Fish. Cast your line, reel in your catch, but beware -- reel in a sardine, and you'll be sorry! Outbluff your opponents to score the biggest catch!

In this twist on a childhood classic, you'll play Go Fish as usual. However, when giving cards to other players, you do so face-down. They must then decide whether you're being honest or bluffing -- a decision that will end with either their reward or ruin.

—description from the designer

Find out what your vanlifer profile is thanks to this board game which is aimed at adventure enthusiasts of all ages. With family or friends, you will develop your experience by planning your trip, improving your equipment and learning to manage your resources well.

Throughout your evenings, you will discover 100 “spot” maps magnificently illustrated in the style of famous tourist posters. A real invitation to travel and road trip through the great wild natural spaces.
 Sharing and helping each other between players will also be values ​​that will be rewarded throughout the game. The player who has avoided pitfalls and obtained the most experience to reach great spots wins the game!

Memorize fanciful groupings? That's the challenge in the party game MFG.
In german MFG stands for “Best regards” but in this game it could also stand for something completely different!
MFG could also stand for "Mysterios frogs grow". But this is not about well-known abbreviations. In MFG, we dedicate ourselves to unknown letter combinations and give them a meaning together. NVD might stand for "Numerous vegan delicacies". OSW could become "One-eyed sleepy watchman" or something completely different. There is no right or wrong here. The only important thing is that you remember the abbreviation later. Because that's the only way to score lots of points!

—description from the publisher

Which little bathroom monster can roll off the longest strip of paper from the toilet roll? Lay your toilet paper cards smartly to form as long a row of cards as possible to collect points. But keep an eye on the others’ cards too. Maybe you can interfere  somehow and tear their strip of toilet paper. The one who has the most points at the end, wins.

It’s Pancake Picnic time at the zoo! The animals are really hungry and you’ve got the tricky job of getting the pancakes onto the picnic blanket! Take aim and throw your pancakes like the animals on the cards. Will your brilliant throws win you the game?

Who is the best skimboarder? Take beach waves anywhere and see who can catch the coolest tube ever in Skim Waves!

Setup: with the measure of three boards (Card in Vertical), make a wave tubular with a hand or object.

Game: Players try to slide inside the tube. Who doesn't passing through the wave is eliminated. If more than one player passes by the wave, make a new wave with three more boards away.

Winner: The player who catch the farthest wave alone.

Solo mode: Record video of your furthest wave

—description from the publisher

In the blue city there are several cars, but there is one that awakens great emotions in people wherever it passes: the FUCAZULl! Scientists and sociologists cannot explain what this car generates in people. The only certainty is that when FUCAZUL appears, chaos is guaranteed! Let's bug your mind, test your luck and end the day at cards! Enter this incredible world of 3 games in 1 with Fucazul.

Game 1
The game 1 aka "FUCAZUL!" is a dexterity game that aims to collect as many fucazul (beetle cars) cards as possible. In case of a tie, whoever has the most car cards wins. If the tie persists, the victory is shared.

Game 2
The game 2 aka  "Where is my Fucazul?" consists of a push-your-luck game. You must go through the cards and get the highest score possible at the end of 3 games. Try to go as far as possible without stopping in a fucazul(beetle cars). Did you find a fucazul(beetle cars)?
I am sorry! You must take it to the workshop.

Game 3
The game 3 aka Fucazul Race is a trick-taking card game that has trump, dynamic super trump, and the score is a race. In this fucazul trick-taking card game  your objective is to make your fucazul cross the finish line or go further than your opponents' fucazul!

—description from the publisher

Solve the Case is the ultimate escape room experience packaged in a portable suitcase! With five unique plays to choose from, you’ll be challenged to solve puzzles and clues using 3D support. Each play comes with a set of cards that will guide you through the game, challenging you to think outside the case! You’ll be immersed in a world of mystery and intrigue as you work your way through each level, using your problem-solving skills to reach each outcome.

--description from publisher

In the vast land of China, spanning five millennia, every corner boasts its unique cuisine. As a delivery driver, your task is to freely navigate this land, delivering local delicacies nationwide and celebrating the culinary culture of each region. Transform mouth-watering dishes into city cards, view the world through the eyes of a delivery driver, and embark on a magical journey exploring the flavors of exquisite cuisine. From taking orders to picking up and delivering meals, you can traverse the mountains and rivers in seconds, visiting 57 representative cities across the country and discovering the characteristic cuisines of each.

Game Objectives

Complete orders to earn more points.

Game Setup


     Unfold and place the game board on the tabletop.
     Each player chooses and takes a pawn of the same color and three takeout boxes.
     Shuffle all order cards with the back facing up, randomly reveal three cards beside the board, and place the remaining cards as the order card deck.
     Deal four order cards to each player. Choose two cards, place them face up in front of you, and return the remaining two to the box.
     Each player selects one card from the two face-up cards as their starting point card.
     Place your pawn on the corresponding takeout city on the board according to the starting point card.
     Finally, place the takeout boxes above the starting point card.


Gameplay


     Accept orders
     Roll the dice to determine the number of steps to reach the destination and score points.
     The first player to reach 15 points wins.


Components


     Game board x1
     Order cards x57
     Rider pawns x4
     Dice x1
     Red takeout boxes x3
     Green takeout boxes x3
     Blue takeout boxes x3
     Yellow takeout boxes x3
     Instruction manual x1

DRA&B: Devils, Robots, Aliens & Beasts is a strategic card game where 2 to 5 players compete by assembling an army of monstrous creatures. The deck consists of 60 cards, 40 of which are unique. Each commander must recruit 5 allies and try to gather the most points to defeat their opponents. Draw, trade, choose blindly! At the end of the turn, you will have five monsters in hand, each with its own ability that increases (or decreases!) your power.

Whoever manages to recruit the strongest creatures will be the winner and will earn a victory token. Obtain three tokens, and you will be the ultimate winner!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Битвы Fantasy: Битва за маяк (Battle for the Lighthouse)
description:
This is an introductory action game to the Fantasy Battles system. The system uses actual firing devices, similar to Crossbows and Catapults.

Players roll a die for actions and use them to either move pieces or fire. Any soldier that is knocked down is eliminated from the game. Players can also move their soldiers into contact with the enemy and fight melees. Players can also use magic cards to assist them.

The attacker can win by capturing the enemy's flag or by eliminating all soldiers.  To prevent this, the defender must eliminate all the attacking soldiers.

The game comes with:

     Company "Asteriscus" - 5 figures 
     Squad "Konung" - 5 figures 
     Gang "Grobag" - 5 figures 
     Brigade "Gulrog" - 4 figures
     Assembled figure "Dragon" - 1 piece 
     Modular construction set - more than 100 parts
     Heavy catapult "Porcupine" - 2 pieces 
     Assault gun "Hammer" - 2 pieces 
     Rules of the game "Ultra" - 1 piece
     Magic cards "Ultra" - 25 pieces 
     Army lists - 4 pieces Artifact 
     Map - 4 pieces
     Dice - 1 Piece 
     Painting Guide - 1 Piece


The game can be further expanded by adding other boxed sets and blister packs of figures.

Throw a die to stroll around the carnival grounds of a World's Fair, spending tickets to play games and enjoying the rides. Win Prize Cards as you do so. The player who has the most complete set of Prize Cards when the game ends is the winner.

boardgame: Битвы Fantasy: Бастион в осаде (Bastion Under Siege)
description:
This is an introductory action game to the Fantasy Battles system. The system uses actual firing devices, similar to Crossbows and Catapults.

Players roll a die for actions and use them to either move pieces or fire. Any soldier that is knocked down is eliminated from the game. Players can also move their soldiers into contact with the enemy and fight melees. Players can also use magic cards to assist them.

The attacker can win by capturing the enemy's flag or by eliminating all soldiers.  To prevent this, the defender must eliminate all the attacking soldiers.

The game comes with:

     Magnum Legion - 5 figures 
     Typhoon Legion - 6 figures
     Throwing Machine "Bug" with a set of shells - 1 model 
     Siege Gun "Thunder" with a set of shells - 1 model 
     Rocket mortar "Grad" with a set of shells - 1 model 
     Light cannon "Bulldog" with a set of shells - 1 model
     Modular constructor for assembling fortifications - more than 50 parts
     Rules of the game "Ultra" - 1 pcs
     Magic Cards "Ultra" - 25 pcs.
     Army lists - 2 pcs
     Artifact Cards - 2 pcs
     Painting Instructions - 1 pc
     Dice - 1 pc.


The game can be further expanded by adding more armies to the game.  There are more than two dozen additional forces that can be added, as well as other boxed sets.

Players becomes a thief and play1 card from your hand per 1 round at the same time.
When thief plays the card of the “same number” or “same color” with Phantom thief, he/she can steal the gem from Phantom thief.
If you steal or save the gem at the end of the game, you will be the winner!
However, in the middle of the game, Phantom thief play the card to the Notice and save the gem, Phantom thief becomes the winner too. Let’s try to steal or save the gem and be the winner.

—description from the publisher

Tsubushiai is abstract game like death game.
You are aim to win the game to “escape to exit” or “make opponent immobile” or “squash opponent”.

On your turn, You do the two steps in sequence.
●“Move the your Pawn”
 You can move your Pawn anywhere vertically and horizontally as long as there are no walls, cubes, or opponents. 
 You can escape to your exit is win before you move cubes.

●“Move the Cube”
 On your turn, you must move the cube.
 “Moving” the Cube means “pushing” or “pulling” a cube that is horizontally or vertically adjacent to your Pawn.
 In this step, you win by either immobilizing or squashing opponent.

—description from the publisher

Shit Happens: Ice cold winter edition! Life is hard with uncomfortable situations during Christmas and the temperatures drop below zero.

Players have to arrange cards in order of degree is misery. If a player's ranking matches that assigned by the publisher, they get to keep the card. The first player to gain ten cards wins the game.

Scout, sign, train and manage 240 real LaLiga players. That’s 12 players from each club

Player names, images, star ratings and club crest on all player cards

Strategize your tactics with the new attacking midfielder and defensive midfielder player categories

Use the new La Liga draft board by itself or together with the Superclub components you know and love

Compatible with all other game products from the Superclub universe

Reaction game: the well-known card game in the Turbo version is together with the Alexa playbar or as well as independent.

Run: the cards are distributed and you are ready to go! The cards fly over the table: who first gets rid of all the cards have chances of victory.

Obstacle: but it must be careful to take care of who's not well during the game, must start again from the front.

Program: for this game requires an Alexa enabled device; the Skill is available to download from the Amazon Skill Store

Versatile games: the Noris brand develops games with fun for the whole family; age-appropriate skills are encouraged.

Scandinavian Big Box includes Copenhagen and Helsinki in their Deluxe Edition including two expansions each!

The Danish city of Copenhagen is traversed by canals and harbors, and part of it — "Nyhavn" (New Harbor) — is famous for the colorful gabled houses along the water.

In Copenhagen, players design new façades for these houses so that they fit seamlessly into this beautiful harbor setting. On a turn, a player may choose to take two adjacent cards from the display (they may never hold more than 7 cards), or use cards to buy facade tiles in various shapes and colors. Any tile may be purchased, but you get a discount if you place a tile touching another one of the same color. Building over certain spaces or completing designated rows grants the players new abilities to make their job easier. Finishing a row or column will award the player points (doubled if it is all windows), and the game is a race to 12 points.

The famous Senate Square in the center of the Finnish capital is surrounded by government buildings, the university, the cathedral and the Sederholm House. The players ́ task is to design pavilions across the square to protect pedestrians in the rain. Helsinki is based on the design of Copenhagen by the same design team, but with some added complexity.

The center board depicts the Senate Square with 8 spaces (two per side) and 8 cards, with each card connected to two spaces. The player on their turn advances their pawn 1-3 spaces, then must choose to take cards or build a pavilion. Each space is connected to two face up cards, and the player may choose to draw these into their hand (making sure to respect the 7 card hand limit, a key challenge in the game). The cards are in one of 5 colors and show a shape of size 2 to 5. To build a pavilion, the player plays a card showing the piece they want to play, and then must play an additional number of cards of the same color to match the size of the piece. Finally, the piece must be "slid" onto their game board (which also represents the Senate Square) from the side of the Square their pawn is on on the main board. Covering certain spaces earns a coat of arms, which may be spent to use one of the 15 one-time special actions.

—description from the publisher

Brain Ache is the game where you race to identify familiar catchphrases or words represented by perplexing pictures.

Can you guess which sayings the pictures on the cards represent?

Quickest to guess wins the card so think fast and shout out the answer before anyone else. Brain Ache contains hundreds of picture puzzles that will baffle and boggle your brain.

—description from the publisher

Star Navy is new solitaire game set amidst the boundless expanse of space in a distant future. Assume the commanding role of a Space Destroyer alongside its fleet of agile fighters, embarking on a series of ten diverse missions that span the spectrum of interstellar conflict. Engage in open battles, navigate critical escort duties, and strategize during intricate blockade operations. Your decisions will shape the outcome of these high-stakes missions, each offering unique challenges and requiring astute tactical prowess.

The missions are playable either as standalone challenges or as integral segments of a campaign game, that will add a strategic level to game with the introduction of maintenance duties and opportunities for ship upgrades, with an increasing level of difficulty of the missions.

In order to play the game you only need to print the Mission Maps and use some colored dice: for a more immersive game a full set of counters is also included.

—description from the designer

Aboard the prison ship headed to Fort Joy, an archdemon offers near limitless power to the prisoners. Taking up the offer ensures survivability but terms of the deal are vague.

In a game where actions have consequences, Origins must outwit Adramahlihk herself or pay a steep price. This ever-escalating pact with an archdemon grants many boons, the cost of which may be worse than eternal damnation.

How many strikes can you throw in ten frames? Can you pick up a spare? Avoid a split? Find out in this three-card nanogame of dexterity and planning that simulates a full game of bowling in under 20 minutes. Test your eyes, curse your hands, and leave the lane open for better options as you bowl your way to a high score in Strike, Spare, or Split!

In this "place-and-write" game, one player bowls ten frames, creating a path down the lane by placing one card on top of another. This path will cross a variety of icons, which can sometimes help the player, giving them upgrades and abilities, but may also cause trouble, forcing them into the gutter! While the icons are marked off with a pencil, the game continues to evolve until the 10th frame is complete!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Divinity Original Sin: Haunted Keep Expansion
description:
In the Haunted Keep expansion, an old mage greets Origins at the entrance to a strange tower and tells the tale of a grave misfortune that cursed the place. Undead now roam the dark passageways, but somewhere within is a ritual that may end the curse.

First, the Origins must accept one of many hexes to survive the challenges of the keep where unnatural monstrosities lurk. Potions, weird science, and hexes greet them at every turn.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chased: 1 card survival game with dinosaurs
description:
You are on an island that is made up of 4 areas, you must go through each one avoiding or facing the different dinosaurs that inhabit it.
In each turn you have 2 different actions to perform with your character.  Then it is the turn of the dinosaurs that will go after you, if they manage to be adjacent to your character you will receive damage.
Your actions are run, hide, shoot and distract. With the health/action die you indicate which ones are available.
The game is based on a very simple system of movements (for your character and the dinosaurs) and intelligence (by the dinosaurs).

Arcane Runes is a dynamic board game where players lead Viking villages in a relentless quest for supremacy. Engage in strategic engine-building as you grow your forces with powerful Viking-themed cards each turn. The twist? Utilize mystical runes to enhance your own cards or disrupt your opponents' plans.

In this intense competition, players balance offense and defense, managing resources and runes to outwit rivals.

Arcane Runes combines cunning tactics, resource management, and a touch of rune magic. Will you be the chieftain who leads their village to glory, or will your rivals consign you to history?

—description from the designer

3Motion features eighteen pieces in three different shapes and colors. Your goal is to move three pieces of the same color or shape into one of the 2x2 marked squares on the game board inside of the box bottom.

When you move, however, you can't move a figure of the same color or shape as the one moved by your opponent on the previous turn.

In Take the Throne, you play as rival houses competing to take the throne and leave a lasting legacy on the realm. In this fast paced card game you will be constantly second guessing your opponents in a race to reach 8 victory points.

The best way to gain victory points is to sit on the throne each round but only one player can hold the throne as the Monarch.  Each turn, players select one card to play - The Monarch aims to retain the throne and all the house players try to play a card that will take the throne from them.  All cards can impact each other so selecting the right card is key to victory!

Try to outthink your opponents and play the best card that will get you onto the throne in order to gain the most victory points!

Can you master the right tactics to take the throne and leave your legacy?

Requiem takes place on Earth but this version of Earth is much different from the world you know today. About 100 years have passed since "The Event" happened and the effects of that calamity have begun to surface all over the globe. Creatures have mutated, weather patterns are different, the physical make up of the environment has fundamentally changed. Lush green forests have become lush purple forests. Mushrooms that grew a few inches tall now grow dozens of feet tall. The shell of "what was" is still here; buildings still stand but most are crumbling and overgrown. Large cities are more like literal concrete jungles at this point. It is essentially a new world. This radical change has caused the recovery of humanity to stifle and it seems fairly obvious that things will never quite return to the way they were. In spite of these setbacks, humanity has made some progress.

Accompanied by their warband, the player is out to seek their fortune in the wastelands, playing through adventures and encountering all the wild environments, creatures, and characters that await them.

Summary of mechanics...

Solo-play, skirmish level miniatures game, played in a narrative style
Miniatures agnostic...use whatever figures you like
D6 based, no other dice required
Charts for various outcomes
Simple stats and stat tests to accomplish tasks
Rules are easy to learn and don't get in the way of the story
Character advancement with skills and mutations
Settlement mode, your in charge now
Includes intro adventure "Wide Open"

—description from the publisher

Enjoy the perfect vacation as you and your fellow sightseers journey across the globe! Around the World in 15 Minutes is played simultaneously, with each player traveling around their own personal map. You'll take turns as the Navigator and choose which of the 3 actions all players will have to take that turn - Travel, Explore, or Collect.

Have the most rewarding vacation out of your travel companions to win the game!

—description from the publisher

In astronomy, The Big Crunch is a hypothetical scenario for the ultimate end of the universe in which it dramatically collapses in on itself.

The Big Crunch is a card game focused on point collection and survival. Players either race to gather the most mass and complete a solar system, or to simply survive as they all get drawn into the black hole.

The goal is to possess the most mass upon the completion of a solar system. Any player may complete a solar system, however only the player with the most mass at that point in time will win.

Alternatively you may attack other players by overloading their system with mass, attracting the attention of the hungry black hole, forcing them to lose valuable mass. Mass simultaneously acts as both a life system and point scoring system.

Darkness is coming... Can you survive?

—description from the publisher

2039, a grieved father tries to resurrect his daughter after finding a mysterious book talking about the powers of a strange kind of crystal. His attempt turns into a disaster, not only does he not resurrect his daughter but also he ends up unleashing thousands of spirits of fabled warriors and warlords into the world.

The earth becomes unrecognizable, factions form, and wars rage on!
Legends say Are, the god of war is behind this disaster, and delights himself at the sight of the best warriors in history clashing swords for what seems like eternity!

The goal of the game is to defeat the opposing factions by, capturing their castles. This is done by placing workers, gathering resources, placing warriors, developing new technologies, and using the spirits of some of the most famous warriors in history to your advantage.

Players move their pawns similar to chess, defeating an enemy pawn is a simple matter of placing yours on top of it. Unlike chess, if you lose a pawn you can always purchase it later, provided you have the resources necessary. Making resource management an important part of your strategy.

There are three different types of pawns (the hero, warriors, and workers) each pawn has its own particularities.

You can also build walls to defend your castles, or to attack strategically!

Some luck is also required as in order to use the spirits of famous warriors, you have to roll a die.

Multiple game modes are available, making the game very replayable!

—description from the publisher

Iron Coffins: North Sea Expansion is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine simulating the German Kriegsmarine U-boat war against Allied shipping in the North Sea during World War II.

As the captain of a Type II 'duck' U-boat during 1939-1940, your ultimate goal is to locate and sink as many enemy Allied ships as possible, while at the same time bringing your U-boat and crew safely back to base after each patrol. It won’t be easy due to persistent enemy aircraft and escorts continuously hunting your U-boat, but the German people are depending on you. Don’t let the Fatherland down!

The game includes the following components:

     1 Tables Book (12 pages)
     1 Tables Book Cover Page
     3 Game Mats (A4 / 8.5 x 11)
     10 5/8 inch (16mm) counters
     1 Warship Status Sheet



     Two D10, one D12, and one D20 dice are required to play.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.



     Total play time to complete a full campaign takes approximately 3-4 hours, with a typical patrol averaging 30 minutes.



     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with water depth rules, compressed air rules, oxygen rules, flood damage control rules, silent running rules, numerous crew skills, weather effects, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Iron Coffins: North Sea Expansion offers memorable experiences and high replay value. Are you ready to serve the Fatherland and defend Germany against the onslaught of Allied shipping?

—description from the publisher

description:

boardgame: Iron Coffins: Caribbean / West Africa Expansion
description:
Iron Coffins: Caribbean / West Africa Expansion is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine simulating the German Kriegsmarine U-boat war against Allied shipping in the Caribbean and West Africa areas during World War II.

boardgame: Iron Coffins: US East Coast Expansion
description:
Iron Coffins: US East Coast Expansion is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine simulating the German Kriegsmarine U-boat war against Allied shipping along the US East Coast during World War II.

As the captain of a Type IX U-boat during 1942-1943, your ultimate goal is to locate and sink as many enemy Allied ships as possible, while at the same time bringing your U-boat and crew safely back to base after each patrol. It won’t be easy due to persistent enemy aircraft and escorts continuously hunting your U-boat, but the German people are depending on you. Don’t let the Fatherland down!

The game includes the following components:

     1 Tables Book (12 pages)
     1 Tables Book Cover Page
     1 Game Mat (A4 / 8.5 x 11)



     Two D10, one D12, and one D20 dice are required to play.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     Total play time to complete a full campaign takes approximately 4-6 hours, with a typical patrol averaging 30 minutes.
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Warcry – Nightmare Quest
description:
In the terrible and voracious depths of the Gnarlwood, warbands battle over ancient Realmshaper Engines – strange stone ziggurats that imbue the forest with cosmic energies. To meddle with these arcane constructs could unleash catastrophe far beyond the Ravening Ruin, but the deranged ghouls of the Royal Beastflayers believe they are on a quest to save the realm, and will devour anyone who stands in their way. It falls to the heroic Questor Soulsworn – itinerant champions of the Stormcast Eternals, guided by visions and prophecies – to defend the Seraphon relics from those who would defile them.

Warcry: Nightmare Quest brings two new warbands into the Gnarlwood – the elite Stormcast Eternals bound to the Questor Soulsworn, and the ghoulish Royal Beastflayers of the Flesh-eater Courts. The boxed set also includes a ruined and overgrown Realmshaper Engine with a host of modular Gnarlwood terrain, a double-sided game board, a book with background information, incredible artwork, and the rules for both warbands and the included terrain, plus a set of battleplan and warband cards to keep your games running smoothly.

CasiNoya is an innovative card game that seamlessly blends the worlds of traditional casino card games and strategic combat. In this unique game, players use specially designed decks of cards to engage in thrilling duels, combining the rules of classic games like Poker, Blackjack, and War with combat card mechanics such as healing, poisoning, shielding, and dealing damage. The objective is to be the last player standing, and winning a traditional casino hand can sometimes lead to unexpected defeat as you face off against other players in one-on-one duels.

CasiNoya sets itself apart by eliminating any real-money or gambling elements, focusing solely on the strategic aspects of card gameplay. Players can choose from a variety of well-known card games, each with its own strategic nuances, including Five-Hand Poker, Blackjack remastered without the need of a house, Chinese Poker, War with an actual army of up to 7 cards, and way more!

With its emphasis on strategy over luck and its dedication to providing an alternative to traditional gambling games, CasiNoya aims to appeal to card game enthusiasts, competitive gamers, and individuals seeking a fun and safe gaming experience. It's a game that offers a fresh take on age-old card games, making it accessible to a wide audience while promoting skillful gameplay over chance.

—description from the publisher

Figgie is a card game that was invented [by professional traders] at Jane Street in 2013. It was designed to simulate open-outcry commodities trading. Most of the skill in Figgie is in negotiating trades that benefit both the buyer and seller. Like in poker, your objective in Figgie is to make money over a series of hands.

—description from the publisher

Figgie is a turn-less, timed game of simultaneous action. Players call out offers to buy or sell and accept offers from others as they choose in an attempt to collect the most valuable cards. Chips are exchanged for cards to settle the action.

Figgie is played with a 40-card deck of four suits in an unequal distribution of 12, 10, 10, and 8 cards. An unusual feature is that the suit for each count in the distribution is unknown to players when a round begins. (In a face-to-face setting, this is managed with one or more prepared decks.) Figuring out the distribution is key, because the 12-card suit determines the payout at the end of each round.

—user summary

The theme of the game is dating based around the classic daisy picking game "She Loves Me, She Loves Me Not."

Player’s compete by creating pick-up and break-up lines for different characters. The player with the best line wins!  
The Judge draws a Petal Card from the Game Board. The Judge reads the character on the Petal Card aloud.

Each player writes their best pick-up or break-up line on their Dry-Erase Board based on the character on the Petal Card. For example, if “Doctor,” is the character drawn from the Game Board and it is a whole heart, players will create a pick-up to use on the Doctor.

After all of the Dry-Erase Boards are in front of the Judge, the Judge shuffles them before reading each response out loud. The Judge picks their favorite line and gives the winning player the card. Once all Petal Cards have been selected, the winner of the game is the player who has the most cards!

—description from the publisher

Survival tasks concepts informed and inspired by the community during the Unsettled campaign.

Includes 3 survival Task D cards each for Yendraal, Strannos, Kaelyfos

You have given yourself the challenge to be the most disgusting of your gang of friends.

Players must therefore collect series of 5 identical repugnant images. To get there, they make exchanges between them, while distrusting flowers that smell too good! We cannot complete a series with flower cards. On the other hand, flower cards can pass for any image during exchanges. In Yark!, There are no tricks: everyone plays at the same time.

Special challenges make the game even crazier and more chaotic that will take out the clown in you! Guaranteed laughter and disgust!

In a land where many treasures are hidden There is a legend that has been passed down among pirates. Who can possess the treasures of the four realms? Get the most value of your treasure That person will be the king of all pirates. You are a pirate who wants to be number one in this land. But where you can find treasure It is necessary to gather people in the area. To help find treasure But gathering people isn't just about gathering a lot of people. But because of the small boat used to find treasure There can only be 10 people per boat, so you have to manage people just right. and gathering people of the same kind It makes the results even better.

Game Goal 
Players collect Crew Cards to exchange for Small Ship Tokens, which are used to redeem Treasure Cards in the Skull Island area. Players can exchange small ship tokens only if the sum of their crew cards is divisible by 10, such as 10, 20, 30. The value of each treasure is based on the points of the area that the treasure hints and the location hint that the treasure cards match. Players can view or switch hints. to create an advantage for yourself. The player with the highest total treasure value wins.

The Last Stand is a competitive survival game set in a post-apocalyptic world where you find undead enemies, and you are set to achieve your survival by arriving to the last known shelter, named “The Last Stand”; however it is not an easy task, as shelter´s managers care a lot about the sustainability of their site, and have some requisites to allow any foreigner to enter:

IF players manage to find “The Last Stand”, to be accepted they need to also have achieved at least 10 Victory points, the first player to achieve it is the winner and the game ends; as alternative game ending conditions, if all hex tiles are revealed (with or without the shelter found), the game immediately ends and the player with the most VP wins the game. Or, if a player is out of luck and fail to survive, they become a very thinky undead enemy, when half of the players (rounded up) become undead, game ends, and the one alive with most VP wins.

The game’s board is constructed by hex tiles, which can be set in any desired configuration face down, the players start at a marked location with a basic weapon (setup changes with the difficulty chosen) and each turn they explore the world, with any new found location many things can happen, such as finding enemies, valuable resources, and some other surprises. Characters have asymmetric abilities and all weapons have different specs.

-description from designer

In Tekkadice you fight with a tekka which is a Tantalium Enhanced Kinetic Killer Armor. You build your mecha unit and give it a pilot using simple rules. You need to print two A4 sheets of game component and some player sheets plus some D6s to play. You play on a hex grid arena battlefield on which also 16 obstacles are placed like explosives. Distribute your tekka's attribute points, then pick a Omega special move and a Pilot trait to gain advantages in combat.

You play the game in turns, all against all or in teams. You must manage your Ready dice, which are your tekka's energy, to perform the actions it is capable of. The dice used go on the matching attribute bar then in the Cooldown box, and sometimes in the Locked box. At the beginning of one's turn, the dice bubble up one box.

You keep track of the used Ready dice by placing them on the attribute bar that corresponds to the used action. The blackened letters indicate the attribute scores, which represent the limit of Ready dice that can be used per turn to perform the corresponding action. The used dice move from the attribute bars to the Cooldown box, and sometimes in the Locked box. It's better to keep some Ready dice to roll GUARD between turns, as the dice will be Ready again at the beginning of your next turn.

Game after game you'll increase your tekka's attributes, Dice pool (starting Ready dice) and MP (its hits points), and you'll have the opportunity to pick a second Omega special move and a second Pilot trait.

boardgame: Битвы Fantasy: Битва за редут (Battle for the Redoubt)

This is an introductory action game to the Fantasy Battles system. The system uses actual firing devices, similar to Crossbows and Catapults.

Players roll a die for actions and use them to either move pieces or fire. Any soldier that is knocked down is eliminated from the game. Players can also move their soldiers into contact with the enemy and fight melees. Players can also use magic cards to assist them.

The attacker can win by capturing the enemy's flag or by eliminating all soldiers.  To prevent this, the defender must eliminate all the attacking soldiers.

7 Ages is a game covering 6000 years of human history from 4000 BCE to today. Players lead dynasties that decide the fate of the world across the ages. You, as a dynastic head of one or more civilisations, must guide your people through history gaining glory points for historical, scientific, religious, and cultural advances as you increase their health, wealth, and prestige. All the leading figures of history are included, as they attempt to influence events and their society through their great words and deeds. Are you game to change history?

7 Ages: the Collector's edition has been enhanced, expanded and streamlined. It includes 67 new civilisation cards, 165 in total. There are counters for 6 new empires, 21 in total, allowing up to 7 players to control 3 empires each, as you vie for a peaceful prosperous planet.


    Maps have been graphically updated, new areas included and they will be hard-mounted on linen boards.

The number of options has been doubled from 6 to 12 and includes 3 that are part of the 7 Ages Expansion sets, Secret Victory, Neutral Kingdoms and Deus Vult!.


    Secret Victory is an easy way to keep everyone's glory secret so no one knows who is really winning.
    Neutral Kingdoms means provinces are no longer denuded when an empire is discarded. Even if you have given up on them, the people still live there and don't take kindly to strangers (not to mention former overlords).
    Deus Vult! is a religious expansion that allows empires to explore a variety of religions and the building of temples to their God or Gods. Not cheap, but what price Glory?

boardgame: 三国志 新武将疯云录 (Three Kingdoms: New Chronicles of Warlords)
description:
In 三国志 新武将疯云录 ("Three Kingdoms: New Chronicles of Warlords"), players assume the roles of warlords during the tumultuous Three Kingdoms period. This game, inspired by the classic Monopoly, allows players to buy or conquer other players' cities, obtain land deeds, and charge tolls to opponents. Victory in battles is determined using warlord cards, while special tactic cards can be used to activate unique abilities and gain advantages. Recruit iconic characters, lead your faction, and strategically employ your resources to emerge as the ultimate conqueror.

Game Overview

Components: The game includes a game board, character cards, tactic cards, item cards, land deeds, currency, and player pieces.
Gameplay: Players move their pieces on the board, recruit characters, conquer cities, and engage in battles using a combination of tactic cards and items.

Special Features

The central Three Kingdoms map serves as a battleground for players to launch strategic attacks on nearby opponents.
The game incorporates a rock-paper-scissors element with land types affecting battles and troop strengths.

Game Setup


     Place the game board flat.
     Shuffle character cards, tactic cards, and treasure cards, placing them face down on designated spots on the board.
     Place land deeds face up on the board.
     Each player selects a game piece and places it at the starting point.
     Players draw character cards, receive currency, and set up their initial forces.
     Determine the starting player by rolling a die.
     Gameplay proceeds in a clockwise direction.


Victory Conditions
The game ends when:


     A player collects 8 land deeds or a predetermined number for victory.
     A player collects a set of 4 land deeds of the same color (excluding ancient battlefields).
     A player bankrupts another by landing on their territory without enough currency to pay the toll.
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

boardgame: MLEM: Space Agency – BLEP: Cosmic Pioneers
description:
BLEP: Cosmic Pioneers is the return to an ancestral competition between cats and dogs... Because the felines, during their space exploration, discover that dogs were already there, on distant stars! These 6 dog tokens are to be placed on the game mat adding another variant to MLEM: Space Agency.

What is MLEM: Space Agency?
It is a family board game where you step into the shoes of fearless captains of cosmic expeditions, but you're a cat. Travel into the unknown and send your brave astronauts into space by trying to match your dice rolls with each step of the journey. Do you hop off to explore a planet, or dare to whisker it all by venturing further?

2 Player tabletop game with an instant magical hook... Balls that Glide!

Omshanti is easy to play. It involves 2 players racing to get their marker home by gliding the game’s 3 balls along the roll out playing pitch. This skilful and satisfying action of gliding the balls with precise speed to land in your target zone, is the key to the games instant and long term appeal.

—description from the publisher

In Posłani , you are one of the twelve apostles. Each of you has unique skills, it is not without reason that Judas is not afraid of the Pharisees, and John climbs mountains faster - this type of references to the Scripture will especially please those who know its content, but you do not have to be a believer to play the game!

During his turn, a player can perform as many actions as he wants as long as he has enough rest points. The player ends each turn by rolling a rest die. The player who scores the most victory points wins. They are obtained by healing the Needy, proclaiming the Kingdom of God and completing tasks from the Event Deck.

The game ends when the last day is drawn from the Day Deck or when all Needy tokens are removed from the board.

-description from designer

•••

Uzdrawiaj, nawracaj i wypędzaj złe duchy.
Jesteś jednym z dwunastu apostołów. Każdy z was posiada unikalne umiejętności, nie bez powodu Judasz nie boi się faryzeuszy, a Jan szybciej chodzi po górach - tego typu nawiązania do Pisma szczególnie ucieszą tych, którzy znają jego treść, ale nie trzeba być wierzącym żeby zagrać w grę!

Wygrywa gracz, który zdobędzie jak najwięcej punktów zwycięstwa. Zdobywa się je poprzez uzdrawianie Potrzebujących, głoszenie Królestwa Bożego oraz wykonywanie zadań z Talii Wydarzeń.

Gra kończy się wraz z ostatnim dniem wyciągniętym z Talii Dni lub po zdjęciu wszystkich żetonów Potrzebujących z planszy.

County Road Z is a solo game of miniature tabletop combat and resource management, set during the zombie apocalypse. Scavenge materials to build watchtowers, workshops, generators and more, and then take it to the tabletop, where the zombies react to every grenade, shotgun, and scream! Combat zeds (and perhaps worse) across warehouses, graveyards, road wreckage, mansions and more.

Upgrade your base facilities and build new weapons and armor to keep your survivors safe for the next mission. Where will your next community base be? A distillery? A firehouse? A hydroelectric dam? Build your defenses bigger and better... but quietly, or the zombie horde will be at your door.

Dungeon Twist is a turn-based tactical dungeon crawler with simple rules, textless game pieces, and a great strategic depth. A gang of questionable rogues strays across the Shifting Dungeon, a procedural maze full of loot, dead ends, and pesky hazards. Trick your opponents, grab the Treasure… and get out alive!

-Build together:
The core mechanic consists in building the map by placing or twisting square Tiles. Every board piece can be manipulated in order to create new paths, spawn loot and resources, or lock up and slow down opponents. A unique and ever-changing scenario will come to life by collaborating with other players (or just messing up with their agenda).

-Grab that Loot:
Every player has a simple objective: get filthy rich and escape. Wealth can be accumulated by pillaging around or collecting combos of matching Tiles (that can be traded for Rooms), but most of all... you can just steal from other players. Robberies are settled with dice and allow to grab any Token from the opponent's inventory.

-Choose your Rogue:
Each playable character can drastically change your approach to the raid, thanks to their unique skills and wacky personality. But there's always room for customization: scavenge for Objects and Potions in order to cheat fate or unlock a variety of bonus effects.

-Escape in time:
When someone manages to collect a Treasure Token and take it out of the Dungeon, the endgame triggers. The entire map will crumble to the ground after just a turn, burying everyone still inside. Basically, you don't just have to be the richest in order to win, but also plan your escape route in advance.

-description from publisher

Story Time Backgammon is a high seas adventure that teaches kids as young as three how to play one of the world’s most popular games.

This game tells the tale of a Viking chieftain, Chompus the Great, and a swashbuckling pirate, Peglegra the Bold. Aided by magical dice and their animal companions, Wind and Wave, Chompus and Peglegra race their ships from dock to dock in search of treasure and glory!

Story Time Backgammon’s hilarious stories, unique mini-games, illustrated board, and custom pieces enable anyone to teach their youngsters the game—no backgammon experience necessary!

—description from the publisher

FAST-PACED AND ACTION-PACKED: Players race to build the structure on the card by using their set of 8 colored blocks to match the colors and arrangement of the structure. Sharp eyes, fast hands, and quick minds prevail in this fun-filled structure building frenzy! 



     SUPER FUN AND EASY TO LEARN: Uzzle games are loved by over 100,000 families in the USA. Stack Royale can be learned in 10 minutes and played for a lifetime.



     BOOSTS COGNITIVE SKILLS: You will need sharp eyes, quick thinking ability, strong observation power, and fast hand speed all at the same time. It is great for enhancing problem-solving and many cognitive skills in children. 



     FUN FOR ALL AGES: The Uzzle Stack Royale comes with 32 colored wooden blocks, 160 cards, & 4 difficulty levels. Levels 1-2 are great for young kids. Whereas, levels 3-4 are extremely difficult for adults.



     A PERFECT GIFT: Stack Royale is the perfect gift for Christmas, birthday gifts, back to school, and for classroom activities! It's also a great choice for travel games.


—description from the publisher

TransAmerica: China Map is a simple railway game. Each player has a set of five cities strung across China that need to be connected by rail. Players first place their station on an intersection on the map, then take turns placing one or two rails each turn on the lines of the map. Initially they can place only adjacent to their station or on a line that touches their station, but once their network connects with another player's, they can place anywhere along the combined network.

The game ends when the first player completes a connected route between their five cities. The player who can make the best use of the other players' networks is generally victorious.

In this particular amusement park, players will do everything possible to enter the attractions and collect the most stars.

Getting into the attractions is not that easy! There are a lot of people and the lines are not exactly linear. We will have to learn THE ART OF LINEUP.

With the cards, each player will try to place themselves in the exact place in the line that allows them to enter the attraction, but be careful, since the order of play changes in each round.

Whoever is in the right place will win the stars, and whoever collects the most stars WILL WIN THE GAME!

Kickstarter booster promos that came with Precious Family Jewels.

These are 17 goal cards that provide bonus points for their "smash" games: Grab Your Asses, Hands Off My Pecker, and Precious Family Jewels. Some goals are specific to one game, others are for combining two or all games into one big "smash" deck.

Print run of only 250 where the first 150 backers received it for free.

Independent Brazilian card game about the folklore and culture of the country. The objective is to reduce the opponent's PC (Ponto de Conhecimento in Portuguese; Point of Knowledge in English) from 20 to 0.

The game is divided in 5 categories, each of them plays an important and different roll to win the match: Folklore (you must discard cards to use the effect. No ATK or DEF; no "Pontos de Conhecimento"); Historical (higher ATK/DEF. Not all of them have effects; all of them have "Pontos de Conhecimento"); Support (mid ATK/DEF; has effects. Not all of them have "Pontos de Conhecimento", since a Support card can be an "Action card"; not a character); Wild animals (large range of ATQ/DEF. All of them have effects and "Pontos de Conhecimento") and Plants (no ATK, no DEF, no "Pontos de Conhecimento". To use them/their effects, you must place you card facing down and respect the different type of activation [Waiting / Immediate / Counterattack].

To reduce your opponent's PC, you have to attack their character. A "character" is a card with ATK/DEF [All Historical, some Support; All Animals]. The "PC" of the characters is informed in the left-superior border. It represents the amount of damage you'll take on your life if you character "dies" – if their DEF goes to 0 or lower. You can also reduce your opponent's PC by attacking them directly, as long as their field is empty (no characters) or if you have an effect that allows you to attack directly.

—description from the designer

A game of bees, blossoms and bluffing, Bouquet is a strategic and dynamic area control game where competition blooms into beauty!

On their turn, players will either PLACE petals in the bouquet, PLUCK their opponents petals, or DRAW more petals into their hand.

Petals may be placed either face up, which gives certain abilities to particular petals, or face down, which allows you to ignore placing requirements at the cost of having those abilities active.

With multiple petal types to choose from, each with their own point value and abilities, players have to carefully consider how to place each petal into the bouquet.

The player with the most points at the end of the game wins!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Peak Courage: The Battle for Mount Hermon – October 1973
description:
At the beginning of the October 1973 Yom Kippur War, Syrian helicopter borne commandos captured the Israeli top-secret military outpost at the top of the 6,000ft Mount Hermon. Two days after capture, the Israeli's failed at retaking the outpost in a daylight attack. Two weeks later, they launched the second assault at night and after heavy fighting, recaptured the outpost. Peak Courage is an area movement - impluse game that covers both Israeli assaults.

Game Scale:
Game Turn: 2 hours
Area: 1" = 340 yards / 25mm = 311 meters
Units: Individual vehicles to Companies

Game Inventory:
One 11x 17" full color map
One dual-side printed countersheet (80 1/2" counters)
One 8-page rulebook

Solitaire Playability: High
Complexity Level: Medium
Players: 1 or more
Playing Time: 1-2 hours

Note: Players will need to provide one 10-sided die and one deck of standard playing cards for game play.

Play as Hungry Hippos who are not willing to eat anything other than the Fruit they want or meddle with other Hungry Hippos by feeding them everything other than their desired Fruit by being a hypocrite.

Aim of the Game:
Collect the most points by collecting and locking spaces in the buffet before the Draw Pile runs out.

How To Play:
1. Draw 3 cards from the draw pile to create an buffet space.
2. Play 1 card from your hand face-down.
3. Simultaneously reveal the card with other players.
4. Lowest revealed number plays first on the buffet and draw a new card from draw pile.
5. Lock spaces in the buffet to force opponents into collecting fruits prematurely.
6. Replace the buffet row of cards with your card if you cannot play it anywhere.
7. Player with the highest points wins.

Contents:
1 deck with a total of 108 playing cards + 2 Keychains (mementos).

Benefits of Game play:
Think Fast and Out-Smart Your Opponents.
Challenge Others By locking their points.
Family Friendly Fun for Kids and Adults Alike.
Spatial Awareness and Recognize Deception.

In Blábol, your goal is to create a grammatically correct sentence using the cards in your hand.

There are 156 cards of seven types: nouns, adjectives, pronouns, verbs, adverbs, prepositions and conjunctions. All these cards are shuffled together to form the draw deck and each player draws seven cards.

The game is played in turns and on their turn, players have to play cards from their hands to form sentences. They can also add cards to previously played sentences (including those of their opponents) to make them longer and thereby score more points.

The game ends when the draw deck is depleted. Players then score the sentences in front of them: each sentence scores the number of cards used squared (a sentence consisting of eight cards will gain 64 points). Points are then deducted for unplayed cards still in players' hands. The player with the most points becomes the master of Blábol!

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
description:
Round up the gang and go for the glory with this collectible edition of Trivial Pursuit featuring 600 questions from the hit FX show It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia.

Answer questions from across 6 categories - Episodes, Quotes, About the Gang, Friends & Enemies, What Happens at Paddy's, Schemes and Plans.

This fast-flying trivia game is officially licensed and sure to be a great collectible gift for the diehard It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia fans!

Includes: 100 Question Cards featuring 600 Trivia Questions, 1 Die, 1 portable storage Wedge, and 1 Rulebook.

The players become drivers who are using a die to move along the game board. The goal is to get from Prague to the cities assigned by the cards faster than the opponents.

Every player gets three destination cards and six action cards. Then in turns, players roll a die to move the car of their colour following the path of their choice. Highways offer higher speeds than local roads, but everything can change at any time if players use their action cards. Also, two cars cannot end up in the same spot. If they do, they collide which has its consequences.

The game ends when one player successfully reaches all their destinations and gets back to Prague. This player becomes the winner, but others can still compete for the second place.

Finally, and at great cost, the dragons lay defeated. But now, a greater threat looms. Ancient evils have claimed Northvale, the warrior civilization in Northern Valorfall. These winter-hardened tribes would not have stood for loss without a fight. Surely this aggression must have been committed by only the most powerful adversaries. Some say they are the fathers of dragons. Others, dormant titans awakened from deep beneath the earth. It will require the greatest of Valorfall’s Ether Mages to answer this challenge. Etchinstone calls for every elemental guild’s best Mage to travel northward--to glory or defeat.

Northvale is an 18-card expansion for Dragons of Etchinstone. The player takes the role of one of four powerful Ether Mages, one from each guild. There are four major guilds in Valorfall (red, blue, gray and yellow), each with their own school of thought on how best to wield the elements.

You get the chance to pit your chosen Mage against a new landscape, progressing from the brutal dangers of the Severed Seaboard to the open--but deadly--horizons of the Whispering Fields. Then, navigate the dark terrain and evil secrets of the Forgotten Foothills. And, finally, traverse the icy mountain terrain and unmatched power of the Elementals in the unforgiving Northern region of Northvale. Then, if you are brave enough, face one of four terrifying new bosses.

-description from designer

A game about building a street food empire by buying and selling stock and hiring staff. Hire, invest and deliver your way to the top!

Made in conjunction with the exhibition of the same name.

Phrazzle Me is a board game made up of 200 rectangular wooden pieces with words on each face. Use the 800 words to make sentences and questions. You get 1 point per piece used. Add to sentence for additional points. Plays like Scrabble, but better since you don't just make words, but full sentences. If you like Scrabble, you will love Phrazzle Me. If you are trying to learn English, this game is for you. Follow the asterisks, underlines and color-coded words to making sentences correctly from the get go. Even if you don't speak any English. Learn 4 times faster than the norm.

—description from the publisher

ARTICULATE! CHRISTMAS: Bring everyone together with this fast-talking description board game as players try to describe as many card entries as possible. But there's a new Christmassy way to play; you must complete a Christmas jigsaw to win! For each correct answer you add a piece to your jigsaw! The first team to finish their jigsaw wins!

CHRISTMAS FUN: Brimming with classic Christmas food, films, songs and traditions to describe - Christmas Articulate will get the whole family into the Christmas spirit faster than Santa gets down the chimney!
Bring the Articulate! Christmas challenge to any kind of party with friends or family and watch their competitive side come out as teams compete to win the crown!

Sealed City, you are mercenaries who have come to a town to rest and resupply when the local governor asks for your help. But what starts as a competition to build reputation with the town turns into a desperate fight to survive once the town gets sealed in by a magical dome.

Throughout the 13 games, you will create a unique story with the other players. Who your mercenaries are, how the town develops, and what ultimately becomes of the town are all in your hands. The first several games have you exploring several locations of the town and determining your background based on your choices while gathering food, water, and reputation

From there, mysteries will become revealed, and you'll find that what seemed like a relatively safe little hamlet is rapidly turning into anything but, putting your combat skills to the test. And you can work as cooperatively or competitively as you desire—will you work to protect the town no matter the cost, or will you make sure you stand supreme over the other mercenaries?

—description from the publisher

It is the night of March 25th 1941 in the Suda Bay; 2 destroyes of the Regia Marina have just deployed 6 MTM (Motoscafi Turismo Modificati), manned by six men from the XMAS.

Their aim is to force the Harbour in Suda and sink British vessels!

Will you manage after seven Action Card to reach the British ships and check which one you will able to sink? Would be the HMS York or a merchant ship?

Will you avoid to raise the Alarm Level?

16 cards ready to let you taste the flavour of the success in this SOLITAIRE game!
