Each round in a game of Migo, the player with the most points wins a flag, and whoever collects two flags first wins.

In the first round, shuffle the 25 double-sided cards, then lay them out in four or five columns depending on the player count. On a turn, draft a card from the end of a column and add it to your collection. Each card depicts one or more symbols — ropes, crampons, etc. — while also showing what's on the back. If you draft a sherpa, discard it immediately, then optionally flip over one of your cards. If you draft a mountain climber carrying vodka, on a future turn you can empty the vodka to take another turn immediately.

Once all the cards have been drafted, tally your score. Wool hats are worth 2 points each, empty vodka bottles are -3 points, and for each other symbol score 1 point per symbol, with a 4-point bonus for whoever has the most ropes, the most crampons, etc.

Ahead of the second round, flip half of the cards to their other side, shuffle the deck, lay out the cards in an inverted pyramid, then draft and score as described previously. For the third round, lay out the cards in three inverted pyramids. If a fourth round is necessary, choose one of the previous card arrangements or create your own.

Kids and parents have a separate decks of 20 cards with 4 questions on each card and 1 extra trap-card. 
The decks are named "Kids ask parents" and "Parents ask Kids" and both contains questions designed to match with knowledge of each side of duel.
One side draws a card from opponents deck, picks a question and chooses 1 of 3 given answers. After good answer the card is given and 1 point is awarded.
In case of drawing a trap-card you loose your turn and a chance to answer a question.
Then other side can draw a card.

After 5 rounds a side with more points wins.

The rain has stopped, and the stream is brimming with water, awaiting an exciting race where cunning, memory, and wit will be your best allies. Take command of your boat and make crucial decisions as you adapt to changes in the currents.

This is an exclusive game for two people where you'll take turns playing your cards to strategically place your boat card on the anchor card.

Let the adventure begin!
Dare to conquer this aquatic race.

—description from the designer

La lluvia terminó y el arroyo está rebosante de agua te espera una carrera llena de emoción, donde la astucia, la memoria y el ingenio serán tus mejores aliados. Comandá tu barco y toma decisiones importantes a medida que te adaptas a los cambios de las corrientes.
Un juego exclusivo para dos personas en el que jugaran por turnos sus cartas para lograr colocar su carta de barco sobre la carta de ancla.
¡Que la aventura comience!
¡Atrévete a conquistar esta carrera acuática

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Bread Basket is a simple family game about making sandwiches. The deck is a mix of sandwich ingredients like lettuce, cheese, ham, and so on, plus five Chef cards.

Players take turns playing one or two cards into the center of the table, making a string of cards. Certain arrangements are "sandwiches" - two matching larger cards, with two or more smaller cards between them. Other arrangements are "soup" - two matching smaller cards, with two or more larger cards between them. Every Soup and Sandwich is worth points, and the player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Whispers is a quick-playing push your luck card game for 2 to 8 players, set in the Vinlant universe. It is presented as a New World version of a dice game from the Old World. The Whispers deck is an 8 suited deck containing 54 cards, including eight suits numbered 1 through 6, and six "Whispers" with a value of 0.

boardgame: Through Hell And Back: Plane Expansion Pack 1
description:
This aircraft pack is for the game Through Hell and Back. This expansion includes 10 P-47 escorts from the 56th Fighter group from 61st and 62nd squadrons that served in the European theater.

There are 10 Enemy Aircraft cards featuring the JU-88, BF-109, and BF-110 from Zerstörergeschwader 26, a Luftwaffe heavy fighter wing that served on the Eastern front.

12 out of the 20 new Aircraft cards have a new optional mechanic for more interesting gameplay.

Included is a double-sided Ammunition card that gives new abilities, allowing less reloading. This card can be bought in-game using the crew action card point system.

As a bonus, there are two new missions for Operation Thunderclap Bombsight Cards with Dossier Cards.

—description from the designer

In this edge-of-your-seat party game you race to place your cones on letter discs whilst calling out examples to teasing topics. But beware, any other player can Top It with another answer and place their cone on top of yours! Only the last cone counts and bags all the points!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Played with a deck of Spanish-Italian cards, all cards get distributed evenly among all players (if not possible, remove as many Kings (10s) from the deck as necessary). The player next to the dealer starts.

The goal of the game is to get rid of all your cards by creating 4 (one for every suit) shared sequences (1-10) in the middle of the table. Every sequence must start with a 5, then players can add on either side of the 5 to go higher or lower. On your turn, you get to play one card. If you can play a card, you must play it. If you can't play a card, you say "pass" ("passo") and it's the next players turn. First one to rid all cards wins.
One player reads the question on the card. Everyone else attempts to answer the question. If answered correctly you get the card. First person to get five cards is the winner.

Three young trees, the White Bodied Beech, the Red Mahogany and a Dark Wild Chestnut are aspiring to be the tallest in a vast forest to rule the World.

Players on their turn are stacking their wooden blocks on a supporting base with limited stability aiming to grow their trees higher than the others. The more complex the elevating tower builds, the less stable it is bringing extra tension to this fast paced party-game.  
The shape of the wooden block to be installed by the player is determined by the roll of a die. Players are requested to build their trees with the corresponding colors fitting to each other.
The game ends if the structure collapses or a block falls out from the tower or all blocks have been installed.
The winner is, who has the tallest tree, i.e. whose wooden block reaches the highest in the structure before the game ends.

This fast pace, high tension party-game makes family members of different ages playing together.

(Solo mode: pack the supporting base and the 44 wooden pieces back in to the tin container by solving this challenging 3D puzzle.)

—description from the designer

Raising Robots is a competitive, simultaneously played, engine-building game.

In Raising Robots, you are a famous inventor seeking to assemble the greatest collection of robots. Each round, you simultaneously choose and perform two or more actions: upgrade, assemble, design, fabricate, recycle. Every action will be performed with a variable amount of power to make the action better or worse. However, the most powerful actions will also help your opponents.

Whoever has the most points after eight rounds wins.

—description from publisher

The Deluxe Edition contains the base game, the "Pets" expansion as well as upgraded components.

Ceci n'est pas une Poire is a Push your Luck, simple and addictive game, inspired by the surrealists, Magritte and his portrait of A.Breton named “André Breton ou les feux de position.”.

It's time to play fast and new games with a short 23 cards deck based on the "Grand Dalmuti" card distribution, but really faster I told you : The cards value from 1 to 9 are shuffled into the deck. The deck is composed like this : there is only one 1, two 2, three 3 and so one... Plus one Joker !
 The dealer will offer you a card but remember you should not have the same value you already have ! Two different ways helps you to avoid this "loosing Pair" : First, the same value cannot be drawn on both side of the same card, you have to think a bit, but you also have the other option to take some negative points and get back your previous cards under the deck.

Cadeau Bonux : Every 100 copies, a variant will be published online!

—description from the designer

In Oz, Dorothy just wants to get home — and you want her out of the way as well so that you can finish building the yellow brick road!

In Yellow Brick Road, each player starts with an imaginary 3x3 grid in front of themselves, and they place a face-down winged monkey token above each column and to the right of each row. They each draw one card from the deck.

On a turn, you draw a card, then your opponent flips down any face-up monkey by your play area, then turns a different monkey face up. Place one of the two cards in your hand in an empty space in your grid that isn't in the same row/column as the face-up monkey. Two cards have special powers; one lets you swap two cards in your grid, and the other grants monkey immunity on your subsequent turn.

Once each player has placed nine cards, the game ends. Each card is worth as many points as the total number of cards in the same road segment, so an isolated road segment is worth 1 point, a road segment on two cards is worth 4 points (2 per card), etc. Whoever has the most points wins.

Great chaos is happening at the farm, and all the animals are scattered across the field! The mission now is to find the animals so they can be safe!

Pega-Pega! Bichos da Fazenda is a different memory game that can be played in cooperative or competitive mode. Have fun saving the critters from danger. But be careful, the wolf is lurking!

—description from the designer

Great confusion is happening in the forest, and all the Brazilian folklore characters are lost! The mission now is to find the characters so they can be safe!

Pega-Pega! Folclore is a different memory game that can be played in cooperative or competitive mode. Have fun saving the characters from danger. But be careful, the Werewolf is lurking!

—description from the designer

Time to don your deerstalker and see whether you have what it takes to be a detective — but do it quickly, or others will solve the case first.

In Crazy Sherlock, you need to identify five clues related to the crime: the eye color of the criminal, their height, their accessory, the location, and the time. The game includes 18 clue cards divided across these five categories, and to set up, place one card from each category to the side, then shuffle the remaining cards and deal them out as evenly as possible.

When the round begins, start a 15-second timer. Each player has this much time to review their cards, after which they pass them left, the timer starts again, then they can look at their new cards. They keep passing cards until someone grabs the announcement token, which stops the timer. This person announces their guess in each clue category, then looks at the set-aside cards. If correct, they receive a case token that reads 1; if they already have a case token, they win the game.

If they're wrong, they lose a case token (if they have one), then the round continues, with this person passing cards, but unable to guess. Continue to play until someone guesses correctly, or only one player remains, in which case they win automatically.

With the "Doctor Watson" variant, each player takes a vote token at the start of play, and whenever someone announces their guess, each other player reveals at the same time whether they think the guesser is correct. If everyone votes yes, the guesser wins a clue token automatically. If the voters are split and the guess is correct, the guesser wins a clue token and awards a clue token to someone who supported them; if the voters are split and the guess is incorrect, everyone who voted in favor of the guesser is also out of the round. The first player to collect three clue tokens wins.

Cursed Lands: Morgulia is a miniature agnostic  horror skirmish game in which players control warbands seeking to amass wealth, renown, or power at the expense of their rivals while staving off the inevitable corruption that comes with success in a harsh world thrown into the grips of even greater chaos and horror after the passing of a strange black comet...

Designed to be played on a 3'x3' table or board using 28-32mm miniatures, most games should last anywhere from 30-90 minutes for two players, but can be played with as many as nine players at a time!

"Romeo has taken over PJ masks HQ! It's up to you to save the day! Race your hero through the city and up headquarters!"
Using a cardboard spinner, kids of all ages move their heroes on the track to reach their headquarters and silence their foe Romeo.

Full Spectrum Dominance is a miniature wargame for small-scale miniatures and OnTableTop Choice Award 2022 winner.
It allows to play with miniature in any scale from 3mm to 15mm and over. Vehicles and mechs are handled individually, and infantry troops move in small teams, either as grouped figures or multi-based stands.

You can fit two armies and a full battlefield on a coffee table, as little as 2 x 3 feet, moving platoons and tank groups with the same ease you would do in a skirmish game!

All you need to remember is tracked on handy Unit cards, that are also at the center of the activations engine which ensure that at any moment all players will be kept on their toes!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Escape the realm of Zun!

This new location card expansion will take your whole table on a journey to distant lands that change the rules while you play. It’s a new way to play the game you already know!

Each Location card has small rule changes which affect everyone while it's active
On your turn, you may move everyone to a new Location, hopefully changing the rules of play in your favor!
When you want to play a new Location, draw one from the Location deck and replace the previous Location.

Dungeon Murder! consists of a large deck of cards, featuring different types of cards such as "Dungeon Chamber" cards, "Action" cards, "Gold" cards, and various, "wild" cards. The objective of the game is to be the first player holding three complete Dungeon Chamber card sets. 
Dungeon Murder is an original game, with inspiration from many "collect and trade" style games.

Dungeon Murder is fast paced, with each game lasting about 15 minutes. It is easy to learn but requires strategy to win, making it an enjoyable game for both casual and experienced players. It's also a game with lots of humor and has some great illustration work, making it a fun and entertaining game for all players.

—description from the designer

CAPE COD OPOLY is a cool, property trading board game, that features Cape Cod & the Islands. Experience all the fun of wheeling and dealing against your opponents while buying and selling property on the Cape. From the beaches on Nantucket to shopping on Martha's Vineyard, enjoy the classic gameplay. Whether you're just visiting on vacation, or living on Cape Cod, experience all the sites, landmarks and attractions Cape Cod has to offer. The National Seashore, the Hyannis Port Compound, scenic harbors and much more! Anyone can play from kids to Grandparents. It's a game of chance with adventures along the way. Build your wealth by owning inns and cottages on property from Falmouth to Provincetown. You will be entertained as you strategize to bankrupt your opponents and try to stay on the Cape!

—description from the publisher

You better werk for the superstardom that awaits in this ultimate game for drag queen fans! MONOPOLY: RuPaul’s Drag Race puts players in the heels of the Emmy Award-winning reality show’s contestants to buy, sell, and trade iconic locations from the competition, such as Winners Circle, Judges Panel, and more! Sissy that walk across the board with runway essential tokens such as duck tape or a lipstick tube, then place Badges and Crowns to rack up Ru Dollars. Be the last player to watch all the others sashay away to win!

—description from the publisher

Get in the zone with the “Princess of Pop” in this chart-topping edition of the classic family game! MONOPOLY: Britney Spears takes players and fans on a musical journey through her most popular hits to buy, sell, and trade songs like “Baby One More Time”, “Toxic”, “Lucky”, and more! Tokens inspired by unforgettable music videos such as an astronaut, milkshake tray, and basketball take you around the board to rack up Gold Records and Platinum Records. Be the last one standing with hard-earned riches (until oops, you’ve bankrupted your friends again) to win!

—description from the publisher

This all-new Family Feud Gamestar+ Edition of Family Feud brings the excitement of one of the most popular game shows of all time into your home. Invite your friends and family around and have a Family Feud Gamestar+ Party. Stream Steve Harvey right to your TV to ask your friends and family over 300 survey questions and reveal the answers - just like the real show. All you need is 2 or more people, an internet connection, and this game. Discover why Family Feud is one of the most popular game shows of all time. Does your family have what it takes to win? Survey says... yes!

—description from the publisher

Take control of all the planets before your opponents and use unique effects to hone your strategy and keep them at bay. But watch out for the ruthless pirates and other galactic villains who could jeopardize your mission! Are you ready to take control of the galaxy and conquer all its planets?

Players take alternate actions to steal planets from other players, conquer planets on their own and use special cards for unique effects. Each round a player can choose to gain a new planet or use the effect of one of his cards to manipulate the board.

—description form the publisher

boardgame: Twilight of the Reich: Endgame in the European Theater 1944-45 – Module 16
description:
Twilight of the Reich (TotR) primarily features actions at the end of WWII in Germany and adds several SS MMC types and some terrain rules (including Rail Cars) to the ASL Core Rules set.

• 4 double-sided 11" x 16" ASL maps
Major urban terrain
Major factory terrain
Large city park area in the middle of urban terrain
Transition from light urban to heavier urban
• 2 countersheets

—description from the publisher

This campaign celebrates the '10th year anniversary' JamSumo and the game has a new larger design that allows up to 6 players.

Overview:
JamSumo is 2 dice flicking games played on one board. Each player receives 5 dice.

Jam - is a race to flick your dice into the middle hole.

Sumo - is a dice flicking ’knock out’ battle.

JamSumo - is 1 round of Jam and 1 round of Sumo.

Game That Song is the ultimate party game for music lovers! First, pick a music related topic from your cards. Next, each player plays a different song from a music app on their phone to go with the card. You pick the winner of the round!

Each player represents a scientist and, simultaneously, a trader who, by moving around the game board, invents creations, ensures their legal protection, and, through licensing trades, gains additional financial resources or, conversely, pays licensing fees for the inventions of fellow players. The winner is the one who best estimates the situation, weighs risks most effectively, invests money wisely, and, of course, has a bit of luck on their side as well.

Players move their meeples along the game board according to the rolls of a die. The game board displays various inventions that players acquire (invent) and trade among themselves. Additionally, there are four spaces for patent agents, enabling the protection of individual inventions with different degrees of safeguarding. If a player lands on a space occupied by an invention owned by another player, they must pay a fee, with the amount increasing based on the level of protection for that particular invention. The goal of the game is to eliminate other players by causing them to go bankrupt.
publishers: Útvar transferu technologií VUT v Brně

description:
In Cultists & Cthulhu, you lead a Cthulhu cult bent on completing nefarious rites while beset by incompetent underlings, meddling kids, and imminent madness.

Cultist cards are the cornerstones of your plans, who must roll custom dice turn by turn to progressively complete Rituals. Madness, of course, is never far away. Each Ritual completed contributes points toward the 21 total needed to win.

Although each cult is primarily trying to complete its own rituals, gameplay is decidedly of a "take-that" nature, with many player activities devoted to harming the other players' attempts to carry out their objectives.

—description from the publisher

Fae folk have emerged from their long hibernation to find the innumerable remnants of human civilizations, crumbling and covered in natural and unnatural flora.  It is an archaeological wonderland of latent radioactivity, old military technology, and the descendants of the alien arthropod invasion force.

Battle robots sit hunched at their last posts, awaiting reactivation prompts.  Long-dormant automated countermeasures are still relatively dangerous.  Yet, the sun shines brightly on this new age of magic, music, merriment, mutation, and spirited, competitive exploration.

You represent a coach in the Fae Gauntlet League.  Create your very own team of faerie daredevils and lead them through challenges and random events.  Collect points for meeting dice combo challenges successfully, roll against your skill stats to avoid danger or to collect bonuses, and cross the finish line with the lowest accumulated time!  Your team members can learn talents, earn team skill stat boosts, and maybe gain an animal friend... or your team could get wrecked by bugs, drones, and your own miscalculations!

We reach the Drop Zone in ten seconds.  Watch out for the lurker and we'll see you at the Extraction point!  Good luck, teams!  ...two ...one ...GO GO GO!

—description from the designer

Card Golf Tour is a playing card game where players aim to get the lowest point total possible by strategically drawing, matching, keeping, and discarding cards. In Card Golf Tour, each hand is like a golf hole and can be either a par 3, par 4, or par 5, just like in actual golf. The par of the hole determines how many cards are dealt to each player. Each hand will also have a "stroke index", which will make the hand more or less difficult by removing cards from the playing deck. Each card in the deck, which is made up of 54 cards, carries a point value that will range from -5,-2,0-11. Each player is dealt either 4,6, or 8 of these cards face down and try to end up with the lowest point total at the end of the hand. The hand is over when one player has all of their cards face up on the table. When this has happened, each player adds up the total of the points in their hand and records their score for the hand.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legends of King Arthur: A Quest Card Game
description:
Who will win the quest for power, fame and glory? Collect cards to complete quests, gain points, defeat your opponents and win a place in legend. This easy-to-learn card game comes with a detailed booklet that will introduce you to all the stories and characters behind the gameplay!

COMPLETE QUESTS including the search for the Holy Grail and stop your opponents stealing all the glory

SCREEN-FREE FUN for 2-4 players ages 8 and up

PLAY AND LEARN: learn the legend of King Arthur as you play

BEAUTIFULLY ILLUSTRATED by Adam Simpson, bestselling illustrator of The World of Shakespeare 1000-piece jigsaw puzzle.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
Dive into a thrilling twist on the classic card game you thought you knew. Welcome to a world where Geishas, Alpha Cats, Strays, and the luxurious Fat Cats clash in a battle of wits and strategy. Are you ready to unleash your inner feline?

Features:

Four Unique Cat Clans: Geisha Clan, Alpha Cat Gang, The Strays, and the opulent Fat Cats. Each with its own special power to tip the scales in your favor!

Explosive Power-ups: From the enchanting "Female Charm" to the disruptive "Dynamite", strategize your moves and watch the tables turn!

Magic, Weapons & Creatures: Engage in epic "Rock, Paper, Scissors" showdowns with Melee Weapons, Ranged Weapons, and Creature Riders. Plus, use magic to revive discarded cards!

Play with Friends: With support for up to 10 players, gather your friends for a purr-fect game night!

Note: No actual cats were harmed in the making of this game. All "attacks" are purely fictional and meant for entertainment only.

—description from the publisher

A deck building tactical and strategic card game for 1-2 players.

(VS)Deal 7 direct damage to the opponent
(SOLO) Defeat 3 waves of enemy Slimes then challenge the Boss

To play Pixel Powers (VS and Solo), you may Draft a 24 card deck, or you may pre-construct one. Players take turns - the objective is to as efficiently as possible use the resources, magic spells, item cards (and others) in your hand to pay the cost of Slime and Base cards that can be spawned onto the battlefield with a Health/Attack value. Hand and discard pile management are key, as well as a healthy amount of hoping to draw the cards you need.

Once spawned, Slime cards may attack enemy cards on the battlefield. Or when playing Versus, may attack the enemy player directly which is how to win! Base cards have powerful abilities that can be triggered each turn, and can quickly shift the odds in your favor.

(SOLO) Challenge 3 waves of enemy Slime cards, then flip over the dungeon and reveal the absurdly overpowered Boss Slime.

boardgame: Battle Break: Sengoku Tactics – Kawanakajima's Decisive Battle
description:
This was a special limited edition stand-alone mail order game that used the mechanics of Battle Break to recreate the 4th Battle of Kawanakajima, fought between the Uesugi and Takeda in 1561.

The game is played on a gridded map. Figures are stacked onto chips that reflect their level of power and life. The chips can also be spent to execute special moves--found under the figure bases. The round is basically split into 4 phases, and battles are resolved with a combination of movement (including flight), special moves, and dice rolls.

The game includes 20 models, two sets of chips, dice, a double-sided playmat and rules.

boardgame: Sengoku Folio 10: The Battle of Ishigakihara
description:
The Battle of Ishigakihara is a Japanese doujin (fan-made) game produced by Ludens Faber and is game 10 of his Sengoku Folio series. The game simulates the battle between the Kuroda and Otomo in 1600.

The game is a typical wargame, with movement and attack values, zones of control, a CRT for combat results, and a turn track.

The game includes a map, rules, historical guide, and mounted counters. The counters need to be cut out.
publishers: Dōjin Club Ludens Faber (同人会ルデンス・ファベル)

boardgame: Apex Legends: The Board Game – Squad 1 Expansion
description:
Outside the ring, they’re champions. Inside, they’re equals. Not all fighters are heroes, and each competes for their own reasons. But the goal remains the same—survive, win, and become a Legend.

The Squad 1 Expansion introduces 6 new Legends into the roster of Apex Legends: The Board Game.


    The Toxic Trapper Caustic uses poison traps and gas grenades, creating hazard zones on the battlefield and weakening enemy Legends before closing in for the kill.
    The Surveillance Expert Crypto utilizes his surveillance drone to scout and loot the area, as well as drain the shields of his enemies with EMP attacks.
    The Holographic Trickster Mirage uses Hologram decoys to confuse his opponents, shield himself from harm, and conceal his location.
    The High-speed Daredevil Octane outsmarts other Legends with spectacular stunts and pure speed.
    The Synthetic Nightmare Revenant uses his Silence Device to prevent his foes from using their abilities, while his Death Totem serves his squadmates in cheating death.
    The Static Defender Wattson helps out allied Legends by recharging their shields and creating fortified safe zones on the battlefield.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Apex Legends: The Board Game – Board 1 Expansion
description:
Push the conflict into new ground! The Board 1 Expansion adds new Map tiles for use in Apex Legends: The Board Game, based on the industrial themes of Kings Canyon and offering new buildings and terrain features, including fences, which function as impassable obstacles. The new board can be attached to the default one using a set of connector tiles, which enlarges the potential battlefield.

The expansion also introduces a new game mode—Control Point—and a set of new pre-made Maps.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Apex Legends: The Board Game – Solo and Co-op Expansion
description:
Designed with Radek Ignatów and Dávid Turczi, the Solo and Co-op Expansion introduces the option to play Apex Legends: The Board Game in single player mode or in cooperation with another player against a squad of automated opponents.

Inside, you will find a set of components that turn each of the core six Legends into unique, automated enemies.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Apex Legends: The Board Game – Loba Expansion
description:
The Apex Games arenas are brimming with treasures just waiting to be plucked. And if you want something valuable and well guarded—Loba is who you go to.

This expansion offers a wealth of new content for Apex Legends The Board Game, adding Loba into the game’s Legend roster. Loba uses her Burglar’s Best Friend bracelet to escape from perilous situations and makes sure her squad has access to the best equipment available by deploying her Black Market Boutique directly onto the battlefield.

The expansion also grants access to the Nessie Special Feature, introducing the loveable character of Nessie into Apex Legends™: The Board Game as an interactive component, and spicing up each session through the use of Nessie Feat cards, which grant the players access to powerful and subversive gameplay effects, encouraging them to diverge from their usual strategies.

Also included are plastic miniatures for the Control Beacon and Heat Shields game modes, which replace the cardboard ones found in the Core Box.

—description from the designer

Catharsis Sagas are true solo game boxes that include one character & one boss. If you own the base game, the boss plays up to five players. The game requires strategy, cooperation, and a touch of luck to succeed as the party fights through traps, monsters, and more. You will hunt down the boss while going through an epic narrative. Roll your dice to activate your powers, but be warned, your powers are also your health. You must carefully decide when it's wise to activate a power and how to resolve damage. Spend your morale wisely, or else you and your party will fall to the corruption of Verity.

Learn about commonly used traditional Chinese herbs as you outbid your fellow sinsehs (Chinese physicians) for patients. Designed in partnership with Eu Yan Sang, a traditional Chinese medicine giant, Master Sinseh is a meticulously researched unique bidding card game.

The game is played over 5 days. Each day, players will prescribe exactly 2 herb cards to each patient. Each herb card has values in the 4 categories that each patient is suffering from. Players will need to juggle and combine herbs in the cleverest way possible to become the Master Sinseh!

Have you ever experienced a Singaporean New Year Salad? Everything is tossible in this frantic card game about Lo Hei (New Year salad). You'll need speed and accuracy to find and assemble your salad before all the other players!

In this real time game, use your chopstick cards to push cards around to locate pairs of ingredients in your colour. Are the cards not in your colour? Use your chopstick cards to flip the cards around, because all cards have different colours on each side!

You don't need to know anything about Chinese New Year to have instant fun for the whole family in Huat's Up!

—description from the publisher

Fifty new double-sided cards with a hundred new questions about the Swedish author Astrid Lindgren. The que
Also includes a Smartbox that fits 50 cards. Can be played as a stand-alone game or as an expansion to the original.

CLUENECT is perfect for people who love crafting witty clues and decoding hidden messages.

A clue giver secretly chooses one of the six available picture cards and gives a one word clue. Clue receivers (everybody else) attempts to pick which picture card they think the clue refers to by placing an associated number card that refers to the picture card.

Clue receivers get points if they guess right, while the clue giver only gets points if some but not all guess their picture correctly - thus making the clue giver try to not give too easy or hard of clues.

A fast-paced sci-fi card game set in the year 2386, where humanity has relocated to an artificial world that has replaced our own.  The second Great Age had been founded upon principles and hope but has since reverted to the same foils and pitfalls of greed, corruption and crime.

In this game, you control a protagonist - a central figure of prominence in the world of Beta Earth - and work to either secure your hold on its limited resources or fight to protect it from those who would see its downfall.

The game utilizes three different card types, with multiple strategies and a unique system of combat triggers that make combat feel unique and fresh.  Victory is achieved by either being the first to score 20 victory points or being the last one standing - reducing all other opponent's endurance to zero.

—description from the designer
Similar to Scrabble you have to extend existing characters by your own letters to build long words. The longer the words you build the more points you score at the end.

boardgame: Arrival Push: A Game of Vectors
description:
Dive into becoming air traffic controllers, taking over airspace and airplanes from another controller. As air traffic controllers, players must vector their aircraft across their respective airspace, around other airplanes and nasty weather to Approach Control. All airplanes move every round and new events pose challenges to controllers and their airplanes. The closer the airplanes get to the airport, the more limited airspace becomes. Maneuvering quickly becomes difficult or nearly impossible without the help of neighboring controllers. Players must work together: When one player struggles, it affects everyone around them. Players score by handing off airplanes to Approach Control. Approach Control will only take one airplane per player per round, so players must work carefully to sequence their arrivals. After 15 rounds (12 at high player counts), the next controller comes back from break; whoever has the most points wins and earns the right to take a break.

—description from the designer

Decorate the sky.
Arrange colorful umbrellas all over the sky.

Collect points by aligning colors in the same row, interacting with many colors, and pushing out other umbrellas.
However, the same colors should not be placed next to each other.

Why don't you use a five-colored umbrella to brightly color the sky?

The game is based on a festival called the "Umbrella Sky Project" in Portugal.
In order to be close to the actual scenery, it is based on the rule that "the same color is not adjacent".
The last is also a beautiful looking game.

Rediscover the thrill of exploration and the point'n'click video games of your youth with this Pixel Adventures! A wild Seychelles island, an ancient pirate treasure, secret codes, a paranoid uncle, a missing woman! Will you come back alive and rich? Cleverly combine locations and items from your inventory to unlock passages and complete your quest!

In this cooperative game, you choose together which actions to carry out. Search for clues or objects. Use objects to try to move forward. Explore new locations.

Your time is limited, and you'll have to solve several objectives. You'll lose if you don't get far enough in the adventure. But even if you complete the objectives, you'll still have to choose what to take back to the island. And your choices will determine the end of the story.

—description from the publisher

This expansion introduces: 

     One new driver w/ all essential player specific components in orange (now allows up to 7 racers)
     Two new maps (Japan & Mexico)
     New upgrade cards (introducing the purple drop - a different cooldown that allows the player to take a heat card from their discard pile and place it back into their engine)
     Submerged track sections (must spend an extra heat for down shifting gears in these sections)
     More heat, stress, championship, sponsorship, and event cards.


—description from the publisher
family: Game: Heat – Pedal to the Metal

description:
Royal Turmoil is engaging for kids and adults alike. It's quick and easy to learn for beginners, with plenty of strategy for the more advanced players. Sure to keep you and yours coming back for more!

During the game you have a hand of resource cards that you play on the Royals out on the table. Each Royal requires certain resources the player to play the final resource on a Royal, they take that Royal and "settle it" (cache it for its points). But beware, other players can steal your unsettled Royals, so you have to be ready to defend them. Once the last Royal is settled, each player counts up the points on their Royals (with some bonuses) and highest score wins!

Royal Turmoil is easy to learn, fast to play, and plays well at any player count in about 30 minutes.

—description from the publisher

ROBBATTLE your Medabots in the arena!
You can battle up to 6 figures at a time! Exchange robot parts with opponents to win!

4 Game Modes

     One-on-One battles (basic & advanced versions)!
     Battle for points to win!
     Battle to cross the bridge first!
     Team battles for bigger challenges!

Family game night just got even better! Pick your monkey, collect your blocks and turn over the top card. Be the first monkey to build the stack and take the card to win the round. Collect three cards to win the game. This game is recommended for ages six plus, but friends and family of all ages will love Monkey Trix!

This action packed board game comes with everything you need! Game includes monkey heads, cards, and blocks. Some assembly is required - but it is so simple! It comes with step by step instructions. You will be going monkey for the this game in no time!

This feverish paced game will keep family game night fun for all! Monkey Trix keeps the game play action packed and lets you create your own strategy for a thrilling victory! Play with two players or play with teams! First - put on your silly monkey head, then draw a challenge card and get ready to build. The game starts when you yell 'monkey, set go'! First to build the puzzle, wins the card. 3 cards wins! Those are the rules...But as with any game, it's always fun to make your own, too!

—description from the publisher

A pack of 6 promo cards for the Crawler's Grimoire DungeonQuest expansions: 3 for The Undead Merchant, 2 for Events and 1 for Haunted Halls. These were included with orders that purchased all 3 sets of Crawler's Grimoire expansions. Included:

1x Selenic Spear
2x Death Coin
1x Pack Mule
1x Glowing Mark
1x Stalk Card

Grouille! is a trivia game from Quebec. Every player starts with 10 questions cards. The winner is the first player to get rid of all their question cards.

Question cards are discarded after being read out loud. The player who shouts the correct answer before everyone else gets to read the question card next.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Tokens containing different words are spread out in the middle of the table. Players are split into two teams.

Every turn, one player from each team has a duel: a clue is read out loud and the first to grab the correct token scores 3 points. The first team to reach 30 points wins.

Shoppers at a convenience store are shocked to discover the cashier's body by the cash register. He was alive just minutes ago! Who was actually browsing the aisles as all the shoppers claim, and which one of them slipped past the cash counter to get rid of the poor victim? What motive did they have and what clues did they leave behind?

Piece together clues, uncover hidden messages, assess suspects, and catch the culprit in this murder mystery game set in the 1980s.

—description from the publisher

Grappling with political unrest during the 1985 mayoral election, the city of Oddtown is thrown into further chaos when the Editor-in-Chief of the local newspaper, The Oddtown Post, is found murdered in his office. The only other people on the premises were six of his coworkers. They all seem to be hiding something and may even have motive to kill the victim. The Sheriff has reached out for your help with the investigation and is sending over clues.
Piece together clues, uncover hidden messages, assess the suspects, and catch the culprit.

—description from the publisher

Basketball × trick-taking × team competition

As the name suggests, Basket Lite 2-on-2 is a card game with a basketball motif. Four players are divided into two teams and compete for points.

Play cards that represent your playstyle and strength, and compete for the ball. In order to receive a pass or for the defender to win the ball, match the style of the ball man. If you dribble or pass, you have a chance to shoot! Shoots that are usually weak become powerful.

We focused on the feeling of playing basketball and the fun of team games. Let's aim to be the strongest combination with like-minded friends!
publishers: Red I Games (レッド・アイ・ゲームズ)

description:
Don't Match is a game in which the objective is to eliminate hands more quickly by playing cards that do not match the cards in the playing area in terms of number, illustration, color, etc. The game counts as one round until all cards in the hand or all cards in the market are eliminated.

The elements that must not be matched are specified by Don't match cards, and new Don't match cards are added each round. Any cards remaining in the hand at the end of a round are minus points, and the winner is the player with the fewest minus points at the end of three rounds.

boardgame: コレクトリテ～ボディビルver～ (CollecTrick: Bodybuilding Ver)
description:
In order to participate in the bodybuilding competition, collect and train your team's macho muscles by winning tricks, earn evaluation points, and help your team win. However, an unbalanced body will result in points being deducted, so be careful not to train too much.

コレクトリテ～ボディビルver～ is a trick-taking game where you need to balance the collected the upper and lower training cards bodybuilding poses.

Historical cars, kept in pristine condition by teams of dedicated mechanics, give The Royal Limited its extravagant appearance. Only the most skilled engineers can drive these aging engines.

The Vintage Engines expansion features 5 new Engine cards, each requiring the train to include a specific color, number, or sequence. One of these is added randomly to the game at setup. If the Engine’s goal is not met at the end of the game, the player must add 2 points to their score, making it difficult to secure victory.

Working on The Royal Limited is almost as much of an honor as riding on it. Correspondingly, each voyage draws a host of fresh-faced conductors as well as new celebrities.

The Conductors & VIPs expansion builds on the core gameplay with 3 new double-sided Conductor cards as well as 3 new VIPs. The Conductors can have special rules, change the number of cards drawn each round, or even increase the number of rounds played. The VIPs, meanwhile, work similarly to those in the standard game. However, 2 of them have multicolored tickets with numbers, constraining their placement.

boardgame: Host Your Own Escape Room: Ancient Egypt Edition
description: The mighty pharaoh curses all who disturb him... and in this escape room, that's you! Escape to Egypt, land of tombs, magic, sand dunes and disgruntled ancient mummies. For the next 60 minutes, you and your team must put the pharaoh back into his eternal slumber by solving the puzzles and revealing the spell to send him back to the afterlife. This high-stakes, immersive adventure game is an exciting family activity perfect for the chilly winter months.

boardgame: にゃーにゃー大フゴー (Meow Meow Daifugō)
description:
In the world of cats, an exciting battle unfolds in a minimal and eventful ways. Put it out, pass it, take it, put it out! Whether you save up or break through.

にゃーにゃー大フゴー is a climbing game that when you pass, you have to take one card from the field.

What was sleeping in the deepest part of the dungeon wasn’t a weak little gargoyle... it was a dragon.

This isn’t what I was told! Everyone, hurry and run away. If you waste time, you’ll end up getting roasted whole. Huh? But in this situation, maybe if the dragon eats about two people, it’ll be satisfied, don’t you think??

Trick Runaway is a trick-taking game where you keep at just the right distance from the dragon to escape, competing for survival.
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

boardgame: ダンゴれ！魔女子さん (Dango! Majoko-san)
description:
There is currently an unprecedented dango boom in the witch world! Identify the magic that dominates the field, and try to get as many dumplings as possible without dropping them!

ダンゴれ！魔女子さん is a trick-taking game where you're competing to collect the most 5 types of dango drawn. There is a limit to how much dango you can have, and if you exceed it you will lose points.

An exciting touring card battle between bike girls and bike swamps. It's a fun party game that you'll want to play over and over again, just like playing cards. It feels good to be the only one who can play while interfering with other players!

Most war games focus on the front-line battle; this one focuses on the cost borne by those who survive.

Your country's colony is under attack by a rival superpower, and you have fully mobilized. In the front lines you have defenders of varying degrees of combat experience, and you have your Boot Camp filled with recruits - and a pool of civilians ready to join if necessary.

If you can hold out for 12 battles outside aid from the Mother Country is scheduled to arrive to end the conflict. Fail to have a full roster in the front lines before then and you'll be overrun and defeated.

In this SOLO game, the successive "battles" are pure abstraction where the Dice Defenders come out of each battle in one of four categories: (1) dead; (2) recoverably wounded; (3) untouched & gaining more combat experience; or (4) totally burned out. Battle outcomes are measured using the roll of one RED (wounded) D6 die and one BLACK (killed) D6 die. The numbers rolled go against the Combat Experience values of the Dice Defenders. HOWEVER, once all eligible civilians are pulled into Boot Camp, morale at the colony plummets and the two D6 dice are replaced with D8 ones - potentially increasing the number wounded and killed in future battles!

With each battle engagement, the survivors become more powerful and less likely to fall into categories 1 & 2. Push their luck too far and they'll fall into category 4. The only way to avoid category 4 is to remove them to Rest & Recreation Centers for a time - which means that while they're recovering, you have fewer experienced defenders on the field to take the brunt of future RED & BLACK dice rolls.

As Commander, it's up to you to perform this balancing act of who stays in and who gets time off - at the cost of those under your command! Show too much compassion and you'll lose the war from having too few defenders on the field. Show too little compassion and you'll lose the war from losing too many defenders through their breakdown. Who stays in without recuperating and who has to come back early? All in the name of keeping the front lines fully occupied!

Do you have what it takes to make the right call, following the credo - 'Mission first; Men second'?

This is the second part of a trilogy.

You and your party of property investors are eager to view the illustrious Thornbright Mansion that has just gone up for sale, but not everything is as it seems. Strange damages, missing documents, and cryptic notes alert you to something far more sinister inside this home.

Envelescape brings together the immersive nature of pop-up books with the puzzling mysteries of escape rooms. Explore a miniature 3D room by finding clues and solving puzzles. By utilizing intricate pop-up engineering, our rooms and clues fold down to fit into a single envelope.

Each Envelescape game (also known as a Chapter) includes a pop-up room to explore, papercraft and digital clues, and a thrilling mystery to uncover! By solving puzzles in the pop-up room you will unlock clues that will help you progress even further - just like a real escape room!

Just like Monopoly, players take the part of land owners, attempting to buy and then develop their land. Income is gained by other players visiting their properties and money is spent when they visit properties belonging to other players. When times get tough, players may have to mortgage their properties to raise cash for fines, taxes and other misfortunes.

Gameplay
On his turn, a player rolls two dice and moves that number of spaces around the board. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned property, he has the opportunity to buy it and add it to his portfolio or allow the bank to auction it to the highest bidder. If a player owns all the spaces within a color group, he may then build houses and hotels on these spaces, generating even more income from opponents who land there. If he lands on a property owned by another player, he must pay that player rent according to the value of the land and any buildings on it. There are other places on the board which can not be bought, but instead require the player to draw a card and perform the action on the card, pay taxes, collect income, or even go to jail

Puoi chiamarli arrustelle, rustelle o arrosticini, ma alla fine conta solo una cosa: chi è stata l’ultima persona del gruppo a mangiarli!
È così che inizia Abruzzo – Forte e gentile, il gioco da tavolo di contrattazione e investimento che ti farà esplorare questa meravigliosa regione stando comodamente a casa tua, con i tuoi cari o i tuoi amici, progettando, tra un lancio di dadi e l’altro, i vostri viaggi.

Corri lungo il tabellone per mare, monti e rocche, incontrando bizzarri personaggi tra storia, arte, cultura, tradizioni, buona cucina e luoghi mozzafiato.
Chi riuscirà a guadagnarsi il più grande titolo di questo territorio: forte e gentile?
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: This box of bafflement contains a series of cunning ciphers…but what do they mean? It’s up to you to crack the codes and reveal the mystery phrases, songs, film titles and so much more. Challenging? Possibly…but help is at hand because each card has a teasing topic that points you in the right direction. Can you determine what the mystery letters stand for?

Nestled beneath a frisky pine forest lies a land of lakes and streams. Navigate through the charted map system by water, portage or take a bush plane, putting kilometres behind you, thwarting disasters and aquiring new skills to survive while setting up new tent sites and obtaining the points you need to win.

Along the way you will pick up valuable equipment needed for your survival. “Weather or Not” you have what it takes to return safely to the Parking Lot with all of your points, might be up to you, but someone else may have another idea!

Each player starts with 2 Equipment Cards (which is the currency each turn, no dice), 2 Survival Cards, 3 Tent Site Markers, a Map Reference Card to establish your starting position and a MNOO player piece. Point tokens are scrambled and placed randomly on star locations throughout the board.

Gameplay is to strategize the best, one way, route to collect 15 minimum points and get back to the Parking Lot to win. You must thwart inevitable disasters with Survival Cards or purchase special Weather or Not Cards that can help or hinder you along your journey. Take a bush plane to move greater distances as an additional transport mechanism.

—description from the designer

Dark Horizons Mechanized Corps is a fast-paced, skirmish-scale tabletop miniatures game set in a futuristic, dystopian world in the mid to late 2300s for two or more players. The game features four main factions and several corporations battling for supremacy in a world divided into wild lands, megacities, and wasteland zones.

Players take control of armies consisting of a number of Mechanized Corps units (usually 1-10), including large manned mechs, drone mechs, and individual power armors. Each unit is customizable, with various weapons, equipment, and abilities tailored to suit individual play styles and strategies. 
Combat in Dark Horizons Mechanized Corps utilizes the X10 Combat System. This system employs up to four d10 dice per player for various rolls, such as initiative, damage, and critical hits, and is played on any standard hex map board of approximately 20 x 20 inches with hexes of 1-1.25 inches across.

The game offers several objective modes with varied victory conditions, allowing players to create diverse and exciting competitive scenarios. Balance is maintained through Battle Values, which help players construct fair and evenly-matched fights.

With a focus on streamlined gameplay and an emphasis on tactical decision-making, Dark Horizons Mechanized Corps offers an exciting and accessible entry point into tabletop miniature gaming.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Think Like a Shrink is not just a game, it's an experience that will have you laughing and learning at the same time. Created by two best friends - a psychologist and psychiatrist - who served together in the military, this adult party/conversation game is designed to help players connect.

Here are some key features and benefits of the game:


     Quick to learn. Always entertaining, Within five minutes, you'll be diving into the engaging world of "Think Like a Shrink". A party game designed for 2 to 6 players with infinite card combinations, ensuring that boredom never makes an appearance.



     Race around the clock-shaped game board to be named "top shrink" by correctly predicting the thoughts, emotions, and experiences of other players thru associating random photos with intriguing phrases… throw in some “feelings cards…” because we’re shrinks after all. Who knew fake therapy could be so much fun!



     Enjoy hilarious moments and "Ah Hah" insights that will leave you feeling closer to your friends and family.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Game of Wolf: Expansion Pack
description:
Which is stronger? The Lone Wolf or the Pack? Find out in this strategic trivia game where friend quickly becomes foe!

A unique game that is equal parts collaborative and competitive. Pick your Pack based on the player's knowledge of the subject or go Lone Wolf for a chance at double the points!

—description from the publisher

This expansion adds 125 new categories to the core game.

Each player is a mixologist attempting to complete as many classic tiki drinks as quickly as possible to earn the most money in tips. It’s fast, fun and competitive!

In Rhum Rush, players collect ingredient cards, placing them in ordered stacks according to a drink recipe. They must be quick, though, because if another player completes that recipe first, all progress on that drink will be lost. Whoever completes the drink first will receive the drink card, collecting the tip money on the card.

Special cards are dual-use cards that players can use to gain an advantage!

The game ends when one player reaches a certain amount of money in tips, depending on player count.

boardgame: Escape Game: La dernière heure du Titanic
description:
Will you survive the final hour of the Titanic?

You are a 3rd class passenger aboard the most prestigious liner ever built, the White Star Line's RMS Titanic. On the night of April 14 to 15, 1912, a crash of crumpled metal made you fall, followed by the noise of the crew members running in the passageway. You understand from their outbursts that a serious problem has occurred...

It's up to you to choose between three possibilities to escape the inexorably sinking ship.

Recruit other passengers to help you and search for items that will allow you to survive before the bridges go underwater.

–description from the publisher (translated)

Survivrez-vous à la dernière heure du Titanic ?

Vous êtes un passager de 3e classe à bord du plus prestigieux paquebot jamais construit, le RMS Titanic de la White Star Line. Dans la nuit du 14 au 15 avril 1912, un fracas de tôle froissée vous fait chuter, suivi par le tapage des membres d’équipage courant dans la coursive. Vous comprenez à leurs éclats de voix qu’un grave problème a eu lieu...

À vous de choisir entre trois possibilités pour vous échapper du vaisseau qui coule inexorablement.

Recrutez d'autres passagers pour vous aider et cherchez les éléments qui vous permettront de survivre avant que les ponts ne se retrouvent sous l'eau.

–description from the publisher
family: History: The Sinking of the Titanic

description: Fight the hungry seagulls and be the first granny who can eat! Acquire the eight (8) kung fu maneuvers to foil the tricks of the seagulls and launch attacks at your opponents in order to finish your sandwich first.

There is a pigeon in the village that often poops on the windowsills of the villagers and cannot be chased away. In order to keep their own windowsills clean, the villagers decide to lure the pigeon with birdseed to other people's windowsills. Thus begins a battle in which each household cleans up their own mess!

Fast paced party game for 3-8 players - the goal of this game is to not get DOVE POO on your windowsill!

Road of numbers - children's game from 6 years to learn addition and subtraction up to 50
Math is no longer boring!
The focus is on fun combined with an effective learning process for the children, which makes "Number Street" so popular with children and parents alike.

Are you looking for an educational game for children aged 6 and over to learn how to add and subtract numbers in a fun but effective way? Then you've found the right game!
A fun math game for children aged 6 and up! Kids learn the basics of addition and subtraction in the number range up to 50 and try to deliver ten deliveries to the number line as quickly as possible by rolling the dice with a lucky hand. Whether bread, fruit, milk, meat or fish, the fresher the delivery, the happier the customer. So everyone get in the delivery cars, roll the dice and let the fun delivery service race begin.
Add Heo Nanseolheon, famous painter and poet of the mid-Joseon dynasty, and Yi Sun-Sin, renowned admiral and military general during the Joseon dynasty, to all your Similo games!

boardgame: EXIT: Das Buch – Adventskalender: Das Ticket ins Verderben
description:
An Exit advent calendar in book form; open the closed pages, read a chapter and solve a puzzle to determine which door to open for the next day.

Lea wished that her annoying little half-brother Mikkel would be taken away by the elves, and now he's on the train that takes the elves to Santa Claus in Lapland because he wants to become one himself! Lea has to prevent this at all costs and secretly sneaks onto train.  Help her to solve the puzzles she needs to solve to take her brother home again...
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
Cask Strength is a mini-expansion for Distilled, designed to add a touch more complexity and variety to the game. This expansion has four parts: new distillery upgrades, special flavors, upgraded premium ingredients, and an exciting new dynamic market mechanic. This expansion is a small box that packs a big punch!

Upgraded Premium Ingredients: Each premium ingredient card in the game now has a unique effect, allowing players to replace some or all of the current sugar cards for additional complexity and combos.

Dynamic Market: This brand new element to the game presents players with more economically driven decisions that will arise and change throughout the game

—description from the publisher

Make the silly faces to match your Mango Cards. Think another player is making the same face then shout, "MANGO!" If your cards match add them to your Ripe Mango pile. If they don't match pick up a Rotten Mango Token. The player with the most Mango Matches is the winner!

STEERY is the party game of stories that will never be told again.

Drop ludicrous plot twists, spark seemingly impossible conflicts, and hijack the narrative with your cards to score points. No tale is too weird, and no ending too unhinged.

In STEERY, you score points by steering the story of one or more characters, using the story cards that you have in hand. To amplify the element of surprise, the game also includes special cards hidden in the main deck to prompt you to introduce new characters into the story or to straight out end it when you least expect it.

Learn it in 5 minutes. Bond with your people over unpredictable stories. Unleash your creativity and wit.

STEERY contains character cards and story cards (with point values). The first player starts the story by revealing a character card, introducing the character in the story, and playing story cards from their hand to get the ball rolling. From then on, players take turns in moving that same story forward by playing their story card(s), incorporating the concept of the card(s) they're playing in the story, and scoring points along the way. The game ends when "The End" card is drawn and announced.

STEERY is balanced between strategic and casual gameplay and the sweet spot between collaboration and competition. The game has also been designed to be family friendly, but it can easily take a NSFW turn if players wish to go that way.

—description from the publisher

Advanced Tic Tac Toe (also referred to as Ultimate Tic Tac Toe) where there are nine 3x3 play areas for two players to play in.  Where the last player played within any of the 3x3 spots determines where the next player needs to play. For example, if Player 1 plays in the top left spot, then Player 2 needs to play anywhere in the top left 3x3 area.  It's an interesting spin on and old game, that can also be played on any metal surface as the board is magnetic.

Here is a faraway sea area. In this oceanic zone, there was a circular current that was like a eden for sea creatures.
When the current exceeds a certain speed, it stores air, takes in food, and begins to circulate. Because of its unique appearance, this ocean current is called a “MARINE GOROUND”.
Why don’t you dive into the ocean and swim with the creatures? Let’s create a paradise cooperating with marine creatures.

Marine Goround is a tile-building backgammon game in which players aim to score Victory Points as quickly as possible by freely arranging the Tiles and creating chains of them. Each creature has its own ability, and your strategy will change greatly depending on which Creature Tiles you collect.

Meka City is a dangerous place, crawling with villainous scum bent on overtaking the thriving metropolis. Luckily, there are a number of SuperSquads on patrol, taking baddies into custody and removing them from society. But, alas, even the SuperSquads are at odds with one another, each seeking to cement its place as the most LEGENDARY team in Meka City...

Do you have what it takes to be the best? Build your team, maintain their collective strength, and do battle against a bevy of rotten-to-the-core bad guys!

—description from the publisher

Name and find these cute cats in this naming / Go Fish game.


     Check the kitten's name on the card
     Call out the kittens' names and look for them
     Once the kittens are found, let's go home together!

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Warcry – Core Book (Second Edition)
description:
The second edition of fast-paced skirmish games set in the Mortal Realms featuring updated core rules for dynamic games, plus expansion options for narrative, open, and matched play.

In the tangled depths of the deadly Gnarlwood, rival warbands battle over territory and plunder!

Enter the grim world of Warcry in the Core Book – your guide to fast-paced skirmish combat in the Gnarlwood, a bizarre jungle in the Realm of Beasts. This book contains all the core rules for the second edition of Warcry, including new rules that give you even more opportunities to react to the ebb and flow of battle, as well as expansion options for open, narrative, and matched play. Campaigns and quests will allow you to grow an inexperienced warband into a legendary force, renowned throughout Ghur and beyond – there's also a wealth of lore on the hungry Gnarlwood and fabled Talaxis, a foreboding ruin rumoured to contain ancient artefacts that can alter reality itself.

This book is an essential companion for anyone setting out to explore the Gnarlwood – whether you're an experienced player or founding your first warband.

This 160-page softcover book contains:
– Background material and sumptuous art to immese you in the horrifying Gnarlwood
– The complete core rules for Warcry, all you need to play this skirmish combat game
– New rules for Reactions, allowing more dynamic and aggressive gameplay
– Rules for open play, including two types of multiplayer battles for three or more players: Coalition of Death and Triumph & Treachery
– Rules for matched play, including guidelines to balance your battleplans and advice on how to run a tournament
– Rules for narrative play that allow you to run thrilling campaigns and forge epic stories where your warband progresses in power and notoriety
– A campaign section featuring quests and rewards for all Warcry factions, plus two full campaign arcs: The Path of Ven Talax and The Chotec Valley
– An open-ended battleplan generator, allowing you to quickly and easily set up a game with any scenery in your collection"

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: The Transformation Game: Solo and Earth-Solo
description: A supplement to The Transformation Game. Enjoy the many benefits of The Transformation Game in a solo setting. The Solo and Earth-Solo Games provide clarity on personal issues and give you feedback on the far reaching impact of your actions, attitudes and choices. Enhance your game experience by using a meditative approach, playing at your own pace and recording your thoughts and feelings as your game progresses.

Two-player simple abstract game featuring chocolate so real - there's numerous warnings not to eat it.

How to Play:

     Place the chocolates randomly in a circle
     Arrange the chocolate in the chocolate square box according to the rules.
     If you can't legally place a chocolate, you lose and opponent gains hearts.

boardgame: Gutterhead: The Fiendishly Filthy Expansion Pack
description:
FIRST EXPANSION PACK - Because what you really need is an additional 112 fiendishly filthy drawing cards to make your life complete.
NOT FOR THE FAINT HEARTED - Yup, we're pretty convinced our 'online research' for this pack has landed us on the FBI watch-list.
REQUIRES GUTTERHEAD CORE GAME - By all means buy it on its own, however, it will be about as useful as a knitted condom.
WHO KNEW WE COULD TOP THE CORE GAME? - we did, so we did.

Contains 25 Person/Place cards, 25 Object cards, 25 Action cards, 25 Feeling/Sensation cards, 12 Naughty or Nice cards

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ...I should have known that! Yes or No
description:
Does an orange float in water? Can you be allergic to a hairless cat? Does Transylvania really exist? Is C the letter between V and N on a keyboard?

The trivia game ...I should have known that! Yes or No contains more than four hundred "yes or no" questions about things that you should know. Sound like a piece of cake? You'll be surprised how baffling even the easiest questions can be when you don't have Google at your fingertips! Instead of receiving positive points for correct answers, you are burdened with negative points for wrong answers — and the number of points you received depends on how easy the question is.

The player with the fewest negative points wins.

Show off your “correct” opinions on ridiculous topics and prove you’re the true Genius! When faced with a question like “How many spoons make a respectable spoon collection?”, the only answer that matters is Genius. All the other players try and guess what the current Genius would answer. The closest guess to theirs wins the point! Don’t worry—everyone gets a chance to be the Genius! In this game where your answer is THE answer, it’s all about what you have in mind!

boardgame: Interstellar Abduction: A Card game of Capturing Humans!
description:
Interstellar Abduction! Is a fast paced resource & strategy card game where 2-4 players take control of one of four alien factions in a game of high stakes intergalactic human acquisition. Utilize your factions' different strengths and weaknesses as you outsmart your opponents to abduct earthlings, gain scientific knowledge, and avoid gaining evidence of your existence!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: CyberQuest: The Game of Cyberpunk Adventures
description:
You are in the year 2081. The Black Chip agency, formed by members who participated in the Second Corporate Conflict on both sides, try to avoid a third conflict between Nakaware and Biosync. 
Max, an employee at Nakaware, discovers a confidential file that could spark a war. He contacts his sister Raven and meets her in an old warehouse, away from Neon City, to explain her what he is up to.

Cyberquest is a narrative cyberpunk adventure game for 1 to 5 players. You play in 3 different phases: Mission phase, chase phase and den phase.

Mission Phase: Advance with your runner through a dungeon crawler-style scenario in which anything can happen in every room.

Chase Phase: Escape the site driving your car into den. A mini-game in which you draw event cards with the objetive to finish the route.

Den Phase: Heal your wounds, upgrade your cyberware, get jobs and repair your car. A little maintenance to prepare you for the next mission.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: När då då?: Mat och dryck
description:
In När då då?, players take turns taking cards and forming a timeline with ten different events from the beginning of the era until today. Put events, people, places or gadgets in the right order of time.

This edition of the game focusses on food and drink.

In När då då?, players take turns taking cards and forming a timeline with ten different events from the beginning of the era until today. Put events, people, places or gadgets in the right order of time.

This edition of the game focusses on crime.

In När då då?, players take turns taking cards and forming a timeline with ten different events from the beginning of the era until today. Put events, people, places or gadgets in the right order of time.

This edition of the game focusses on famous women.

Collect colourful fish by turning over tiles and matching them to your penguin colour, but watch out for the seal who will steal your fish. A fun penguin-themed game with 2 exciting ways to play. Choose a collaborative or competitive game. Develops matching and memory skills.

In the collaborative game, players must work together to fill the iceberg playing board with different coloured fish, before the seal gets to the middle of the iceberg! For the competitive game, players break apart the playing board to creative individual lotto boards, playing against each other to be the first to fill their board with matching coloured fish.

Designed for ages 3+, Hungry Little Penguins is Orchard Toys' first introduction to collaborative gameplay. Players will develop matching and memory skills, as well as encouraging personal and social skills.

Dogma is a minimalistic party game in a rich package that seems like a total overkill but at the same time very in line with the luxurious and wealthy style of the Church.

In this game, you will become corrupt and immoral cardinals who would lie and deceive to hide their filthy sins.

Hell, you can even change sacred Dogmas and transform vice into virtue if that helps you win!

In short, you are the most righteous people on the planet. But also the worst!

That’s the world of Dogma.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ソクラテスラ: 神々の宴 (Sokuratesura: Kamigami no Utage)
description:
ソクラテスラ: 神々の宴 (Sokuratesura: Kamigami no Utage) is the third expansion to ソクラテスラ (SocraTesla) and introduces powerful gods from various world religions. The rules limit the number of these characters in a players hand, but they allow for more powerful chimera. For example, some gods have more than two arms, or more than one torso.

The designer advises that the expansion is fun, but does not make the game more tactical.
user summary

Demography is a political strategy game. You are a candidate in the UK General Election Race. Travel hastily around the UK to win the support of voters in the regions you visit. Campaign to undecided voters or Oppose to steal voters from your opponents. If you are brave enough, Sleaze one of the other candidates to diminish their support but watch out it doesn't backfire. When you feel the moment's just right, take the express route to London and perform a Political Masterstroke to completely outmanoeuvre the other candidates!
When campaigning is over its out of your hands and down to the voters to decide. Remember anyone can emerge the winner on Election Day

オボンダービー　精霊馬合体グランプリ (Obon Derby - Spirit Horse Combination Grand Prix) is a racing game from Japan, where players combine cards to form their spirit animal for the race.

Across Japan, the Obon Festival is celebrated with various events during the summer, to remember and honour family ancestors. These events include a significant ritual, crafting a cucumber horse and eggplant cow, known as shōryō uma (精霊馬, "spirit horse") or ushi uma (牛馬, "cow horse"), that act as a vessel for the ancestors to come back home and return, respectively.

In this game, players create these spirit animals in order to race them. By combining the Head, Body, and Tail cards from their hand (2 of each type), turning them up one by one and following the strategy written on the card, players advance their horse or cow along a linear course to aim for first place.

Cards have activation conditions, but there are some that advance you two squares at once, and others that move the animal in front of you back one square, creating a dead heat feeling in a horse race. The downside is that the order in which you reveal your cards during the race is fixed, so the first player tends to have an advantage. This tendency was especially noticeable when the number of players was small, because some of the actions require interference with the animal in front, and this could not be achieved with too few players.
user summary

Which of your friends share your future? Find out in this mystical game of matching answers!

Pick a card with a question about your future, which could be anything from "In ten years, I'll be working as ...", to "What crime will I go to prison for?" Each player then writes their answer and revelas what they wrote. If a player matches your answer, they win a point.

Will you and your besties prove you have a spiritual connection, or will you be on totally different wavelengths?

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Mirrorstone Earrings  & Fierce Paint Promo Cards
description:
This is a set of two promo cards, the Mirrorstone Earrings  gear card and the Fierce Paint gear card. They were included with the Edlen miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

The Mirrorstone Earrings read: On Arrival, gain +★ vanity tokens.

The Fierce Paint reads: On Arrival, for each vanity you have, add 1 [armor] to each hit location that you are not wearing armor at or wearing armor with the swagger keyword.

boardgame: カニの行進〜遅すぎた覚醒〜 (Kani no Koushin Ososugita Kakusei)
description:
カニの行進〜遅すぎた覚醒〜 (Kani no Koushin Ososugita Kakusei) is a Pandemic style co-operative game from Japan. The game is packaged in a large styrofoam box of the kind used for seafood, and the theme is based on the annual migration of Christmas Island red crabs.

Players are crabs, smart ones that try to help the other crabs survive the migration and spawn successfully. The game features lavish components, including lots of crabs and special bridges to help the crabs get over a busy road, and spawning grounds in the sea to save the crabs from being eaten by sharks.

The crabs spawn in large numbers on one board (the land), and relentlessly march across the land by the most direct path. This relates to player number, so with four players, four crabs appear and move four places each turn. The players must collect logs of one colour, to be able to steer them across the bridges and to the other board (the beach). There, they must be guided into spawning pens, which the players build. But the pens might be damaged by storms.

To win the game, three pens must be built and collect three red crabs each. But if five red crabs are killed, on the road or in the sea, the players lose.

The game has had updated rules and suggestions from the publisher after it was released.
user summary

A game book set in the Austerion world.

Similar to a "Choose your own adventure" book, with added component, you can play either an agent of the gods or a cultist of the Titans, both adventuring in the Bleak vale region to investigate strange occurrences following an earthquake.

You choose the character, its build, take the decision across the adventure, including fighting alone or with your troups.

A card game variation of the classic cabo and golf games. You trade your cards and try to collect the not so horrible house pets. These provide you low points and that is what you overall want to achieve.
Interesting USP for this game is the fact that you initially do not know all of your four cards but only discover them during the course of the game.
great Fun!

boardgame: How Am I Weird: Square Edition
description:
How Am I Weird is a simple, fun party game to play with family and friends, in a group of 3 - 10. There are LIFE cards, COMEBACK cards and blank WEIRDO cards. LIFE cards present situations, sometimes awkward or embarrassing, sometimes annoying or familiar. COMEBACK cards present responses that are weird and wonderful.
