One person is nominated the Big Weird Chief, and the rest of the players each take 8 COMEBACK cards and one blank WEIRDO card. The Big Weird Chief reads a LIFE card. Each player chooses the weirdest and funniest COMEBACK response, or comes up with their own answer using their WEIRDO card! The person who gives the weirdest and funniest answer wins that round. At the end of the game, the player who has won the most rounds wins the game, and becomes ruler of the universe. Then you should WEAR THE CROWN!

—description from the publisher

The square edition contains 300 new cards and can be played either as a stand-alone game or as an addition to the standard version of How Am I Weird.

boardgame: クソポンチ絵選手権 (Kuso Ponchie Senshuken)
description:
クソポンチ絵選手権 (Kuso Ponchie Senshuken) is a party game from Japan. Players must put together a fake research proposal to win grant money, using only 'punch pictures' left from a slide show. The best presentation wins the game, based upon votes.

Players are researchers, invited to present their projects to the Minister of Okura to gain huge government grants. But a rival in the ministry has sabotaged the event, infiltrating the hotel where the presentations are to be made and destroying all the research material. All that's left are some punch pictures on a slide show, dramatic images used to give punch to the presentations.

Players are dealt a series of punch images and keywords, and must put together a presentation for the group. For example, dinosaurs, Moses parting the Red Sea, penguins and drones becomes a research project about extinction of eels. Somehow. Players make their pitch to the group and the one voted most liked is the winner.
user summary

This fun and original quizz will reveal everybody’s musical talent!

When a sound is played, be the first player to recognise the melody of one of the 54 famous classical music masterpieces, or one of the 44 instruments, and find the corresponding illustrated token!

Chopin, Mozart or even Bizet… This game pays tribute to all the great classical music composers who’s works of art have been used in movies, adds or even in pop music.

This is the perfect game to enhance your musical culture and your reflexes!

—description from the back of the box

Sway the Jury  is a card game about the courtroom, but not as it is supposed to be. The crime and the evidence don't matter, only how well the opposing lawyers can manipulate the jury.

Gameplay is relatively simple: the Accused is drawn from the top of the character deck and placed on the game table, then Jurors are drawn from the same deck and each player may reject or accept Jurors until 12 are selected and placed in three columns (Guilty, Undecided, and Not Guilty). After this, 5 cards are dealt to each player - these are their Witnesses. Each Witness has a "Bias" that either helps or hurts either player's case. Then, as each player plays their cards onto the table, the cards played "sway" jurors one way or another, convincing them to support either the defense or the prosecution. After all Witness cards have been played, the Jury deliberates and the player with the highest point score based on the Jury's biases wins.
A trivia game about the Polish language, but also about savoir-vivre, geography, biology and history. The gameplay simulates a school lesson. The biggest swot wins the game.

Hilarious Family-Friendly Card Game - How well do you know your family and friends? And how well do they know you? This game will test who knows you best! Take turns asking questions to find out who knows you best in this hilarious and heart-warming showdown!

This game is similar to Bingo. Children are trying to fill their player board with image tiles.

Every player gets one board and then one by one takes an image tile from the draw bag. This tile is then placed over the same image on the player board. The player who first fills their whole board becomes the winner.

Suitable for children aged 3 and over to practice spatial orientation, attention and vocabulary.

You are the spirit of a pond, and spring has come once again! On each of 20 days (turns), roll the two animal dice and two organ dice, then use any or all of the dice once each to take actions – placing animals and bestowing organs upon them, moving your focus from animal to animal, and using their organs to have them think, spot food, eat it, and mate!

Pack-A-Pond is a medium-weight puzzly roll-and-write game for one or more players. It involves polyomino placement, figuring out how to use most of your animals' organs, and more. All you need to play is 2 light dice and 2 dark dice, a pencil, the 8-page rules, and any one game sheet.

The game's playtesting and development is documented in: [WIP] Pack-A-Pond - 14th Roll and Write Game Design Contest - Contest Ready!.  It's sort of second in a series to the 2020 Worm & Cozy, which won 'Best Thematic Game' in the 4th Roll & Write Game Design Contest.

—description from the designer

マナとぴあ (manatopia) is a game of social manners from Japan. Players try to score positive points by following ever more complex rules, and avoid negative points from their errors being spotted.

In Japan, handing over and receiving business cards carries an important social etiquette. There is a particular way to hand the card over, and a way to take it and handle it that shows respect to the other person.

In this game, players take turns handing business cards out and taking them. But the social rules change for each card, based on the colour of the card and the type of business. Players must try to follow the complex rules and not get caught out.

At the end of the game, the players' net scores are totalled up and the player with the most positive total is the winner, being the most socially adept and well-mannered.
user summary

In High Rise you play the part of a construction company trying to win the contract to build City Hall. Each turn a player can use one of their six special action cards to place blocks, demolish buildings, collect rent, earn money, gain ownership of a building and ultimately win the contract to build City Hall.

—description from the publisher

Mosaic is about placing shapes to build mosaics. Each shape's style has two attributes: color and pattern. Players score points by matching the goal configurations in either one color or one pattern, or both. Completing a goal also allows the player to choose a shape to add to their own personal collection. At the end of the game, players will arrange their collected shapes into a mosaic of their own that will add to their score. The player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

Little Cities is a light strategy game in which players add settlers to their city in an attempt to attract villagers to live there. Players simultaneously draw settler tiles and place them in their city, choosing a column number that will activate their ability. Two dice are then rolled and all activated settlers attract villagers, which players add to their city. The first player to fill their city is the winner!

—description from the publisher

Players are attempting to build the best estate possible by meeting the requirements of lords and their servants. Begin by adding statues, trees and water features to your garden, then build upwards by adding rooms filled with paintings and fine furniture. Each card generates coins used to upgrade or add to your manor. When your manor is ready, score points by populating it with servants and lords. The first player to reach the target score wins.

—description from the publisher

ソロル (Soror) is a shedding card game from Japan. Players are trying to shed a card from their hand each turn until they are down to a single card. The value of their last card is their score, and the winner is the player with the highest score after a few rounds.

The cards are numbered and are either black or white. The player must play a card following the value lead. If they play a white card, it must be higher, if they play a black card it must be lower. So following a 4, a player can pace a white 5 or higher or a black 3 or lower. If the player cannot follow, they are out of the round with no score.
user summary

boardgame: ダンジョンクリエイター (Dungeon Creator)
description:
ダンジョンクリエイター (Dungeon Creator) is a co-operative game from Japan. Players work together to construct a dungeon with traps and treasures. Then they take turns to reveal the dungeon tile by tile. Their goal is to not discover their own treasure items until the very end, proving they are good dungeon creators.

Players are civil servants, learning the onerous task of making dungeons for adventurers. With traps and bombs, monsters and magic items, and treasure to be discovered, these civil servants must work hard to craft the best dungeons. By attending college at the Dungeon Academic Laboratory (DAL), aspiring dungeon creators who want to take the national magic civil service exam hone their skills.

In order to submit their summer assignments, the students collaborate with each other to create dungeons in order to find out how deeply they can hide treasures and how to confuse adventurers.

In the first phase, players get 20 tiles including tiles with traps, stairs and treasures in their own colours. Players take turns to build up the dungeon as a whole by placing tiles face down in a grid pattern.

In the second phase, each player takes turns turning up a tile to explore the dungeon. The player that can explore the most and be the last to turn up their own treasure is the winner, proving themselves best at design creation.
user summary

Universal miniature wargaming rules for sci-fi, fantasy, and historical settings.

JeMiWar was designed to create an wargaming experience that adds some elements of tabletop roleplaying games, with the players controlling multiple units instead of a single character.  Play is scenario driven, with a gamemaster running the opposing forces and the odds stacked in the players'  favor.  Units gain in experience after each battle, and  the loss of an experienced unit in an ongoing campaign stings more than a similar loss in a single isolated game.

JeMiWar is a simple system, with no charts and no mathematic modifiers used.  The intent is to appeal to both young players new to the hobby and to older players who have grown tired of complex time consuming rules.

—description from the publisher

Little fans of Mog will love the colourful, fun, and engaging games on this double-the-fun, double sided board game, featuring the beautiful illustrations from the bestselling children's classic books!  The perfect first board game specifically created for young pre-schoolers, aged 3 years and over.

Two different games both with quick set up and easy to play rules:  Dominoes – A great way to play basic dominoes using the brightly coloured board and pieces.  Board Game – Help Mog escape the Big Bad Dog!  Encourages the development of fine motor skills, hand-eye coordination, social development as well as aiding colour and object matching skills. One day Mog got a present. "Here you are, Mog," said Nicky "This is for you. It's called Bunny."  Mog loved playing with Bunny, until one day she lost him! Can you help Mog find her favourite toy 'Bunny' and race back home before the Big Bad Dog catches up with her?  For added fun, turn the board over to reveal the Matching Dominoes Game 'My First Dominoes'. A great way to teach children the basics of dominoes using beautiful illustrations and a colourful guide.

You’ve seen the stars, but have you ever wondered what is truly possible when we are out there in the black among alien suns?

The Five Parsecs From Home Compendium gives you all three expansions for Five Parsecs From Home collected into a single print volume for the first time, along with the fully updated standalone game Bug Hunt.

The new edition of Bug Hunt includes all the mechanics from the original, updated to fit into the core game rules. This includes features like alien spawn points, alien sub types like the spitter and screamer and alien leaders like the driver or lancer. With 6 sub-types, 6 leader types and 16 alien species to fight, there is a lot of scope for players who enjoy painting, building and converting cool miniatures.

Your troopers can be supported by any of 12 support units from the scientific survey team to a team of tough K’Erin assault troops.

Bug Hunt is a full campaign game with character progression as your troopers advance through their careers. You can even carry over characters from both games - take your crew members from Five Parsecs From Home on a Bug Hunt or vice versa, allowing your characters to experience a whole new level of adventures.

The only question that remains is whether you forgot to activate the security grid. They do say the vent crawlers are active in this sector...

The three expansions for Five Parsecs From Home allow you to experience a wide range of new adventures but also tailor the game to fit your vision of space beyond the Fringe, offering new characters, new mechanics, new options and new missions.

Trailblazer’s Toolkit offers a new introductory campaign, random name tables, new options to tailor the difficulty of the game, PVP and player co-op scenarios, new AI action tables, enemy deployment variables, escalating battles and the fearsome elite-level enemies.
Freelancer’s Handbook offers two new alien species: The Krag and the Skulkers, psionics, new equipment, non-miniatures combat rules. Expanded missions and quest rules, fringe world strife mechanics, expanded ship loan rules and the salvage job scenario type.
Fixer’s Guidebook offers terrain tables, additional rules for injuries both on and off the battlefield, a grid based movement system, faction mechanics and the stealth and street fight scenario types.

—description from the publisher

Off to the field: The potato harvest is coming up! Collect the most potato triplets and become the new KING KNOLLE. The principle is simple: For three potatoes of the same variety, you receive a potato sack. And whoever has the specified number of potato sacks in front of them first wins the game. But be careful: both the evil potato thief and the potato beetle can spoil your harvest at any time... KING KNOLLE - a quick and fun collecting and annoyance game for young and old.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Ab auf's Feld: Die Kartoffelernte steht an! Sammle die meisten Kartoffel-Drillinge und werde der neue KING KNOLLE. Das Prinzip ist einfach: Für drei Kartoffeln der gleichen Sorte erhältst du einen Kartoffelsack. Und wer zuerst die vorgegebene Anzahl an Kartoffelsäcken vor sich liegen hat, gewinnt das Spiel. Aber Vorsicht: Sowohl der böse Kartoffeldieb als auch der Kartoffelkäfer können dir jederzeit noch die Ernte versauen… KING KNOLLE – ein schnelles und lustiges Sammel- & Ärgerspiel für Jung und Alt.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Alter Schwede!: Woher wusstest du das?!
description:
In this game you have to answer 4 questions per round. For every right answer you get 1 point. The player with the most points is the winner.
—user summary

Alter Schwede! (~Gosh) is an exciting and entertaining game with more than 400 original and funny questions, where you could surprise and outplay the other players. Just ask questions, answer in turn, be amazed and have fun. Guaranteed without boring questions about dusty art era, physically expertise or forgotten monarchs.
What is the most popular hashtag of instagram? Do insects sleep? And may a goalkeeper in soccer execute a throw-in? It will not take long, before you could hear: “Gosh! How did you know that?
The Game is the perfect souvenir and real mood buster for parties, family celebrations or successful game nights.

–description from the publisher

boardgame: Las Ligas de la Ñ: Las Pequeñas Eñes
description:
Las Ligas de la Ñ: Las Pequeñas Eñes (The Ñ Leagues: The Tiny Eñes) is a board game for 2 to 8 players that includes classic characters of Spanish literature characterized as children. The time has come for the little Don Quijote, Sancho Panza, Dulcinea, Lazarillo, Fortunata and Jacinta and others!

In this new Ñ game (the sixth in the series), players have to control one of the playable characters in order to score more points than their opponents (after summing up the points for final position and for eliminated elements). Each turn, the players use their cards or movement to eliminate other players (occupying their squares, as in chess) and make other actions.

The game's main source of inspiration is the Super Smash Bros. video game series and it maintains important similarities with both the Unmatched board game series and battle royale style games. And, therefore, it is a game related to Las Ligas de la Ñ: El juego de las fuerzas primigenias y existenciales.

—description from the publisher

In 1939 Girl Scouts of the United States of America came out with a card game called Trupe. It consisted of 89 cards, and the suits were fashioned after the four levels of Girl Scouting at that time — Brownie, Girl Scout, Senior, and Mariner.

The goal is to get rid of all 13 cards in your hand, by laying face upward on the board, 2 or 4 matching members of the same group; 3 or 4 sequences of the same group; 3 or 4 of one honor; or a series of the same group.

The highest hand to strive for is a TRUPE

boardgame: Middara: Gameplay Card Pack – Vestalia / Lo-fi
description:
The Vestalia / Lo-Fi Gameplay Card Pack is for players that want the physical gameplay content inside these seasonal release resin kits, but don't need the miniatures.

This bundle includes:

Vestalia 2023 - Resin Kit A
    1 Box
    5 Art Cards
    5 Adventurer Cards
    9 Item Cards
    5 Initiative Cards

Vestalia 2023 - Resin Kit B
    1 Box
    5 Art Cards
    5 Adventurer Cards
    9 Item Cards
    5 Initiative Cards

Vestalia 2023 - Nyx
    1 Box
    1 Adventurer Card
    2 Item Cards
    1 Initiative Card

Lo-fi Nightingale
    1 Box
    1 Adventurer Card
    2 Item Cards
    1 Initiative Card

—description from the publisher

A team of rival journalists are traveling around the world to collect "dossiers" about Kurt Cobain's suicide. With a limited amount of cash, they have to visit L.A., Rome and Seattle to interview the witnesses and collect the dossiers.
When a journalist has a dossier for every place (they're 5 in total) s/he has to return to the center to publish the article and win the game.
The dossiers are less than the players, so many dirty tricks have to be used to hinder other's job. Every trick has a price, but the price can be shared among the players.

Ultimate Mafia is a game of social deduction, role-play, and storytelling. Set in a town where the Mafia are trying to kill all of the townspeople and the townspeople are trying to identify and root out the Mafia, the game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, the Mafia decide who to kill and during the day, everyone accuses others of being in the Mafia and are voted out.

The game ends either when all of the Mafia are identified and voted out, or when all the townspeople have been eliminated from the game, either from being killed or voted out.

In addition to the roles that the players receive, each player also is provided with random power cards, such as providing an extra vote during the day to vote out players, saving the player once from death, protecting a specified player from being voted out for one round, etc.

There is one game master who facilitates the game and the various powers that the players use without revealing information to the other players. The game master is also responsible for the storytelling, creating a narrative to reveal the actions of that each of the players performed in the past round.

Someplace where the wind blows over the fen, the copse becomes the thicket, and the thicket opens to the meadow...a great mouse empire fell. Deceit and skullduggery from the noble houses led to the Dark Ages. Now 100 mouse kingdoms fight to keep what is theirs, and take what isn’t. You rule one such kingdom and war is upon you!

Mice-at-Arms is a fast-play skirmish miniature game. Each army is composed of about 5 units (7-23 models total). There is minimal record keeping and the average game takes about an hour to play. The rules are designed to be simple and straightforward, but with levels of tactics that can be skillfully implemented to defeat your opponent.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
カルトゲバルト (Quartett Gewalt) is a fast fighting card game from Japan, using the rock-paper-scissors mechanism (well known as Janken in Japan).

In this game, each player starts with the same 8 cards, Soldat (attacker), Zauberer (magic user), Ritter (defence), and two life crystals. In rock-paper-scissors style, Soldat beats Zauberer, who beats Ritter, who beats Soldat.

One player starts by placing a card face-down, picking an opponent, who also places a card face-down. Both cards are revealed, the combat resolved and the winner picks up the loser's card. Play continues this way until all but one are eliminated.
--description by user

The publisher says:
In a world where magic rather than science was developed, the civil war in this country was also extremely intense due to the power of magic. This is the story of four people fighting on the front lines.

The goal is to reduce the opponent's physical strength by playing 4 cards one on one: attack, magic, defence, and life crystal. Let's win this battle with your smart strategy!

You start with a total of 8 cards, 2 each of attack, magic, defence, and life crystal cards. The first player lays down one card and designates the second player, and when the second player also lays down a card, they all open their cards and play! The winner receives a card from the loser and the game continues.

If you lose two life crystals, you will be eliminated. The one who survives is the winner!

科学ではなく魔法が発達した世界、
この国の内戦も魔法の力によって
熾烈を極めていた。
これは最前線で戦火を交える４人の物語。

攻撃、魔法、防御、ライフクリスタル、４枚のカードを１対１で出して勝負し、
相手の体力を減らすことが目的。
あなたの頭脳的な采配でこの戦線に勝利しましょう！

攻撃、魔法、防御、ライフクリスタルのカードを２枚ずつ計８枚を持った状態でスタート。
先攻プレイヤーは１枚伏せて後攻プレイヤーを指名、後攻プレイヤーも伏せると、
一斉にオープンして勝負！勝った人は負けた人からカードをもらいゲームが続行します。
ライフクリスタルを２枚失うと脱落。生き残った一人が勝者です！
--description by publisher

boardgame: 【うたってみた！】うたかい！【かいてみた！】 (Utattemita! kaitemita!)
description:
【うたってみた！】うたかい！【かいてみた！】 (Utattemita! kaitemita!) is a party game from Japan, with a players trying to interpret lyrics as drawings. The title translates as something like "I tried singing it!" "I tried drawing it!".

One player is the singer, and improvises a song based on the keywords they are dealt. The other players try to draw what they think the song is about. Then the artists name their work and explain its merits, and everyone votes on their favourite.  the most popular artist is the winner.

For struggling artists, the lyric cards also have simple drawings to help inspire creativity.
user summary

Roll and (re)write the 2019 UK general election in the tense electoral strategy game for British politicos. A truly competitive roll & write – NOT your usual multiplayer solitaire!

Pick from six political parties contesting the 2019 general election with the aim of winning a majority of the battleground constituencies to enter government. The three 'main' parties (Labour, Conservative, and Liberal Democrats) aim to win a majority or failing that become a coalition, while the three minor' parties (Scottish National, Green, and Brexit Parties) are intensely thematic asymmetric factions with their own win condition.

Each turn the leading player rolls two dice. All players write these numbers on their party sheets, selecting leaders, hammering home slogans, and completing and promoting policies. Completed sections of the party sheet allow players to claim voters in battleground constituencies, until one party wins a plurality of voters in each constituency. Winning a majority of constituencies means you enter government and win the game. Available in 2024 as a print at home game – approx. 1 A4 sheet per player, plus 2 pages of rules.  Grab a pair of dice and pencils, and the game is on!

—description from the publisher

In Crazy Cat Lady players must fill the void in their life and cross the threshold into becoming a Crazy Cat Lady! While trying to acquire cats players must balance multiple jobs, renovate their home, and deal with general cat maintenance. lord-chub.png The entire game is represented by a deck of only 18 double-sided poker-sized cards. Players draw cards using a set amount of points each turn, based on their current job. By acquiring and playing these cards you can put them in to play to apply for more jobs, gaining more points per turn - renovate your home to house more cats - and, of course, acquire more cats! But beware - based on that same hand of cards, at the end of every turn you draw a game-changing event card that adds cat complications to your life. Can you manipulate your hand to prepare for such events, while not losing the cards you need to win? Clever hand management and forward-thinking strategy are required to win!

The game uses double-sided card system that serves multiple purposes. On one side of every card are various events, but once these events occur the card is flipped, revealing a new cat, renovation, or job card, and is made available for the player to gain into their hand either instantly or for a price, depending on the scenario of the event. The order of the events change up the game, allowing it to feel like a new challenge each play-through!

Plays in 5-10 minutes. Play Solo or Co-operative 2-player!

—description from the publisher

Take your conquest of the stars to the next level with the Campaign Expansion for the Homeworld Fleet Command Board Game. Expand your fleet and take your battles in space to a whole new level with 100 new ships, new Unit cards, Upgrade your ships through the new 8 part Campaign plus new rules for Nebula, Planetary Facilities, Heroes, Cloaked ships and Barrage attacks.

—description from the publisher

The players (up to 4) need to find their way to the hidden temple and bring back the precious stones back to their bases. 
They will have to avoid the temple keepers, the dinosaurs and the lava from  the volcano.

The winner of the game is the player with at least one explorer alive and the most precious stones in his/her backpack. 
Every turn, a player can move one of his explorer (from 3 to 6 depending the number of players) 1,2 or 3 spaces on the board, then he draws an event card and follow instructions on the card (usually odd event that needs to react). 
Once the only card "volcano" is drawn, no more explorers can enter the valley.

You're running late for the Christmas family reunion, and you still haven't purchased their presents. Opting for clothing seems like the easy choice, but everyone's facing the same dilemma right now, so going shopping is chaotic!

Welcome to Gamarra, Peru's most bustling and expansive clothes market, where you'll find everything you need and encounter stereotypical characters from Lima's streets like 'el pituco' (the rich guy), 'el guachiman' (the security guard), or 'el choro' (the robber), among others, who will assist you with your shopping list.

In Gamarra, each turn involves buying an item, summoning a character, or discarding any number of cards to replenish your hand. The objective is to be the first player to purchase between 3 to 5 items, with the winning number depending on the total number of players.

Furthermore, each time a player makes a purchase, it triggers an event from the Events Deck. These events cause random occurrences that affect all players' hands or characters, such as 'Sismo de 8 grados' (Earthquake), 'Hora Punta' (Rush hour), or 'Fin de mes' (Payday), among others.

—description from the designer

Something has been a little bit off about Grandma lately. You’ve got the strangest feeling your Grandma might have been bitten by a Werewolf, but you’ll need to work hard to convince her to see the doctor.

In Grandma Theseus one player is Red Riding Hood who is choosing and revealing cards, looking for evidence of the Werewolf, and the other player is the Werewolf swapping cards secretly and trying to take over Grandma.

Red Riding Hood wins if they manage to discover Grandma's transformation, or if the Werewolf has not completely transformed Grandma by the end of the game.

The Werewolf wins if they manage to fully transform Grandma into a wolf without being detected, or if Red Riding Hood's relationship with her Grandma breaks down.
family: Contests: Button Shy Game Design Contests and Challenges
Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
A compilation of 4 expansions for The Royal Limited:


     The Royal Limited: Murder On Board
     The Royal Limited: Second Line
     The Royal Limited: Vintage Engines
     The Royal Limited: Conductors & VIPs

Ascendant mages from all across the known realms gather in a duel of wizardry. In their possession are the ancient Runestones of Fate, the key to victory. Harness their energy to unleash powerful spells and become a renown wizard of legends.

In Polyheduel, players will draft cards and dice in an attempt to cast the most powerful spells. When you choose your cards you will receive the corresponding polyhedral die. Carefully choose which die you'll be dueling with each round. But beware, each loss gives your opponent a chance to re-roll one of their dice, or if they're bold enough, one of yours!

Club Fight is a quick solitaire game played with a traditional deck of cards. Using the 13 cards of the club deck, you must clear all the remaining cards of the deck from a 3x3 grid, using six types of actions available to you. However, each of the other suits has the ability to block one of your action types, if cards of the same suit appear at least three times in the grid.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Killer Bunnies and the Quest for the Magic Carrot: Radioactive Robots Booster Deck
description:
While emitting the alpha and beta type, these brave bunny bots are worth the hype. The Killer Bunnies Radioactive Robots Booster Deck adds quite a bit (110111 cards) to your existing set plus 16 Radiation Markers, and 6 small cards!

It's the player's choice, if you decide, Robot bunnies protect those inside! In a single direction she will go, beware the devastation of pyroclastic Flo! A Neutronium Barrier destroys cards that roam, but they’ll be no groans after playing Bunny Of Thrones!

If Casting goes bad, then you’ll be glad with the appearance of a Hanging Chad. Whether you’re playing at home, or on your yacht, you’ll enjoy the protective powers of a bot! With a crushing grip and a microchip, this booster deck is quite a trip!

—description from the publisher

Expand your intergalactic might in the Homeworld Board Game with the Taiidan Fleet Box. Take the fight to your foes with 102 RED plastic spaceship models and an extra Command Card Deck and unit cards to support more players. Wage truly epic tabletop battles in Gearbox’s award-winning universe with an expanded arsenal of units and abilities.

Expand your intergalactic might in the Homeworld Board Game with the Kushan Fleet Box. Take the fight to your foes with over 102 BLUE plastic spaceship models and an extra Command Card Deck and unit cards to support more players.. Wage truly epic tabletop battles in Gearbox’s award-winning universe with an expanded arsenal of units and abilities.

Monopoly Edizione Brescia, this edition takes in the Italian city of Brescia in Lombardy.
The board shows the toponymy of Brescia: places such as Piazza della Loggia, Castello, Piazza Vittoria or Val Camonica.

Ranking is a party game for the whole family. Each player must order the 5 options creating their own Ranking, trying to guess the actual order. To do this, players use 5 cards that shares icons with options.

This self-playing sequel is based on geek culture - video games, anime and comics. It can also be mixed with the original game.

—description from the publisher

Ranking es un party game para toda la familia. Cada jugador deberá ordenar las 5 opciones creando su propio Ranking, tratando de acertar el orden real. Para ello, los jugadores usarán 5 cartas que comparten iconos con las opciones.

Esta secuela autojugable está basada en la cultura friki - videojuegos, anime y comics. También se puede mezclar con el juego original.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

A partnership trick-taking game designed for the video game Saltsea Chronicles.

Over the course of three hands, each team tries to collect the most points in their score pile. Gameplay is similar to other trick-takers with the following twists:

     Each card rank is worth a different amount of points, from 0 to 11.
     Hands start with a bidding round, where players each reveal a card, to share information with partner and determine what the Sweeper (trump) suit is.
     The winner of the trick gets to collect the other 3 cards, while the loser gets to collect the winning card.
     At the end of the hand, if a team collects 100 or more points, they get 0 points, and the opposing team gets a 100 points - hoarding is discouraged!


Each island community in Saltsea Chronicles has developed its own variant, which reflects their ideals.

boardgame: 幻想のジュエル (Gensou no Jewel)
description:
"幻想のジュエル (Gensou no Jewel)" is a party game for 2-4 players that can be played in 5-15 minutes.

The rules are simple: you win when you make a set collection with gemstone cards in your hands.

boardgame: Dutch Resistance: Orange Shall Overcome! – Extra Halt! Cards
description:
24 extra Halt! cards for the boardgame Dutch Resistance: Orange Shall Overcome!

The cards have the numbers 47 to 70.

The shore looks different than before. You used to have every crooked tree and worn pebble memorized, but as the nightmares attack things look... strange. Has your lighthouse changed? Is it all in your mind?

The Strange Shores expansion introduces 3 new Lighthouse cards, each with their own unique rules and strengths. Each Lighthouse replaces the one found in the base game, adding new twists to the core gameplay.

Weak sunlight barely filters through the low winter clouds. The nightmares seem to draw strength from the oppressive darkness, gathering in ever-larger hordes.

The Long Nights expansion adds 5 new Nightmare/Trap cards. These cards are shuffled into the standard deck and then 3 cards are removed, creating an unpredictable and more challenging experience for veteran players.

This special expansion features all 151 Pokémon originally discovered in the Kanto region as cards refreshed with gameplay mechanics from the Scarlet & Violet Series.

—description from the publisher

Take your five gnomes to the opposite side of the board, jumping over the mushrooms you meet on your way.
The game is played by either two or four gnome teams. Each team is composed by five gnomes and six mushrooms.
Place your mushrooms in order to allow your gnomes to jump, but be careful: your own mushrooms can lead your opponents to victory!

Porta i tuoi cinque gnomi sul lato opposto del tabellone saltando i funghi incotrati per via.
Il gico vede sfidarsi due o quattro squadre di gnomi, ciascuna composta di cinque gnomi e sei funghetti.
Piazza i tuoi funghetti così da far sa.tare i tuoi i gnomi, ma fai attenzione: i tuoi stessi funghetti possono portare i tuoi avversari alla vittoria!

Cor, it’s Linkee 4! The yell-out-the-link party game is back for its fourth outing. If you haven’t played Linkee before you’re in for a treat. All you have to do is answer four simple questions and be the first person to shout out the link between the answers.

Get it right and you win a letter. Collect enough letters to spell LINKEE and you’ve won! If you’re new to Linkee, welcome. If you’re here for more, welcome back!

Playing football on street or take your team on grass pitch and win Championship and Legendary League.
All that by team that you create, from players jersey and club logo.
Game contains solo play with player who start on street and won Street cup, then take your first club to win Championship and L.L.
Beware you can buy and sell players through transfer window, and even train them to gain better skill, but game could end for you if you End season in minus on the club account.
Game can be played in various ways 1on1 2on2 3on3 or 6 players in same time on 3 different pitch.
Including Tournaments for 4 or 6 players.

—description from the designer

The Sumo-beya are crumbling, and now factions have risen within World Insect Wrestling. With powerful insects and arachnids joining the league, new teams and tournament formats emerge. The Era of Swarms has begun.

In Kabuto Sumo: Swarm, you'll draft 3 Kabuto Sumo wrestlers for a multi-match swarm showdown. A different wrestler must enter the ring each match, but they can use the unique swarm moves provided by their ringside partners. Set up powerful combos and overwhelm your opponents!

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Venice / Tokyo
description:
Part of Alban Viard's Age of Steam Map Set 2023.

Venice: for 2 players. A Ghost player bids (once) and takes Locomotive or Urbanization if you let her! Use the canals to move goods, and don't ignore San Marco -- which could get up to 3 goods per Turn, or none!

Tokyo: for 3-4 players. The main feature of this map: citizens (cubes) move back and forth between cities each Turn!

The players try to get Beauty, the Beast, Maurice and Mrs Potts together to celebrate the wedding in the castle. But Gaston tries to prevent this. And if the last rose petal falls, everyone loses.

The players have to collect the cards that match their mission card. To do that, they trade cards with each other or pile of cards.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Beauty and the Beast

description:
You have ten of them at home...but does the "them" refer to pets, houseplants, or sex toys? Well, the other players can only guess...

In the party game Nichts als die Wahrheit ("Nothing But the Truth"), you ask very personal questions and are trying to judge how others will answer — but the questions are posed in such a way that no one has to expose themselves completely when answering them.

Oh dear! Pino Postino, the messenger falcon of the Forest Kingdom, has broken its wing. Not only is he fast and reliable, he is also often entrusted with secret missions for the royal couple. A replacement must be found quickly. Anyone who can move quickly and inconspicuously through the country is exactly the right animal here, so the stag, wild boar, rabbit, fox, and bear enter the competition: Who will be allowed to take on this important task until Pino is healthy again?

At the start of Pino Postino, everyone is given a secret animal identity. The five animal messengers are moved on their way through the various landscapes of the land by using cards. Who will behave nimbly and with foresight in the terrain? Who will be able to unmask the identities of the others and win extra points?

—description from the publisher

Wargo is a battle themed card game in which 2-4 players can compete for victory.

Players can build armies to attack their opponents and defences to defend those armies from incoming attacks.

When a player gathers enough complete armies he is the winner.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Combat Commander: Battle Pack #9 – Island Hopping
description:
Lack of supplies, disease, and skirmishes at night were common in the Pacific. Combating the tropical setting as well as an enemy determined to battle to the last man, fighting on the islands was completely different than in Europe. The thousand-yard stare was a common trait among the fighting troops in the Pacific due to the strain of these conditions. This Battle Pack represents the hardships facing the men on both sides of the fluctuating front line.

Focusing on situations where the US Army and Marines commonly battled the entrenched Japanese Imperial forces, this Battle Pack highlights engagements on Attu Island (where the first Banzai charge took place), Tarawa, and Guam (where the famous incident of Gen. Smith relieving Gen. Smith took place), among others. The Japanese were experts at night actions, so a number of them are included in this set to illustrate their deadly proficiency.

Four new maps are included which give us another airfield and a new invasion beach, as well as beaches on Tarawa and Wake Island. Ten to twelve new scenarios will include terrain new to the Pacific game (coral, sea wall, and trenches) and a new weapon (AMTRAK).

boardgame: GeoQuest Vol 1: An Interactive Quest Game for Family
description:
GeoQuest Vol. 1 is an Interactive Quest Game about World Nations. A fun and education focused family game suitable for all ages.
It's all about exploring the world.
With a whopping 200 countries across 6 continents, divided into 4 volumes. Each Volume comes with 100 Cards | 4 Card Sets | 50 Card Pairs.

GeoQuest encourages you to engage, discover, and match nations, fostering a fun and educational experience.

Surprise! Experience it through TWO UNIQUE GAME PLAY LAYOUT.

Why?

We wanted to innovate the classic memory game by adding interactive features that will make it more engaging and enjoyable with rich information. That's why we came up with the idea of O-BYE Quest Games, an interactive quest game that allows players to ask questions before matching the right card.

GeoQuest is among our dozens of games in the O-BYE Quest Games lineup.
All game cards follow color, shape, or number themes. In GeoQuest, each card is color-themed based on its respective country flag.

Unlike the traditional memory game where cards are facing down, our interactive quest game presents the cards facing up.
This way, players can already start familiarizing themselves with the information or images on the cards, and have a better chance of retaining them in their memory.

What's in the Game?

In each round of the game, you will need 50 cards to get started, with 25 cards for each player.

The best part? You determine game duration by opponent's cards for victory.

Each Card includes the following info: Country, Capital, Continent, Demonym, Language, Dialing Code, Flag, Map, Landmass, and Alpha 2/3 codes.

The game Layouts:
GeoQuest offers a versatile gaming experience with two distinct play layouts.

Layout 1: This is designed for compact spaces, eliminating the need for a playmat. This layout requires only the GAME CARD SETS to initiate your gaming experience.

Layout 2: This layout necessitates the use of the giant playmat for an immersive game play session. This layout enables you to seamlessly engage in all three playing modes using a single play mat.

—description from the designer

Planet ZAD is a cooperative strategy game on an alien world!
Asymmetrical alien tribes band together to stop an enemy polluting the planet.
Harvest and share resources to upgrade abilities while fending off an enemy determined to expand its toxic control of the planet. Can you save the planet?


     Easy to learn -- good for wide age range, from ages 10+
     Very interactive... trading resources and working to build strategies and attack together.
     NO downtime! Player phase is all simultaneous.
     Coop, 1 V All, Solo, and Semi Cooperative variants included
     1-6 Players!
     Many maps and new tribes on the way.


A bright light raced across the sky, ending in a thunderous crash that echoed across the land. Weeks later, rivers started to dry up, the air developed a rancid smell, and the tribes of Zad discovered strange aliens building monstrous structures. It became clear that these new visitors were harming the planet. Some Zad tribes tried to communicate and plead with the strangers to cease their destruction. Those tribes were annihilated. The remaining tribes of Zad set aside their differences and banded together to confront this new Enemy of Zad. And so, the war began.

—description from the publisher

The party game with few rules! Everything is possible here to explain or describe terms to your fellow players. Which player will guess the terms fastest? The aim of the game is to guess the terms described.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Das Partyspiel mit wenig Regeln! Hier ist fast Alles erlaubt um seinen Mitspielern Begriffe zu erklären oder zu umschreiben. Welcher Mitspieler errät die Begriffe am schnellsten? Ziel des Spieles ist es, die umschriebenen Begriffe zu erraten.

—description from the publisher

Faunatopia: Glacier Realm is a strategic turn based Set Collector Card game. A game that shows the magical side of the animal kingdom such as the mobs of penguins that mean business to the hypnotic gaze of adorable seal eyes. Prepare to step into the Glacier Realm as you battle your way to the Alpha status of this Faunatopia.

The goal is to collect the set of 4 unique Alpha Walrus Gear cards and use them to summon the Alpha Walrus. Then the game enters to final showdown which involves using the Alpha Walrus to battle each player 1 on 1. If the Alpha walrus wins all battles then the game ends and you are the champion otherwise you are eliminated from the game.

There are 4 phases per turn and each turn will provide cards to cause mischief, battle opponents, collect cards, and provide protection from other players.

—description from the designer

The Acts returns with the all new Letters from Rome expansion. Compatible with both the first edition and deluxe edition copies of the base game, Letters from Rome offers new strategies for adding believers as you can now travel all the way to Italia on your mission. Along with a sideboard depicting Italia as a seventh accessible region, there are epistles that can be delivered, tithes generated by churches, and more to take into account as you spread the word.

Letters from Rome remains a tight, worker placement game with multiple paths to victory. In addition to the actions from the base game, players can now gain coins as tithes from their churches, and collect epistles for both immediate rewards and set collection end game bonuses. Coins can be used to book passage to Rome, enlarge your churches, and to feed and care for the crowds while preaching. Beware, hording coins can lead to corruption, costing you believers over time.

Take on the added challenges as you spread from Judea throughout the Roman Empire to share the gospel message. Although true to the Bible, no Biblical knowledge is required to play and enjoy.

-description from designer

Flag Break is a tabletop game that combines the strategy of chess and the thrill of capture the flag.

The goal is to capture the other team's flag and return it to your side. If your player is caught on the opponent's territory, they go to jail and will need rescuing.

Like chess, Flag Break takes minutes to learn, but has infinite game play options.

—description from the designer

Rolling Tricks is a 4-Player trick-taking game designed for the Dice Deck from We Heart Games. Players will use the custom deck to determine trump for the round and play a must-follow trick-taking game with teams.

Teams help balance out the card RNG and card passing introduces from fun twists for passing to teammates and opponents.
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Rival Panels: Battle for the Throne
description:
The throne of Wakanda is on the line in the latest Rival Panels pack for Marvel: Crisis Protocol! New versions of T'Challa and Killmonger battle for the throne on a beautifully sculpted waterfall terrain piece that can be customized with 4 optional plant pieces. In addition to the miniatures for both characters, players will also find holographic foil stat cards outlining all of their abilities in this pack, along with 4 new Team Tactic cards that further augment their abilities.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Zellij is a pen and paper solitaire game that consists on drawing a path on a given grid of points, by trying to cover as much of the grid as possible, under the following limitations:

     The starting point must be random.
     From any point, a segment can be traced to only one adjacent point, either vertically, horizontally or diagonally.
     The path can never intersect itself.
     Every new segment can not be traced equally to the previous two. The trace must differ either in direction and/or orientation respect to the previous two (for example, a vertical segment traced from top to bottom is not equal to a vertical segment traced from bottom to top).
     Whenever no more legal moves are possible, the untouched points are counted. The smaller the number, the better the score.


For the grid, graph paper can be used, with the border of the grid being either a square or a squared rhombus. The size is arbitrary, though 7x7 is challenging enough to start. For own clarity, trace the border of the desired grid leaving a margin from it, so that all of the points of the desired grid are visible.

About the name: it's taken from the ancient zellij art style, to which the patterns that result from this game are remarkably similar.

—description from the designer

Are you on a run? Then savor it! Play as many cards as possible in FINITO!...ideally all of them at once, but if another player has an identical card in their hand, they can slow you down with a malicious "Finito!" and give you a bunch of penalty cards!

—description from the publisher

Playing on a famous horror movie trope, Final Girl is a solitaire-only game that puts the player in the shoes of a female protagonist who must kill the slasher if she wants to survive.

The S3 Ultimate Box includes ALL series 3 items:

Final Girl: The Killer from Tomorrow
Final Girl: Hell to Pay
Final Girl: The Falconwood Files
Final Girl: The Marrek Murders
Final Girl: Don't Make a Sound
Final Girl: Vehicle Pack S3
Final Girl: Terror From Destiny
Final Girl: Special FX Crate (includes Accessories Box, Guest Stars Promos Box, and Guest Stars Miniatures Box)
Final Girl: Miniatures Box Series 3
Final Girl: Lore Book Series 3
Final Girl: S3 Bonus Features Box
Final Girl: Fated Victims Miniatures Pack
Final Girl: S3 Cast and Crew Box 
Final Girl: S3 Gruesome Death Books

description:
Playing on a famous horror movie trope, Final Girl is a solitaire-only game that puts the player in the shoes of a female protagonist who must kill the slasher if she wants to survive.

Final Girl Core Box

Playing on a famous horror movie trope, Final Girl is a solitaire-only game that puts the player in the shoes of a female protagonist who must kill the slasher if she wants to survive.

The S2 Ultimate Box includes ALL series 2 items:

Final Girl: Box of Props
Final Girl: Into the Void
Final Girl: Panic at Station 2891
Final Girl: A Knock at the Door
Final Girl: Once Upon a Full Moon
Final Girl: Madness in the Dark

Final Girl: S2 Game Mat bundle

Final Girl: Zombies Miniatures Pack (Terror from the Grave Miniatures)

Final Girl: S2 Gruesome Death Books (digital only for S2 kickstarter)

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Pop Culture Bundle
description: The Pop Culture Pack comes with six different themed packs vaguely related to pop culture that you forgot to buy when they came out, plus ten brand new cards that we desperately crammed in at the last second. Includes the new Culture Wars Pack, 90s Nostalgia Pack, 2000s Nostalgia Pack, Scary Pack, Photo Card Pack 3, and Climate Catastrophe Pack. This is an expansion. Requires Cards Against Humanity core game.

Safe and Hound is a game of 18 cards full of clever combinations for scoring and play for 1 or 2 players. Players arrange the cards in the shape of a triangle and each turn will rescue one of the cards from the triangle which represents the mountain. Each card features a skier in one of five colors, one of six trees, and one of three tools. The unused cards each game are used for the scoring area which affects both players.

In this game of Alpine rescue dogs, players will have to cleverly select a card each turn that helps their chances of winning or keeps their opponent from scoring. Additionally, the turn order changes depending on the time value of the card selected. Sometimes a player will want the higher time value as it has a color skier or tool they need, but doing so opens them up to going second on the following round if their opponent takes a lower time value. As each card is rescued from the mountain, the selection of cards shrinks. Taking certain cards opens up other cards which may help an opponent or it may hinder their path. Each choice of card presents a path down the mountain.

But which of the mountain rescue dogs will be the winner? Will it be the rookie, Beethoven and his slobbery jaws or will it be Barry, the veteran who has lead the team over many winters?

Safe and Hound is played over 3 rounds and presents over 800 different scoring combinations! Players will find plenty of fun in this small card game that packs a lot of strategy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Duel – Lex: Prismatic Cage Preorder Promo
description:
This spell (foil version) was a promo for preordering Black Rose Wars: Duel - Lex. It can be used in every Black Rose War game.

Prismatic Cage
Forgotten Spell

In this round each time the target activate a Spell choose an effect:

     Inflict 3 damage on the target
     Convert 3 damage on the target
     Place 2 room damage in the room of the target
     Inflict 2 damage on another target in the same room as the target
     Place the target in your room and inflict 1 damage to the target

Hello, fellow gamers and monkey enthusiasts! Welcome to Mad Match-Up, a thrilling 2-6 player card game set in the heart of the jungle. Prepare for laughter, strategy, and the quest to become the ultimate champion of Monkey Madness!

OBJECTIVE:-
The goal is to get rid of all your cards...!
The player who skillfully gets rid of all their cards first wins the ultimate champion of Mad Match-Up.
Can you swing your way to victory in this wild jungle adventure..?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Toodles: It Takes Two to Doodle
description:
In Toodles, two people draw something at the same time with NO communication. Players rack up points as a team by choosing to draw an object from three distinct categories, Easy, Medium, and Hard. Once a category is selected, players select a card and roll the dice to find out what they need to draw. Without talking, players have 30 seconds to draw a picture together at the same time using their Toodle wands. After 30 seconds, the other players must guess what has been drawn before the 30 seconds is up!

Players win points for guessing the object correctly – 1 point for Easy Cards, 2 points for the Medium Cards and 3 points for the Hard Cards. If playing as one team, count your score after 10 rounds and see how well you have fared against the TOODLE-O-METER. If playing in two teams, the team with the most points after 10 rounds wins!

Be as naughty or nice as you want as you fill in the blank on a card. After that, take a turn guessing who wrote what, choosing a random card from the pile or picking one someone else already guessed wrong. Whoever guesses the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Remember to disguise your handwriting. The point of the family game is to be anonymous, making it difficult for the other players to guess who wrote what.

Whether at the office holiday party, or spending time with friends & family, this card game is guaranteed to bring up fond memories while creating new ones.

A trivia game where you have to find animals, characters or cities. Listen to the first clue. If you find the answer, you earn 15 points. On the second clue, you earn 10 points, on the third clue, you earn 5 points. First team to 100 points win.

Do you know how many of your friends have been to Africa, how many sleep in their nightgowns, and how many of them vacuum up spiders? Even if you don't know, you can guess.

In the party game Tipni si! Známe se? (Take a Guess! Do we know each other?) you'll learn the first and last thing about your friends - although you can never be sure who chose the answer. And if you're not shy, you can play an open game and bet directly on a particular player's answer!

There are 900 statements, divided into 6 categories: Travelling, Cooking, Dressing, Hobbies, Household and Pets. So you'll really learn a lot about your friends - and maybe even some surprising things.

—description from the publisher (translated)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This set will change up your game with a brand-new element: location cards! Discover new landscapes as you venture across the Inklands in search of lore that was scattered by the recent flood. These locations add a whole new dimension of gameplay and a fresh new spin to every deck.

One of the other thrilling aspects is the introduction of more beloved Disney properties, including DuckTales, TaleSpin, and Treasure Planet. You can send Disney's Kit Cloudkicker questing through the air on his airfoil, draw a solar sailing Disney's Jim Hawkins card into your hand, and more! There’s so much that awaits in the Inklands.

—description from the publisher

In Axé, you control a group of "Pais de Santo" (a type of Candomblé priest), trying to create the largest group of your color.
Each card has 4 quadrants with different colors of "Pais de Santo" in each. In your turn you must always place one card partially covering cards already in place on the table, trying to hide rival Pais de Santo and leaving yours visible.

Ross, the Boss is a pleasant not only children's game, in which you, as a cat, have to watch out for the adorable dog Ross! Ross faithfully guards his yard and is not very happy if you sneak around him. Can you get to safety before he spots you and scares you? The combination of a flashlight dog and cats flying through the air out of fear is sure to entertain younger players.

As the captain of a United Federation of SCUDs ship, you defend SCUD248 from attacks by Lawless ships.

You and your fellow UFS Captains have devised a friendly competition to capture the most Lawless ships.

Choose which Lawless ship you want to capture, roll the dice and see if you are successful.

—description from the publisher

In Rebuilding Chicago, a standalone successor to 2023's Rebuilding Seattle, you're responsible for managing the zoning and expansion of a major neighborhood following the "Great Chicago Fire" of 1871.

Each round, your population grows, then you can either build a new building, expand into a new suburb, activate an event, or build a landmark, after which you earn profit based on your neighborhood's commerce. You'll buy building types from a shared market — looking to find shapes that fit your grid and types that fit your strategy — and construct landmarks on the right tile combinations. Suburb tiles connect to your grid however you like, creating uniquely shaped neighborhoods. Triggering citywide events can change the tide of the game, offering points, money, and expansions for the players ready for it. You can even enact laws to give yourself the advantage!

You earn points for building types, upgrades, landmarks, events, and remaining cash, and at the end of the game, whoever's neighborhood has earned the most points wins.

Rebuilding Chicago also comes with a solo player deck so that you can compete to build the best version of Chicago even with just one player. Enjoy gameplay against a deck designed to simulate the actions of a second player to discover strategies and configurations you can use to improve Chicago.

You and other knights scheme to be the best knight by selling homes to the locals in the area. Show them different beautiful properties that they might like or modify the property to fit what they want. Be careful though! The other knights can go behind your back and modify your property, talk about you to the client, or even outright sell to the client from underneath you.
In this game, you and your friend(s) will draw client cards and utilize different actions cards and your character's abilities to modify the property cards you have to make or stop the sale of a property to prove you are the best knight in the kingdom.
This game comes with 3 sets of cards and trackers. The main deck will contain action and property cards. The client deck will have your clients that you sell to. Lastly you will have your character cards, or knights that you will play as.
publishers: Coalition of Gamers Geeks and Nerds

description:
Sludge encourages players to build grim fantasy armies using various historical miniatures. The rules represent a black powder age with a fantastical emphasis on powerful knights and dark magic. Battle hardened line infantry will form the backbone of an army. These core troops are supported by noble knights and strange magicians.

Sludge is an invitation to collect and convert an army from a dark ahistorical setting, and a delightfully dark battle game with a brutal and distinct play style.

This is a stand-alone game originally released as part of BLASTER: Games Anthology 03 with clarified rules and expanded with faction-specific units.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
It’s the year 1865 and the American Frontier is full of opportunity, but you’re not the only one hoping to stake their claim. Will you farm the land and earn an honest wage? Form a gang of ruthless outlaws? Bet everything on a gold rush? Gamble your fortune? Become the fastest gun in the west? It will take a solid strategy, nerves of steel, and a little luck to survive.

No matter how you choose to earn your money - the objective is the same: Build all four of your buildings and hire the Sheriff to win.

—description from the designer

Will your reluctant donations create a charity disparity? Set ambitious goals, but be careful - if you can't raise the funds, you'll get nothing done!

—description from the book, Random Fun Generator 2

The first player will set X dice (based on player count) to create any total goal they wish to try to reach.  Then all players roll their dice to see what their totals are (there is subtraction involved).  If the total of all the dice (including the subtracted dice) is equal to or greater than the total goal, the first player scores points equal to the goal.  The next player sets their goal and repeats the process.  First player to score 30 points wins.

boardgame: Caper: Europe – Dice Tower 2023 Promo Cards
description:
Originally available as part of the Druid Pack from the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

The Critic is a substitute for The Mime, and is based on Zee Garcia from The Dice Tower.

The two Blaze Suit Shields are substitutes for two Eaves Droppers.

These cards do different things than the cards they replace (ie: they are not alternate artwork cards).
A trivia game about the history of Venice. Can be played as a card game or a board game. The trivia is in six categories: Gran Turismo, Acqua Alta, Moto Ondoso, Gondola, Vogalonga, and Storia

Use the original draft format to acquire cards of spirits consisting of 4 attributes and build a 24-card deck.!

Battle by combining spirit cards! The player who captures 5 of the opponent's cards wins.!

There are 60 spirit cards in total! 32 types of newly drawn illustrations! Tarot card size of 120 x 70 mm!

This is a product distributed at "Game Market 2023 Spring"。

In this party game, players will take turns acting as monsters from the Monster Prom video game franchise. Each round one player will act as the "Bachelor" (or "Bachelorette" or "Bacheloraire") and will judge and score all the other players, who will be the "Romantic Interests."

The Bachelor places a Monster Card of their choosing face up. They can then introduce themselves, including an explanation of how they will judge the Romantic Interests. The Romantic Interests each choose a Monster Card and place it face down, these will be a secret until the end of the round. The Bachelor draws a Question Card and reads the first question. Each Romantic Interest then choose an Answer Card and place it face down. One by one, the Romantic Interests will reveal their answers and can elaborate a bit to try and woo the Bachelor.

The Bachelor chooses their favorite answer and the Romantic Interest who gave the winning answer scores one point. This process is repeated with the 3 questions on the question card. The round is won by whoever scored the most points. If there's a tie, the Bachelor decides who deserves the win based on their overall answers. The winner then reveals their Monster Card. The Monster Cards from the winning Romantic Interest and the Bachelor are placed together and each player scores an extra point.

The Bachelor role is passed to the next player and a new round begins. Play as many rounds as it takes for everyone to have been the Bachelor at least once. Whoever won the most points overall wins!

Welcome to your first day at Doomscroll, rookie! We're an up-and-coming social media company set in a fantasy, steampunk world, and your job is to curate a feed of posts addicting enough to sell ads. The first advertising imp to sell three ads wins!

To play Doomscroll, play various Post cards to your feed to generate enough interest in one of the four topics - politics, entertainment, hobbies, and sports. Once you've got the feed full of juicy content, drop an Ad card with the same topic in there to make the sale to the user!

In addition to playing Post and Ad cards, players can use their AI (Advertising Imp) cards to either compromise their opponents' feeds or improve their own. Players can also recruit Influencers to their feeds, bringing them game-long bonuses that make it easier to sell Ads or abilities that help them boost their feeds' addicting nature.

Alright, rookie, time to shine. Let's see if you're cut out for Doomscroll!

—description from the publisher

Private eye, gumshoe, shamus you’ve heard all the names. Don’t care for any of ‘em. When you’ve got a case, you’re more like a dog with a bone. You pull up your collar and adjust your hat. The pelting rain seeps in anyway. Of all the nights to get that call. This case’ll take some finesse. Too bad you haven't got much of that. Maybe Lady Luck will be on your side  and if not, there’s always your trusty .38. Sometimes, you’ve got to make your own luck.

Stakeout is a competitive noir-themed game of dice placement and manipulation. In Stakeout, you step into the shadowy world of a 1930s private detective, vying to complete investigations and close cases.  Roll and spend dice to investigate leads, wiretap or go on a stakeout. Hot Tip cards give you dice manipulation powers you can use to gain an edge over your opponents. Careful timing can trigger powerful combos that can mean the difference between shaking down a suspect and going home empty handed.

Players collect claimed Investigation cards to match cases they are investigating. The game ends when a player has closed 3 cases.

There are people looking forward to receiving souvenirs from your trip...
For those people, it's great to talk about memories, but I also want to bring something home that will make them happy!
Pack as many souvenirs as you can into your travel bag and take them home without overflowing!

What is the best thing about rainy weather? The big, muddy puddles, of course! Each player bounces their character in as many spaces in the game box as possible, and leaves behind muddy footprints. The first player to leave their footprints around the whole town wins the game. Practicing your motor skills is fun with Peppa Pig!

—description from the publisher

This is the 4th expansion of Escape Room: the Game.

The difficulty is expected to be higher than the previous expansions. One of the scenario will even require players to separate in team to solve the puzzle.

The 4th expansion include:
- Alice in Wonderland
- Another Dimension
- Titanic

These escape rooms were all published in other languages as Escape Room: The Game 2.
family: Series: Escape Room – The Game (Identity Games International B.V.)

description:
The secret organization Eye in the Sky has ordered you to retrieve the lost treasure of the Temple from an old notebook belonging to Georges de Laprée.
To prevent the holy relics from falling into evil hands, you decide to start in Jerusalem.
However, this is not an "entrance exam" in the evil elevator, this is an organizational mission.
Show the organization that you're smart once again!

It is a game where the objective is to collect all the jewels before your opponent.

In order to win, you must be careful not to reveal the jewels you want to collect, and deduce the jewels your opponent wants to collect, and stop them from collecting them.

Easy and gorgeous with cute frames! Amidakuji is a game that everyone knows, can be played in a small space, and the rules are simple.

If you play the road tiles in order and collect a certain number of chicks, the Amidakuji game begins. The chicks on your path will follow you, thinking you are their parent. Guide the path well and collect as many chicks as possible!

If you're not careful, the chicks you collect will fall into holes, so it's a simple party game where you have to shape the road into a convenient shape.

A cheerfully fast-paced game for little Moomin fans.

In the game, each player has their own pretty stones and Moomin character tiles, and character cards drawn from the deck always show each player who they need to find, i.e. catch. The timer ticks in the background, and when it rings, the hippy has to give up one of his pretty stones. The game is won by the player with the last pretty stones left.

—user summary

Iloisen vauhdikas peli pienille Muumi-faneille. Pelissä leikitään hippaa Muumien kanssa ajastimen määrätessä tahdin. Voittaja on se, jolla on viimeisenä hienoja kiviä jäljellä. Peliä voi pelata samanaikaisesti 2-4 pelaajaa. Peli kestää n. 15 min ja se sopii yli 4-vuotiaille. Vauhdikkaisiin pelihetkiin!

Leiki hippaa Muumien kanssa! Uusi hauska peli vaatii nopeutta ja havainnointikykyä – ja hyvää onnea.

Pelissä jokaisella pelaajalla on omat hienot kivet sekä Muumihahmolaatat, ja pakasta nostettavat hahmokortit osoittavat aina kullekin hipalle kenet hänen täytyy löytää eli saada kiinni. Ajastin raksuttaa taustalla, ja sen soidessa hippana oleva joutuu luopumaan yhdestä hienosta kivestään. pelin voittaa se pelaaja, jolla on viimeisenä hienoja kiviä jäljellä.

Pelin väritys ja kokonaisilme on aurinkoisen ihana, ja hahmolaattojen sekä -korttien kuvat esittävät Muumihahmoja suloisimmillaan. Pelin hullunkurinen ajastin viheltelee milloin sattuu, ja se tuo lisää yllätyksellisyyttä peliin.

Hersyvän hauskoihin hetkiin – HIPPA!

—description from the publisher (Finnish)

Barrinium Dawn

A competitive RTS-inspired strategy board game where players build bases, manage resources, and command armies to outsmart their opponent. Plan carefully, predict enemy moves, and make every decision count on a hidden battlefield.

Key Features:
-Fully playable 2-player strategy game per box
-Expandable system: combine multiple boxes for more players and/or larger armies
-RTS-inspired gameplay: base building, resource management, and army control
-Hidden production system enabling bluffing and mind games, while remaining fully transparent and cheat-proof
-Wide variety of units, buildings, and weapons for deep tactical gameplay
-Modular board for high replayability and evolving strategies
-Combines long-term strategy, tactical combat, and economic progression
-Easy to learn, hard to master
-Language-independent gameplay using intuitive icons & numbers
-English rulebook included; additional languages planned via the website

The Gods & Myths expansion revamps the base game by introducing four key new elements to the game!


     Mythical Creatures: The Mythical Creatures have a powerful set bonus you can achieve when collecting all three copies and banking them. This bonus will stay active for you for the rest of the game!
     Godly (Prolonged) Abilities: This expansion introduces a new type of ability card - the Prolonged Abilities.  When used, these Abilities have a prolonged effect, granting you an ongoing bonus. But stay vigilant, as your opponents can deactivate or even steal them!
     Godly Heroes: When playing with the expansion, each player will get not one but two hero cards - one being a Godly Hero. Each Godly Hero has a unique and powerful ability that can alter the game in your favor!
     A New Phase: Gods & Myths introduces a new Phase - the Dawn Phase. But in contrast to Day, Night, and Dream Phases - the Dawn Phase is triggered at random. In this Phase, Players have the opportunity to end the game early. Or to get rid of cards they don't need. It is the balance of risk-reward gamble!


And the best thing about the Gods & Myths expansion is that all four components are interchangeable. Meaning - do you like everything - excellent, use all the cards! Or maybe you don't like the Dawn Phase? - no problem! Just remove the Dawn Phase cards, and it won't affect the other components!

—description from the publisher

A unique combination of a detective board game, escape room and film. The game is intended for a group of 2 to 6 people, but it is also possible to play alone.

In the game, we play the role of secret agents and have to stop dangerous members of the Black Fox organization, whose operations threaten global security.

In the game, we have access to the materials included in the box, as well as to the online panel. Our goal is to combine components from the box and clues from recordings or information found on the Internet in order to solve subsequent puzzles.

In this solo / two-player-cooperative expansion for Warline: Maneuver Strategy & Tactics, command your army in an against-all-odds struggle versus supermassive Lesser Dragons. Dragons War features the core game's "Strategicraft" setup-is-gameplay systems and rich battlefield mechanics — and adds a quick, easy to operate Lesser Dragon "automa". As you command your army successfully, the Lesser Dragon's life is depleted, but the dragon's power grows. Gameplay is tense, strategic, tactical, and brain burning.

Also included in Dragons War are two new kingdoms (factions): the Kingdom of Blud ("Blood") and the Kingdom of Goyld ("Gold"). These kingdoms expand the mastery ceiling of Warline with their own new asymmetric abilities and play styles. The Blud kingdom sacrifices its own units to build tremendous power. The Goyld kingdom exerts its infinite wealth to hire on an endless swarm of unscrupulous mercenary troops. Blud and Goyld can be played in both the Dragons War expansion and the base game.

—description from the publisher

Scare B&b is a microgame played by two to four players. It is a simple game based on bluffing, take-that, and memory mechanisms. In this game, the players set up each other's B&Bs and then try to get all their guests out first. However, if any player finds the exit before finding the key, the building rearranges itself.

This game is designed for ages five and above and is a fast, light game that everyone in the family can enjoy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Activity: Das gibt's ja gar nicht
description:
This is a party game from Piatnik played in teams with at least 2 players each.

The teams draw cards and have to explain up to three phrases. A phrase could be something as "sparkling water melons" which most of the time cannot be found in a dictionary whereas the pair of first two words and the last pair could. This is specifically the case in languages such as German which combines single words to much more lengthy words.

Depending on the category of field indicated beside each phrase, one player has to act (pantomime) or to describe or to paint the phrase given on a card. The team scores depending on the difficulty of the phrases. The team which gets 25 scores at first wins.

Trivial-pursuit like game where you manage to reach the center of the galaxy answering question on 6 different science topic:

FUTURE
NEW TECHNOLOGIES
ENERGY SOURCE
BEYOND SPACE
KNOW YOUR PLANET
LIFE ON EARTH

A trick taking card game with a bicycle racing flavor. The object is to play several Grand Tours, where each hand represents a stage, and to combine the scores to win the overall classification for the series.

You've turned into a cat! Steal, lie ... anything goes. Battle for food! Will you survive?

In response to the bait (score) that comes out each time, put one card from your hand face down onto the field.

At that time, you declare the contents of the card, but
you can lie only 5 times during the game. Once everyone has played their cards, they are flipped face-up and the player with the highest number wins the food.　

You win if you get more bait (total points) than anyone else.
designers: おーつぼじゅうじん (Jyujin Ohtsubo)
publishers: Joynt Game Factory (ジョイントゲームファクトリー)

description:
The King has departed on a voyage across the great sea, entrusting you, his esteemed stewards, with governing
the realm during his absence. Travel the kingdom, hiring workers in service of the King, and turn the wealth
you’ve been given into even greater riches.

Parable Kingdom is a resource management game where you are traveling the kingdom, gaining specialized workers that you can spend at various locations. The more workers you can spend on a turn with the correct specialty required for that location, the more powerful your actions will be.

Throughout the game you will be building up your workforce, wheat production, and reputation. As well as focusing on building projects that will offer cool end game bonuses, fishing expeditions where you will attempt to collect sets of different types of fish, and so much more.

There are many paths to victory, and also various overlays that you can use to change up the game each time you play.

—description from the publisher

New paths lead to forgotten lands, and with that comes a host of exciting possibilities!

Explorers or Navoria: Forgotten Lands brings new content that changes how the game plays. Players will now have asymmetrical powers, and new starting equipment. There is also a new worker location and a brand new deck of cards.

Bonuses at the end of the explorer tracks are now replaced with variable scoring objectives, and there are additional faction bonuses for reaching the end of the explorer tracks first. Additionally, each round will have a unique scoring objective. Will you play the short game and aim for the current objective, or plan ahead and try to score big later in the game?

Explorers of Navoria also comes with all the components needed to play the game with five players. Plus, it now has a solo campaign, that has three difficulty levels and variable side missions, making each solo game highly repayable.

—description from the publisher

Wankul is the first trading card series based on the Wankil universe.

Wankul is a trading card game that doesn't just let you collect cards, it also lets you battle it out in fast-paced duels where combos are the main feature. Face off against your opponents using a deck of cards inspired by the best of the creators Laink and Terracid.
