On your turn, you can move five spaces divided amongst your pack of rats however you like. You'll also get two movement abilities to help your pack along their way. Combine the abilities together for an even stronger effect. Be aware, whichever ability you chose on your turn, your opponent will get it on their next turn, so choose carefully.

Build the bridge as you race your rats across it. Get your entire pack of rats to the end first and enjoy the leftover spoils.

Stack your bells to build the most impressive towers, but you will only score big by following the logic cards on the table and finding efficient stacking combinations. Lay four cards on the table and try to stack your bells according to the rules on at least three of the cards. Play quickly but carefully as mistakes will benefit your opponent!

The variable difficulty levels make the game great for the whole family or players of different ages.

Auto-Dungeon Enter the corrupted land of Abros, a once bountiful and glorious world of sword and sorcery, where a gateway has been opened to otherworldly dimensions by an evil Sorcerer, Morden the Terrible. Formerly ruled by a just and righteous king, Morden overthrew the crown, and the realm was thrust into chaos and disarray. Many proud and powerful warriors attempted to end Morden's wraith, but all failed. You brave few adventurers are what is left to defeat his minions, seal the portal and restore Abros to its former glory. Do you have what it takes to end Morden's reign of terror?

Auto-Dungeon is a cooperative game made for 1-6 players, where the goal is to navigate the dungeon and defeat the boss monster. If at any point all heroes have 0 health, the game is over, and you have lost.

To play, the only just roll the dice and choose what happens. That's the core of the game. There are status effects to deal with, treasure cards which can be a boon or detriment to your party, and hero ability cards to aid your party at dire moments!

Players roll for the monsters and choose what they do to your hero. Will you choose to take damage, or become poisoned?

Then, roll for your hero and choose what skills they use against the monsters. Does your Cleric decide to fell the Skeleton, or heal a downed party member?.

When monsters are defeated, rewards are given out in the form of “treasure cards”. Mostly helpful in your quest, you could receive aids that strengthen your attacks, heal allies, poison monsters, and more. But beware, there are a small number of trap cards lurking in the chests!

Heroes also have 1 special ability card that can be used to turn the tide of battle. 
In a moment of crisis, the Knight may decide to take all damage from a large attack on the Ranger, saving them from certain doom! Enraged with anger from a massive strike, the Warrior can launch a powerful counterattack to an enemy. Discover all of the Hero Abilities!

Players enjoy a small story to start off their quest involving “Morden the Terrible”, an evil Sorcerer who's corrupted the once majestic kingdom of Abros. Every dungeon offers three continuing “Story Cards” per level to guide you through the narrative.

As you continue to advance, each monster also has an interesting introduction to engage the imagination.

In between each round of monsters, players read the next story card, and get a moment of respite to either heal or gain more treasure.

—description from the publisher

Desi Feud is an easy to learn, fun to play game-show style party game that is equally fun for all age groups. Below are the 5 quick steps to learn, master, and play this game

[Premise] We surveyed hundreds of desi people and asked them interesting questions. We aggregated their responses and created 125 interesting question cards with top 6 answers.

[Objective] Your goal is to guess answers that match the desi janta's (desi population) top 6 answers for a given question. There is no factually correct answer here, all that matters is "Can you think like an Aam Aadmi ?"

[Setup] Select one person to be the host, just like a gameshow. Divide the remaining players in 2 teams

[Gameplay] Host shuffles the cards, picks one and asks the question on the card to the teams. The two teams take alternate turns to guess a popular answer. Each incorrect answer gets that team a strikeout card. The first team to get 3 strikeouts loses the round.

[Winning] First team to win 5 rounds, wins the game!

—description from the publisher

You and your partner have mastered moving in together. You’re totally fulfilled! But wait... Now you have to move out!? How will your relationship hold up when the moving van runs out of room for all your precious curios?! When your smart car can’t carry your boxes, your partner will surely pack them for you... right?? Onto the next adventure - Decorum: Movin’ Out. Let’s get movin’!

Play through 20 unique scenarios, as you wrestle with the best ways to persuade… erm, agree... on which vehicle to pack your lamps and antiques in.

The Movin’ Out expansion introduces new challenges, including moving boxes, bubble wrap, and vehicles!

Explore how best to wrap your breakables, what goes on top of the car instead of in it, and of course, what stays in the house while you move out!

-description from publisher

A push-your luck game that combines dice rolling with pattern recognition.

Players are part of the rhythmic gymnast's team and are preparing for the Olympics. The aim? Be elected team captain to guide your team as the world tunes in. Give it your best and balance your ambitions with personal drama to lead your team and be the best.

In this game you use the pattern recognition element from Botanists (released 2019) but combine it with dice rolling push your luck elements. Your ability to manipulate your dice-rolls will be linked to your apparatus: hoop, ball, clubs, and rope.

Your personal dramas are related to family pride, personal ambition for success, your impulse to be famous, or with your love interest. However, your coach will be putting pressure from a different angle also. With hearts ablaze: balance your acts, train your routines, command admiration, and be the best!

boardgame: Clash of Empires: The Biblical Conquest Game
description:
By using innovative game mechanics and a quick battle system Clash of Empires enables the player to make extensive use of tactics and strategies to out-wit and out-manoeuvre rival powers and thereby control the destiny of an ancient empire. The objective of the game is for 2 or more players to compete against each other using guile and strength of arms to increase their wealth and prestige and ultimately gain control of the Biblical World. They can do this by raising armies and fleets of ships, advancing their economies, and conquering new territories, while at the same time having to react to various favourable or unfavourable events. Clash of Empires is designed as a stand-alone strategy game but can also be used as a campaign setting for wargames set during the Biblical period.

For 2-5 players 12 to Adult. (Can also be played solo)

Contents

A1 sized Quad-Fold Game Board showing a map of the Biblical World.

96 plastic counters representing 8 armies in different colours.

50 blank poker chips in various colours to denote number of army/navy units.

2x4 sided dice, 6x6 sided dice, 2x8 sided dice and 2x10 sided dice.

Pack of Egyptian themed Playing Cards by the Green Board Games Company. Clash of Empires uses a playing card based economic and random event generating system.

Rule Book.

Everything you need to play Clash of Empires is included with this set except a pen and paper for working out troop points.

Background to the Biblical World

In the Near East, during the last three centuries of the Bronze Age, three great civilizations arise. These are the imperialistic Hittites of Hatti to the north, Egypt, a military state and land of the pharaohs, to the south and the expanding state of Assyria, a dynamic and ruthless military power, in the east. They all share a common desire to control the Levant to dominate and exploit the economic resources and trade of the region. In addition to contending for control of this vitally strategic region these chariot-using warrior societies struggle to suppress the numerous nomadic tribes and bands of Sea Peoples marauders who pose a threat to law and order. Meanwhile, in the Aegean the warlike Mycenaeans have recently taken over the powerful maritime state of Crete and now look set to launch a major military expedition against western Anatolia (the Trojan War). Thus, the scene is set for an epic Clash of Empires.

Horror Season is a last man standing Card Game with a twist.  Change your role from a Human to one of the Monster roles to get a special ability in the game. Those who lose their hearts may not be able to win, but are still part of the game. Eliminated players are buried in the graveyard and become Ghosts, continue playing to the very end, and there to cause havoc in the game. Ghosts don't get to have a hand, they draw one card and discard all cards in their hand at the end of their turn.

Welcome back! Your cafe is expanding, and drinks are no longer enough. Thankfully, you can now serve cakes to keep your customers happy. Hire staff, and use their skills to survive the rush!

The Coffee Rush: Piece of Cake expansion has players fulfill new orders with the help of cake cards. It adds new player boards, new order cards, beautiful 3D cakes, and new upgrades.

-description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Bolton Heroes 1
description:
The cruelty and sadism exhibited by House Bolton is not only reserved for their enemies. The leaders of their troops can be just as harsh to their own subordinates as they are to captured soldiers. Steelshanks Walton, Skinner, and Sour Alyn all have their own special forms of cruelty they inflict on others.

Ben Bones, Tybald, and Damon Dance-for-me perform their atrocities with sadistic glee. However, even they must cautious, for if they ever gain the ire of those above them, they would not be safe from having their own brutal technique unleashed upon themselves. Life in House Bolton is always on a knife edge, sometimes literally so.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: Forward Defense '85: Company Command in WW3
description:
Forward Defense '85 is a solitaire tactical tabletop wargame where the player leads a company-sized NATO element in a hypothetical, realistic WW3. Forward Defense '85 simulates the fast, ultra high-intensity battles that would have been expected in the opening phase of a Cold War gone hot. Game mechanics are built to reflect the AI using actual Soviet doctrine versus a more nimble, but dramatically outnumbered, NATO player.

The experience of your units, terrain, your objectives, and the balance of the overall campaign affect each and every procedurally-generated battle in the campaign. Every decision matters and requires you to value your resources: Is it worth holding that town and securing at tactical victory? Or is it wiser to withdraw your best armor platoon to fight another day?

How the game works
The conditions for every battle are generated based on tables on the campaign sheet - these are influenced by momentum (winning/losing battles in the campaign) and attrition on either side. Losing units makes it more difficult for the player to get reinforcements, while Soviet unit quality will degrade as second-tier units are brought forward to relieve their battered forces. Based on performance, the player gets supply dice prior to each battle - these can be used to call in replacements, get air support, or attach additional platoons from friendly forces. Loses can often be replaced, but the replacements will lack the experience your surviving units build through surviving battles. Unit quality (experience/training/morale) is a key factor providing bonuses in several different ways.

Battles can place the player on the attack or in a defensive position, aiming to overcome numerically superior Soviet forces. The Soviets will use their mass to attempt to break through your lines, while the player must maneuver carefully to use (generally) superior units and clever tactics to win. Units operate at the platoon level, with losses tracked by each individual vehicle. Combat is D20-based, with attacks rolling to hit, then rolling to destroy, enemy vehicles. Dismounted infantry, following doctrine of the time, operate with their transport vehicles and provide bonuses to them while not suppressed.

The Shady & Cutthroat Side of Beach Umbrella Rental.

It's your last summer before university, and you spend the sunny days working in the Umbrella Rental Hut at the local beach. One day, you boss announces a contest for you and your coworkers: "Whoever rents out the most umbrellas, with me as the judge," she adds, "wins some big bonuses!" You're the best umbrella rental agent on this entire coast, and it's going to be a big payday for sure!

Shady Business is a 1-4 player game where players bid to cordon-off sections of the beach, and while they do they can also sneak an umbrella of their color into that section too!

Tiles show cordoned-off ropes to mark different sections of beach. When a section is completely closed, the boss will judge the section and give bonuses (points) to the player, or players, that have the most umbrellas in that section.

However she also gives a bonus to the player who closes the section, based on the number of unique beachgoers in there; the more unique, the more money earned!

The tiles that you place also are used for bidding. Each has a number going from 1-15. The highest number bidder goes first, placing their tiles and adding an umbrella. The beach begins to fill with umbrellas, and you must follow the rule: You cannot place an umbrella in a section already with at least one umbrella... but if you can set it to the side, and then sneakily connect it to an already existing section... NO, that would be SHADY!

Or wait... do you know what would be even shadier?! If you bid the highest, you instead can move one of your umbrellas into a section, even if there are umbrellas there. OR HOW ABOUT... if you bid the lowest, you can decide to put up to two umbrellas on your tile.

Score as many bonuses before the day is out, then you'll have enough money to buy at least one textbox for classes this fall!

—description from the designer

Set in the Italian countryside, La Città features various city-states vying for population. Each player starts with two cities and is charged with expanding the different aspects of those cities to attract larger populations.

Players build farms to feed their people, quarries to finance their expansions, marketplaces to allow growth in their cities, and bathhouses to keep their people clean and healthy. Additionally, structures can be built that give the city influence in one of three categories; superiority in one of these categories causes population to shift from other nearby cities when the "Voice of the People" is decided at the end of each turn.

At the end of six turns, the player with the most points — which are distributed for well-rounded cities, well-fed populations, and the overall size of population — wins.

This game, a reimplementation of the original La Città, contains a new expansion that includes new buildings, new politics cards, and new terrain tiles.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Of Knights and Ninjas: Medieval Legends
description: Another mini-expansion trilogy for Of Knights and Ninjas: Medieval Legends, adding ten personages from legend, from Beowulf to King Arthur, each with a special ability. Eastern Warriors: adding four characters to deflect attacks or steal cards. Events: four events from the glorious crusade to the horrible plague, that affect all players. A bonus card, the Torch, to burn down those pesky ladders and catapults. Plus ten cards to add to the base game to keep the deck balanced.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
Start the round by drawing a card. Your goal is to stack the barrels to match the challenge card and then place that stack on your platform first using ONLY one of the two toy forklifts. Can you win the most cards AND keep your warehouse accident-free? Classic vehicle play becomes a thrilling stack-and-match challenge with Forklift Frenzy - no forklift license required!

Forklift Frenzy
Game of stacking barrels with toy forklifts
Encourages fine motor skills, spatial reasoning, sequencing skills, problem-solving
Use the toy forklifts to stack the barrels so they match the challenge card
Press down on the forklift to lower the forks, release to lift barrels
Forklifts roll on free-rolling wheels
First to create a matching stack and place it on their platform wins the challenge card
Win the most cards to win the game!
Includes 2 forklifts, 2 platforms, 12 barrels, 40 challenge cards

Serve up some yummy fine motor fun!

Pile all the donuts onto the table, set out the spinner, and give each player a pair of tongs and an order card. Order up! - It's time to play!

On each turn, players spin the spinner to choose a donut shape and color. Next, they have to find a donut that matches the spinner. Then, if that donut matches a donut on their order card, they get to grab it with the tongs and place it on their card.

The first player to completely fill their order card wins the game!

Motor and matching skills will grow fast with the Grab That Donut Fine Motor Game.

Grab That Donut Fine Motor Game
Game of grabbing and sorting donuts
Encourages fine motor skills, matching skills, logic, imaginative play
Spin the spinner to decide on a donut shape and color
Find a donut that matches the spinner
If the donut matches one on your card, grab it with the tongs and place it on your card
First player to completely fill their order card wins the game!
Includes 24 mini donuts, 4 sets of tongs, 10 order cards, donut spinner
Instructions included
Packaged in a sturdy storage box
High-quality materials - Exceptional play-and-learn experience

An unpredictable and intense game of skill, where speed in picking out ingredients is the most important. Sounds simple? Think again. You can use only tongs and your nimble hands!


     Grab the tongs in your favorite color.
     Put the pasta and ingredients into the bowl.
     Draw a card and check which ingredients you need.
     Fish them out of the bowl as quickly as you can. Everyone plays simultaneously.
     Be the first to grab the ingredients from 5 cards to win!

This character, inspired by the homonymous song by Al Tall, has the ability "Rebound." Despite having only one point of attack and defense, Tio Canya always returns to the hand of the player who used it, whether they win or lose the battle. Therefore, the player who has it always has at least one point of defense. No more losing to "cagallons"!

boardgame: La Fallera Calavera: Ausiàs March (Promo Card)
description: This 15th-century poet cultivated various themes in his literature, especially love. That's why this extraordinary card will spread love among the players of La Fallera Calavera. When a player has Ausiàs March in their display, all rivals who want to battle for the treasures of this player must say "amor, amor" to him. If they don't, the player with Ausiàs March in the display can randomly steal a card from their hand!

This celebrated Valencian DJ, musician, and producer became popular in the 1990s thanks to his techno music. He will be remembered for lyrics of songs like "Exta sí, exta no." This special level 1 card, to be used with La Fallera Calavera 2, pays homage to that song. When you use it, you can have +7 points of attack and defense if the coin lands on heads ("exta sí"), but if it lands on tails ("exta no"), nothing will happen.

boardgame: La Fallera Calavera: Rata de marjal
description: Also known as a water rat or irrigation rat. Did you know that in the past, paella was cooked with this animal? In the game, it is a treasure that will transform into an ingredient when there are 7 or more conventional ingredients on the displays. That is to say, at the beginning of the game, it won't be useful for anything, but at an unexpected moment, it will transform into an ingredient!

Maria Abradelo, queen of Valencian television in the 90s and 2000s. Her power is "Mortal Song," and it will make her attack 6 whenever you initiate a battle by singing. But keep in mind that nothing prevents you from initiating a battle by singing... with a "cagalló"!

boardgame: La Fallera Calavera: La Paella Russa (Promo Card)
description: The paella russa, which refers to the mythical roulette in the form of paella from the Valencian television program "El show de Joan Monleón". With this card, if you're lucky, you can get 2 ingredients! But if chance penalizes you, you will lose all your ingredients. A daring move!

Initial and multicolored character with the "chaotic" trait. Whoever has this character in their team can, in each turn, introduce a bit of "chaos". Before starting the forest phase and before applying the thrifty character, you can:


    Shuffle the market stack, or
    Shuffle the forest battle stack.

This is a children's game with 2-4 players.

Players take turns rolling 2 dice and move their bullfighter according to the dice results. And the bullfight might start.

When bullfighting, press the button on the bull toy, and the bull will move randomly. If the bull hits the bullfighter, the bullfighter must start again.

When a player's bullfighter reach 30 points, the player wins.

boardgame: Xing Ling Wang: Trading Card Game
description:
Xing Ling Wang is a TCG (Trading Card Game).
The background story is as follows:
One day, extraterrestrial goats invaded the entire universe.
The fearsome goat clan consumed the spacetime lines, causing chaos in time, space, and reality.
Xing Ling Wang, the supreme guardian of universal peace, exerted all efforts to sacrifice himself, sealing these spacetime goats within the Xing Ling Cards.
However, time, space, and reality are irreversibly disrupted!
This led to a universal war, and you will become the successors of Xing Ling Wang, using your exclusive skill "Xing Ling Cards" to rule over your own race, vying for the next era of Xing Ling Wang.

PS: "星靈王" is pronounced as "Xing Ling Wang," directly translated as "Star Spirit King."

PS2: The game is primarily a 2-player TCG, but will also have solo mode, 2v2, and 3v3 modes in the future.

The same as always, like never before. Champion's Journey is a fan Print to Play board game that brings the experience of the 1st Pokémon Generation to a complete board game.

In this totally free experience for 1 to 5 players, the first trainer to beat the elite 4 and be crowned Champion of the Pokémon League will win the game. To do this, you must travel through the entire Kanto region obtaining the 8 medals while catching Pokémon, obtaining objects and special cards that will help you to take advantage.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Flood is coming... You’ve been working on your ark for a long time and it’s finally ready to shelter the animals. You must hurry! The first raindrops are starting to fall and the animals are getting nervous.

You have to get going! Travelers talk about other arks built in distant lands... Will you be able to make your ark the best? Each round, you will reveal an Animal card and compete to be the fastest to get the same values on your dice as the ones shown on the Animal card. You may modify the result of each die using the FLICE: you must change the die face by only using your index finger!

The goal of the game is to be the player with the most victory points at the end of the game. You will gain them by obtaining Animal cards and winning Objective cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Harry Potter Miniatures Adventure Game: Wizarding Duels Starter Box
description:
Wizarding Duels is here! A new beginning for the Harry Potter Miniatures Adventure Game. A faster and more intuitive game with fast-paced magic combat full of emotion and great epic moments.

Assemble your party of witches and wizards and challenge your rivals, now with amazing plastic miniatures fully assembled and ready to paint.

Join the battle for the Wizarding World with the definitive game in the wonderful Harry Potter saga.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: When Nightmares Come: An Investigative Wargame of Supernatural Horror
description:
A solo and cooperative skirmish wargame for investigating and battling the supernatural mysteries of the modern world.

When Nightmares Come is a tabletop miniatures wargame about modern day monster hunting and occult investigations. Players will form a team of paranormal vigilantes; self-taught occult specialists and monster hunters who call themselves the Nightwatch. These self-appointed members of the 'watch look to tackle the supernatural horrors and investigate the strange disturbances that plague their city.

The core of the game, using the Action Dice Pool with its multiple die types, is fast and bloody, with tiered enemies, flexible player classes, quick combat resolution, and straightforward mission objectives.

When Nightmares Come also contains a roleplaying element that allows for non-combat challenges and dramatic encounters. This system uses the same dice types as the core game's Action Dice Pool and emphasizes quick resolutions. This narrative system adds a fresh dimension to the core miniatures experience, particularly in longer campaigns where the promise of different foes and new storylines encourages long-term playability.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Dark Providence Investigators
description:
A set of 5 investigators which were offered as a bonus when pre-ordering Cthulhu; Dark Providence.

This set includes 5 investigator miniatures and dashboard.


     Agent Monday
     Agent Tuesday
     Agent Wednesday
     Agent Thursday
     Agent Friday

The New Year's Eve party at Atelier Augustin was a grand celebration, until famed Creative Director Ace Augustin turned up dead!

Murder at the Atelier is an escape room style puzzle game. Across the 18 tarot cards are 10 puzzles that can be solved in any order. Work together to solve the puzzles and catch Ace's killer.

Extra material: We recommend that you have pens, a notepad, and scissors on hand when you play the game.

—description from the publisher

Viking Chess is a smaller version of the game "Hneftafl". You can play multiplayer with 2-3 players. The objective is to get all your pawns out of your base and to the middle valhalla. Odin in base is +5, enemy in base is +2 and having your own viking is +1. Everybody is control of Odin who is in the center.

—description from the designer

The Big Mama expansion contains 16 tiles and allows the player to create the Big Mama Monster in as many tiles as desired and score the visible points.

4 x Big Mama Head
4 x Big Mama Body
4 x Big Mama Tail
4 x Baby Monster
The Giraffe expansion contains 12 giraffe tiles. Players can assemble two giraffe tiles and create different pairs of scoring conditions.

Alibi is a murder mystery game series inspired by dinner parties with a crime theme, designed for players aged 14 and above.

In the game Alibi: Murder on the Nile there is a scenario of a vacation on the river, which ends in tragedy.

All five players are suspects, and through clues and discussions, they must reconstruct the events while concealing or revealing the secrets of the story.

In Alibi, it is important to carefully follow the order of card decks. If there are six (or more) players, the Detective (or investigation team) comes into play. With only four players, one innocent suspect deck will be removed from the game.

No one knows who the killer is — not even the guilty party — until the final phase of the game, which begins with the Revelation when each player learns their guilt or innocence from their own deck of cards. At this point, all players provide their reconstruction of the events before the voting. Will the killer manage to direct the suspicions of other players towards an innocent person to win the game?

A mystery to experience firsthand in an innovative blend of board game and role-playing game.

—description from the publisher
The Ice Expansion contains 37 tiles and allows new mutations (which could be a 3-headed bear!).

boardgame: Alibi: A Dish to Die for
description:
Alibi is a murder mystery game series inspired by dinner parties with a crime theme, designed for players aged 14 and above.

In the game Alibi: A Dish to Die For there is a scenario of the last "poisonous" episode of a cooking program.

All five players are suspects, and through clues and discussions, they must reconstruct the events while concealing or revealing the secrets of the story.

In Alibi, it is important to carefully follow the order of card decks. If there are six (or more) players, the Detective (or investigation team) comes into play. With only four players, one innocent suspect deck will be removed from the game.

No one knows who the killer is — not even the guilty party — until the final phase of the game, which begins with the Revelation when each player learns their guilt or innocence from their own deck of cards. At this point, all players provide their reconstruction of the events before the voting. Will the killer manage to direct the suspicions of other players towards an innocent person to win the game?

A mystery to experience firsthand in an innovative blend of board game and role-playing game.

—description from the publisher

Alibi is a murder mystery game series inspired by dinner parties with a crime theme, designed for players aged 14 and above.

In the game Alibi: Blood on the Tartan there is a scenario in the villa of a Scottish gentleman inspired by a Sherlock Holmes story.

All five players are suspects, and through clues and discussions, they must reconstruct the events while concealing or revealing the secrets of the story.

In Alibi, it is important to carefully follow the order of card decks. If there are six (or more) players, the Detective (or investigation team) comes into play. With only four players, one innocent suspect deck will be removed from the game.

No one knows who the killer is — not even the guilty party — until the final phase of the game, which begins with the Revelation when each player learns their guilt or innocence from their own deck of cards. At this point, all players provide their reconstruction of the events before the voting. Will the killer manage to direct the suspicions of other players towards an innocent person to win the game?

A mystery to experience firsthand in an innovative blend of board game and role-playing game.

—description from the publisher

Feral State Chapter 2 continues what Feral State started, only this time you can also play cooperativly. 
Again your mission is to reach the basement of the house and eliminate the Zombies all over the postapocalyptic town. On your way down you will fight zombies using unique battle system, which involves both luck and skill.

Feral State Ch. 2 is a two-page PnP dice game. You roll a D6 to attack and the choose which body part you want to hit on a "pizza wheel", where you can choose the rolled number or the opposite side of the "pizza wheel" and do damage to.

You can also get additional rolls to defeat each zombie. You can also collect items like weapons and medikits in order to better overcome the zombies and survive for the boss battle in the basement.

Ad Infinitum is an expansion for An Age Contrived. It introduces 3 modules to add new variability to the core game, as well as cosmetic trophy pieces to celebrate your victories.


     Advancement Tokens grant benefits at the start of the game to certain players, based on their performance in the previous game and whether they are new to An Age Contrived. They add an extra challenge for skilled players and a small boost for new players.



     Achievement Tokens are mixed in with those from the base game, increasing the number from 6 to 12. All 5 of the Achievements are randomized with this module in play, rather than only the top 2.



     Infinitum Benefit Tokens give players new benefit options when completing Monuments, adding to the decision space of how to build and run your engine.


We refer to this expansion as a "repeated play pack", as your benefits in one game will be influenced by your performance in the last game. It can accommodate any combination of players entering, exiting, or re-entering your group.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Scholars of the South Tigris: Dismiss Promo Cards
description:
Originally available from Dice Tower 2024 campaign in Pack B.

4 new action cards (1 per player).

These cards come with a pre-printed white-2 die.
When resting, they allow players to dismiss any of the first 6 translators (1-5 range).

—description from the designer
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

description: The time has come for you to show off your entrepreneurial and negotiating skills in Brazil. Monopoly Brazil has a special design that will provide exciting and surprising moments with friends and family. Travel from Roraima to Porto Alegre and earn money by investing in tourism and hotels. But be careful, because the dice and the cards can bring you face to face with some unforeseen events and disrupt your business, after all, only one will end the game successfully. Analyze the opportunities, make the right choices and take the risk to win!

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C9 is a two-part medium difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Designed by ARND FELTEN

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C10 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Designed by TROY MACKAWAY

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C11 – Flooded with zombies
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C11 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Designed by MATT HIBBARD

In Zirkhaki everyone acts as diggers, digging for treasures and trying to gain wealth by collecting as much treasure as possible. It works for 2 - 4 players, but combining two sets of cards can extend that from 5 - 8 players. First, the players will make a "discard" pile by removing the lowest value card from each type and putting them aside. Then they will shuffle all the rest of the cards and put them on the other side of the table, creating a "dig pile". The distance between the dig pile and the discard pile is the "dig site".

Then each player will start their turn by flipping a card from the dig pile onto the dig site. Each card has a special power that can help the player, or make it harder for them. The player then keeps on flipping cards and exacting their powers for as long as they can, or want. If a similar card is flipped up (for example if a coin card has been flipped earlier onto the dig site and then another coin card is flipped), their turn is over and they have to put everything in the dig site onto the discard pile. If they stop before getting a similar card, they will gather the cards in the dig site and put them in front of themselves, creating their "treasure pile".

The game ends when the dig pile is emptied. Then each player will calculate their scores by counting the highest number of each card that they own, and the player with the highest point wins. After a few plays, when players are a bit more advanced, they can use "special cards". They will shuffle the cards and give each player a card. Each card has a power that corresponds with an item, and they can use those powers in combination of those items.

Each character comes with it own character game sheet, with each own unique skills to learn, and attributes to be filled with numbers and dice.
     Each turn, players roll 2 d6 dice, one dice for own character as an action for this round, another dice resul will go to opponnent. Actions have unique numbers assigned to them.So each turn, both players perform 2 action at the same time.
     Players will be expanding and learning skills, adding various dice and numbers into 4 attributes. 
     After 7 turns, characters are ready for final battle.


PnP, one page, roll&write, dice drafting, action selection, character building and dueling game for 1-2 players with solo mode and Variable Player Powers.

—description from the designer

The title of the game Senedong comes from the Korean words "Semo," "Nemo," and "Donggeurami", which mean triangles, squares, and circles.

In this game, players must quickly determine whether the symbol cards in their hand match the top card on the central pile, following the rule card. Playing a card correctly earns 1 point, while an incorrect card results in a deduction of 1 point. Success in this game relies on speed and concentration.

—description from the publisher

Duel collectible board strategy with fast games (5-15 minutes).

You are one of the collectors collecting a variety of unique units.

For many, it's enough just to own a huge army of interesting creatures, but there are those who don't have enough of it. For people like you, they hold championships among collectors, where everyone can prove themselves, show their units and the ability to manage them.

Assemble a unique army and challenge the rest. Prove that you know the strengths and weaknesses of creatures of different races and professions better than others.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Played while watching a football match on tv. You are placing bets on the outcome in the match. Gaining points when your bets come true. Most points win.

Catapult is a decisive two-player army game of total annihilation.

Rules:

     The goal of Catapult is to capture all your opponent's pieces.
     Starting with light, players move one of their pieces each turn. 
     If you cannot make a legal move, you must pass. 
     Pieces move in a straight line, up to its range, passing only enemy or empty spaces, and capturing all passed enemies, including one landed on. Note, you do not have to land on a piece to capture it.
     Each piece has default range of 1. Range is doubled for each of the three forward directions in which its movement is blocked
     Non-capturing movements (a move that only passes and lands on empty spaces) must be in one of the three forward directions, towards the opposite corner. 
     Capturing movements (a move that lands on or passes through 1 or more opponent pieces) may be in any of the eight orthogonal or diagonal directions.


Optional balancing mechanism: 
This game can optionally be played using the dagger. Dark would start out in possession of it. On your turn, you may pass the dagger to the opponent. If you do, for this turn, make two moves instead of one.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Heroes of Might and Magic III: The Board Game – Tower Expansion
description:
A new faction to play with.
New heroes, new creatures, new spells, new tiles, new campaign.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Game: Heroes of Might & Magic III
Video Game Theme: Heroes of Might & Magic

description:
The deck of letter cards is evenly dealt among the players. On their turn a player reveals the top card of their deck, and either leaves it face up on the table, or uses it with one or more cards already on the table to make and keep a word.

When all cards have been played, players total the points on the cards they kept, and the highest total wins.

Florilegium is a card game that showcases the botanically accurate illustrations of Elaine Shimwell. Players are artists building their collection of botanical art over the year.

Players draw a card from either the draw deck or the discard pile and then play a card to the correct season.

At the end of the game, the player who has the lowest total value in points across both the seasons and the remainder of their hand is the winner.

—description from the designer

Your country was always hanging by a thread, perpetually at war with neighbouring regions. Now the age of resisting has ended, it's time to end the disagreements once and for all... only one must remain.

In Zone of Distrust, 2 players will compete for control over the Contested Territory; the first player to enter the opponent's homebase wins.

The game is played in turns, where each player will be able to take up to 3 actions, which vary from enlisting new troops to building supply depots to rallying foreign support.

The main resource, supplies, doubles as research indicators: when researching an upgrade, the cube on the player mat will be removed, now able to be used as a resource! Also, each mat has different upgrade spaces.

All of this, coupled with simple dice combat, 3 different maps and intuitive graphic design will make for a great gaming experience for you and your friends. Have fun!

—description from the designer

Rock, Paper, Scissors meets Dominoes in this cute game for young children. Each player gets 10 life points and is dealt a hand of 4 cards, printed with anthropomorphic roshambo figures and a penalty value of either 1 or 2. The last card is dealt face-up to begin the domino line. Players take turns placing one card per turn to beat the open end of the last card played. If someone is unable to make a play, they lose life points equal to the points listed on the cards in their hand. The winner is the last person with life points.

Age of Pericles is a historical strategy board game for 1–4 players, the standalone second chapter in the Legends of Hellas trilogy (following Pillars of Heracles).

Command Athens at the height of its power, when the city transformed the Delian League - originally a coalition of Greek city-states formed to defend against Persia, into its own empire, channeling wealth and power to construct the Parthenon as a symbol of unrivaled glory.

—description from the publisher

Unveil the grand tapestry of Hellenistic history in Sons of Heracles, a standalone board game rooted in the mechanics of Pillars of Heracles. Forge the path of the Argead dynasty, chart the epic conquests of Alexander the Great, and navigate the complex web of successor states. From the rise of Macedon to the sprawling Hellenistic period, this strategic experience immerses you in the dynamic interplay of empire-building, diplomacy, and war. Craft your legacy, encounter historical figures, and lead your civilization to greatness in this captivating journey through one of antiquity's most transformative epochs.

—description from the publisher

A roll-n-write game featuring a drawing of a bowl of soup with a grid of letters worth 1 to 3 points. Each round, roll a d6 and a d8 to pick letters from the corresponding row and column to create a word at least 4 letters long. After three words are created, tally one's score. Can be either played as a solo game or competing against others for higher score.

A thrilling game for the whole family
Your compass directs you.
Travel the high seas and pull in a record catch - but beware of the shark. Learn about real fish and shark behaviour in a fun way.
—description from the publisher

The aim of the game is to catch the most fish (by weight) as possible.
On their turn, each player spins the compass - the numbers indicate the direction and number of spaces to move. If the player lands on the mouth of a fish, they take one card from the "fish pool" of the appropriate type.
The game ends when all of the fish have been caught, or the players agree that they have done fishing for the day.
The players tally the numbers on all the fish they have caught, and the winner is the player with the highest total - the biggest catch.

Each player gets a sheet covered in a grid of bugs and clovers. Drawing two domino-like cards from the 10-card bug deck, players fold a corner of their page with the same number of clovers matching the card drawn, then place their initials over any bugs matching the other card and pass their sheet to the player on their left. After four rounds the initialed bugs in the folded corners are tallied and whoever found the most bugs wins.

CTHULHU RISES!!

It's been centuries since the Cultists attempted to open a portal to bring Cthulhu into Our World. Defeated by the Mystic Council, Cthulhu cursed the Cultists and their families with lycanthropy for their failures. The Cultists have scoured the world for the ancient rituals. Now they have found them and are once again attempting to raise Cthulhu, and end their curse. Times have evolved, and now Investigators seek out those who dabble in the Arcane Forces. The Mystic Council must defeat the Cultists while not getting caught by the meddling Investigators. Take on the role of a tireless Investigator, a powerful Mystic, or a crazed Cultist. Will Cthulhu be raised?

In this game, you will take on the role of either an Investigator of the Arcane, a member of the Mystic Council pledged to protect Our World, a Cultist bent on watching the world go mad under the rule of Cthulhu, or even as Cthulhu himself ushering the group through the events of the game.

In each round, the cultists will perform their rituals, attempting to succumb players to the madness of Cthulhu.  The mystic will then attempt to thwart their treacherous schemes, without getting caught.  Finally, everyone will attempt to determine who the Cultists are.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Late to the Fight:  The Battle of Palmetto Ranch, May 13, 1865
description:
Late to the Fight is an historical simulation of the battle fought on May 13, 1865 just east of Brownsville, Texas along the Rio Grande River. Although the Confederate forces and their leaders knew of the surrender of Confederate forces in the east, as well as the assassination of President Abraham Lincoln, die-hards in state government and the remaining forces in Texas remained defiant. Union forces had occupied Brownsville, Texas in November 1863, but most of the Union forces there were withdrawn in November 1864 to where the Rio Grande emptied into the Gulf of Mexico at Brazos Island, Texas to guard against smuggling by pro-South Mexicans. A “gentlemen’s agreement” between the Union and Confederate forces in the area meant a relative calm had prevailed since then. However, when Union General Lew Wallace’s attempts to negotiate a surrender of all Confederate forces and the state of Texas were rebuffed in March, tensions grew. When General Wallace learned of a large Confederate force assembling at Brownsville, orders were issued for the 34th Indiana Infantry Regiment and 62nd United States Colored Troops Regiment to retake the town and disperse any rebel forces found there. What ensued as the last battle of the American Civil War, fought nearly a month after the surrender at Appomattox Court House. Can you do as well or better?

Each turn in Late to the Fight represents one hour of time. Infantry and Cavalry units represent Companies. Artillery units represent 2-4 guns. A hex on the map is approximately 250 yards across.

—description from the designer

In the depths of the ocean tensions are rising. Neptunium, a rare new element with unprecedented uses, has been found in abundance under the ocean floor. In a frenzy, industrial superpowers race to the depths, sparking a fierce competition for control, development, and resources. Limited in what each individual has access to alone, these industry leaders forge strategies and pacts, each hoping to be the first to collect enough Neptunium.

In Subterfuge, players control Industry superpowers competing for Neptunium (Np) by drilling new mines across the ocean floor, and by stealing existing mines of other players by force. Balance control of generators, providing you with electrical output, and factories, which produce your units used for combat. Excellent strategy will set you ahead of the pack, but diplomacy is where the game is truly won. Form alliances with other players, negotiate trades, and when the time is right, break all ties to shoot ahead for a victory!

Subterfuge is based off of the hit mobile game of the same name (which already plays like a tabletop game). Like the mobile game, turns are taken simultaneously for all players and actions play out in real time! Each player will also be hiring random specialists throughout the game to alter the gameplay and give special abilities.

The game features incredible artwork based directly off the game's minimalist style, giving players a truly authentic experience!

—description from the designer

This is budget company Games Hub's version of Sorry Sliders.

It's a dexterity game where you try to get your pawns home by shooting ball bearing loaded disks at a scoring field.

A Town Called Despair is a Wild West miniature game set in a small town in the late 1800's. The game comes with six multi-level buildings, wagons, cacti, barrels, crates, gallows, etc. to set up your town on a 3' x 3' neoprene playmat. Miniatures can be placed inside buildings with detailed floor plans and move up/down levels, even onto the roofs.

From 2-4 players construct outlaw gangs armed with a variety of pistols, shotguns, and rifles, name their characters, and assign random skills. After deploying on the edge of town, each player is dealt a hand of 5 cards that are used to determine turn initiative, outdraw opponents in a gun fight, and engage in brawls. Each player takes up to three actions on their turn moving, aiming, shooting, reloading, recovering, or healing.

Hitting and wounding opponents is determined by dice rolls with customized dice and players keep track of their gang members' wounds and rounds expended on specially designed cards.

Gold coins are awarded for wounding and killing opponents, carrying money bags to the edge of town, or completing objectives.

Each game normally ends with a bloody showdown on main street, with gold awarded to the last man standing. The player that has collected the most gold wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: A Town Called Despair: Zombies Expansion
description:
Add zombies to your Wild West experience in A Town Called Despair! The Zombies expansion adds a whole new macabre dimension and new play modes to A Town Called Despair! Comes complete with 16 zombie miniatures in a variety of poses, 4 new zombie cards, the three-storied town church, the town cemetery, illustrated rule book, and terrain assembly guide.

Zombies can be played in Hoard Mode where the zombies are controlled by a different player each turn, or in Player Mode where the zombies are controlled by a single player for the entire game.

Zombies move slower then their living counterparts, are easy to hit, but hard to kill. And each time another outlaw dies, another zombie takes his place. If you're wounded and they surround you, it might be better to take the easy way out.

—description from the designer

Each player gets four 7x7 maps: Castle, Forest, Desert, Dungeon (in ascending difficulty), a pair of d6, and a hero token. Ten cards are randomly taken from a poker deck to create the Treasure pile. Each round, a treasure card is drawn, and everyone rolls their dice, assigning one to hero movement and the other to a chart of polyominoes that will be drawn on the map to try to collect treasures or kill monsters. After ten rounds, everyone adds up their bonuses to see who has the highest score.

Welcome to the world of Jeu de Tapisserie, a mesmerizing card game that has captivated players for centuries. More than just a trick-taking game, Jeu de Tapisserie is a strategic dance of wits, bluff, and a touch of the arcane. This captivating journey through the 78 cards invites you to:


     Master the Art of Trick-Taking: Form alliances, outmaneuver rivals, and claim cunning victories in a game where each card holds a hidden agenda.
     Embrace the Power of Trumps: Unravel the mysteries of the 21 Major Arcana, powerful trump cards that can turn the tide of battle and rewrite the destiny of the round.
     Navigate a Tapestry of Scoring: Points lie not just in conquering tricks, but in the artful collection of specific cards and cunning bids. Every decision becomes a calculated wager, pushing you to become a master strategist.
     Dive into a World of Rich Symbology: Each suit and card whispers a secret, hinting at hidden narratives and ancient wisdom. Immerse yourself in the evocative artwork and uncover the mysteries woven into the very fabric of the deck.


These pages contain the rules for a parlour game using the 78 card Jeu de Tarot deck. 
You will need your own deck to play. A notepad to record scores is recommended.

—description from the designer

REVEAL LEONARDO DA VINCI'S LAST SECRET WITH EZIO AUDITORE! Amboise, 1519: Leonardo da Vinci writes his Last Will and Testament on his deathbed. And it concerns you! Take on the role of Ezio Auditore and his students and follow the clues left behind by the famous artist and inventor throughout Europe. Will you succeed in finding all the Assassins' hidden hideouts?

In this box:
3 repeatable tasks with 9 possible variations
9 cities to explore and travel through Europe
200 puzzle cards that bring you closer to the story and the solution to the puzzles
2 booklets to accompany your adventures

The scenarios can be played several times as no cards need to be folded or painted. However, there is only one solution per adventure.

—description from the publisher (translated)

LÜFTET MIT EZIO AUDITORE DAS LETZTE GEHEIMNIS VON LEONARDO DA VINCI! Amboise, 1519. Auf dem Totenbett verfasst Leonardo da Vinci seine Letzten Willen. Und der betrifft euch! Schlüpft in die Rolle von Ezio Auditore und seinen Schülern und folgt den Hinweisen, die der berühmte Künstler und Erfinden in ganz Europa hinterlassen hat. Wird es euch gelingen, alle versteckten Zufluchtsorte der Assassinen zu finden?

In dieser Box:
3 wiederholbare Aufgaben mit 9 möglichen Varianten
9 Städte erkunden und durch Europa reisen
200 Rätselkarten, die euch der Geschichte und der Lösung der Rätsel näherbringen
2 Hefte zur Begleitung eurer Abenteuer

Die Szenarien können mehrfach gespielt werden, da keine Karten geknickt oder bemalt werden müssen. Es gibt jedoch nur einen Lösungsweg pro Abenteuer.

—description from the publisher

ESCAPE GAME: THE TREASURE OF THE TEMPLARS

900 years after the founding of the Order of the Knights Templar in 1129, a notebook is found in Midlothian, Scotland. It was written by Georges de Laprée, one of the last knights of the order, and it begins with the following words: "Rosslyn, 1341. I, Georges de Laprée, have traveled the world to protect the treasure of the Templars from envious and wicked men, according to the orders I received at the beginning of the year 1307. It is neither a lie nor excessive pride to write here that the treasure cannot be recovered by anyone other than a knight of the Order."

It is your task to find the Templar treasure before it falls into the wrong hands. Your search begins in Jerusalem. Good luck!

Contents: - 99 puzzle and clue cards - a ruler and a yardstick to determine your route - a map to show you the way - the notebook of the last knight of the order, Georges de Laprée

—description from the publisher (translated)

ESCAPE GAME: DER SCHATZ DER TEMPLER

900 Jahre nach der Gründung des Ordens der Templer im Jahre 1129 wird im schottischen Midlothian ein Notizbuch gefunden. Es wurde von Georges de Laprée verfasst, einem der letzten Ritter des Ordens, und es beginnt mit folgenden Worten: „Rosslyn, 1341. Ich, Georges de Laprée, habe die Welt bereist, um den Schatz der Templer vor Neidern und Gottlosen zu bewahren, gemäß den Befehlen, die ich zu Beginn des Jahres 1307 erhielt. Es ist weder eine Lüge noch übermäßiger Stolz, hier schriftlich niederzulegen, dass der Schatz von niemand anderem als einem Ritter des Ordens wiedergefunden werden kann.“

Es ist eure Aufgabe, den Schatz der Templer zu finden, bevor er in falsche Hände fällt. Eure Suche beginnt in Jerusalem. Viel Erfolg!

Inhalt: - 99 Rätsel- und Hinweiskarten - Lineal und Gradmesser, um eure Route festzulegen - eine Karte, um euch den Weg zu weisen - das Notizbuch des letzten Ordensritters Georges de Laprée

—description from the publisher

Standard Modern Wargame (諾曼第登陸戰:標準現代戰棋) is associated with the publication of Reader's Digest "World War 2 Record" in 1982.

The battle system depends on the strength and lifespan of each chess piece. And the weather and terrain also have a significant influence.

In Bio Bay, students play as tuna and anchovies trying to collect as many tokens (food) as possible. There are plentiful zooplanktons for the anchovies, but you might get caught by a tuna! And anyone could get caught by the Fishing Boat (the teacher) at any time! How long can you survive? Can you collect the most tokens?

Richterskala is a game of creating structures and seeing how well they withstand earthquakes.  Game is played between two people (or two teams).  Each team has a set of cards (0,0,1,1,2,2,3,4,5,6,7,8) and chooses 11 blocks.  There is also a wooden "robot" for each team.

Rounds alternate between teams.  On their "turn", a team lays down 1 or 2 cards facedown and chooses a number of bricks for the build. Each team then builds a structure with the stated number of bricks.  The box is placed on the board blocking view of the building.  The robot may be added in addition to the bricks.  The team with the tallest structure before the earthquake gets a point.  The card(s) are revealed, determining the strength of the earthquake.  The rubber band mechanism is wound and released.  The team with the tallest structure after the earthquake gets 3 points.  If there is no clear difference in height, neither team scores.  The other team then becomes the active team and you repeat.

Game continues until both sides run out of cards. It is possible for one team to be out of cards, thus leaving the other team to continue taking turns without alternating.

The game was apparently originally presented to a local geology group for demonstration purposes.

After the battle between the VHS system and the BETA and with the victory of the former, a new business model began to spread: the VIDEO CLUB. 1999, video stores are at their peak. Titles of dubious quality and the most diverse flood the shelves and customers rush to rent the latest bizarre B movies. Gamers are one of those customers who eagerly await the latest novelty.

Take your voucher, rent without stopping and watch as many movies as you can... and those you can't.... prepare to pay the late fee.

—description from the designer

Tras la batalla entre el sistema VHS y el BETA y con la victoria del primero empezó a extenderse un nuevo modelo de negocio: el VIDEO CLUB. 1999, los videoclubs están en su apogeo. Títulos de dudosa calidad y de los más variopintos, inundan las estanterías y los clientes corren prestos a alquilar la última bizarrada de serie B. Los jugadores son unos de esos clientes que esperan con anhelo la última novedad.

Toma tu bono, alquila sin parar y visualiza cuantas más películas puedas... y las que no.... prepárate para pagar la multa por demora.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Moving-target game, played on a hex grid, where you play as a dog, with the goal of accumulating "treasure" in your hand or buried on the board, where it's worth more points. Your treasure is never safe in the game, as other players can steal from your hand, using their magpie, or dig it up from the board, which creates fun tension, and just a bit of push-your-luck. But it's not all up for grabs, as there are mitigation mechanisms to keep your treasure safer.
Treasure and (extra) actions are drawn from a bag, which also acts as a timer - when the bag is empty, the game-end triggers.

Gameplay
Your goal is to accumulate the most treasure (bones and toys), by having them in your hand or buried on the board.
Each player controls two pawns: Your Mutt can bury and dig out treasure. Your magpie’s goal is to annoy other mutts to steal their tiles. Additionally, there are 3 moles that can be controlled by any player, unearthing things on the board.
Tiles that you acquire during gameplay will give you the bones and toys, but also the actions you can use. You start the game with 7 tiles in your hand and 8 buried on the board, and can get more by:

     having less than 7 tiles in your hand at the start or at the end of your turn,
     picking other players’ Xs up, or
     poking other players’ mutts.


The best and safest way to get more tiles is by clearing space in your hand, so you should use your actions and bury your tiles often. Any tiles you bury under your Xs are still yours, and will make you more points at the end of the game, but only if other players do not steal them.

—description from the designer

Tell me you're a board game fanatic without telling me you're a board game fanatic!  We'll go first  We made a game out of the poular #tellmechallenge on social media.


    TellMeChallenge, also known as "Tell Me Without Telling Me," is a fun trend where social media users ask others to share personal opinions and beliefs without outright saying what they are talking about.


On TikTok and other social media platforms the challenge has gone viral with millions of views!  Bring home this great party game and jump on the trend with competitive, cooperative, and remote play rules included.

—description from the box

Aquaris is a "roll & write" board game for 1 to 10 players, with a duration of 15-30 min,  from 8 years of age onwards.

Roll the dices and choose well where you use each result. You should get the most beautiful aquarium with the best combination of fish, seahorses, turtles, crabs, plants, corals and ornaments. But always compensating the dirt in the aquarium with a good maintenance of the filters, to have clean water and create a good habitat for your pets.

Complete common goals, get first the goals that you share with the players next to you, use your character's skill to gain advantage, get more animals than your rivals, complete plant and ornament sets or collect pearls along the way. There are many ways to get the best score.

—description from the publisher

Bitter Pickles is a fast-to-learn, fun-to-play passing card game for family and friends of all brines.

Press your luck by passing for a higher card, but don’t
get caught with a pickle in your hand at the end of
the round or you’ll lose a pickle chip. Wild cards add spice and advanced rules keep the fun growing.

Become the last pickle standing and win the pot of “Pickle Chips” by consistently avoiding the lowest card in each hand.

Pass the pickle, or pay the price.

boardgame: エイジオブウィル 拡張：インフ城の反乱 (Age of Will Expansion: Inf Castle Rebellion)
description:
Architecture, once again.

The king thought he could retire after his previous work. However, his abdication was postponed due to signs of rebellion. The decision was made to lead his army to subdue the rebels.

The reluctant king is always thinking about architecture, even when he is nodding off in his saddle from exhaustion.

"I'm concerned about the capital's cathedral...I wonder if it will be completed before I die...

Yes. Let's build a small house near the rebel's fortress and take command from there. This battle will probably take a month or two. That doesn't seem so long..."

There are 32 cards, same as in the base set. Shuffle them together. The deck has doubled so now up to five people can play without any problems.

The 'Legion' icon has been added as a new element. It's a symbol that usually doesn't matter but, depending on the effect of the building, it can give you huge points - or it can just be a decorative icon. It brings various advantages (and, sometimes, disadvantages).

By combining over 60 cards (including the base set), you can enjoy more diverse developments!

It's a drafting game that can be finished in 15 minutes! A game will make you feel like you've played it in your spare time.

After drafting, you only need to build once! You can play as many games as you want by changing buildings.
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
Contando Ovejas "Counting Sheep" is a unique and entertaining board game that combines fun and strategic challenges.

The game features a simple yet addictive mechanic. Each player takes on the role of a farmer, and their goal is to count and collect as many sheep as possible in their flock. However, beware of cunning wolves that will try to steal your precious sheep! You'll need to make strategic decisions, use special cards, and seize opportunities to ensure your sheep are safe and that your flock grows.

—description from the designer

In the time of the Red, every city has a Combat Zone: an area of the city where the law doesn't reach and cyber-enhanced gangs fight for control over the streets.

The Arasaka Corporation is a worldwide megacorporation dealing in corporate security, banking, and manufacturing. The Arasaka Corporation maintains the largest and most powerful armed forces of any corporation in the world!

This box set includes all the miniatures and cards you need to field a complete Arasaka starter gang in your games of Combat Zone!

boardgame: Escape Game Spécial Famille: Mystères et Secrets de la Pyramide
description: Your group of explorers finds themselves locked in a sacred pyramid. You have 60 minutes to unravel the mystery of the Egyptian god and escape from his cursed grave.

In the time of the Red, every city has a Combat Zone: an area of the city where the law doesn't reach and cyber-enhanced gangs fight for control over the streets.

When the Law has to enter the Combat Zone, they try to hit hard and get out fast. Well-organized and well-armed, the Lawmen are a force to be reckoned with!

This box set includes all the miniatures and cards you need to field a complete Lawmen starter gang in your games of Combat Zone!

In the time of the Red, every city has a Combat Zone: an area of the city where the law doesn't reach and cyber-enhanced gangs fight for control over the streets.

The Bozo gang are bio-sculpted nightmares, surgically altered to look like twisted versions of old circus clowns, complete with actual bulbous red noses, outrageous hair, and long flat feet. This killer clown gang delights in sadistic and deadly pranks, twisting their victims fears into cruel traps!

This box set includes all the miniatures and cards you need to field a complete Bozos starter gang in your games of Combat Zone!

La Toupie Voyageuse is a solo or cooperative game for all ages from 6 upwards.
Each mission represents a journey that players must complete with a spinning top, moving it from one board to another. They represent 8 marvelous worlds in relief, with several targets. At the start of each mission, players identify together the targets to be reached and divide up the boards among them.
The first player launches the spinning top with the help of the thruster, and moves it by tilting his or her tray towards the target. Once the target has been reached, the player guides the spinning top towards the exit and onto the board held by the next player. The relay between players continues until the mission is completed. Challenges progressively increase in difficulty (narrow passages, obstacle jumps, etc.). Trophy stickers validate each successful mission!

—description from the publisher (translated)

La Toupie Voyageuse est un jeu solo ou coopératif pour tout âge dès 6 ans.
Chaque mission représente un parcours que les joueurs vont devoir effectuer avec une toupie en la passant d’un plateau à un autre. Ils représentent 8 mondes merveilleux en relief avec plusieurs cibles. Au début de chaque mission, les joueurs identifient ensemble les cibles à atteindre et se répartissent les plateaux indiqués.
Le premier joueur lance la toupie avec l’aide du propulseur et la déplace en inclinant son plateau pour la diriger vers sa cible. Une fois la cible atteinte, le joueur guide la toupie vers la sortie pour la faire passer sur le plateau tenu par le joueur suivant. La dynamique de relais entre les joueurs se poursuit ainsi jusqu’à la réalisation complète de la mission. La difficulté des défis s’accroit progressivement (passages étroits, sauts d’obstacles…). Des trophées autocollants permettent de valider chaque mission réussie !

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cyberpunk Red: Combat Zone – Generation RED
description:
In the time of the Red, every city has a Combat Zone: an area of the city where the law doesn't reach and cyber-enhanced gangs fight for control over the streets.

It’s one thing to live through the time of the RED – it’s another to be born into it… In parts of the city where the adults have fled, the youth run the streets!

This box set includes all the miniatures and cards you need to field a complete Generation RED starter gang in your games of Combat Zone!

Psiechadzka is simple a game about dogs and people. The mechanics is based on the evergreen Snakes and Ladders: players choose whether they want to perceive the world from the dogs' or the owners' perspective. As they walk their dogs - or owners - they throw dice to move along the board and learn how to interact with each other, avoid dangers and have fun together. The first to return safely and happily from the walk wins.

boardgame: Cyberpunk Red: Combat Zone – Danger Gals
description:
In the time of the Red, every city has a Combat Zone: an area of the city where the law doesn't reach and cyber-enhanced gangs fight for control over the streets.

Danger Gal is a private investigation, bodyguard, and security company. They sport tactical and exotic cat outfits in the field!

This box set includes all the miniatures and cards you need to field a complete Danger Gals starter gang in your games of Combat Zone!

In the time of the Red, every city has a Combat Zone: an area of the city where the law doesn't reach and cyber-enhanced gangs fight for control over the streets.

Scrappy, natives of the Combat Zone, the Zoners are always ready to throw down!

This box set includes all the miniatures and cards you need to field a complete Zoners starter gang in your games of Combat Zone!

A game made for the Dice Deck by We Heart Games.

In this solo deckbuilder, a large group of meteors are heading straight for earth! Build up your defence system to ward them off before they strike.

Use white cards to buy new cards from the row, and red cards to damage the black meteor cards in the row. After every turn, new cards cover any cards remaining in the row, so you need to keep up to prevent those meteors from getting buried!

There are also a few default actions you "buy" at any time during your turn, like drawing a card, scrapping a card from your hand, and moving a card in the row left or right.

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

description:
Designed for the Dice Deck by We Heart Games.

Rival captains pilot their ships between three islands, picking up and selling cargo for the best rates.

Move your boat between three coloured islands to pick up various cargo, then sell it at an island matching its colour. Islands can be upgraded to make each sale worth more; race to get the best price for the highest-value cargo!

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

description:
Designed for the Dice Deck by We Heart Games.

A Blackjack-style push-your-luck game.

With a deck of cards from 1-6, start with two cards. Each turn you can choose to be dealt another card from the deck. Your goal is to create as many sets of cards that total 7 as possible... but if you get 2 cards of each color, or 4 cards of any one color, you bust!

Each set is worth one point; the first player to 7 points wins.

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

description:
Zakodowany Kosmos ("Encoded Cosmos") - is a follow-up to Coded Castle designed for children who are already a bit familiar with the mechanics of programming.

Players race to their opponents' galactic bases. To do that, they draw tokens that represent and place board pieces allow them to move, and tokens that represent coding commands. Players may also encounter commands IF and FOR. Whoever plans their algorithms the best and visits their opponents' bases, wins.

From rule sheet:
This classic dice game is all about who can bluff the best about what numbers they roll. Perfect for playing on the go.

Each player starts with 5 dice. After rolling all their dice, each player keeps their results hidden and, in succession, makes a “bid” about how many of a particular result they think all players have in total OR challenge the previous bid. Each bid must be “bigger” than the last (a higher result or a higher number of the result in the previous bid.

All dice are revealed when a challenge is made. If the total results meet or exceed the challenged bid, the bidder wins! If not, the challenger wins!

Losing bids result in lost dice, so you can only lose 5 times before you’re out of the game.

The Five Clans of Tresibra: Bloodshed by Spencer Walters is an engaging and strategic tabletop game that combines elements of adventure, strategy, and chance. With a recommended age of 10 and above and a quick learning curve of 10 minutes or less, it's accessible to a wide audience.

The game accommodates 2 to 6 players, each representing a warrior from one of the five clans or the formidable antagonist, Gummosis, the evil King of the Crowns. Players embark on a journey by drawing and strategically placing field cards, creating a dynamic battlefield. The game's adaptability allows for variations in difficulty, with the option to adjust the number of participating clan members.

The turn-based structure adds an element of anticipation and strategy to each move. The inclusion of special attributes for each clan member, such as Drutt's unique ability to roll a die for health increases, adds depth and diversity to the gameplay. These special attributes contribute to the overall strategy, making each clan member's turn crucial in the battle against Gummosis and his forces.

The battle rules are straightforward, ensuring that even new players can quickly grasp the flow of the game. The use of health cards for clan members and the strategic placement of cards on the field create a dynamic and evolving battlefield. The introduction of Tunnel cards adds an interesting twist, transporting players to a new location where they face challenges and strategic decisions.

The variety of field cards, each with its own effects, keeps the game engaging. Whether navigating obstacles like water or mountains, encountering helpful features like healing peaches or traps, or facing off against Crown Gall squads and wild beasts, players must adapt their strategies to survive and progress.

The inclusion of individualized clan member cards with unique stats and abilities adds a layer of personalization and strategy. The careful selection of cards and the use of special attributes can turn the tides of battle, making each playthrough unique.

Overall, The Five Clans of Tresibra: Bloodshed is a well-crafted and immersive tabletop game that offers a balance of strategy, chance, and storytelling. With its quick setup, accessible rules, and dynamic gameplay, it's sure to provide hours of entertainment for players seeking an engaging and strategic gaming experience.

The tabletop is based on the hit fantasy novel "The five clans of Tresibra".

—description from the publisher
Just like in real mini golf, the objective is to knock your ball in the hole in as few strokes as possible.
This expansion contains 121 new cards. It adds new unit types: monks, demons, dragons and insects.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Get ready for BALKAN DOMINATOR. A new  boardgame where you can fight for supremacy over the Balkans. Gather your armies and conquer your friends in an effort to recreate the most famous Balkan empires and kingdoms! A satirical look at area control and, more generally, war, Dominator is an area control game with secret objectives. Each player chooses a color and with that color tanks tries to conquer as much of eastern Europe as you can. Which tanks start in which territories are randomized at the beginning of the game. With four different ways to win, no two games are ever the same.

—description from the publisher

Dexterity game of building a vertical house of cards.
The box contains 13 ceilings (the size of thick standard playing cards) and 26 walls (smaller illustrated cards which can be     folded into an L shape).
On your turn, bend two walls and place them on edge to build a new level, then add a ceiling on top. If the tower doesn't fall, score one point per level.

The expansion consists of 3 modules: 

     Six-player support: Introducing 12 new cards to extend the magic of Quibbles to 6 players, so you can invite more friends to join in the fun.
     The Magnificent Sevens: To elevate the strategic depth, this module adds 7 cards with a value of 7. These new cards open up some fun new strategies. And the cards look really fun!
     The Zero Heroes: Finally, this modules adds 4 new cards with no Quibbles at all. They allow you to capture cards you would otherwise not be able to take.


—description from the publisher

A set of 25 promo cards for the board game Herring: North Seas. The set includes 4 mini-add-ons:

     Natural Selection (6 destruction cards)
     Myths and Legends (8 mythical creature cards, 1 destruction card)
     Ancient (5 cards)
     Ships (5 cards)

Tribuito a Mondrian ("Tribute to Mondrian") is a simultaneous actions game where players follow the restrictions on cards to draw lines and fill areas with colors, creating drawings in Mondrian style.

Choose your favorite color, take a look at what the evaluation cards ask you to do and create your art!

In a turn, players choose to either draw a straight line or fill an area with the color shown in a card. After ten rounds, they score composition points according to two evaluation cards and the use of their favorite color.

boardgame: Cavalcada a repartir tots els paquets
description: This game is thematically based on the Christmas celebration of "Cavalcada de reis" from Alcoi, the oldest in Spain. In the game we will have to imitate the work of the "pajes", they have to put stairs in the street to reach the balconies to deliver the gifts to the children!
