An action/dexterity game about flipping okonomiyaki! We offer two courses depending on the level of difficulty:

     Simple rules for skillfully flipping okonomiyaki that everyone from the elderly to children can enjoy!
     Enjoy the real-time nervous breakdown of collecting ingredients in a hurry and the exciting competition of skill to flip okonomiyaki!

boardgame: Windows: Fairy of the Small Window
description:
Did you know that there are two worlds, the [Human World] and the [Fairy World]? In the past, the boundary between the two worlds was not clear, but as the [Human World] advanced, the connection between the worlds has become less.

However, the two worlds need to support each other, and the Fairies cannot survive if their existence is forgotten...

So, the Fairies created a doorway between the two worlds called "Small Window". You, as a fairy companion, must connect the "Small Window" and help the fairies!

—description from the rulebook

Aim for a high score by placing cards whose colors connect with the tableau piles. Your color remaining in the final field will be an additional point and a chance for a comeback. Optional rules allow you to experience a fresh game each time you play.

This expansion gives each player unique tool to take with them into the field.

Setup: Before dealing bird cards, deal two tool cards to each player. 'Return any excess to the box. Each player chooses one, places it faceup next to their tree, and returns the others to the box.

Players represent Anglo-Texans, Mexicans, and Native Americans struggling to control and develop the untamed lands of Texas. They will seek wealth, power, and glory through claiming title over raw lands, cultivating a growing population, promoting commerce, and protecting their citizens.

Players deploy cards to build interlocking tableaus representing farms, ranches, and communities and can also play cards representing political leaders, spies, and military forces to dominated, subvert, or ravage their opponents' tableaus.

Players win by scoring points at game's end for cards in their tableau and having completed secret objectives they drew at the beginning of the game.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Fifth Brother and Seventh Sister Operative Expansion
description:
Two terrifying Inquistors are on the hunt in this new pack for Star Wars: Legion! Tasked with hunting down Jedi who survived Order 66, the Fifth Brother and Seventh Sister are deadly Imperial Operatives who bolster Imperial armies with even more Force users. In addition to their innate Force abilities and lightsaber skills, this pack also includes a new Training upgrade card and 5 command cards that further enhance their Jedi-hunting abilities.


     A new Galactic Empire expansion for Star Wars: Legion
     Adds the deadly Inquisitors Fifth Brother and Seventh Sister to players' armies
     Also includes a new upgrade card and 5 command cards to further augment these characters' abilities


—description from the publisher

Are you prepared to face the interview for the role of Wimpy Chicken Sniffer? How about Eccentric Hole Sharpener? In Hire Me!, you'll have to convince the other players of your suitability for a variety of quirky jobs. However, there's a catch - the other players don't know the exact job title!

To start the interview process, one player assumes the role of interviewee and must secretly generate a job title using a random selection of attributes. The other players must then use question cards in an attempt to figure out the interviewee's role!

Interviewers will score points if they manage to guess the job's attributes correctly. The interviewee will not score points if one of the job's attributes is unanimously guessed, and so it is important for the interviewee to hint enough that players will be able to figure out your full role, but not be so obvious that they will all get it completely correct.

—description from the designer

Spare, Strike, Steal your way to victory. Frantically race to score the most points by making sets totalling 10 pins, or by brazenly stealing them from your opponents. At the end of each round, tot up the points. The first player to score a total of 160 points or more wins the game and claims the coveted TROPHY card.

A fast-paced family game for everyone. Easy to pick up, difficult to put down! This competitive, quick-play, family card game will keep the whole family entertained. It's great fun for any games night to break the ice and can be played with 2-6 players, aged 8 years and over. Average gameplay is around 15-20 minutes.

—description from the designer

Get ready for a wild ride of laughter, creativity, and teamwork with Bangarang, the fun family game that takes clue-giving to a whole new level! In this high-energy game, players join forces to help the guesser unravel the mystery word on the card using a variety of entertaining clues.

Bangarang features a dynamic twist on traditional clue-giving. Players must use their wits and creativity to communicate the secret word in five different ways: through a single word, a mime, a single sound, a drawing, or by crafting the letters with their bodies. It's a riot of diverse communication styles that keeps everyone on their toes and guarantees endless hours of fun.

Laugh-Out-Loud Moments
Prepare for uncontrollable laughter as the guesser deciphers the clues, leading to unexpected and amusing outcomes. Bangarang guarantees a lively atmosphere and joyous memories.

Family-Friendly Fun Game
Suitable for players of all ages, BANGARANG is designed with family fun in mind. It's the ideal choice for bringing generations together for a shared adventure in the world of words and creativity.

Enjoy Endless Replayability
With a wide range of words and countless ways to convey them, Bangarang ensures that no two games are ever the same. Keep the endless excitement going for game night after game night.

Give the noisiest player the GUESSER card – they will be the first to try and guess the words on the card. Shuffle the Bangarang cards and deal one to each of the other players.
Scan the QR code within the rules booklet to access a digital timer for the game. Place all the other components in the middle of the table, and you’re ready to play Bangarang.
Decide which side of the cards you are going to play, pink or blue, and then pick the top card and place it in the cardholder for all players, except the GUESSER, to be able to see it.

All together now! Start the timer, and the CLUE-GIVERS give a clue to the first word or phrase on the card simultaneously. You’ll have to fight to make yourself seen and heard!
The GUESSER can guess as many times as they like. Once guessed, move to the next word on the card (or shout NEXT if you can’t guess) and play continues until the timer sounds.
The team scores 1 point for each correctly guessed word. If all 5 words are guessed within the time limit – that’s BANGARILLIANT! Score 1 bonus point. Make a note of the score.
Play continues with the GUESSER passing their GUESSER card to the player on their left. All of the Bangarang cards are shuffled and re-dealt to the other players and the next round of mayhem begins.

Play 12 rounds and then tot up your score to see how well you play as a team. You can try to beat your team's score the next time you play Bangarang!

—description from the designer

A multiplayer card-based strategy game that pokes fun at the illicit ways of gaining wealth in a corrupt Mzansi.
Join our 2 corrupt citizens, “Steal” and “Plunder” as you plan your strategy and try to set yourself up for success at the end game.
Deploy Wealth cards, Wild cards and Power cards to challenge your opponents and build your Wealth stack.
Quick rules:
Accumulate R10 Mil in illicit wealth to be crowned king of the underworld.
Enrich yourself by building your own WEALTH stacks or by Stealing and Plundering your opponents’ ill-gotten gains.
POWER cards will help you in controlling the game and impeding your opponents progress, to tip the balance of power in your favour.
WILD cards will give you the upper hand in ‘negotiations’ and will add to your accumulated wealth after the end-game.

—description from the publisher

The Memorace board game develops not only memory but also general knowledge and literacy in a fun way: now you can travel around Hungary and discover all its wonders.

Along with the challenges, the game develops logical thinking, memory and attention - all in an interactive and fun way.

Gameplay:


     Draw a card and place it in front of you.
     Turn the clock over and try to remember as many details as possible while the sand is spreading - this is about 10 seconds.
     Roll the dice!
     If you rolled a 1, you must answer the thought-provoking puzzle on the front page. Look at the picture and count or guess correctly!
     If you did not roll a 1, turn the card over and answer the question on the back next to the rolled number. These will test your memory, but if you pay attention, you will definitely know the answer!


Memorace can be played alone or with several players. During Memorace, you can learn easily and playfully. Sit down with the whole family or just try to learn as much as possible about the world, in this case, the human body. You will surely find information that you have not heard before!

A very nice and beautiful board game by Keller Mayer, in which the players have the joint task of getting the mother duck and her 3 ducklings to the lake before the sun goes down.

On the fields of the game board we can see the same figures as on the sides of the dice. The players take turns rolling the dice and then discuss which duck to move on the board.
It is important that the mother duck should always be in front; one field cannot contain more than one duck; after each roll, you can move only one duck, by moving it to the nearest square to the one you rolled.

The last few fields of the board are no longer the usual ones. The mother must be the first to arrive at the first puddle visible on the lake, and she can be placed there with any throw other than the sunbather. Since the pond can be rotated and the ducklings must also arrive here, pushing the mama doll forward will reveal another small puddle where you can place the next duck.

In the event that a sun is thrown, the sun marker must be placed one space to the right on the vertically positioned rainbow board.
If we were lucky with our throws and stepped the ducks skillfully, all four of them could be swimming in the lake before the sunshine reached the end of the rainbow.

Cute cooperative game for little ones, which can be used to develop social skills and speaking skills.

A match and create learning game about the secrets of taking photos. Collect four-of-akind by looking at the pictures and icons carefully and discovering the underlying visual elements that connect them. After matching all sets of four, take pictures applying the characteristics of each theme and you'll become a great photographer!

boardgame: Het Spel van Vos en Haas
description:
The exciting party game where cooperation, good memory and tactics are key.
A bird has lost her egg. And rascal Jak is sneaking around in the forest! Help Fox and Hare and their friends to put the egg in the nest before Jak finds it. But hurry, because together you have to be faster than Jak!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Het spannende gezelschapsspel waar samenwerking, een goed geheugen en tactiek centraal staan.
Een vogel is haar ei kwijt. En boef Jak sluipt rond in het bos! Help Vos en Haas en hun vrienden om het ei in het nest te leggen voordat Jak het vindt. Maar schiet op, want jullie moeten samen sneller zijn dan Jak!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Harry Potter: Le Jeu Des Sortilèges
description:
Immerse yourself in the magical world of Harry Potter. Be the quickest to answer the questions and cast spells on your opponents to win the game! You're guaranteed to have a good laugh!

Replongez dans l'univers magique de Harry Potter. Soyez le plus rapide à répondre aux questions et jetez des sortilèges à vos adversaires pour gagner la partie! Attention, fous rires garantis!

Sabo"ten" is a game in which players try to get rid of all their cards by playing them in the right order to spell サ(Sa)- ボ(bo) - テ(te)-  ン(n). Some cards only have one character on them, but others have two (サボ, ボテ, or テン).

Players start with 5 cards in their hand and take turns  playing one card (or two if they either don't own a cactus token yet or have been prickled in the previous two turns) that can help spelling "サ ボ テ ン" the right way.

If  the player can't play a card from their hand, they have to either draw one card from the draw deck and eventually play it if possible or draw the card on top of the playing area.

If the current player can't play any card on their turn turn despite drawing from the deck, one adequate card can be played by any other player, but they'll have to be the fastest one to do so.

There are also several special cards - the Prickly Cactus (with all four characters on it) and the テン (TEN) card. When a player plays them, all the other players have to grab a water token from the center of the table, and the one who didn't manage to get one gets prickled. If the card was a Prickly Cactus, all players give one of their cards to the prickled player (there is a hand limit of 10 however). If the card was a TEN, the prickled player must instead draw cards until they have ten in their hand.

Once a player gets rid of all their cards for the first time, they get a flowerless cactus token, and on the second time, they flip it over, making the cactus bloom and winning the game!

It is a new climbing game and a board game composed mainly of cards, whose objective is to reach the mountain meeting first using different materials. It is set in the Aragonese Pyrenees, in North Spain.

Climbing cards, action cards, event and climbing objects. Tokens, energy meters and dice.

You have to get a certain amount of climbing cards to reach the finish line. Other cards will facilitate or hinder that objective.

A puzzle-ly 13 cards solitaire game about building a garden fence.
The game provides a win condition. Additionally you may add up points at the end to see how well you did.

Choose which boards to put vertical and with horizontal to meet as many objectives as you can. Though there are also impossible ones or do you expect any UFOs to land on your fence??!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Profiler: A Social Deduction Game
description:
Embark on a cerebral journey with The Profiler - a social deduction game. A masterpiece crafted for the true social deduction connoisseur. Delve into the art of deduction, strategic bluffing, and unraveling the human psyche.

Gather your group of 6-15 players, divide the roles of the townsfolk and the killer, and begin your quest of investigating the crime scenes to discover the killer in your midst.

The Profiler is played in day and night phases. During the day the players can investigate the evidence found left by the FBI Agent, Medical Examiner, and Lab Analyst. They can question the suspects and arrest a suspect. During the night, the killer will be able to draw a killer opportunity card to determine if they will kill and when. The FBI Agent, Medical Examiner, and Lab Analyst will have the opportunity to review the case file and extract some evidence.

The Townsfolk will win if they can arrest the killer and correctly identify their killer profile in 60 mins or less. The killer will win if they can outlast the 60 mins, deceive the townsfolk into getting their profile wrong, or by killing everyone in the town.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: CRYPTID: The Creepy Card Battling Game
description:
Conjure terrifying creatures from myth and legend to do battle! The first to kill 10 of the enemy's cryptids wins.

CRYPTID: The Creepy Card Battling Game is a unique card game with an exciting ruleset. Conjure monsters, evolve them into more powerful beasts, and plan carefully to get and stay ahead of your opponent. Positioning is extremely important, and each cryptid has different strengths and weaknesses depending on where you place it.

With the powerful Pyre Burn mechanic, any player can come back from a losing game! CRYPTID: The Creepy Card Battling Game is one of the most skill-based and strategic card games out there where any deck can win. No more single-turn matches and overly-meta deck builds. Customize your deck, learn the best strategies and timings, and win!

—description from the designer

This is the outrageous NSFW edition of Manic Minute. Be prepared to learn things about your friends and family that you wish you could unlearn!

How to play: Players each receive a pen and paper and when the first player turns over a category, players must write down as many answers as they can in one Manic Minute. When the timer finishes, each player stops writing and one by one reads out their answers to the group.

Answers that match with another player are worth zero points - so consider blocking your opponents by guessing what they might be writing down too! Players should justify their answers and arguing is the point of the game!

At the end of the round each player totals up their points and the person with the most points wins the round! Another player turns over a card and the next Manic Minute can begin!

This is the game where you can write what you really think – not suitable for children, work or school (or with you parents)! Do you dare to write the most outrageous answer?

—description from the publisher

In the nostalgic backdrop of 1980s Maine, the town's most coveted bachelor weds a picture-perfect orphan woman. Their wedding rehearsal is set along the breathtaking Atlantic coastline. Yet, the happily-ever-afters are cut short when the groom's lifeless body is discovered at the base of a cliff, with the toxicology results beyond anyone's darkest imagination...

Sole Tunnels is a semi-abstract game for 1 player who takes on the role of a light infantry company commander attempting to clear a small complex of tunnels underneath a dense urban area.

The player must make decisions and manage Enablers, the array of skilled troops and machinery that exist to support and augment the power of the main Maneuver Units in the forces they command. They must explore the tunnel complex and contend with enemy defenders, barriers, mines and IEDs that frustrate them in terms of both taking undue casualties and too much time to accomplish the mission.

The game ends when the player has fulfilled the victory conditions of the scenario they are playing. This may involve locating Civilian hostages or intelligence-yielding items, sweeping and clearing the complex, or inflicting casualties on the enemy.

A simple, fast and all-too-modern adaptation of the randomized tile-laying dungeon crawler.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In the sleepy pioneer town of ghost creek, life tends to be pretty predictable. That is until you find yourself behind bars with the sheriff accusing you of murder! Being an upstanding, law -abiding citizen, you're shocked, even more so when you hear that you're destined for the gallows. You strike a deal with the sheriff - if you're able to find the identity of the real killer in under an hour, he'll let you walk free. Otherwise, it'll be time to meet your maker!
You'll need to solve each of the four puzzles to gain clues as to which one of ten outlaws is the real killer. Only by solving the puzzles correctly will you be able to pick the right person and walk out of that jailhouse a free man!
The clock is ticking!
Set contains: four puzzle envelopes, instructions, hints, solutions, & exit reveal

Bon Appetit: the card game is a family-friendly card game that whisks players away to a world of culinary delights and tabletop strategy. The game's theme revolves around a whimsical, food-centric universe where each player is a chef competing to create the most tantalizing array of dishes. Players collect a variety of ingredient cards, from the main entrees to zesty sauces, while navigating through a flavorful journey with characters like the Sea Captain's Dressing and Pumpkin Butter Soup. The characters are not just game pieces; they are vibrant personalities that add spice and humor to the gameplay, each with their unique backstory and culinary charm.

Gameplay is a hand management, drafting, take that card game, as chefs must wisely choose their ingredients and pair them to perfection, all while countering the moves of their fellow culinary competitors. The goal is simple yet engaging: complete the most appetizing food sets to score points. Players must decide when to play it safe with classic combinations or when to risk it all for a dash of unexpected flavor. The game ends when the final card is plated, and the chef with the highest score garners the coveted title of "Legendary Master Chef." Whether it's through collecting the right mix of cards or using the special abilities of the food characters to outmaneuver opponents, Bon Appetit: the card game promises a feast of fun, serving up a perfect recipe for game night.

—description from the publisher

A riveting card game for 2-6 players that combines the classic thrill of War with the strategic depth of Rock-Paper-Scissors, and introduces Blitz Cards - powerful abilities that turn the tides of battle, changing winning conditions, or giving players an edge over their opponents through attacks and counters.

In Oathen: Blitz, you'll take part in an epic battle between three powerful factions: the Norn, followers of the old Gods and the old ways; the Nivet, defenders of the many realms; and the Akhul, the orken clans of the Jotnar. These three armies are locked in a brutal war for Midgard.

Every round players flip over the top card of their War Deck, a randomly dealt collection of cards that feature one of the three factions and a unique strength value. Victory is determined by which factions are present in the battle (Norn beats Nivet, Nivet beats Akhul, and Akhul beats Norn.) If all three factions are present in the battle, the winners are determined by which faction has the highest combined strength of their combatants.

However, war is never so simple..

At any time during the game, players can choose to play one of their Blitz Cards – an arsenal of instant play abilities that change the conditions of battle; such as switching the faction of your war card to be among the victors, annihilating an opponent's card to remove them from battle, or directly attacking and destroying cards from another player's War Deck - these Blitz Cards add a layer of unpredictability and strategic play through player chosen actions.

As the war wages on, cards are permanently removed from the game, escalating the intensity. Players must battle with dwindling forces, maneuvering through the ever-increasing stakes until only one player emerges victorious.

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach the youth hostel on the hill wins the game.

The game was published as a supplement to the youth magazine "ABC-Zeitung" No. 12/1977.

—user summary

One person is appointed as the game leader. He divides the tokens evenly among the other players. He also gets the map of Europe and the scale. The station that most players vote for is used as the starting point for guessing the distance. The game leader asks each player about the distance between the starting point and another transmitter. For the sake of simplicity, guessing is rounded up to the full 50 kilometers according to the scale. If you guess incorrectly, you have to pay the game master 50 tokens by how many 50 kilometers he misjudged. However, if the distance is guessed correctly, the person concerned receives 100 tokens from the game master.
The winner is the person who has the most tokens left at the end. By changing the output station when guessing, there are countless possibilities for the game.

The game was published as a promotional giveaway.

—user summary

As the name suggests, it is for you to play while you are camping. Of course, players are allowed to add more tasks to the game to play it everywhere.
In order to make your camping more fun, 124 new tasks have been carefully designed to trick your friends! A bag is also included to make it easier for you to take the game to the campsite.

Objective: Destroy the death star by launching proton torpedoes at exactly the right time. To do this you will adjusting the position of one of your attack leaders in the trench as they approach and when the launch display is at 000000 Both aiming markers up-down   & left-right need be on the green centre spaces on target tracks to be on target.
Will you risk making small adjustments and make it easier for your escorts and leaders to be hit or make big adjustments and make it harder to be hit.
You will have to decide to take HIGH or LOW risk roll for each roll.
HIGH RISK: Will get you a lower numbered small adjustment counter but there is more chance of an escorts or a leader being Objective: Destroy the death star by launching proton torpedoes at exactly the right time. To do this you will adjusting the position of one of your attack leaders in the trench as they approach and when the launch display is at Both aiming markers up-down   & left-right   need be on the green centre spaces on target tracks to be on target.
Will you risk making small adjustments and make it easier for your escorts and leaders to be hit or make big adjustments and make it harder to be hit.
A hit result will destroy the waves escorts 2 first then the waves leader, remove then and marker and roll again.
When a wave has attacked or been destroyed remove any remaining markers and setup again for the next wave as in the setup phase.
Waves: Gold Leader, Red Leader then Red 5 “Luke”
will attack 1st. will attack 2nd. will attack last. destroyed.
LOW RISK: Will get you a higher numbered adjustment counter and less chance of an escorts or a leader being destroyed.

—description from the designer

A 19th century indoor game designed to simulate the sport of Polo.  It was described, with rules, in "The Young Folks' Cyclopædia of Games and Sports" (John D Champlin & Arthur E Bostwick), published in 1890 by Henry Holt & Co of New York [hence the US spelling. 'Parlor'].  Played on a 15×19 grid of squares, which are shaded alternately like a draughts / checkers board, play taking place only on the shaded squares. 
 The game has some resemblance to more modern games such as Bladder and Phutball, and is one of several 'Parlor' simulations from the same source, the others being Base Ball, Croquet, Quoits, [Horse] Race, and Ten Pins.  Players may move any of their six men on the board, with the aim of securing and advancing a thirteenth piece, representing the ball, into the opponent's goal, but the distance that can be moved is determined by the throw of dice.

You start out as an unknown, unequipped, inexperienced and lonesome pirate with one mission: becoming the most notorious pirate and win the game.

You earn notoriety every time you triumph. To achieve this, you need to expand your treasure- hunting abilities and crew strength to conquer in battle. This is done by acquiring crew
members, items (like a pistol) and ship enhancements (like additional sails). There are also opportunities to trigger happenings and events your way. These assets come in the form of
pirate cards, which you lay next to your player board to visualise the expansion of your crew and ship. Then you will:

1.gather rumors that will enable you to unfold the location of treasures;
2.sail the seas with enough strength or speed to prevail in battle while hunting
treasures;
3.collect treasures by going ashore on islands that you know hide a treasure.

While treasure hunting you encounter ships of other players. Defeating them is a triumph that gains you notoriety. Defeating the ghost ship also gains you notoriety.

And then there is rum, a scarce resource that motivates
your crew into additional actions and special moves.

Will you lead your crew to triumphs and adventure the fastest
to become the most notorious pirate and win the game?

New cards are shuffled into the base game for even more diversity in Forest Shuffle adding even more variety to your gaming tables: Forest Shuffle Alpine

Will you manage to lure the most valuable species into your forest? Because the following also applies above the tree line: depending on the number of players, cards are removed from the game before it begins.

The first expansion of the tactical card game about the animals and the forest by designer Kosch scores with a deck of 36 new multifunctional cards, including two new tree species. Some of the cards bring a nice twist to the rules of the base game.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Three Li'l Birdies is a hand-crushing, chaos-creating, family-friendly card game that is a little like Snap and can leave you in similar positions to those found in a rugby scrum. The aim of the game is to collect sets of 3 identical bird cards. When someone does, everyone must try to grab a wooden egg that is in the middle of the table. If you grab the egg first, you win the set. But just because you win the set doesn't mean you keep it...

Gameplay: Players take turns to draw cards away from themselves from the middle of the table. If a player draws a bird card that is identical to one that they already have, the player can make a pile of the matching cards in front of them. If a player collects * 3 identical bird cards * everyone must try to grab a wooden egg that is in the middle of the table. Whoever gets the egg first, wins the set of cards too.

HOWEVER, there are some sneaky cards called consequence cards. These cards can make players lose, gain, swap and steal entire sets of bird cards from other players, meaning that no set is safe!!! If a player draws a consequence card, they must follow the instructions on the card, and then place the consequence cards and any other relevant cards in the discard pile.

The player who collects 5 sets of bird cards, wins the game.

Simple!

—description from the designer

Embark on an enchanting culinary journey in 吃早餐啦!! ("Taiwanese Breakfast"), where players will serve a diverse array of Taiwanese delicacies.

Players aim to befriend others by guessing their preferences through treating them to meals. With 19 hand-drawn breakfast maps, the game combines local cuisine and cultural charm.

The game involves actions like playing action cards, discarding and drawing cards, and treating friends to breakfast by matching their preferences. The game ends when a player satisfies two new friends or accumulates eight breakfast cards in their rack and nearby area. The player with the highest total score wins.

Unleash the Star League Command Lance! Includes the Atlas II and Thunder Hawk, a new variant of the Phoenix Hawk, and a reposed and pre-painted Orion—no assembly required—along with four MechWarrior pilot cards and four Alpha Strike cards. Perfect for BattleTech and Alpha Strike action!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: BattleTech: Second Star League Assault Lance
description:
Includes the Emperor, Argus and Helios, a new variant of the Dashi, Prometheus, reposed and pre-painted, along with a bonus Coolant Truck—no assembly required—along with eight MechWarrior pilot cards and five Alpha Strike cards. Perfect for BattleTech and Alpha Strike action!

—description from the publisher

This mat centers on the world of Twycross, site of a major engagement between the Federated Commonwealth and Clan Jade Falcon during the initial Clan Invasion and one of the Inner Sphere’s first victories.

This mat showcases battlefields from some of the earliest BattleTech fiction, including Thunder Rift from the first Gray Death Legion novel, Decision at Thunder Rift.

The reverse offers the frigid wasteland of Misery, site of the titanic clash between the Wolf’s Dragoons and the Ryuken in Wolves on the Border.

Expand your battlefield with the newest BFM, measuring a titanic 48 inches (121 cm) by 72 inches (182 cm)! This mat challenges players with the combined maps from Map Pack: City on one side, and the Lunar landscape on the reverse.

Force Manual: Davion details the Armed Forces of the Federated Suns, examining the combat commands of the Federated Suns and their histories, tactics, unit crests, paint schemes, and notable personnel. This sourcebook includes special rules for unique character abilities, faction-specific rules, force building options, and a mini-Technical Readout–everything you need to field your favorite Federated Suns unit on the tabletop, or create your own.

Recognition Guide, Vol. 2: ilClan collects some of these brand-new Inner Sphere and Clan BattleMechs and vehicles, bristling with the latest technology to push the envelope on modern battlefields. This volume is the companion to Recognition Guide, Vol. 1: Classics, and contains material originally published in the bestselling PDF-only BattleTech Recognition Guide: ilClan series.

Force Manual: Kurita details the armed forces of the Draconis Combine, examining the combat commands of the Draconis Combine and their histories, tactics, unit crests, paint schemes, and notable personnel. This sourcebook includes special rules for unique character abilities, faction-specific rules, force building options, and a mini-Technical Readout–everything you need to field your favorite Draconis Combine unit on the tabletop, or create your own.

boardgame: BattleTech: Third Star League Striker Team
description: Unleash the Third Star League Striker Star! Includes the Jackalope, Kintaro, Hammerhead, Havoc, and a pre-painted Lament, along with a bonus J-27 Ordinance Truck—no assembly required—along with ten MechWarrior pilot cards and six Alpha Strike cards. Perfect for BattleTech and Alpha Strike action!

boardgame: BattleTech: McCarron's Armored Cavalry Assault Lance
description: The McCarron’s Armored Cavalry Assault Lance! Included is the new Tian-Zong, a re-posed Black Knight, and new variants of the Awesome and jumping Starslayer—no assembly required—along with four MechWarrior pilot cards and four Alpha Strike cards. Perfect for BattleTech and Alpha Strike action!

Enter the Hinterlands with this comprehensive campaign guide to some of the hottest combat zones in the ilClan era! A spiritual successor to the classic Hot Spots duology, this sourcebook offers all the rules and contracts any unit needs for their ongoing BattleTech games.

Whether defending Sudeten from a Hell’s Horses onslaught, standing with Calamity Kell’s Arc Royal Liberty Coalition, providing some forceful incentives to the Alyina Mercantile League’s latest deal, or securing the Tamar Pact’s place in the Inner Sphere, the opportunities for enterprising commanders are endless!

This sourcebook includes complete campaign rules built out of the BattleTech: Mercenaries boxed set, as well as contract and game hooks for battles with–and against–every major Hinterlands faction.

ilKhan’s Eyes Only is a BattleTech sourcebook detailing the events on Terra, around the former Fortress Republic, and the coreward regions of the Capellan Confederation from 3151 to mid-3152. Included are a full historical summary of events in those regions of space, personality and unit profiles of key players–many appearing here for the first time–and game information along with a glossy campaign map to bring it all to your BattleTech tabletop.

boardgame: Battletech: Third Star League Battle Group
description:
The Third Star League Battle Star! Includes the Wendigo, Excalibur, Peacekeeper, Malice, and a pre-painted Savage Wolf, along with a bonus Savior Repair Vehicle—no assembly required—along with ten MechWarrior pilot cards and six Alpha Strike cards. Perfect for BattleTech and Alpha Strike action!

—description from the publisher

The Olelo deck has 100 letter cards and 3 wild cards.  The game plays like Texas Hold 'Em poker without bidding or betting. Players try to make the highest-scoring word using letters from their hand and/or the community cards (called the Circus) on the table.

In each hand, all players are dealt 2 cards, and there are 3 rounds: 
a) 3 cards are dealt to the Circus (the Drop)
b) a 4th card is dealt to the Circus (the Joey)
c) a 5th card is dealt (the Ringer)
In each round, after the cards are added to the Circus each player may choose to discard one of their cards and get dealt a new one.

After the third round each player shows their hand and announces the word they are making. The player with the highest-scoring word adds its score to their total; any tied players all add their scores. The first player to 50 or 100 points wins the game.

There is an Easy Play version for younger players, with these differences: 

     The deck is split into a vowels deck and a consonants deck. 
     Players' hand cards come from the consonants deck. 
     The rounds are: a) the Circus starts with one vowel; b) the Circus gets a second vowel; c) (optional) the Circus gets a consonant.
     The player(s) with the highest scoring word each get 1 point. The first player to 5 or 10 points wins the game.


The name "Olelo" was chosen because it is Hawaiian for "spoken words", and it's a palindrome.

boardgame: RAID: Random Adventures in Dungeons – Expansion #1
description:
The first Expansion for RAID Random Adventures In Dungeons is here!

This Expansion Includes:
All new event rooms to explore.
New magical items to find and use
Random dungeon encounters
EXTITING FOOTWEAR!
2 new characters classes, a new race, and bonus card.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Embark on a treacherous journey as a cunning Scavenger, working for Diabolical Drilling Co.- an intergalactic drilling company known for its low morality and insatiable greed.

Delve into 10 sinister contracts, plunder breathtaking worlds, and tackle dispicable missions for some of the galaxy’s most nefarious figures.

To win this solo/co-op game, you must navigate your scavenger vessel through uncharted solar systems , fulfilling missions and mining valuable minerals to complete 10 contracts. Strategic decision making and skilful navigation are crucial to impressing management and securing a coveted seat on the Board if Scum!

—description from the designer

In a dystopian future, a once fertile planet has been reduced to nothing but junk and waste. As the head of a robot branch for the mighty RoboBotCorp you're tasked with constructing bots and sending them off to prep and construct buildings, clean up the junkyard, and turn in data to the head Data bot.

Bots! is a worker placement game with a fun bumping mechanism as bots are pushed along conveyor belts and eventually recycled. Play continues until enough bots have been recycled to push the data bot to the end of it's track.

The Hippest of the Hip

Strategy is the name of the game as you compete to be the coolest in town in the game of Hipster. Collect your gear and play against others in your quest to be the hipper of all.

But watch out, because once things become too cool they’re no longer cool. Can you be faster, can you be cooler, can you be Hipster?

The Revenge is a full, stand-alone expansion for Hipster adding in new cards and new ways to win!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Maple Valley: Roaming Raven and Feathered Friends Mini-Expansions
description:
Roaming Raven Mini-Expansion includes:

     1 wooden screen-printed Raven bit
     1 rules card


Feathered Friends Mini-Expansion includes:

     10 Feathered Friend cards
     12 wooden screen-printed feathers
     1 rules card

It's everyone for themselves in Dead Wasteland, the tense, taut, and thoroughly entertaining card game.

Civilization has fallen and the dead have risen. Zombies roam the wastes of our once great cities. The remnants of humanity fend for themselves, scavenging what they can to survive - food, water, and a flashlight - while avoiding the horde.

Along the way, equip yourself with what you need, while dodding the dead. The zombies will constantly chase after you and once one zombie appears, they'll swarm, more and more. Escaping alive will take all the supplies and skills you have.

Avoid the dead, shoot to kill, and play to win. Get the action-packed, thrilling, lively zombie card game and see if you have what it takes to survive the Dead Wasteland.

—description from the back of the box

Four friendly ghosts lived in their big old mansion for centuries. They had tons of fun scaring and tricking the villagers and throwing their cute but, of course, horrifying parties. What a wonderful afterlife!
The ghosts loved inviting guests, but not every one of them was so grateful for the invitation… A creepy Monster decided to have their mansion all to itself, so it kicked them out and nestled in the attic to play the ghosts’ favorite video games non-stop. 
The little ghosts need your help! Together with them, you will explore the graveyard looking for keys to the rooms and the attic, find your way around traps, accumulate power, and then use it to get their sweet, spooky home back, sending the ungrateful Monster to its world.
But be careful! The Monster becomes more powerful every round. Throw the dice and think through all your moves together!

boardgame: What if…?: Choose the life of your dreams
description:
Life is a beautiful adventure with so many scenarios, and in “What if…?”, you can get a taste of some of them to see which ones will make your character the happiest!
“What if…? Choose the life of your dreams” is a board game life simulator where your character can experience various lifestyles, like being an adventurer, entrepreneur, or careerist. Players will move around the board by choosing one of the numbers rolled on the dice—this way, they’ll be able to plan their moves—and perform life-changing actions. Your characters can find love, get a promotion or demotion, adopt a pet, and even buy a place to call home. You may earn or lose money, but after all, to win the game, your character must be the happiest—the goal is to be the first to reach 50 Happiness points.
This colorful and fascinating board game is a perfect choice for kids and families!

Welcome to the grand fair in a small Japanese town! The guests are about to start flooding these cozy streets: extraordinary merchants, gardeners, and even fortune tellers. But you’re not here to sell — Joban Takemura, the dragon who organizes the fair, needs as much help as he can get. And he’s even ready to name one of you his best assistant!
In Traveling Fair, players will help Joban arrange the stalls at the fair so that all the merchants are satisfied. Everyone has their own goal to complete, but who will do it the best? Use cards and place the stalls in the right spots to get the most Influence Points and become the winner and Joban's best helper.
Traveling Fair is a cozy and atmospheric board game, perfect for a family game night!

boardgame: Unfair: Fairytale Legendary Steampunk Underground Expansion
description:
Build the city's greatest theme park, whatever it takes, with more themes now available for your park in the Unfair Expansion: Fairytale Legendary Steampunk Underground. These new themes can be mixed with old favorites and with each other:


    Fairytale: Try your luck as you encounter beanstalks, dragons, talking animals, and more - but be careful what you wish for!
    Legendary: Bring fame and fortune to your park by building rides as iconic as the legendary figures of ancient mythology.
    Steampunk: An alternate future springing from a glorious past where steam power and mechanical automation continue to flourish.
    Underground: Go on a journey to the center of the earth! Dig a big hole. Find exotic treasures buried in unexpected places!


You can also tailor the game to suit your preferred play style with new game changer cards.

—description from the designer

The ultimate all-American trivia game: Screen-free family fun with easy-to-learn gameplay for 2–8 players.

Put your knowledge to the test: Explore the key stats that make up every state of the USA, from Alabama to Wyoming, and learn more as you play.

Will speedy reactions beat expert knowledge? Be first to collect 5 cards to win! ​Explore every state of the USA from Alabama to Wyoming in this fast and fun trivia game that celebrates the geography of America.

With this exciting extension of the best-selling 50 States book series, you’ll go deeper into the key stats behind all 50 states. From palm trees and oranges in the Sunshine State, through the Heart of Dixie, past the Great Lakes, and all the way to Alaska’s last frontier, it’s time to explore! So pack your bags and say later ’gator… the great States await!
 Is Vermont bigger than Hawaii? Which is the oldest state? Are the mountains taller in Alaska or California? Speedy guessing is rewarded in this fast-moving family game, so fire right in and learn as you play!

Players race to slap their hands on the card they think best fits the chosen category, such as ‘largest population’, for example. When all players have slapped, reveal the cards to discover the correct answer. The winning player keeps their card and chooses the next category. First to grab five correct cards wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DCeased: Dark Nights Metal Crossover Set
description:
Dark Knights Metal is a comics event that tells the story of an invasion of Earth 0 by monstrous counterparts of Batman from the Dark Multiverse. With this Crossover Set, players will be able to have these alternate versions of Batman join in the DCeased Anti-Life crisis.

All of the characters, except for the “Robins”, have ID cards to be played as Super Heroes. These vicious versions of Batman are very powerful, each bringing a unique set of skills to fight the Anti-Life zombies.

Battle Freaks: The Card Game is a solo expandable card game. In Battle Freaks, you will assemble your crew of genetically enhanced creatures, send them into a harsh graffiti-soaked city and aim to become the undisputed rulers of the streets by defeating formidable enemies.

You begin the game by building a deck of your favourite fighters and gear through deck construction.

During the game, you will play fighters from your hand, sending them into the city, represented by location cards. You can then utilize the fighters to maneuver between locations and defeat enemies through powerful card abilities.

The player wins the game when the boss is defeated!

—description from the designer

The Black Rose has summoned 4 horsemen who could destroy everything. Reports of their nine strength even reach the past! Can the power of time stop the apocalypse?

This expansion contains 4 new Magi, 4 new schools of magic, 2 new game modes (Apocalypse and Tournament). In addition, you can now create magi yourself.

boardgame: Arkanum: Skirmish Game of Wizard Duels
description: Arkanum is a fast-paced and dynamic miniature tabletop wargame that challenges you to adapt to ever-changing situations. In a dark and grim world of wizardry, power is the only thing that matters. Choose from different magic schools, each with its own strengths and weaknesses, and create your own Wizard character. Summon loyal or treacherous minions to fight by your side and achieve your goals, whatever they may be. Whether you play casually or competitively, Arkanum will test your tactical skills and creativity in every match. You cannot make plans in this world, the only way to power is to adapt.

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Rebirth – Gaea Reborn
description:
Gaea, the incarnation of the Earth Mother, has been reborn and will not rest until the world has returned to its original state - or until you defeat her.

This expansion contains 1 new Mage, 1 new Avatar, 1 new School of Magic and 2 new game modes: Paths and Scenarios.

boardgame: Bite the Bullet: You are a legend – Expansion
description:
This expansion for Bite the Bullet contains six extra scenarios. In addition, a bonus scenario combines two maps.

The expansion includes enlarged maps of all scenarios and eight extra characters with their standees and counters.

Important: you need a copy of volume 1 in order to play this expansion.

The expansion is sold, printed and delivered through the Amazon network.

Here is the link to Amazon US: https://a.co/d/9yA9pVB

Alternatively, you can acquire the PDF files on Wargamevault:
https://www.wargamevault.com/product/478285/Bite-the-bullet--expansion?affiliate_id=4328857

Step into the shoes of a fearless adventurer, following in the footsteps of archeology heroes like Indiana Jones or Lara Croft! In Dice Raiders you will be solving the puzzles of the ancients. Whether you choose to visit the Temple of Anubis in Egypt, solve the Aztec mysteries in Mexico, or uncover the secrets of the Terracotta Army, each adventure promises captivating secrets for you to explore. Take on a journey with the best archaeology adventures in the world! Dr. Elena Cruz, Max Stone, Jack Croft and Professor Evan Harris. Approach the puzzles with the same adventurous mindset as Indiana Jones himself. You will need to use your strategic thinking to overcome obstacles and challenges as you explore the temple and uncover its secrets. With its engaging gameplay, unique mechanics, and immersive theme, Dice Riders is a game that will keep you coming back for more.

Dice Raiders is a Roll and Write Print and Play game. There are a couple of gamesheets available, each set in another ancient mystery theme with some unique puzzles. In this game you will roll the dice and decide what puzzle you will try to tackle in each round. Each of them will give you points in a different way, so you need to decide ahead what you will be focusing on. However, you must adapt to the movements of your opponents, as they can undermine your plan. You want to be the most clever one among the fellow explorers.

Game is fast to play and hard to put down, you can even take a tour and visit all the archeological places in one game at night easily.

—description from the publisher

Polemarchs are a feature added to Mythic Battles: Pantheon. 
They work exactly as Jarls (added to Mythic Battles: Ragnarök).

You can add Polemarchs attachment cards to a skirmish game by including them with the units availible during the draft.

For 1 RP, a player can recruit a Polemarch instead of a Unit. 
At the end of the recruitment phase and starting with the first player, the players attach each recruited Polemarch to a troop, with then gains its ability. 
A troop can only have one attachment.

Polemarchs are attachments and therefor cannot be targeted. They have non of their own characteristics and are not represented by miniatures.

A Polemarch is destroyed if its troop is destroyed. 
It is returned to play if the troop is also returned.

boardgame: Hegemony: Lead Your Class to Victory – Controlling the Narrative Promo Card
description:
A promo card delivered by supporting The Dice Tower 2024.


Controlling the Narrative
Propose a Bill and call for an Immediate Vote without spending Influence. You may spend Influence to affect the outcome, after everyone else has done so.

Get ready for a thrilling political adventure in Kursi, the latest card game sensation for 2-4 players. Assume the role of a political strategist navigating the complex landscape of Indian elections, where strategic decisions and cunning moves can make or break your party's chances of getting into power.

In Kursi, players strategically appoint electoral candidates, manage capital for impactful rallies, and spread influence to control key political assets. Balancing popularity and media control is essential, as is maintaining a delicate reputation to play game-changing cards at the right moment.

The ultimate objective is clear: win the most votes and secure the majority of seats. Engage in strategic battles with rival political masterminds, adapt to the ever-changing political scenario, and emerge as the victorious architect behind the winning party.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 避けられない災害 (Inevitable Disasters)
description:
In this must-follow trick-taking game with a bidding system, players know the color of the cards in your opponents' hands by the color of the back of the cards, but the "0" and the "9" have neutral backs, so you know where the weakest and the strongest cards are, but you don't know which is which. Outsmart your oponents by bidding the correct amount of disasters that will affect the world!

—description from the publisher

Tape l'Étape is a bicycle race run in stages. By getting rid of your cards faster than your opponents, get ahead and win the race!

The race takes place in a succession of stages, each with its specificities. The runners get rid of their cards one after the other. To win the stage, you must be the first runner to have all your cards in hand; the more cards your competitors keep in their hands, the more you gain in the ranking. Use the jokers to spice up the race and catch up or consolidate your lead! For a faster game, play in sprint mode, and for a longer race with more twists and turns, play in endurance mode. Other variations are possible: draw 2, 3, 4, 5... stages, take all the 5 stages but play only one selection per player. A five-minute race or a week-long race, anything is possible!

—description from the publisher

Players will ring youthful exuberance to the table with the Iceman & Shadowcat Character Pack. These two fan-favorite X-Men add new low-cost options to the Affiliation. With show-stopping affiliated Team Tactic Cards in tow, these rising stars are the jewel of any mutant Squad on the battlefield.

Fan-favorite original X-Men are beloved characters in any Roster
New Team Tactic Cards for Uncanny X-Men add options to the Affiliation
Synergies with existing popular miniatures up the ante for mutant fans

Two iconic mutants join Marvel: Crisis Protocol in this new pack! With his mutant ability to teleport, Nightcrawler is able to land a decisive blow or help get his teammates out of the line of fire at the perfect moment. He is joined by Bishop, who wields advanced weaponry and his mutant ability to absorb and redirect energy in tandem. Together, they enhance any team, but especially the Uncanny X-Men and X-Force affiliations with their abilities and 3 Team Tactic cards.

Adds the characters Bishop & Nightcrawler to Marvel: Crisis Protocol
Both miniatures feature multiple assembly options
These characters enhance the Uncanny X-Men and X-Force affiliations
Includes 3 Team Tactic cards that give them even more options in battle

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
This is a card game where you will have to try to preserve the different flora and fauna species in the Serra do Xistral.
It is a balancing game where different species have different advantages and disadvantages in the environment that closely match reality.

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Rebirth – Rotas Box
description:
Rotas box is the deluxe case that contains all unlocked material during the Kickstarter campaign of Black Rose Wars: Rebirth. It is composed of 6 smaller boxes (Madness, The Silk Road, North Wind, Last Dynasty, Antiquities, and Once Upon a Time) so you can use even only theme, with reduced setup time!
Inside the Rotas Box, you will find more than 50 very detailed models (made in high-quality PVC): among them, there are 17 miniatures of 50+ mm high and 1 huge model with a height of 100 mm! Furthermore, 4 new Schools of Magic (Oracle, Heka, Chaos, and Druidic), to have much more choices in your Black Rose Wars: Rebirth games!

You can also use 12 new mages (with their personal spells and mage boards), 7 new Avatars (with their Command Cards, to manage their IA and play breathtaking semi-coop games), 6 new Room Tiles (to combine with the ones in the Core Box and add more variability) and other games material, such Quest Cards, Event Cards, Legendary Scenarios and much more!

—description from the publisher

Challenge your friends & family in this action packed game of challenges. Throw, dodge, Dinosaurch, jump, roll, shoot & balance your way to victory!

Fast Paced Fun - Compete in 132 ridiculous challenges to earn tokens and win big.

Made for Kids, Teens & Adults - This family game can be enjoyed by people of all ages - across the generations!

Challenges are quick to learn which makes it perfect for family games nights, groups of friends or a team building activity. It can be played indoors or outdoors.

boardgame: Earthborne Rangers: Moments on the Path
description:
Moments on the Path adds new cards to the path deck in the form of ephemeral moments, narrative choices, and attachments.

The cards in this expansion capture fleeting moments in time--moments like catching the scent of flowers on the breeze, hearing a tree fall somewhere in the distance, or encountering a predator with unique characteristics--adding texture to the emergent narrative.

Contains 30 unique cards.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Осколки бесконечности: 5 промокарт (Shards of Infinity: 5 Promo Cards)
В погоне за счастьем: KS1 промо – Карты питомцев (The Pursuit of Happiness: KS1 Promo – Pets)
description:
A mini-expansions for The Pursuit of Happiness.
The Pets mini-expansion adds pets to the Item-Activity cards. Pets are different in that you do not have to use card actions to advance them - they do this by themselves during upkeep as long as you feed them (pay the upkeep cost).


Промо мини-дополнение к базовой игре В погоне за счастьем.
Мини-дополнение Карты питомцев добавляет домашних питомцев в набор карт Имущество-Занятие.

Этот промо-набор на русском языке прилагался в награду за предзаказ русского издания базовой игры.

Ready to explore the history of Earth?

Welcome to QUARTETnary: the card game about the geological time scale!

Discover the formation of mountain ranges, the evolution of animals and plants, the changing face of the Earth through the ages and more, all captured within 60 beautifully illustrated cards.

Playable by anyone over the age of 8, QUARTETnary is suitable for groups of 2 to 5 players and can also be enjoyed solo (thanks to its domino-mode). Gameplay follows that of the classic card game Quartets (similar to Go Fish and Happy Families), where players aim to collect sets of four cards belonging to a specific group; in this case, a geological time unit.

Make the most complete timeline of the history of the Earth and win!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Chinese War of Liberation: Prairie Fire Following the Sun
description:
This Chinese War of Liberation System includes the Northern Campaign (Prairie Fire Following the Star) and the Southern Campaign (Prairie Fire Following the Sun).

Both games are released at the same time, allowing you to re-experience the three major battles from a more macro-strategic perspective.

Each round represents three months. Units are on the corps and division level. Each hex on the grid represents a distance of 40 kilometers.

Prairie Fire Following the Sun focuses on the Southern theater of the war and contains four independent scenarios:

     Battle of Crossing the River 
     Battle of Huaihai 
     Battle of Huaihe River in Henan Province
     Catching Fire Starts a Prairie Fire


The game can be combined with Prairie Fire Following the Star to simulate the entire war.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

boardgame: The Ships of the Father Expansion
description:
This "what if" collection of aircraft carriers, suggested by an avid wargamer and drawn up by the folks at Avalanche Press, comprises three ships that did not see action at Leyte Gulf, but are sisters of ships that do have unit counters in the game. The included carriers are the Essex-class Tarawa and Boxer, and the Commencement Bay-class Bairoko (Bairoko sisters in the Leyte Gulf game are Block Island and Gilbert Islands).

—user summary
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
In Widgets n' Digit$ you play the role of a business owner. Your goal is to scale your widget manufacturing process and earn the most money by the end of the fiscal year.

Each turn you'll produce widgets in 4 steps. 

      First you create a raw material, which becomes a part, then a finished good, and finally a widget which sells for $20.


In order to increase the amount of widgets you produce you need to upgrade each of the 4 steps by buying upgrade cards, but due to limited resources you can usually only afford 1-2 cards per turn. This means that upgrading will leave you with bottlenecks in your widget supply chain.

You can still profit with bottlenecks by buying the resources you need or selling excess resources in the market. But each transaction shifts the price of goods.

You'll need to adapt your strategy to market economics and carefully consider the risk of each investment in order to win in this economic strategy game!

There are 2 included expansions:

Worker Wars - Introduces a competitive labor market for the workers who run your widget making process. You'll need to bid against your competitors for the right to hire workers - the wrong price can have devastating impact to your bottom line!

Boardroom Battle - Introduces 12 unique card abilities to the game. You must balance profitability with powerful game controlling abilities.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Brew Up: World War II Miniature Wargaming
description:
2023's revised edition includes concise rules (22 pages) for WWII in a compact 6x9" format. Requires World War II wargame miniatures*, two ten-sided dice, and a tape measure. A few clarifications and updates have been added. 76 AFVs and 20 guns are categorized into 11 armor/penetration levels. Plus 36 pages of Advanced Rules in the 76-page rulebook.
​
You can get playing fast. Brew Up fits between Leon's precursors, simpler Fast Rules, and more complex Tractics. 18 pages of Game Reference Charts (GRC) are spread throughout the book. Because the book has small pages, the GRCs are not collected into a compilation like the centerfold in Fast Rules and at the end in Tractics. Instead, they are free of charge as a PDF file you can download and print, or purchase a coil-bound GRC booklet from Lulu — GRC pdf & booklet do not include rules, just ten pages of Game Reference charts.
​

    Any model scale will work, from 3mm to 28mm, most commonly used are 12 mm to 20 mm. Individual figures are widely used, but multiple men on a stand can work by marking casualties with a lichen "bush" or using the Team Fire variant. 


Each side's unit size is typically from a squad to a platoon (10-40 figures) per side and several tanks and other vehicles (like a platoon or a company, 5-17 vehicles). On a +/- 4x6' table, a ground scale of 25 meters per inch, or with small scales could use centimeters instead on a +/- 30x60" dining table.

Pavlova Coco is Blanc-mange Coco with drawings: each player tries to create the funniest comic book possible with 3 cards.

In Pavlova Coco, each player receives 11 cards with drawings depicting the men from the Coco Blanc-Manger game on a beach. The objective is to make other players laugh by forming a comic book with 3 cards. A game is played on 4 points.
Each player receives 11 cards. A player (the BD master) puts 1 of his cards on the table. All other players choose 2 cards from their game to form a fun 3-card comic book. The BD master chooses the funniest combinations.

Use speed and strategy to make the most money as a cattle rancher in this whimsical card game.

Gain a resource card from the market by being the first each round to spot the most numerous card type revealed.  Use standard actions to gain or exchange cards from the market even if you aren’t the fastest. The more cows you have, the more points you can get from them; press your luck for a massive reward, but be careful because you might lose any that you can’t support!

—description from the publisher

In EGREGO you will Represent the forces of good, evil or chaos and make your play to possess the villagers and conquer the village. Choose from 21 entities and dive into this dark experience of asymmetrical mechanics, strategy and luck.
Discover the village one card at a time and find all the villagers before your opponents. It can also be played in cooperative mode, in which players must unite to make their faction prevail by bending the will of the villagers. You can also play EGREGO in a more narrative variant: Play as your 3 entities and, with the behavioral tendencies they represent, Invent stories about the villagers you possess. EGREGO is the perfect game for lovers of mental challenges, strategic confrontations and storytelling.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Storm Raiders: Resource Specialist Crew Card Promo
description:
Introducing the resource specialists of Atlantis! These five unique Crew will make it easier for you to fulfil Contracts.

Shuffle these into your Crew Card deck for every play count.

Abilities:

     Pay 1 fewer Electrical Parts when fulfilling a Contract
     Pay 1 fewer Mechanical Machinery when fulfilling a Contract
     Pay 1 fewer Orichalcum when fulfilling a Contract
     Pay 1 fewer Fuel when fulfilling a Contract
     Pay 1 fewer General Goods when fulfilling a Contract


—description from the publisher

Object of the game is to be the player who banks the most points by the end of 10, 15, or 20 rounds!​

The first person rolls both dice at once and calls out what their dice numbers add up to: (i.e. a two on one dice + a three on the other adds up to 5 points)

A designated 'banker' will then add that roll to the "Bank Total", then the person clockwise to the first player rolls and does the same thing, with their roll adding to the "Bank Total" for the round. For the first three rolls of a round, points are accumulated with no risk and rolling a 7 is worth 70 points in the "Bank Total" instead of 7. After the first three rolls, if doubles are rolled, the "Bank Total" doubles its value instead of adding the dice value. At any point in the round, before it has ended, a player may say "BANK", adding the current "Bank Total" for the round to their personal score, but will no longer be able to roll or accumulate further points that round. After the first three rolls, if a 7 is rolled all players left in the round score 0 points, the round ends and the next round continues.

There is no limit to how many people bank the points at any time. However, once someone has banked a score, they will sit out the remainder of that round.  They can no longer roll to increase the number of the bank score and they can no longer call out "BANK" during that particular round to score further points.​

The cumulative bank score will continue to increase as players continue to roll in sequence as long as there are still players who have not yet called out "BANK" and as long as no one has rolled a 7.​

Ending A Round:

Once a 7 is rolled OR all players have banked points into their personal accounts, the round will end and another will begin until 10, 15, or 20 rounds are played.

At the beginning of a new round, everyone regains their ability to roll and call out bank again.​

Ending the Game:

Once the 10, 15, or 20 rounds have been played, the player with the highest number of points in their personal bank account wins.​​​​

Two furry friends decided to fill their pantry with the best the forest has to offer. Help the bears find hidden goodies to enjoy during the winter.

Mlsné medvědí příběhy ("Yummy Bear Tales") is a cooperative game in which players work together to beat the game itself (players win or lose together). The task of the players, who together control two pieces (so the pieces don't belong to any particular player), is to gather a supply of food for the winter in the forest using cards.

This simple children's game for children as young as 4 years old is suitable for playing with younger and novice players.

The game is inspired by the fairy tales of the same name about Nedved and Mishka.

The Battle of Kawanakajima is about the fourth Kawanakajima battle that took place in 1561, in which Takeda Shingen and Uesugi Kenshin clashed in Shinano Province (present day Nagano Prefecture) over territorial expansion.

This game is a revival of Shingen vs. Kenshin: Blood bath at Kawanakajima released by Sho Kikaku in 1988 with a modern design.

At the beginning of the game, the Takeda army has a small army compared to the Uesugi army, but as the game progresses, the main forces heading towards Mt. Tsumame come to the main battlefield, so the offense and defense are reversed in the first and second half of the game.

This new edition also includes new units, rules and variants. Both the old and new versions are included.

Game scale

         Distance on the map (1 hex) = approximately 330m
         1 unit = approximately 500 to 1000 men


Number of players = 2 people
Play time = about 90 minutes

Game information:

         Solitaire quality: high
         Rule difficulty: low


—description from the publisher (translated and adapted)


永禄 Eiroku (era) - 1558-1570
辛酉 Metal Rooster (58th term of the sexagenary cycle)
川中島合戦 The Battle of Kawanakajima

In Las Vegas of the 1950's, improve your casino's and be the one that attracts the most visitors!
In this worker placement game, you try and have the most attractive casino's by building VIP facilities, upgrade rooms and hire better staff. Staff members enhance your actions or give you permanent bonuses depicting on slot machines. Some even give you alternative ways of scoring points! Every visitor gives you income and garners points for the end score. Visitors step off the plane on one side of the board and it is your job to attract them to your casino's on the strip on the other side of the board. Beware of mobsters that make your life a bit more difficult!

Have you heard about Inca "chess"? 
It's dynamic intellectual duel where you can fight not only with your own pieces but with common ones. Here they are, ladybugs, Sun God's messengers. And these ladybugs you share with your opponent can fly all over chakana (Inca or Andean cross) and sit on your "ordinary" pieces making you stronger. Trick circle is a game for 1-4 yoyoers that helps develop and innovate your trick set in a fun and interactive way. There are 3 ways to play but I’ll just explain the main way to play here. :)


     Deal the cards to all players.
     Next each player chooses one card from their hand to play in the center.
     All players then use the cards played to create tricks.

In Ubongo, players compete to solve individual puzzles as quickly as they can.

Ubongo: Die Schule der magischen Tiere – Der Film features the same gameplay as the Ubongo base game, but with players now trying to collect the magic animals instead of gems. The game also includes a co-operative variant in which players try to help one another complete the challenges.

Dark Hearts is a solo wargame with RPG elements. As the chosen Dark Heart, the player will lead the dark elf armies to conquer and dominate the world below the surface in the name of the Spider Mistress.

The gameplay revolves around a solitaire wargame with RPG elements contained within a book available in PDF and physical formats. Players will navigate through distinct phases, including conquest, relations, treachery, and events. The modular play feature allows customization, offering a variety of campaigns, enemies, and supplements for replayability.

Players utilize military force, intrigue, and dark magic to conquer territories, manage resources, and overcome rivals, resolving battles and tests through uncomplicated mechanics.

The goal is to lead the dark kin to victory within the allocated number of turns, all while managing relationships with advisors, disposing of rivals, and making decisions to survive the deadly game of power. Failure results in swift death, and only the strongest and most twisted Dark Heart can triumph.

Setup
Use a hexagonal board of any size and topology. A hexhex board of size 4 is recommended. Also have on hand plenty of stones in two colors.

The Pie
Player one places any number of stones on the board in any arrangement. At least one stone placed must be black. Player two selects the color they would like to play. Then the game begins.

Gameplay
There are no turns. At any time a player may place a stone of their own color on an empty space subject to the following restriction: the newly placed stones must have strictly more sight-lines to enemy stones than to friendly stones. It is illegal to place a stone which cannot see any other stones (as the comparison is zero versus zero, which is not a strict majority).

A Sightline
For completeness, a stone has a “sightline” to another stone if there is a straight line of empty spaces between the two stones or if the two stones are directly adjacent. Thus, a stone may have up to six sightlines.

Game End and Scoring
The game ends when neither player wishes to play any more stones. The player with the largest connected group of stones wins. If there is a tie, compare the second largest groups for each player, and so on. If a player runs out of groups for this comparison, they are considered to have an infinite number of "0-size" groups.

The red player is considered to have an additional, imaginary, "1/2-stone" group for the purposes of scoring. In other words, if the game is otherwise a draw, the red player wins.

—description from the designer

Freak War breaks down the classic elements of Trading Card Games to their most basic form to make it simple for beginners, but still engaging for seasoned players. This new style of strategic Battle Card game has Opponents choose a new Creature each round to face off against each other to be the strongest. In this game, being the strongest doesn't just mean having the highest number at a glance, but instead skillfully using a combination of Creatures' effects and Modifier Cards to come out on top.

The winner of each round puts all of the in-play cards into their Win Pile, and in the end the player with the most cards wins the game.

Astralia Chronicles in an trading card game with classic game design and anime aesthetic. Its easy to learn, hard to master gameplay and stratified lore will provide an immersive game experience for both new and expert card gamers.

Astralia Chronicles is a game that is played between 2 and the objective of the game is to win by defeating the opponent Lead card.

Each player choose a Protagonist (his companion during the battle: it determine the cards that player can put in the decks and who will start the game), a Persona Deck (a 5-card deck that represents the idendity of the Lead and are the heart of the strategy, usable via Protagonist unique skill) and the Main Deck (a 45-card deck with all other Characters and action that player can play during the game). Each Protagonist has a peculiar gameplay, strength and weak points.
Each player has a field of 6 slot, where they can call cards from their hand. There are 3 types of cards: Characters (that can fight with other Characters, Scenography or Protagonist), Scenography (cards that remain on the field but they cannot attack or block) and Actions (cards that can be played in both turn with immediate effects).

Across the thirty-three worlds of Iskander’s old empire, the resurgent legions of the Neo-Iskandrians sally forth, seeking to topple the power of the Tripartite Confederacy, and bring about the future of the ‘New Man’. The Great Tyrant has nothing but contempt for the empires of mankind, seeing only the bickering and squabbling of children, children whose small-mindedness would hold back the glories of his Cyclops Dream.

Neo-Iskandrian Forces 
Though numerically few, the Neo-Iskandrians are well equipped, highly motivated, and fanatical believers in Iskander’s vision. In the tumultuous landscape of 1923, Liberty Ignited unfolds amidst relentless continental wars. The heartland, staunchly advocating US neutrality, finds itself at odds with the East and West Coast states. These coastal powers, seeking to shift the nation's power dynamics, make a bold decision to invade the central US states. The struggle for valuable resources becomes the epicenter of a dynamic conflict that will shape the fate of the entire nation.

Liberty Ignited introduces a revolutionary deck-building mechanic. Craft your initial deck and shuffle, the sequence of units determines their availability. This innovative approach adds a layer of depth, allowing players to adapt their tactics based on the evolving composition of their decks.

Embark on tactical endeavors as you execute strategic Actions, including launching attacks, orchestrating movements, conquering territories, and acquiring new units to bolster your forces. Navigate the ongoing continental conflicts of 1923, where dice rolls dictate the outcomes of your decisions. Employ careful unit shifts, aiming to convert your opponent's territories into crucial assets under your faction's control. Refine your deck's makeup through strategic thinning, ensuring a precisely tuned draw deck to confront the diverse challenges.

Diverging from the frozen landscapes of Arctic Ignited, Liberty Ignited invites players to immerse themselves in the ongoing conflicts of 1923. Balancing chance, strategy, and deck-building finesse, the game promises an immersive dieselpunk saga. Liberty Ignited unfolds against the backdrop of a nation in turmoil, offering an unparalleled gaming experience in the rich historical setting of 1923.

description:
A city building game for 1 or more players using the Looney Pyramids game system.

During the game, you will be drawing random pyramids and adding them to your city's grid. Pyramids represent different municipal building zones. Red is Residential ("redsidential"), Yellow is Industrial ("industriyellow"), Blue is Commercial ("bluemercial"), and Green is Parks ("green space"). Black pyramids represent certain necessary, but perhaps unpleasant to be next to, facilities such as wastewater treatment plants and city dumps.

Large pyramids are large developments, and smaller ones are smaller. You will be building out your city subject to certain constraints. Generally, you want colors to be grouped together with the exception of the black pieces, which you usually want touching as few pieces as possible. Depending on which scoring rules you're playing with, these rules of thumb can vary, however.

The scoring rules you choose will have target scores written on them. The sum of all three target scores will determine the score you need to win.
