This expansion pack includes:
• 5th captain components (captain tile, player ship, pirate ship, battle die, 7 nav tool links)
• 5 asteroids
• 1 salvage piece
• 2 fuel canisters
• 4 market demand tokens
• Extra cardboard space coins

This is the pack you missed, the one you were waiting for so much. If it can be said that the genres were the most representative of the era of video stores and the domestic format, they are undoubtedly the fantasy, horror and science fiction genres. This pack includes a range of subgenres around horror such as: Slasher, that unstoppable killer ready to kill any teenager who has not behaved well. Zombies were also resurrected along with the classic vampires and werewolves. Less human were all kinds of monsters and creatures that terrorized homes and cities and in the slipstream of Jaws all kinds of animals and creatures emerged killing humans. For the paranormal genre, Indian cemeteries gave a lot of play.

Genres included:
Dangerous Animals (You're gonna need a bigger boat.)
Monsters (Never feed him after midnight)
Supernatural (Here's Johnny!)
Slasher (She can't hide, no place to hide.)
Vampires & Werewolves (The blood is the life.)
Zombies (Braiiiinsss!!!)

—description from the designer

Este es el pack que echabas de menos, el que tanto esperabas. Si se puede decir que géneros fueron los más representativos de la época de los videoclubs y del formato domestico son sin duda el género fantástico, terror y ciencia ficción. En este pack se incluyen una gama de subgéneros entorno al terror como son: Slasher, ese asesino imparable dispuesto a matar a todo adolescente que no se ha portado bien Los zombis también resucitaron junto con los clásicos vampiros y hombres lobo. Menos humanos fueron todo tipo de monstruos y criaturas que aterraron hogares y ciudades y es que a rebufo de Tiburón surgieron todo tipo de animales y criaturas matando humanos. Para el género paranormal dieron mucho juego los cementerios indios.

Géneros incluidos:
Animales y Bichos peligrosos (Necesitaremos un barco mas grande.)
Monstruos (No les des de comer después de medianoche.)
Paranormal (Aquí esta Johnny!)
Slasher (No puede esconderse; no hay lugar donde esconderse.)
Vampiros & Licántropos (La sangres es vida.)
Zombis (Cerebrooos!!!)

—description from the designer (Spanish)

boardgame: Cyberpunk 2077: Gangs of Night City – Exclusive Edition
description:
Cyberpunk 2077: Gangs of Night City is a big box strategy game with miniatures in which gangs fight for control of a city, with the city also being a living part of the game.

1 to 4 players take on the role of ruthless gangs vying for control of the underground in the glittering hellhole that is Night City. Clash with other Gangs in the meat or on the Net, as your enterprising band of toughs seeks to gain dominance over the criminal underworld that rules the streets. Only the boldest will be remembered, and your Street Cred will pave your way to the top.

In Single Player mode, you are pitted against an opposing Gang and an Edgerunner (Johnny or either V's) in a free-for-all for Night City. Similar to the multiplayer mode, the faction with the most Street Cred wins.

In the game, players assume the role of a gang leader, with you organizing your gang members to take power in the city. Your actions affect not only your game and your opponents but the city itself. Each gang has different attributes and goals, giving each player a different gaming experience.

The Kickstarter Exclusive Edition (vs Retail Edition) additionally has:

     35 extra Edgerunners sculpts & ability cards
     2 Exclusive Single Player AI Edgerunner Opponent ability cards & sculpts
     6th Steet Gang (sculpts & player dashboards)
     3 story modules
     2 modular sets (sculpts & rule cards) for Delamain & Trauma Team

اوهام (Oham, "Hallucinations") is a strategic and completely Iranian-made game in the resource gain and resource management category. It's been designed by Faezeh Khomeirani, and Pouya Taymorian has done the art for it. 
The goal of the game, is to have the most mental illnesses. Players, by using event cards, increase their negative emotions in order to gain mental illnesses. Also they will try to create positive emotions and give medication to their opponents, in order to stop them from getting the illnesses. The illness cards have variable points. As soon as a player reaches 20 points, the game ends; after calculating special points, the player with the most points wins the game.

—description from the publisher (tranlated)

boardgame: 激闘 関ヶ原 (Fierce battle at Sekigahara)
description:
激闘 関ヶ原 (Fierce battle at Sekigahara) is a tactical board wargame based on the same system as Yukimura Gaiden, and depicts the Battle of Sekigahara.

In addition to the historical scenario depicting the Battle of Sekigahara, the scenarios include Mori Terumoto's troop at Osaka Castle, Tachibana Muneshige and the Otsu Castle siege army, Onogi Kimigo and the Tanabe Castle siege army, and also the Tokugawa Hidetada and Sanada troops fighting at Ueda Castle. A hypothetical scenario is included in which Toyotomi Hideyori's and Mori Terumoto's units participate in the main battle field.
In the historical scenario, the Western Army's response, units' defection and participation in the battle are determined in a specified turn. There is also a scenario in which all units of the Western Army participate in the battle.

The game has a simple rule of repeating movement, defensive fire, and attack. A fierce battle between large armies from both sides unfolds with a playable game system.

Number of counters:  Approximately 700 (3 sheets)
Map:   Sekigahara main battlefield (full map) + Minamiguyama area (half map)
Unit types: generals, samurai generals, warlords, guns, and foot soldiers / 300 to 400 men per counter
Scenario: total of 8
Difficulty level (5 levels, 5 is the highest):  2

—description from the publisher (translated and adapted)

In the game Monopoly: Tatry i Zakopane the player sets out on the mountain trails of the Tatra National Park. Kościeliska Valley, Giewont, Kościelec, Gubałówka, Kasprowy Wierch and Morskie Oko are beautiful, delightful and unique places that can be reached on the board.

Wordit! is a a word card game with a deck of 72 letter cards and rules for three different games:

WORD. SET. GO.

A rummy game where players take turns drawing and discarding cards until one player can from their 12-card hand into a 5-letter word, a 4-letter word, and a 3-letter word.

RAPID SPELL

The deck is spread out face down. To start, and whenever play is stuck, all players turn one card face up simultaneously. A player calls out a word they can spell from the exposed letters, and can gather its letters unless someone calls out a longer word within 5 seconds. After the deck is exhausted, players add the scores for the words gathered, and the highest score wins.

GUESS

Each player makes a 4-letter word from their 10-card hand and places it face down in normal or reverse order. The first to place their word chooses to reveal 2 cards of their word, and each other player has one guess for the hidden word. Then in turn each other player reveals the same 2 cards (e.g. the first and third), and others try to guess it. A correct guess earns the guesser the word's unexposed cards; the first to 4 cards earned wins the game.

boardgame: Cyberpunk Red: Combat Zone – Strontium Cow
description:
One Gas-mask equipped Cow and 2 (3, actually) Beefy Scenarios.

This expansion includes a miniature of a cow with a gas mask, its own stats card, and a gear card (the strontium saddle).
It also includes 3 (instead of the initially advertised 2) scenarios:

Ride-em cowboy (2 players)
Fry me up a steak (3 players)
Where's the beef (4 players)

Silo! is a set collection card game for 2-4 players, who take on the role of amateur photographers, travelling the country in search of Silo Art.

There are five geographic regions where the Silos can be found: Mountains, Desert, Forest, Lakes and Plains. The object of the game is to collect as many matching pieces as possible from one of the seven themes, or to collect one unique piece from each as a wild card set.

A central marketplace forms a swap meet for players to exchange cards, while keeping an eye out for opportunities that may benefit their search, or hinder others.

Action cards introduce variables such as adverse weather conditions, transport or equipment failure and other events that effect gameplay in a positive or negative way.

When a player chooses a geographic region on their turn, all other players holding a card from that area must also play it to their set, regardless of the theme they are collecting.

The end game is triggered when one player completes their collection of five pieces, and scoring begins based upon the value of the collected pieces.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gloom: Christmas Creep Story Promo Card
description:
Story Card for Gloom Second Edition.
Given as promo when purchasing Gloom at GenCon in 2017.

In Happy Endings, each player has a hand of 7 letter cards. On your turn you play cards to the table, to start spelling a word, or to extend a partial word if one is already on the table. If you finish a word of 4+ letters, you remove it and score 1 point per letter, and start another word if you can. You can play as many cards as you want and can, and at the end you fill up your hand back to 7 cards.

A partial work can be challenged as to whether it starts and actual word. The winner of the challenge get 2 points per card on the table.  If a set of letters goes all around the table with no additions, the player who last played gets double points for the cards on the table. In both cases, the cards are removed and the current player can continue.

The game can go to a set number of points, such as 50, or it can go until the deck runs out, and the highest score wins.

Challenge your friends and family and get to know more about the different types of pollinators, their importance for humanity and their risk of extinction.

Each card have a pollinator species with some basic facts. Players look at the first card in their hand and the leading player decide which fact should be the considered. The player with the highest number on that fact win.
The more you know about the pollinators, the bigger your chances of winning.
publishers: Publication Office of the European Eunion

description: Based upon the rules of Snooker, Rota Snooker is a puzzling game for one or two players that is simple to play.  The object of the game is to remove all the balls from the outer circle to the inner wheel.  Scoring is the same as Snooker; each ball is valued as Red 1, Yellow 2, Green 3, Brown 4, Blue 5, Pink 6, and Black 7.

Even though it is simple, it is also suitable for adults. This wooden game is perfect for parent-child brain training and free time.

Competitive balancing game is the winner of the game that takes 13 wave pieces of different sizes and goals to the blue waves without dropping the top ship.

You can even enjoy playing time attacks that set the time limit or how you stack the wave pieces.

boardgame: Chip 'n' Dale: Christmas Treasures – Collector's Edition
description:
Chip 'n' Dale have found their way into the Christmas tree! Help them collect treasures and treats for their winter stockpile, and avoid getting caught by Pluto! Flip over cards to discover the hidden treasures in the tree, then collect cards to score points. Make the best collection to win!

—description from the publisher

Differences from the Original realeas:

- Have a metal/Tin box
- Have a playboard that you can make with the puzzle pieces
- The card's are shinny layered

The game comes with 18 cards (6 candidates, 12 actions) with players needing to supply the other materials: tokens to keep track of resources, one d3 die, fifteen to twenty d6 dice, and a means to keep track of votes.

Players pick their candidate and start with a pool of three d6 dice, three resources, and a deck of three action cards. Turn order is decided by the sum of one's dice rolled at the start of the round.

Players take one action per turn by playing a card to either gain resources, buy another die, or steal two dice from another player (keeping one for yourself). At the end of each round players roll their dice to determine how many votes they got. After five rounds the final vote is cast and the person with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

A mysterious smith has begun to appear in the marketplace providing unique and mystical wares, created from resources laid down by the locals.  In times past, staffs, shadows and rags have seemingly been imbued with a similar ancient Power. But, these objects hold no Power in and of themselves. No, the Power comes from another source, and the Power provides witness. Is it the Hard Fire, or is it something more? The locals have taken to suggesting that the smith is selling his wares. But, this smith cannot be bought. Nevertheless, he doesn't offer the provision only to those who see him rightly. He offers freely to all who approach.

The Upgrades | A Market Expansion expands on The Market | A Pocket Game with a number of exciting features.

In The Upgrades, you no longer simply trade Temporary Resources for more Permanent Resources. You will now sacrifice your only source of sustenance for hope that you might find power to change everything.

What's new?

     Hard Fire (a new resource building card)
     Upgrades (new wares to be purchased that can be leveled up)
     Power (a new potential win condition)


In a temporary world, even permanence is fleeting.

—description from the designer

Abominations: Eldritch Beasts is a monster-building card game for 2-6 players that combines set collection, light resource management, sabotage, and tile laying.

Play as a sorcerer competing to summon a beast that will usher in The End. Roll the sacri-dice to collect colour-coded bones, and feed them to your beast to attach new limbs, or offer them up as sacrifices to charge spells that lay dormant in some monster parts, and use them to sabotage the other Abominations. Players can also use sacrifices to grow scales on their beasts for added protection.

Each limb-type requires a certain amount of sacrifices to be grown, and can only connect to bones of a matching colour.

Grow matching sets of limbs to score points, or the most of a limb type to claim the corresponding Monster Title - Hydra, Harpy, Kraken, and Leviathan, which come with special abilities when won. Claim additional titles, like Behemoth and Bloodlust, by growing the largest beast, or stockpiling the most sacrifices, and win instantly by summoning The Terror, a pre-determined set of limbs prophesied at the start of the game.

The End is nigh. Will you be its champion?

—description from the publisher

Join Poppy and pals on a cooperative musical adventure.

Everyone works together to collect tokens from each musical land before Barb hits a sour note.

Fill the Team Trolls Music Ticket with at least one token from each musical land and then get everyone's mover to the end before Barb fills her Music Ticket with at least one musical tokens from each land.

Postcard Remagen, 7 March 1945 is a tense, mutually frustrating little game about First US Army attempting - historically successfully, of course - to be the first to fight across the Rhine River into Germany in March 1945.

9 US Army pieces - from one of the first M-26 Pershing platoons up to 78th Infantry Division regiments - urgently and desperately trying to get the Ludendorff Bridge - intact. Our Ardennes/Bulge survivor (barely) 9th Armored Division gets payback! :-) ? :-/

15 German pieces, including fleeing refugees and military units (including the platoon of a "top secret" SAM (surface-to-air-missile) weapon) as well as the engineer company trying to destroy the Bridge after enough military units and refugees get across
... despite maddening chain-of-command and technical demolition difficulties and delays.

Refugees in the way clogging the road/approaches, and fleeing German military units safely across the Bridge may suddenly up and desert and disappear! instead of helping to hold the northern end (as the remnant of an elite parachute battalion did!, historically).

Sudden death ... of the Bridge ... can end the game.  Or if we get across, depleted German panzer divisions (including local commander Fritz Bayerlein's Panzer Lehr) can try to push the Allies back - maybe even to get back to the northern end of the bridge to try to blow it again.

Included are my Pressed Attack and Desperate/No Retreat! rules, and in its very first (solitaire) playtest game, our 27th Armored Infantry Battalion's attack 2:1 attack across the Bridge ended in only a Disruption of the German engineer/Pioneer company, enabling it to then (at 1:2 odds) blow the Bridge (in our faces) at the start of the next turn.

The fleeing German units west of the Rhine are hidden even to the German player - CHAOS! - although he gets to peek at 2 of them every turn after the first.  Disrupted German military units stay so and stay visible, unless they are not re-Disrupted in the next US combat phase.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Mars Expedition: SOL43 features 43 Martian days full of scientific journeys and discoveries. Forty-three missions during which you will experience being a cryptographer, navigator, geologist, mineralogist, geochemist, physicist, and futurist.

In Mars Expedition: SOL43, the first challenge is to decode the mission task. The task is encoded in a special code that was transferred to us by scientists-marsologists from the future. Then the player gets to the surface of the red planet, where the task has already been stated. It is necessary to learn how to handle the drone. At first, the landscape is simple because the operator is a novice, but the deeper you immerse into the game, the more complex and diverse the Mars terrain becomes. Finally, it is necessary to warn our cosmologists about geomagnetic anomalies and unusual nanomaterials, such as aerographene, found on the surface of Mars.

The forces of the natural elements come to life in this game, bringing extra hues to the overall panorama of the game board. Natural element of wind is concentrated in one cube. Is it possible to control it? Can it bring the astronauts’ plans to nothing? How to counteract the wind? How to make friendship with it? Science versus chance! This is a riddle that players will have to solve.

Each player gets their share of word magnets from the box, and tries to answer a question using those words. The judge player picks which player gave the best answer. First person to be the best answer ten times wins the game.

War On Terra is a table top miniature wargame. Players control and use a wide selection of miniatures, representing different types of soldiers and vehicles, to fight battles between various warring forces and factions. Battles take place on custom game boards or on table tops laid out with to scale scenery to mimic real life environments. Players make measurements to move their models around the battle ground and to shoot at the enemy. Other game actions are decided through a combination of dice rolls and card draws.

War On Terra is set in the year 2348. After Humans back-engineered faster than light travel and colonised most habitable bodies in the Helius System they sent out waves of Probes containing self replicating Talus robotics in order to prepare other star systems for their arrival. We had no way of knowing that the Grays were trying to shroud their existence and were hostile to any intrusion into their sector. As the skirmishes continued, little did any side know that we were all about to be swept up in an archaic conflict between the Haraka and Watchers as it passed through the galaxy.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Cyberpunk Red: Combat Zone – Kickstarter Stretch Goal Set
description:
Stretch goal set included in all pledges containing a core box.

Includes:

     Combat zone scenery pack (1 arasaka container, cardboard buildings)
     Monster scenery Cyber city (a collection of plastic terminals and antennas/radars)
     7 extra miniatures for existing gangs (with their own stats card
     Drone miniature with its cards
     Party gang expansion (1 miniature + objective cards)
     Guardian expansion (1 miniature + objective cards)
     3 more scenario cards
     many extra cards (gear, programs, edgerunners
     extra plastic scenery: pile of tires, 2x dead bodies, dumpster

boardgame: Cyberpunk Red: Combat Zone – Daily Mini Set
description:
Not to be confused with the Stretch Goal Set.
This set contains 21 extra miniatures (they disclosed one per day during the Kickstarter campaign) and the related Character cards to use them as units in the game. 
The Daily mini set was only included in "Blood on the Streets" (200$) pledges and above.

boardgame: Cyberpunk Red: Combat Zone – Character Cards Pack
description: Includes character cards for all the previously released Cyberpunk Red Miniatures, to bring them into your games of Combat Zone. Also includes veteran character cards, as appropriate.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tactics – Battle of the Trident
description:
A Song of Ice & Fire: Tactics is a two-player skirmish miniatures game in which players construct their warbands and engage in battles across multiple thematic scenarios. Set in the expansive world of A Song of Ice & Fire created by George R. R. Martin, this game transports players to various eras of Westeros, where strategic warfare unfolds with each move.

Battle of Trident is a skirmish set that includes pre-assembled miniatures, terrain, and rules, allowing players to dive into the action right out of the box.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire

description:
Building a teenage-full wizardry college in the middle of a cursed swamp didn't seem a bad idea, somehow. At least, not until the Evil Sorcerer showed up with his little minions, craving total annihilation and with no teachers to defend you in sight! Take on the mission and defeat the Evil Sorcerer by collecting and destroying the pieces of his cursed soul to save your beloved school and get the teachers to give you an A++ in the subject "Dealing with Ancient Evils."

In Sinister Institute, players will work co-operatively to complete Rituals in order to defeat the Evil Sorcerer. You’ll explore the School and the Swamp that surrounds it, by flipping the tiles and moving on them, and collect the Items you’ll find scattered around this world. Each Ritual is worth a certain amount of points and, once completed, you score it by reducing the health of the Evil Sorcerer on the Points board.

Bring the health of the Evil Sorcerer down to zero before the Story deck runs out or all the Minions are placed to win the game!

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
Become the Evil Genius! You are a loyal minion of the infamous Dr. Evilstein, who wants to create the ultimate army of monsters. His invention of Monstrous Jelly Molecules enables the creation of terrifying mutations. You will roll and write on your lab sheet to design the DNA of the most dreadful monster possible using Monstrous Jelly Molecules. But be careful, your rivals are also working on their own experiments. Will you be able to impress your master and become the evilest scientist in this thrilling and fun board game?

Evil Lab is a print and play roll and write game for 1 to 99 players, ages 8 and up, and takes about 20-45 minutes to play. It is easy to learn, yet you must pay attention to every move you make. Creating the best DNA for the most horrific monster possible is not an easy task.

You will roll the dice and put the Monstrous Jelly Molecules into your Monster Mixer filling it from the bottom to the top, preparing the molecule chains in a way that will allow you to create the monster to be in its most terrifying form. Think of your future moves to not trip over your previous steps, because it may result in broken molecule chains. Evil elementary shows you the list of possible molecule chains that will make the most terrible mutations of your monster.

If you love to roll and write games, monsters, dark humor and want to become a mad scientist, you can take a chance to prove yourself. So get your copy and unleash your inner evil genius!

—description from the publisher

Card and dice game where 50 of the 60 cards hold 6 clues about a famous television or film personality. The 1st clues are very hard (typically birth date alone), and get subsequently easier until the final 6th clue which actually tells you the name of the person!
To start, a player (randonly chosen) looks at the first card (in secret). The player to his left then rolls the 6-sided die. The caller then consults his card and reads out the corresponding clue as the player rolled. The roller then tries to guess the name of the famous sporting person. If guessed correctly the card is won by the roller, and then the pack and die both move clockwise for the next player to roll and the previous roller now gets to cross-reference the die score against the next card drawn.
There are some gifts if you roll low, as these are the harder clues.
If you roll 'one' you receive the first clue, but in addition you are allowed another roll to see if you manage to roll a 'two'. If so you receive the 2nd clue. If you still haven't guessed the sporting celeb you are allowed to roll again to see if you roll 'three' etc. This is called the Rule of One.
The Rule of Two - if a player rolls 'two' they get to hear the 2nd and 3rd clues.
If you roll a 'six' you effectively get given the card as the 6th clue is the person's name.
The remaining 10 cards in the pack are bonus cards that when drawn are revealed by the caller. They either allow him to steal subsequent cards if not guessed correctly, or ask for extra clues, or whether the subject is male or female when it is his own turn in rolling and guessing.
When all the pack is used up then the game is over and the player with the most won cards is the victor. However as this can last a long time (especially if knowledge of the subject is poor), time restraint can be imposed by pre-agreeing a number of celebs for victory.

A razor-sharp, cooperative and edge-of-your-seat game where speed can save your life. Move fast to escape Jason’s machete!

Ned, Alice, Jack, Marcy, Steve, Bill and Brenda are camping at Camp Crystal Lake for a week. A beautiful place to make memories… or is it? Jason is lurking in the shadows, ready to kill the campers. The players must move fast and complete survival missions to try and survive the week. Think fast… or get slashed!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ashes Reborn: Red Rains – The Siege of Lordswall
description:
Phoenixborn and Chimera Expansion Decks!

It began with war, the dead being unceremoniously dumped in an old mine. But the Red Rains flooded those tunnels, producing something horrifying. Now a veritable tide of human thralls surges toward Lordswall, their will controlled by the crimson crystals growing from their flesh. Choose from four returning Phoenixborn: Odette, Xander, Dimona, and James, each wielding a brand new signature card. Use the included new divine magic player deck to repel the enemy at the city’s gates! Hold the line, protect your comrades, then smite the wickedness of the crystalline plague. Stop the horde at Lordswall or brace for extinction!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Ashes – Rise of the Phoenixborn

description:
Clusterfuss is a two-player game played on a square board of any size, initially filled with interspersed checkers. The two players, Red and Blue, take turns moving their own checkers, one checker per turn, starting with Red.  Passing is not allowed, but if you don't have an available move, your turn is skipped.

OBJECT  Remove all enemy checkers from the board.

MOVES  Every move must be an orthogonal king capture.  That is, you must move to capture a checker immediately above, below, to the right, or to the left. You can capture a friendly checker or an enemy checker.

GROUPS  All groups are comprised of checkers interconnected horizontally or vertically, or both. Diagonal adjacencies are irrelevant in Clusterfuss.  Groups may contain checkers of either or both colors.

At the conclusion of your turn, there should only be one group on the board. You can only make a move such that after your move there will only be one group containing your checkers, which group may also contain enemy checkers.  If your move detaches groups comprised only of enemy checkers, immediately remove those groups from the board, concluding your turn.

Clusterfuss is predated by a similar game, Advanced Forms (2011), by Steven W. Meyers.

In color wars, we choose, our warrior, and with the dices we will move around the board, and fight with the rest of the players, the last one standing will be the winner.

—description from the designer

Use your chop sticks with easy to use connectors included to fight for your object.
Flip an object card, fight for that object with your ‘Quik Stix’. You have 40 seconds to transfer your object from the bowl to your hand.

Objects can be hurled, dragged, and snatched from your rivals when inside the bowl. Will you play nice or go for the win?

Speed and precision are your allies! The first player to collect five objects from the bowl claims the title of Quik Stix champion!

Whether it's a birthday bash or a festive Christmas gathering, it's the ultimate gift for teenagers, adults, and kids alike. Bring everyone together for unforgettable moments.

Clash - D-Day - Special edition is a turn-based board game that is set during the longest day in history: D-Day. Take control over the Allies and Axis forces and get ready to conquer the necessary beaches and areas. Outwit your enemy, make tactical decisions and think ahead. Prepare and take action in the greatest seaborne invasion that changed history on June the 6th, 1944!

Clash - D-Day is very easy to learn and there is no need for historical knowledge.  Clash - D-Day is based on the popular board game Clash of the Ardennes and is a stand alone version of the game.

Use all of your (special) units wisely, because you will run out of units very quickly. Place them strategically and tactically on 1 of the 7 streets, always think ahead and try to reach your objective a little bit sooner than your opponent.

—description from the designer

In this radical reimagining of Amabel Holland’s popular Table Battles series, players fight a series of short, snappy battles that are forgone conclusions. One side brings to bear overwhelming advantages while the other struggles to stave off certain doom as long as possible. Eventually, they will weaken. Inevitably, they will break. There is, mechanically speaking, no way for the doomed side to prevail – no victory condition at all, and no way for the other side not to achieve theirs. But it’s not about that.

It's about how hard you fight when you know you’re going to lose. During each battle, the doomed side will score points – a running tally that carries over from one scenario to the next. If by the end of the third battle they’ve hit a certain threshold, they win the game. But that threshold increases when you choose which of the game’s six battles to fight next – easier battles raise your threshold more than hard ones. The doomed player also has access to Advantage cards – once-per-battle powers that let them break the rules in some way. But each of these you take on also increases your threshold.

From the encircled Romans at Cannae to the trapped paladins at Roncevaux Pass, the outmatched French fleet at Trafalgar and the desperate highlanders at Culloden, this expansion spans desperate, hopeless battles from across human history and points toward an exhilarating new direction for Hollandspiele’s bestseller.

—description from the publisher

It’s a race to get rid of your cards first in a classic game of Snap. All you’ve gotta remember is Snap the burrito card or get a Tortilla Slap
Stay alert and be the first to snap a Burrito card. The slowest player gets slapped with the foam Tortilla by the quickest player.

—description from the publisher

Draw a card, be the first to stack 10 snowballs, and win the game. But beware of fellow Yetis trying to knock down your stack. Draw the Snowbrawl card and unleash an all out snowball fight! Perfect for knocking down your opponents snowball stack or getting back at friends or family.

—description from the publisher

This is a boat racing board game. Players take on the role of competitors, competing for waiting actions, flying starts, and straight turns in a single lap. A single round will be held to determine the winner. You place cards and navigate on top of them, so you play on a large desk or floor.

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Deathgorge – Malevolent Masks Rivals Deck
description:
The ice-coated tunnels of the Deathgorge contain untold treasures frozen within their walls. Those brave enough to traverse the freezing continent of Bjarl and weather the Everwinter's curse must still battle rival warbands to have any hope of claiming these riches. The Malevolent Masks lay long-forgotten in the depths of Bjarl. Lost for centuries, they have now been discovered in the glacial chasms of the Deathgorge. Donning them grants the bearer power, but often at a cost, as few truly realise the consequences of wearing such an item.

This deck contains a 32-card universal Rivals deck consisting of:

     12x objective cards
     10x gambit cards
     10x upgrade cards
     1x background card that includes a deck list of the Malevolent Masks cards


—description from the publisher

Dominus Charta is a strategy game with game pieces and rules that are easy to understand but engaging once play starts against an opponent.

To win, you must use one of your Scissors to take your opponent’s Dominus Charta.

The game board has on the right hand side illustrations of how each game piece moves for easy reference.

The game comes in a "Dominus Charta Lite" Version which saves cost by shipping the playing board, game pieces, storage bags and down loadable rule book, but excludes the box to save on cost.

—description from the designer

Oh man, that was a party!!! This one will be legendary even among dwarves! And that young wife of your cousin Gringhli, what a cute beard she has! Lucky man. But now it is time to return home...

“Will you guys find your way back?” says Gringhli. “Wicked creatures lurk in the shadows beyond the main corridors... And you look still totally smashed. Drunk and stoned at the same time, ‘droned’ as we call it, hehe.”

“Never mind, my dude. I remember the way as clearly as my own name, which is, er... ehm... errrr... Well, farewell! And best wishes to your marriage again!”

Of course, you, a mighty dwarf warrior, would never admit you could not do something, but to be honest – you cannot remember the way back to the light as sure as you can’t remember how many beers and pipes you’ve had, not to mention kegs of mead. So, the epic memory game begins!

Which tunnel is familiar to you? Which stone wall or staircase do you remember? – Look for pairs of cards and try to avoid dangerous encounters.

Droned Dwarves is based on the classic Memory Game, also called "Pairs" or "Pexeso", enhanced with a bit of dungeon crawling.  Players choose a dwarf and some starting equipment, then flip over tunnel cards.  If they reveal a matching pair, the player is one step closer to escaping and winning the game.  If not, they are flipped face down again.  If the player flips over an "!" card, they immediately apply its effects, then flip it face down.  A player is eliminated if any of their stats are reduced to zero.

—description from the publisher

One fine day, when churches on Earth were too many, representatives of them made a wise choice to prevent constant struggles finally: Each church chooses its champion and these blessed ones are put deep into a grim dungeon. The one who fights his way to the top is the one God has chosen. His church will rule the Earth and followers of all the lying sects will be voluntarily executed as stinky heretics.

What an idea! And here is the best part of it: You are one of these champions!

Freak Cults Dungeon is a fast and simple dungeon crawler/boss-fight card game on just 12 business cards that fit in your wallet.

The replay value is not high and is based on the fact of needing repeated plays to beat the high level of difficulty. Box not included, as the whole game fits in a wallet perfectly, making a super compact travel game, needing just a one-sheet-size of space on a table (no dice needed either, just 3 coins or other small tokens).

A black background is recommended for the full visual experience. And you can burn incense or two while playing, listening to Valaam choirs.

—description from the publisher

Doom Pilgrim Micro is an advertising/teaser mutation of the Doom Pilgrim board game.

Doom Pilgrim Micro is worth playing if you want to get yourself immersed into a similar atmosphere of despair and gloom as with the Doom Pilgrim big game through just a few small cards that fit in your wallet, but do not expect any high replayability. It is about finding the right way through all the dangers after you got severely beaten several times.

And yeah, as the title suggests, it is really hard, you’ll get DOOMED.

—description from the publisher

Napoleon's End presents four battles on the Seine, February-March 1814.

BATTLES SIMULATED 
The first of the four battles occurred on the 17th and 18th of February: Mormant took place 3 days after the Battle of Vauchamps, covered in "La Patrie en Danger." The last two battles were fought in late-March: Arcis followed 8 days after Reims, from "Napoleon Retreats." With all three games you can contemplate a 12-battle Grand Campaign.

Here you have a series-first, a 40" map (which extends La Patrie East map with the potential for a 9' long map). The Mormant and Montereau maps combine to make one 44 x 34" map. The Arcis map is square-on with Arcis dead center and the Aube dividing the map in half. All four quadrants have troops moving through. Arcis was perhaps the Emperor's worst-planned mistake.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: The Library of Napoleonic Battles (OSG)

description:
USS Harder: Rack Patrol is a solitaire, tactical-level, card-driven submarine war game. You take on the the command role of a single Gato-class submarine during WWII, on the hunt for Japanese targets in the Pacific.

The game is entirely card-driven. The operation cards are held in your hand or set on the table as a draw deck. You will be instructed frequently to draw a card from the operation deck in order to determine the outcome of a given action or to gain other information. You earn victory points at the end of a patrol based on the ships you have sunk during that patrol.

A single patrol (round) only takes about 10 minutes to play. However, due to the round/patrol-based structure, and because every patrol is an independent session of play, you can easily play longer, campaign-style games across multiple sittings. The game can be entirely packed up and put away between patrols. Simply record your running score at the end of any patrol and resume the game later.

—description from the publisher

From the woods of the Danube valley to the sandy dunes in the South, from the Greek islands to the Armenian highlands, from Scythian camps in the North to the mighty walls of Babylon... war trembles the Ancient World.

To survive people need a powerful leader, a ruler brave enough to face the challenges of the Past. Unite your kingdom, build fortresses and your capital, strengthen the economy, and raise a glorious army to stand against your enemies. Use skillful characters, whose path brought them to your lands. Find ancient maps and send your heroes overseas in search of treasures of forgotten empires.

Through a combination of area movement, negotiation, and dice rolling, players expand their empires, fortify territories, and engage in diplomatic alliances to outmaneuver opponents.

Each turn, players roll dice to determine their actions, draw cards to introduce unpredictable events, and engage in open-ended diplomacy to negotiate alliances, all while strategically deploying their units and resources to achieve their empire's goals.

Lead your chosen faction to establish dominance and become a formidable empire in ARQA, strategically expanding your territories and outwitting your opponents. Victory is achieved through a combination of territorial control, strategic conquest, and economic prosperity.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ヘンゼルとグレーテル お菓子な森の道標 (Hansel and Gretel Sweet Forest Signpost)
description:
Let us all lead Hansel and Gretel, who wander through the forest, to become spirits living in sweet forests of sweets!
The forest is full of sweet temptations!Go this way, go this way... but once you've taken the road, there's a mark, so you don't go through the same place, and I'll lead you well in one stroke!
Who is the spirit that brings Hansel and Gretel out of the woods and gathers lots of delicious sweets?
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Hansel and Gretel

boardgame: つきのうてな (Tsuki no Utena)
description:
Players use common icons between the two bank cards in hand to acquire light markers from corresponding squares on cloud boards and collect them on their own flower boards.
If all the flower boards are filled with light, everyone wins the game.
However, whenever a greedy card appears in the place, the markers collected by the player return to the cloud board again, and the time limit approaches.

boardgame: Street Masters IV: Lament of the Blood Moon
description:
Street Masters IV: Lament of the Blood Moon is an expansion for Street Masters: Rise of the Kingdom, a co-operative action board game inspired by classic beat ’em up fighting video games.

In the aftermath of the Kingdom’s defeat, the world has become a far safer place. No longer united in purpose by shadowy overlords, the world’s gangs have been shattered and retreated into the darkness to lick their wounds. But is all as it seems?

Now the dust has settled, powerful new players emerge into the world stage, and none more enigmatic than the leader of a vast empire carved in blood across Japan, known only to her rivals as the Blood Moon. With each passing day, yet more gangs bend the knee to this new mysterious figure, carrying out her orders with brutal efficiency.

Can you confront and destroy this emerging threat to the known world, before she gains enough power to seize the throne of the shattered Kingdom, and turns her attention from rival gangs to the vulnerable world beyond?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Unsung Heroes: Court of the Pixie Queen
description:
Unsung Heroes: Court of the Pixie Queen is a stand alone expansion to Unsung Heroes by Tone Deaf Bards. It features four new characters with unique gaming mechanics who are vying to take down the Pixie Queen and become immortalized in Song.

Game play is simple, but not simplex, and offers depth of play to the experienced, and accessibility to the beginners. It is a great family game, and full of dry humor. It integrates with the original so that you can play with any of the, now 8, characters.

Unsung Heroes: Court of the Pixie Queen is a resource management game that is unmatched in its class. It comes with more dice than a game has any right to have and is perfect for game night or to pull non gamers into the world of board games. Remember, even the mightiest of adventurers began with only hopes and dreams. Unsung Heroes is the classic 'leveling from 1 to 2' story.

Designed by lifelong nerds looking for good games to play with their non nerd family and friends, Unsung Heroes: Court of the Pixie Queen is quick to learn, yet enjoyable to play. The basic premise is simple: roll dice and play cards to earn points. Highest score gets the rat. Yet the game is deep enough to keep even the most ardent board gamers entertained.

—description from the publisher

Move each other's five hidden pieces to find and eliminate the opponent's boss, or sneak your own boss away.

Bossarino ボスサリーノ is a 2 player deduction game from Japan. The game is packaged in a small plastic can (or standard card box), with a cloth gameboard (5x5) and ten chips, five for each player. The chips show a mafia boss, a pair of goons and a pair of innocent bystander. Players lay out their pieces face-down, hiding which piece is which from their opponent. The goal is to either eliminate the opponent's boss, or get their boss off the other side of the board.

Players take turns either moving pieces one space in any direction, including swapping positions with the opponent's chip, or challenging the opposing chip when they are adjacent.

If the challenge is between a goon or boss and the opponent's goon or boss, the opponent's piece is eliminated (winning the game if it was the boss). If the challenge is between a bystander and a goon or boss, the bystander wins, eliminating the goon/boss whoever's turn it was (winning the game if it was the boss). Alternately, getting the boss off the board through the middle three spaces wins the game too.

--description by user

ボスが去るから"ボスサリーノ！"
・ドラマのような、ドキドキ、ざわつく心理戦を手軽に味わいたい？ 「ボスサリーノ」なら叶えてくれる！
・空き缶サイズで軽い！簡単なので誰でも遊べる！
・性格がでたりして笑える！普段大人しいあの人は実は・・・
「正体の隠されたお互いの５つのコマを互いに動かし、相手のボスを見つけて排除するか、
自分のボスをこっそり逃がしてください。」
めくる瞬間のドキドキ感をお楽しみください。
--description by publisher

Now it's getting tricky, because in this memory game your own memory will trick you. Was the last card really a shark or a banana? And you've already seen the rocket ... but which of the four piles is it in? A game for clever minds. Confusion and fun guaranteed!

—description from the publisher (translated)

DEFUSE is a puzzle roll-and-write game. The players try to defuse the bomb by draw the bomb equal the number it around for get points. Whoever do their best and get the most point in ten rounds win the game.

The game is played over ten rounds. Each round has two phases: DETECT Phase and DEFUSE Phase.

DETECT Phase: One player rolls all 4 dice (2:D8 and 2:D6) then pair the dice in the same color. each player simultaneously chooses 1 pair of dice and write the clue number form number of D8 dice and draw the bomb equal number of D6 dice by respect the rule.

DEFUSE Phase: Player plays this phase if there met 1 in 3 conditions if not skip this phase.

The game ends after 10 rounds Each player totals their points and those who have the most points win the game!

Resequence is a captivating, fast-paced, 1 to 8 player game where you take the helm of a Faction, vying for dominance over a fractured Novus City. Your objective is clear: increase the value of your faction characters while diminishing your opponents’. Resequence offers a dynamic and ever-evolving battlefield, where alliances can shift and power dynamics are in constant flux. Choose your moves wisely, and let the grid become your canvas in this immersive game of strategy and skill.

—description from the designer

Pack of 3 trick promo cards (Jackhammer, Reiman Flip and Nell Raiser) that were unlocked during the Skate Summer Kickstarter campaign and given for free at all backers.

Jackhammer - Get 2 flames per player with higher score

Reiman Flip - When moving, you may discard to move to big air

Nell Raiser - When played, score 1 pt per regular trick card in combo

The Smelly Left-Overs deck has 98 letter cards, 2 Burp and 2 Oh Poo cards.

Each player starts with a hand of 3 cards. During your turn you draw a card, then play as many cards from your hand as you can and want, either playing letters to start a word, or adding letters to extend a word already on the table.  When a word is finished it is set aside and play continues, starting a new word.

Play continues until the deck is exhausted and no one can play any more letters.  The cards remaining in each player's hand is their "smelly left-overs", and the player with the fewest wins. The Burp and Oh Poo cards cannot be played, so they will become part of your leftovers at the end.

A variation is to select a theme, and all words formed must relate to that theme.

boardgame: Firepower: Battle in a Box – American Civil War
description:
Firepower is written by three of the greats of the wargaming industry, Alessio Cavatore, Michael and Alan Perry.

Its designed as a fast play set of rules for the period of the American Civil War, and gives a flavour of the period without too much depth.

This box includes 2 Generals, 145 Infantry, 12 Cavalry, 18 Artillerymen, 4 guns, 1 farmhouse, over 4 feet of fencing, unit bases for all figures, revised ‘Firepower’ rule set by Alessio Cavatore uniform guide and flags.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: 1-2-3 Shout has a deck of 26 letter cards (AABBCCDDEEFGHILMNOPRRRSSTT) and a deck of 26 theme cards. Both are shuffled, and the top card of each deck is revealed at the same time.  The first player to shout out that matches the theme and starts with that letter wins both cards.  The whole deck is gone through, and the player with the most won cards wins.

A classic fantasy wargame… in your pocket!
Warfront is a 2D wargame designed to be fast and played on the go!
Your typical 3D models associated with well known fantasy wargames are reimagined as 2D unit cards within a standard sized deck of cards.
Deploy, move and activate these unit cards across a war torn fantasy battlefield.
The perfect introduction to wargaming and the perfect light distraction for veteran players. All the essential elements of wargaming distilled into a punchy and engaging package.

In Warfront, take command of the righteous Zenith Guard to protect the kingdom or lay waste to the land as the Corrupted Legions led by Belzen the Corruptor.
These two factions, and all future expansions, are designed as a reimagining and homage to classic, beloved fantasy tropes.

Warfront is going to particularly appeal to:

     Gamers new to wargames wanting to play and enjoy the genre without the usual enormous barriers to entry.
     Gamers looking to be able to play fantasy wargames on the go, on holiday, during their lunchbreak etc without carting around their delicate models.
     Gamers looking to introduce fantasy wargaming to their friends and relatives without scaring them away!
     Any gamer looking to enjoy a fun, fast-paced and thematic board game!


—description from the publisher

"Mycelium is a resource necessary to mushroom life, but it is becoming increasingly scarce. The little creatures will fight to take over the last Mycelium stocks."

In Mycélium, players are trying to get the most points by capturing opposing mushrooms. The game contains 60 Mushroom cards numbered from 1 to 10, in 6 different colors. In the beginning 9 cards are laid in a 3x3 grid and the remaining cards are split evenly in three stacks.

On their turn, players choose a mushroom card to attack an orthogonaly adjacent mushroom card. In order to attack, you need a higher value than the defending mushroom card, with the exception of the 1 being able to attack the 10. The attacking card is placed face up in front of the player, and the attacked card goes face down to the player's score pile. Throughout the game, each player will only be allowed to attack with three different colors of cards.

The game ends when a player cannot make a legal move or after a given number of round. Whoever collected the most points (summing up their scored cards) wins the game.

A solo mode is also available.

Toko Maja is a simple, fun, strategic, family and addictive card game. It combines card collection, winning the majority and taking risks.

During the game, you will have to accumulate three of a kind (3 identical cards) to score points and win bonuses. But be careful, the cards that remain in your hand at the end will cause you to lose precious points!

Collect the right cards
Build your hand round after round and place your sets to score points.

Win majorities
Maintain or recover majorities to earn bonuses.

Take risks
Keep the minimum number of cards at the end of the round, they cause you to lose valuable points.

Contents of the game box:
- 81 cards (57x89mm) + 3 rules/variant summary cards
- 5 majority tokens
- 1 rule book
- 1 scorebook

—description from the publisher

Alpha Bet plays similar to 7-card draw poker where players make words with the letter cards -- 102 in the deck -- using provided plastic chips in two colors.

For each hand, players each place one chip in the center and get dealt a 7 card hand. Then everyone has 2 opportunities to discard 1 or more cards and get dealt replacement cards, to improve their hand.  After that there is betting, players in turn start a bet with chips, meet the previous bet and possibly raise it, or fold, dropping out of the hand. The maximum bid is 5 chips. After the bidding is over, those still in the hand reveal their cards and announce the word(s) they are spelling.  The best hand wins the pot of chips. Play until a certain time expires, or one player has all the chips.

Hands are primarily ranked by the length of the words made: 

     7 letter word
     6 letter word
     5 and 2 letter words
     5 letter word
     4 and 3 letter words
     4 and 2 letter word
     4 letter word
     3 and 3 letter words
     3, 2, and 2 letter words
     3 and 2 letter words
     3 letter word
     2, 2, and 2 letter words
     2 and 2 letter words
     2 letter word


If players tie for the length of their words, then the points on the letter cards is added, and the higher total wins. If still tied, they split the pot.

Race other players to match your cereal cards, the first player with no cards left slams the buzzer to start the Udder Chaos battle. The player who slammed the buzzer picks up the squishy Udder Chaos milk carton and throws it at other players. The last player to be tagged by the milk carton when the buzzer goes moo, loses that round.

Mana Mania is a light set-collection card game with hand-management and drafting gameplay. Players collect colored cards matching their own elemental identity, which changes as they shift on a linear board called the Leyline. Across 6 rounds, they’ll burn, block, and freeze fellow players as they gather from a refilling Mana Pool. Utilize nine elements with unique playstyles and effects to sabotage other players' plans. In the end, only one spirit will amass enough energy to claim victory.

Players start each round by refilling their hand (tableau) with elemental cards from a shared deck. A shared resource pool called the Mana Pool is also filled. Players are randomly assigned to spaces along a board called the Leyline. Their position shows their current "Alignment." A player's elemental alignment determines how they score at the end of the game, but also what abilities they can use. The aligned element and its two adjacent elements on the Leyline form that element's "Range."

Players take turns completing 3 actions to move, acquire cards, and use effects. They can only use the effects of elements within their range. Between rounds, hands refill and the Mana Pool is refreshed. The last rounds provide boosted versions of the 3 actions. After the final round, players will count points from cards in their hand that match their elemental alignment. The player with the highest point total wins.

—description from the publisher

To play Word Grab, the 90 letter tiles are spread out face up on the table. At the word "Go", all players simultaneously grab letters from the pile that make words; once they have a word they each add its letters to their personal letter pile.  After everyone is finished grabbing letters, each player goes through their pile and says the words they formed, and their score is the total number of letters used. If there are any leftover letters that they forgot how they used, or if they misspell a word, the letters involved are subtracted from their total. Highest total wins.

The Never Ending Game has over 100 letter tiles. On their turn each player takes a letter and either starts a word or adds a letter to those already in play. The object is not to finish a word of 4+ letters.  Before playing a letter a player may challenge the previous player in one of two ways:


     For completing a word of 4+ letters, decided by consensus of the players or by checking an agreed-on dictionary. 
     For making a string of letters that is not part of a valid word.  The challenged player says what word uses those letters if they can.  


However the challenge resolves, that ends the round, the letters are discarded, and the winner of the challenge starts the next word. The game can be played in one of two modes: 

     Winning: The winner of each challenge gets a token, and the first player to an agreed-on total (like 8) wins.
     Elimination: The loser of each challenge gets a token, and when a player gets an agreed-on total, they are out for the rest of the game. 


There are also 4 difficulty levels: 

     Easy. New letters may only be added at the end of the current sequence of letters.
     Medium. New letters may be placed at either end of the current sequence. 
     Expert. New letters may be placed anywhere in the current sequence, but the letters' order must be preserved. 
     Genius (Anagram level). After adding a new letter the entire set of letters may be rearranged in order. 


Since a player must place a new letter on their turn, bluffing is frequent, and a player must catch a previous error and challenge the previous player in order to end the current round.

Dice Among Thieves is a 3-4 player game of thievery and luck.
Players take turns as the lead thief on a job.
Players press their luck, stealing dice from other players and trying to amass the most points by the end of the game. Sometimes you have to work together for your own benefit.

The game comes in a mint tin, and contains 16 dice (4 each of 4 different colours), 6x character cards with special abilities, instructions, score sheets and a pencil.

Keep score with our free online score calculator or by printing more free score sheet.

—description from the designer

In this cat-themed variant on Go, players attempt to capture territory with cat-shaped pieces that take up 1, 2, 3 or 4 grid squares. Each cat also faces a certain direction, and may not be placed making eye contact with another cat (they would fight) or looking directly off the edge of the board (cats like to know what's going on). They also may never look at the 2x2 boss cat (the boss cat would not stand for it).

Players may capture territory by surrounding it, as long as they do not link opposite sides. When neither player can play, the player who has occupied and surrounded the most space wins.

Play your way with Thrones, a casual -but versatile- card game with distinct mechanics. For the noble strategist with a heroic heart.

Thrones is a tactical card game with elements of strategy and the allure of spoils and advantages.

At its heart Thrones is a far more skillful version of Rock, Paper, Scissors. Having a straight-forward mechanic (deploy 1 or more cards against a rival with the winner drawing replacements) Thrones invites players into a realm of tactical decision-making that is more than mere luck.

—description from the designer

New Challenges: Objective Cards for Tipperary

This promo is part of the Lookout magazine "News from the Lookout" Issue #20.

A mini expansion for D-Day Dice 1st and 2nd edition and the upcoming D-Day Dice: Pacific.

A homage to the great minds that worked on cracking enemy codes and decoding enemy transmissions, this mini expansion offers a new type of Servicemen that allow you to replace d6 results with your own! You can learn more about the Codebreakers here.

This set of 14 cards includes the GC&CS Legendary Unit, 5 Servicemen with unique abilities, 2 War Stories and a Rulecard

In Invocaster, play as a powerful wizard capable of summoning legendary creatures!
Face your opponents by strategically moving your creatures, using their powers and enhancing their strength with elemental tiles.
Your goal: eliminate each of your opponents to become the greatest sorcerer of them all.
Are you ready to take on the challenge?

Invocaster is a turn-based strategy game where each player plays as a powerful wizard.
The goal of the game is to eliminate everyone else's wizard and be the last survivor.
To achieve this goal, each wizard can summon legendary creatures who will reveal themselves throughout the game in a shared common pool.
Once summoned, a creature remains under the control of its wizard.
Players will take turns performing actions such as moving their creatures, using their powers, attacking enemies or to strengthen their team using energy tiles.

For even more complex strategies, advanced game modes exist such as constructing your own team of creatures or equipping artifacts to enhance your wizard.

—description from the designer

boardgame: スパークリングハピネス (Sparkling Happiness)
description:
Sparkling Happiness is a may-follow trick-taking game, but only until you win your first trick with a card offsuit. You can follow suit as a normal must-follow game, but you win more points for winning tricks offsuit. When you win your first trick offsuit, you must follow suit after that, and then you cannot win a trick with the color that you won the offsuit trick or tricks. If you do, you lose both tricks. Also, you need to finish the round with an exact number of tricks. How you organize your things and get rid of what doesn't sparks happiness is up to you.

—description from the publisher

These rules have been developed to allow recreation of the big battles of the Wars of the Roses and the Hundred Years War.

Although I am playing at 28mm, the game centres around blocks of troops - ‘Companies’ - and it really doesn’t matter what scale of figures you use, nor how many you squeeze onto a base.

The rules reflect my views on the nature of medieval warfare, and the way to get a wargame that has a feel of the period.

—description from the designer
