family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Una questione di voto: Leader in prima linea
description:
The first expansion for the board game Una Questione di Voto which introduces important innovations for the enrichment of the gameplay. The Leaders of the political parties come into play, the electoral campaign increases in intensity and scenography thanks to the new scoreboard of the boot of Italy on which the Leaders will move with the chosen means of transport, attendance bonuses and regional media will affect the national votes together with new "thematic matching" cards. For the second part of the game, government life, amendments, parliamentary questions, recovery fund decrees and new wildcard event cards will add further strategies available to players.

—description from the publisher (translated)

La prima espansione per il gioco da tavolo Una Questione di Voto che introduce delle importanti novità per l'arricchimento del gameplay. Entrano in gioco i Leader degli schieramenti politici, la campagna elettorale aumenta di intensità e scenografia grazie al nuovo tabellone dello stivale d'Italia su cui si muoveranno i Leader con i mezzi di trasporto scelti, bonus presenza e Media regionali incideranno sulle votazioni nazionali insieme a nuove carte "tematiche abbinate". Per la seconda parte della partita, la vita di governo, emendamenti, interrogazioni parlamentari, decreti recovery fund e nuove carte evento jolly aggiungeranno ulteriori strategie a disposizione dei giocatori.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Una questione di voto: Squadra di Governo
description:
The second expansion for the game Una Questione di Voto.

The concept of Governance enters the gameplay of the base game with the Cabinet team of Ministers, with the new additional final victory mode calculated in "Governance Points", with the new deck of "Chronicles from Italy" cards, with the new boards to be added to the basic board and the board for the Governance points track complete with summary legend. The electoral campaign will be even more captivating with the new "Exit poll" dashboard and the colored wooden cubes of the political parties for monitoring the regions won at the polls. Two new types of Leader profiles (charismatic and diplomatic)

—description from the publisher (translated)

La seconda espansione per il gioco Una Questione di Voto.

Il concetto di Governance entra nel gameplay del gioco base con la squadra di Governo dei Ministri, con la nuova aggiuntiva modalità di vittoria finale calcolata in "Punti Governance", con il nuovo mazzo di carte "Cronache dall'Italia", con le nuove plance da aggiungere al tabellone base e la plancia per il tracciato dei punti Governance con tanto di legenda riassuntiva. La campagna elettorale sarà ancora più accattivante con la nuova plancia "Exit poll" e i cubetti di legno colorati degli schieramenti politici  per il monitoraggio delle regioni conquistate alle urne. Due nuovi tipi di profili Leader (carismatico e diplomatico)

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Una questione di voto: Bicameralismo perfetto
description:
The third expansion for the game Una Questione di Voto

The mixed electoral system, majoritarian and proportional, becomes part of the gameplay making the simulation even more realistic with the use of the new regional constituency boards including single-member and proportional constituencies. The two chambers of Parliament, the Senate and the Chamber of Deputies, take physical shape thanks to the two new boards and the colored wooden cubes that will represent the senators and deputies, a new deck of "Chronicles from Parliament" cards will increase the interaction of the players, this and more inside the box.

—description from the publisher (translated)

La terza espansione per il gioco Una Questione di Voto

Il sistema elettorale misto, maggioritario e proporzionale, entra a far parte del gameplay rendendo la simulazione ancora più realistica con l'uso delle nuove plance circoscrizioni regionali comprese dei collegi uninominali e proporzionali. Le due camere del Parlamento, Senato e Camera dei deputati, prendono forma fisicamente grazie alle due nuove plance e ai cubetti di legno colorati che rappresenteranno i senatori e i deputati, un nuovo mazzo di carte "Cronache dal Parlamento" andrà ad aumentare l'interazione dei giocatori, questo e altro ancora all'interno della scatola.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Valhalla (fan expansion for A Feast for Odin)
description:
Valhalla is an unofficial, custom expansion for  A Feast for Odin, which adds new action boards, building tiles, exploration boards, and goods tiles. Also, it provides variants for a 5-/6-player game and a 5-round game.

Valhalla is designed for the base game “A Feast for Odin” and all official expansions by 2024 (“Lofoten, Orkney, and Tierra del Fuego”, “New Special Tiles”, “The Norwegians”, and “Harvest Expansion”).

Valhalla is free for every Odin fans to print and play. All content, including pictures and ideas, is attributed to Uwe Rosenberg and Gernot Köpke’s team, the publishers of “A feast for Odin”, and other variant producers. The designer (Bruce Liu) made this fan expansion only due to deep affection for this game without any profit purpose.

Role of Fate: a team-based competitive TCG and strategy skirmish game.

Welcome to the Everrealm, a location that lies beyond corporeal reality.

The Weaver has beckoned you. You are a voyager of the fourth wave.

Are you prepared to face that which is unknown to you?

To stand with or against those who have also been summoned?

To learn of your true beginnings and start your journey?

It is time to seek your role...

Role of Fate brings the ground breaking concept combining competitive team-oriented play, twenty years of Lore creation, campaign-based PvE play, dynamic card rarity system with universal card playability, and the revolutionary Player vs. Creator (PvC) mode.

In Role of Fate, a player chooses a role, builds a deck, and utilizes attunement (a regenerating resource throughout game play) to play  cards from their hand and defend against actions from opposing team members. Players battle it out in PvP with their chosen teams, or engage in voyages and adventures in true tabletop fashion, through PvE or PvC.

Role of Fate's innovative game structure brings the community together for an experience unlike any other TCG/TTRPG on the market. Players' actions can affect the direction of the Lore and how "The Weaver") the omniscient/omnipotent central neural hub for all creation in the game) responds within the Official Campaign and PvC events.

Know your role, play it well...

—description from the designer

Welcome, fellow islander! Let's auction some Land and build our dream island together!

Imagine beaches perfect for sunbathing, a sprawling theme park for endless fun, cosy houses for residents to rest, and lush gardens to enjoy the fresh air.

And what's a paradise island without peace, love, and music? Let's spread them far and wide across our beautiful island!

				
				
					========In this game, you'll claim Land, maximise Bonus Land opportunities, and, of course, auction off Land!

Money flows from player to player as bidders try to get more Land at reasonable prices, while the auctioneer's job is to stir excitement—after all, they keep the proceeds!

If the winning bid isn't satisfactory, you always have the option to buy back the Land. Plus, as the winning bidder, you’ll receive this payment!
Align tiles perfectly and win bonus tiles! An extra layer of strategy and joy.

				
				
					========Turn Summary:

1. Claim Land: Each turn, pick your Land tiles and place them where they’ll score the most.
2. Earn Bonus: If you place the 4th tile in a row or column, you earn Bonus Land; an exciting twist -every tile has value!
3. Auction: Start an open auction for Land, turning the room into a lively bazaar.
4. Endgame: If anyone fills up a 4x4 grid, the game ends. If not, reveal new Land and keep going.

				
				
					========A game about claiming Land, auctioning Land, and building Land to maximise points.

Features:
• Dynamic Market Row; choose tiles that vary in prices from free to expensive.
• Closed economy with a novel buyback system for added tension and interaction
• Gentle-but-deep strategy with dramatic realtime auctions
• A mixture of absolute scoring -for personal satisfaction- and relative scoring -for greater interaction.
• As the game progresses, Bonus Land accelerates the pace and adds a new dimension to the placement puzzle.

2-6 players; works great in every player count! We promise!

The more players, the more you’ll find yourself in a bazaar!
With 2 players, the auctions become a 1-bid auction and things become even more strategic.

Full Rulebook: Follow link

				
				
					========In 2024, Bez sold the rights to this game to Doruk, who continues to tweak it and is pitching it to various publishers.

—description from the designer

Three promo miniatures and their character cards which can be added to either of the Creature Kingdoms games:

     War for Chicken Island
     Dodos Riding Dinos and Dino Dash
     Kiwi Chow Down
     Party Panda Pirates!

Afficionados of the initial Avian Archipelago trilogy will experience a whole new feel to their favourite Creature Kingdoms games using this card pack.

Unleash dinomeeples to wander on Kiwi Island or add dexterity to the game with Dodino racers.
Play with chicken leaders to build special units in Dodos Riding Dinos or Kiwi Chow Down.
Pile on the lead racer with kiwi on Dodos Riding Dinos.
Deploy Dinosaur units in War for Chicken Island that get more powerful.
Encourage becoming enraged in Dodos Riding Dinos with some chicken characters. 
Try kiwi leaders to use their pushing skill or to build outside of your territory in War for Chicken Island.
Or simply unleash madness and power in your games with Cluckthulhu, Yolk-Sototh, Aureus Fulmer and Kuxcoatl.

—description from the publisher

Immerse yourself in a mystical world where magic and strategy are omnipresent.
Wizards, your goal is to summon gems to create alignments or capture your opponent's stones. However, gemstone summoning magic is unpredictable! Which makes the generation of your favorite secret stone uncertain... Analyze, adapt your strategy and anticipate the movements of your opponents to win!

3 game mode: One vs. All, Teams, Asymmetric
2 extensions
Gameplay variations

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Political Fallout, where objectives aren't just set—they're fueled by unbridled AMBITION! Your mission: seize the most Influence currency before the final curtain falls. Navigate a treacherous landscape of cunning bluffs, strategic alliances, and political finesse to secure your dominance. In the chaos of Mutual Nuclear Destruction, emerge not just a survivor, but the mastermind behind civilization's triumphant resurgence!

Political Fallout takes place in the shadowy world of Alealamia, a dark mirror to our own world, where six superpowers vie for unyielding supremacy. The delicate equilibrium hangs by a thread, balanced on a knife's edge of alliances and trade deals on the brink of collapse. Behind each nation's facade lurk Elites, the puppet masters pulling the strings of power.

Take command as one of these nations, orchestrating your Elite cadre to outmaneuver adversaries and claim victory. Your ultimate quest: amass the most influence currency to ensure your nation's resilience in the face of impending nuclear apocalypse. As the world hurtles towards oblivion, only the nation with the highest influence currency can carve out a future amidst the radioactive ruins, ready to lead the charge in rebuilding from the ashes.

—description from the designer

The game contains 300 cards, each featuring a question that players must answer.
Players take turns drawing a card. They read the question on the card aloud, and the players in the group vote on who is most likely to do or become what is described on the card.

The player who draws the first card and reads it aloud also the one one votes first, and they are required to justify their choice so that they can influence the other players in this way. Everyone must clearly explain why they are voting for a particular person. The player, who gather the most points after the votes will ultimately be the winner of the game, also called the "Coolest" player.

Set sail and seize the legend!

In this Liar's Dice type game, challenge yer fellow pirates to be the last mate standing and holding all ye plundered treasure. Bet, parlay, or call your enemy captain a liar, but be careful, your crew mates are always at risk on the high seas of Scallywager! Succeed and you shall acquire treasure and adventure cards to assist you on your journey. Fail and you lose your precious six-sided treasures until you are totally out of the game. A captain wins the game when no other crew mates are left aboard their ship. May ye dice be ever in ye favor!

Olygo is an engaging board game that puts your strategic thinking to the test. Players compete to create intricate polygon shapes on the game board to accumulate points and outwit their opponents. The goal is to strategically place pieces to form unique polygons, earning more points for complex and larger shapes. Be prepared to strategize, block your opponents, and plan your moves carefully in this dynamic and challenging game of geometric wit. Olygo is a thrilling blend of creativity and tactics, providing a fun and competitive experience for players of all ages.

—description from the designer

Monster Nursery ia a dexterity game where you take care of all the little monsters in your nursery as best you can so they don't cry. Some are hungry, others want to play or want to sleep.

In turns,  the active player selects 1 of the available monster tiles from the center of the table to its nursery board, trying to place it near to their friends and away from the little monsters it is angry with. The active playerthen tells everyone the icons marked on the selected tile, and simultaneously, each player tries to take care of their monsters by placing one finger of the same hand on each icon on the tiles that corresponds to the one indicated by the active player and with the other hand on the other icon to prevent them from crying.

All this while you take care of the sleepy little monsters. Some of them want to sleep and need to be carried, by placing them on your head, arm, hand, or shoulders, preventing them from falling and crying.

The player who best takes care of their little monsters will be the winner.

Step right up, adventurers, and prepare to embark on an epic journey through the sacred scriptures in Biblus: Chronicle I! Gather your flock of 2 to 10 eager participants, for a test of biblical knowledge and wits like no other.

Within the confines of this ancient tome lie 64 enigmatic rebus cards, each a cryptic window into the cherished tales of the Good Book. With the shuffle of cards, the game ignites, promising moments of both enlightenment and hilarity.

As players take turns drawing from the deck, the tension mounts. With bated breath, they reveal the intricate symbols, challenging their peers to decipher the hidden truths within. Amidst a chorus of guesses and laughter, one lucky soul holds the key to the solution, their lips sealed as others scramble for revelation.

With each triumphant deciphering, a card is claimed, a badge of honor for the victor. Yet, even in moments of defeat, the journey continues, with unsolved rebuses returning to the deck for another chance at glory.

Passing the torch of inquiry from one seeker to the next, the cycle of discovery unfolds until every card finds its rightful keeper.

—description from the designer

An Expansion Box of Aeon Trespass: Odyssey, consisting of Cycle IV: Gardens of Infinite Growth and Cycle V: Truthsayer. Includes all unlocked Core Game Stretch Goals, Daily Goals and Bingo Goals from our first Kickstarter campaign, as well as all applicable goals from the Second Printing.

In Gardens of Infinite Growth you must withstand the harsh, barren environment, reinvent your economy and rethink your strategies, then push into the very heart of the Empire to stop Xerxes before he murders a second god snared by his puzzle-box - or so they whisper - and stop a calamity to rival the Eschaton.

In Truthsayer Argo returns to the waves - and beneath them - to look for Ur-Fleece, the king that is said to have invited the Zero Point Entity to ravage Hellas. You will have to deal with the legacy of the Atlantean empire, manage your energy and oxygen reserves and journey deep into the underwater kingdom in search for answers. Just a fair warning, O Argonaut, for everything on land, sea and air there is an equal and opposite force. You have your Argo. They’ve got their Nemesis.

Immerse yourself in a captivating story from the moment you step into an idyllic village. Experience an adventure that will transport you into a world of culinary greatness, exploration, and foodfights. Find your way in the village on a modular tabletop game.

Explore the village, meet its people and remember their desires. Collect various objects and ingredients, and strive to keep them. Stay on the move, engage in food battles with your opponents. Venture down unexplored paths and navigate with impeccable timing and the use of fast passes.

Embark on the journey to become the next successor and proud proprietor of the renowned restaurant, ‘Spatûla’! But not before you prove your mettle, overcome obstacles and challenges, and ultimately unveil the secret recipe.

Ancient Greece and its myths await you for an adventure of Mediterranean taste. Defeat the mythological creatures and the enemies hiding among the temples. Then face the terrible Minotaur and become the hero of Greece.

Shuffle the 12-card deck of monsters and other encounters. Place the cards at the numbers around the labyrinth board. Move around the labyrinth from point to (numbered) point to complete each encounter (by rolling several dice). Monsters need to be defeated, while the other encounters give you extra abilities to use. 
Final monster to defeat is the Minotaur. Succeed at this before you lose your last life to win the game.

In Fit the Fishes the players will be populating their aquariums with fish by tracing cardboard stencils and completing various objectives, like feeding the fish and providing them shelter.

In the end, each player is left with a drawing
that can be brought to life with colored pencils!

The game develops visual perception, fine motor skills, as well as attentiveness, and presents a fun and stimulating activity for the children. How many fishes can YOU fit?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bear Mountain Camping Adventure: Rough Terrain
description: This expansion adds a 5th player option to the game as well as off-road tires, water bottles, campsite tiles, camping activities, and more!

Back in 2020, I designed and released a limited edition special roll and write solitaire game called Path of Alexander which followed Alexander the Great and his journey through Asia. Admittedly, the game was incredibly difficult when released and suffered from a few balance issues. Those have since been fixed as the player now earns Movement Factors every turn along with a number of other mechanical improvements. Path of Alexander is now available again in a new and improved edition, currently the only version available is digital but I'm working on getting a physical package together.

—description from the designer
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great

description: Tongue twister reading game with a spinning top timer.

The Lodge Forge has been lighted again and an ancient threat has been awakened. A new opponent is making his way through the ravaged rooms of the Lodge, and a hell of magma and flames is about to break loose! This rich expansion adds Magma, a huge new Avatar, The new cyclopic Mage Etna, and the new Forge School of Magic to your games!

Also included are the cards necessary to add Magma as a monster hunt in Nova Aetas Renaissance.

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Rebirth – Deadly Masks
description: The Mages of the schools of Alchemy and Shamanism join the war for the control of the ruins of the Black Rose Lodge! With Deadly Masks expansion, you will be able to play Rebirth with 5 or 6 players!

The Roll-With-It System is a roll-and-write adventure game system featuring thematic variety and endless adventure possibilities due to its flexible core rules system. The round-based gameplay involves managing a pool of dice, activating them to take actions such as moving, fighting, jumping, interrogation, disarming bombs, searching for treasure, and more. The function of the dice changes from module to module, providing much thematic variety.

The system's basic rules provide a “skeleton” rules framework on which all of the adventure modules are structured. Each adventure module puts the “flesh” on the rules with theme and creative new mechanisms.

The Roll-With-It Modern Series continues the Roll-With-It system with six new adventure modules featuring "modern", real-world themes:


     Highways of Speed
     Outlaws of Deadwood
     Theme Park of Chaos
     Lost City of Gold
     Shadows of Espionage
     Wasteland of Survival


—description from the publisher

In Pirate Poker, you are a pirate in search of treasures and the goal of your quest is to be the first to find five diamonds or to eliminate your opponents.

Players will discover treasures and everybody will bet to have the priority of choice to get their favorite treasure. This is already cool but there is more, players can be sneaky and pay to hide the treasure they have discovered. What’s in this hidden treasure, a Diamond? a Parrot? a Malediction? nobody else knows… except maybe if you have a Monocular to have a peek!

You’ll find plenty of items in treasures that will affect the game, attacking opponents, stealing cards, modifying bet, trading, spying on cards and much more. New players appreciate the ease of play while advanced players enjoy the high level of strategy and the deep mind play.

Strategy, bluff and knowing your opponents will be the key to victory.

Shout it loud and proud in this outrageous boasting card game that’s definitely not for the faint-hearted. Win each round by trumping your opponents with a higher-value card, but be careful if someone plays a SWEATY card you’ll lose the point.

Biota: Strange World is a competitive tableau builder in which 2-5 players traverse the planet of Biota, gathering resources and collecting creatures with unique abilities. Players will use these abilities to move around the map, gather extra foods, and avoid the apex predators that hunt them and their creatures.

—description from the designer

Lie, Boast, and Persuade Your Way to Fame in Dost Thou Deceive Me?!

Collect cards and tell tales to other heroes in an attempt to tell the best, most outrageous, most heroic, most costly adventure you can.

But if you get caught in a lie, you might be eliminated from the game. The best hero (read: the best liar) wins! Boast your way to glory, persuade your friends of your honesty, and cheat your way to a win. Find the power of deception with Dost Thou Deceive Me?!

—description from the back of the box

In this game, you surely won't fall asleep. It revs up, activates, and simultaneously enhances observation skills, hand-eye coordination, vocabulary, and, last but not least, thinking skills.

This game prepares you to tackle obstacles more easily in exams.

Recommended for kids preparing for written exams and those who don't want to forget the wisdom and truth hidden in Hungarian proverbs.

Players move across the game board, trying to guess idioms and proverbs based on cards. Those who guess correctly can advance on the board, and the first to overcome all obstacles wins the game.

As a castle lord or lady, the king rewards or punishes your deeds. You can build a medieval castle and your associated estate.

In the marketplace, you can buy horses, carriages, tools, bows, and animals.

During various missions, you need attention, vocabulary, good mobility, and clever thinking because you receive rewards for good performance.

If you are skilled, tactful, and help others, you can be the one who quickly builds their own castle with the associated estate and thus win the game.

2 developer games in one. For the little ones, the goal is to collect objects by taking steps on the game board. For older children, players need to collect objects on the game board, but here, after successfully completing tasks, they can earn money to purchase those.

A childhood favorite game enriched with novelties and ideas.

Perfect for expanding vocabulary, enhancing concentration, acquiring knowledge, and excelling under exam pressure.

The well-loved game 'Country, City, Boy, Girl...' with its simple yet tactical rules and 'Mind-Twister' thinking provides excellent entertainment for both novice and experienced players

In each game of Knights of the Round Table, players choose a cycle of Arthurian myth to play, setting the tone and starting rules. They rally a company of knights and Arthurian personalities, deploying them to construct Camelot, repel invader hordes, and quest for the Holy Grail. Through their choices, players sculpt a unique narrative and unlock new modules until the grail is discovered, and a winner is crowned high king!

The game features dozens of silkscreened wood pieces, a huge fully illustrated game board, and a 3D castle that players build during play. Multiple game modules allow for high variability and replayability across many aspects of Arthurian myth, with the intertwining of themes and mechanisms allowing players to organically create their own spin on these classic legends.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
On multiple game boards, enjoy 12 types of letter games.

From Letter World to Expressive Signs, Brain Twister, Language Lover, Secret of Secrets, Word Builder, and Word Battle, these letter and word games offer enjoyable experiences for all ages.

Includes Latin letters and runic symbols.

The game makes learning Székely-Hungarian runic writing enjoyable and effortless.

The bike race winds through the historical counties of Great Hungary, where various obstacles await. Stay vigilant as your water and sandwich may run out, leading to trouble.

Smart tactics are crucial; others may outpace you. Courage and daring are needed to be the first to finish the race.

The world is starting to change. People have started sprouting different enhanced abilities, starting a new populace known as the Enhanced. With the growing number of Enhanced appearing around the world, it didn’t take long before the war of Enhanced started, and subsequently engulfed the world.

Use your wits and resources to survive and take on the other Enhanced, battling to prove who is the best. Be prepared to use everything at your disposal, including your unique character abilities, your acquired abilities, counters for your foes, and don’t forget to enhance with mutators to ensure you are the last person standing.

Through death and destruction or enlightenment and invincibility, the way you claim victory is yours and yours alone.

A free-for-all arena fighter where players use different abilities and superpowers to fight other players.The aim of the game is to either reach an immortal state, or to eliminate all other players.
Use counters, to protect yourself by healing from attacks, blocking the damage, reflecting attacks back at the player attacking you, or create chaos by redirecting to another player.
Get into micro-battles by countering counters… And then countering again!
Give players weaknesses to slow them down to help your path to victory. Use character skills to help give you an edge on your foes.
If you are knocked out,fear not! The game is not over for you.. Guide the outcome of the game by giving the surviving players weaknesses or cause havoc by getting a foe to discard their hand.

If the person who knocked you out survives a couple rounds, come back to life in an explosive flurry and challenge them to a revenge duel of sudden death.

Game-play mechanics of Counter Chaos.
Players can play both aggressive or Defensive.
Actions can be countered, Actions and Counters can have consequences.
Variable characters each with a slightly different special that can modify how you play.(Up to 20 different Characters)
Mechanics of the game that can Buff or Debuff player actions
Post Death actions, meaning if you are eliminated you are not just sitting around waiting for the game to end.
Variable win states, allowing for slight change of tactic on how to approach victories

Let’s face it, your Gods are awful. They are arrogant, ego-maniacal, greedy, petty, and well, just crap. In fact, they spend most of their time making the lives of their Villagers arduous and miserable.

At this point, the Villagers have had enough. So, they devise a plan to rid themselves of their awful Puny Gods...

How to Play...
Each player uses their Villager cards to win tricks against other players over multiple rounds.

Players drafts a hand of Villagers and bet how many tricks they will win. Each player plays one of their card in clockwise order. After cards have been played, the winner of the trick is the player with the highest ranking card. If two cards share the highest rank, the Players must Duel.

The winning Player chooses one of their Puny Gods to Denounce and gives that card to the player of their choice.

At the end of each Round of Battles, the player with the least Puny Gods gets a Token. At the end of the game, the player with the most Tokens wins!

However, there are few twists along the way, making play a bit crafty and a lot more fun!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ritter Rost und die Suche nach den Zahlen
description:
This game is perfect for learning the 4 basic arithmetic operations.

All cards are laid out face down, the number cards separated from the arithmetic operation cards. First, an arithmetic operation card is turned over and then 2 random number cards - then these two cards must be calculated correctly according to the arithmetic operation card. If the result is correct, you can keep the number cards.

boardgame: What do you meme?: Schitt's Creek Expansion Pack
description: Schitt's Creek themed expansion for "what do you meme". Features 50 caption cards and 30 photo cards.

Players mission is to make aqua pipe line. The game goal is to make A or B below.

Game mechanics:
Players decide on colors and first player by casting dice or coin toss.
There are no pieces on the board at the start.
Players may place one of their own pipes at any available space on the board or move one of their own pipes to another available space. 
Player can only move a pipe on the board after he/she placed at least one each of the three size pipes. 
Three different size pipes can be placed on the same space because of their structure.

A: To make four pipes in-a-row with same size of your color.
B: To make four sets of three pipes in-a-row with same size of your color.

a One-time, limited edition made in aluminum was available through the Kickstarter campaign.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Field Commander: Robert E. Lee – Seven Days Battles Battle Pack
description:
An optional battle pack which includes five battles:

Oak Grove
Beaver Dam Creek
Gaines’ Mill
Glendale
Malvern Hill

This harbor tile replaces the upper left most tile during setup. When you ships reach the TAVERN harbor, your sailors demand liquid refreshment - or they will turn their backs on you. To pay for your crew's inevitable drinking spree, you should be liquid as well - that means, have enough doubloons...

SETUP:
Set up the game of Volles Fass voraus as usual. Then replace the harbor tile at the corner of the gameboard next to doubloon space 30 with the TAVERN HARBOR TILE. (On that island, you can even see a picture of the tavern with its straw parasols.)

-- These are the 4 berths at the TAVERN --

The berth all the way left (bollard #1):
If your ship lands here, it will become expensive! Every sailor you have wants to take drinks worth 2 doubloons. For each of your sailors, you need to decide whether you pay his bill – or whether you let him go, i.e., put him back into the box.

The berth second from the left (bollard #2):
If your ship lands here, it won‘t be cheap either! Every sailor you have wants to take drinks worth 1 doubloon. For each of your sailors, you need to decide whether you pay his bill – or whether you let him go, i.e., put him back into the box.

The berth second from the right (bollard #3):
If your ship lands here, it won‘t be quite that expensive! Every 2 of your sailors share drinks worth 1 doubloon. Decide for all your sailors whether you pay their bill – or whether you let them go, i.e., put them back into the box. For each doubloon (of the bill) that you don‘t pay, you lose 2 sailors. If you have only 1 sailor overall, he won‘t cost you anything. If you have 3 sailors, you‘ll have to pay only for 2 of them (= 1 doubloon). Even if you have exactly 5, 7, 9, 11 or even 13 sailors, one of them drinks at the TAVERN for free.

The berth all the way right (bollard #4):
Your sailors don‘t drink… or they pay on their own… or the tavern keeper takes care of the bill himself – for whatever reason… So, if your ship lands here, you lose neither any doubloons nor any sailors! Sit back and relax, and wait for the next game turn … that you – with berths in the east – will carry out as usual only when all the other ships have left the tavern and have arrived at the next harbor by now!

Bibi and her friends have to practise a duplication spell on Aunt Mania's hat. "Hocus pocus, fidibus! The witch's hat is there four times. Hex-hex!" And indeed: 4 identical hats are lying in front of the young witches! But when Bibi tries to pick one of them up, it suddenly makes a sound: "Meow!" Something must have gone wrong. But the four of them realize that you can have a lot of fun with the bewitched witches' hats ...

On the outside, the 4 witches' hats look the same, but when you press them, each one makes a different animal sound. Crow, cat, owl and frog - remember which hat makes which animal sound and where each hat is located. This will enable you to correctly complete the various task cards that are revealed one after the other. But it's not that easy, as the hats are constantly changing positions.

For advanced players, there is a second game variant, in which you mix additional reaction cards into the game.

boardgame: Ticket to Ride Map Collection 8: Iberia & South Korea
description:
Ticket to Ride Map Collection 8: Iberia & South Korea gives you the chance to set up rail networks on two peninsulas with wildly different settings.

On the Iberian peninsula, players start the game by drafting six destination cards, then keeping four of them — a process they repeat before the train deck runs out the first time. While you're trying to fulfill those eight destination cards with only 35 trains, you can possibly pick up festival cards along the way. The deck is seeded with 54 festival cards, and when one is drawn, you place it next to the city on the game board. If you build a route that connects to one or two cities with festival cards, you choose one of those cities and collect all of the festival cards near it. The more you collect from a city, the more they're worth at game's end!

In South Korea, you start the game as in Iberia by drafting six destination cards, then keeping four of them. All of the routes are grouped by color: blue in the northeast, yellow in the northwest, and so on. When you claim a route, you can also place one of your trains on the province mat on an empty space of the matching color showing the number of cards you played of that color; you can play more cards than required to claim a route should you want to place on a higher number. Additionally, each player has a set of "express train" cards (+1, +2, +3), and you can spend a card on your turn to either draw more train cards, draw more destination tickets, or claim a higher number on the province mat. At game's end, evaluate each color on the province mat, with the player (or players) with the highest sum of values claimed earning bonus points.

Welcome to the roaring 1920s, where the world has gone wild about aviation. Air races and daring record-setting flights dominate the news. The Golden Age of Flight has just one record that is still up for grabs:

Be the first aviator to fly around the world.

It will require careful selection and training of crews, tact and diplomacy, water landings, extreme weather, and logistical planning of thousands of gallons of fuel and oil, replacement engines, and remote supplies.

A trip lasting 175 days, covering 27,000 miles, and including landings in 22 different countries awaits you…

Do you have what it takes to achieve one of aviation’s most incredible feats?

Where Eagles Fly is an around-the-world racing adventure reliving the 1924 historical race where everyone flies a plane around the globe competing to be the fastest pilot and win the game.

—description from the publisher
Play against an increasingly difficult AI that's attempting to lock you out of the system.

...You have proven yourself to be a valuable asset to PALADIN INTERNATIONAL, and have been hired as an AGENT of THE CORPORATION. New equipment and tools have been made available to you in your ongoing role as a DATA EXTRACTION SPECIALIST...

...PALADIN INTERNATIONAL does NOT accept failure...

You have been betrayed by another agent of PALADIN INTERNATIONAL & are now on the run! Your digital identity has been used against you. Multiple CYBER-CRIMES have been attributed to you, and your efforts to clear your name have been thwarted. The only way to escape incarceration is to steal back your digital identity, stored in the corporate vault, and clear your name. You have also decided to ‘take the fight’ to your previous employer, with a goal to cause chaos, and disrupt business, damaging their manufactured reputation.

Anarchy is the plan. Escape is the goal. Is this still just about clearing your name?

In this Defying Destiny, we will zoom in on a lot of new Realms that we didn't have room to feature in the base game. Some of these will be Realms that took the center stage at a later date than 1444, some will be Realms that had great potential but for various reasons collapsed or faded out of history, and yet others will be Realms that had roles to play throughout the EU timeline but never attained the status of great powers – now, you will have a chance to defy destiny, and bring these Realms the glory they (perhaps) deserved!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Operation Ikarus: The Invasion of Iceland, June 1940
description:
Following the French surrender, an invasion of Great Britain was increasingly likely. However, the severe losses sustained by Germany’s Kreigsmarine with the invasion of Norway, and Hitler’s desire to compel Great Britain to come to terms with Nazi Germany, were significant factors that made such an invasion unlikely. Seeing the increasingly vulnerability of the British to blockade, Hitler ordered Admiral Erich Raeder to develop a plan to invade Iceland, dubbed Fall Icarus (Plan Ikarus). If successful, the island could serve as a base from which German submarines and aircraft could threaten any convoy approaching the British Isles. It could also serve as an advanced base for German surface warships and commerce raiders, further strengthening the German naval strangle hold. The capture of Iceland, on the heels of Dunkirk, could also bring about the fall of Winston Churchill as Prime Minister, and the rise of someone more amenable to a negotiated end to the war between Great Britain and Nazi Germany (Lord Halifax, the King’s preference for Prime Minister perhaps?). This designer has made some assumptions in crafting this game:


     German intelligence efforts, which at this point in the war were actually superior to those currently available to the British, were able to accurately estimate the British defense works and capabilities at Iceland (which were weak in many key areas).
     Operation Icarus is given much higher priority over the preparations for Operation Sealion, the invasion of Great Britain (the latter becomes a diversion and feint intended to keep the Royal Navy and Air Force full attention).
     Unfinished warships such as the battleship Bismarck and aircraft carrier Graf Zeppelin are used in support of Operation Ikarus and to help deceive/mislead the British.


Each turn represents six hours of time. A hex is about 6 kilometers across on the Akureyri map, about 10 kilometers on the Reykjavik map. Infantry units are battalions and tank units represent companies.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Maneuvering to War: The US 1941 Louisiana and Carolinas Maneuvers
description:
We are not seeking to rival the size of these possible enemy forces which surround us, but we wish an Army which in equipment, in training and in spirit, aided by an unsurpassed air force and the full efforts of our Navy, will be able to meet and conquer any attack which may be attempted against any part of our homeland.”—Except from national radio address by Secretary of War Henry Stimson concerning the upcoming US Army Maneuvers in Louisiana, August 16, 1941.

Historically the US had been ill prepared to enter into any war, including the War of Independence. It took nearly a year for the US to mobilize, equip, train and then transport to Europe an army after it declared war on Germany and the Central Powers in April 1917 when it entered the “War to end all wars”. However, by 1940 it was very apparent to President Franklin Delano Roosevelt and the various leaders of the US military, that America could not afford to wait until it entered the next conflict to raise and equip a modern army, and expect to emerge victorious from the conflicts raging in Europe and Asia. After initiating the first peace time draft in US history that same year, despite strenuous and heated debate and a very strong and wide-spread isolationist sentiment, Chief of Staff General George C. Marshall ordered two large scale military maneuvers to be held to test and train the new recruits, their weaponry and tactics in 1941. These maneuvers, the first two held in Louisiana and a third in the Carolinas, were concluded weeks before the attack at Pearl Harbor, and would prove decisive in determining what kind of forces the US would field, and the leaders who would command them, to victory in WWII.

Combat units are mostly regiments, brigades or their equivalent sized battle groups. Exception: A Paratrooper unit represents two platoons (Louisiana) or one company (Carolinas), and an AT unit represents a reinforced battalion. Each turn of the game represents 8 hours of time.  Each hex on the Louisiana map is approximately a 5.5 miles across; approximately 4 miles across on the Carolinas map.

—description from the designer

Scare the goats on the top of the hill to make them faint and win the game. Press the goat button to randomly set a target sound level, pick a card that will instruct you on what noise to make and try to match the decibel meter. Each player will take turns yelling noises like bird chirps, zombie growls, dog barks and more until a player successfully matches the set volume.

Watch as the goats hilariously faint and fall off their hill giving that player one G.O.A.T point, the first player to reach 10 points first wins.

Take the next step in your career as an apprentice. The Academy offers 3 new modules that you can add to your game individually or in any combination. The teacher cards offer tasks, the magic grid cards reward optimal placement and the colorful companion cards allow a certain amount of flexibility in planning.

—description from the publisher

Researcher Rocco has crash landed on a strange planet on his journey back home with no way to communicate out or repair his ship.  With no other options, he was left with no choice but to try to survive by repourposing his research robots into gatherers.
Players compete to collect resources from around the map using refactored robots to forge a path on an alien planet.
Players will have to build their own path to traverse the foreign terrain, while working with Rocco to upgrade their parts to gain an advantage.
In this game you can focus on being the best gatherer and rushing to collect all the resources, or you can be devious and sabotage your way to victory.

—description from the publisher

This family friendly card game was designed by father daughter duo Steve and Isabella Resk. Players are dealt a mix of adorably bright gummy bear and worm cards with the goal of being the first to get rid of all of them. Worm cards only take effect when three consecutive worms have been placed. These activate UNO-like powers - skip, reverse, pass all, pass 1, and take a new hand - you pass the cards in your hand though, and not people! Each turn, draw a new card as you try to make two sets in order to go out. Sets can either be a 3 or 4 of a kind (split bears and wild rainbow bears count for these sets)or a set (run) with 4 different bears (split bears and wild cards don't count for this set of 4). Be the first to play all seven cards in two sets to win!

This is a Monopoly spinoff where the properties are various businesses in the community Lansdowne, Pennsylvania.  Many of the components are generic, and can be used by similar games about other communities.  Only the board and the box are unique to this game about Lansdowne.

It appears this game was produced in 1989 as a fundraiser for the Lansdowne United Methodist Church, which subsequently became The Garden Church.  In 2018 the church building was torn down and replaced by an apartment building for senior citizens.

The game has the usual forty spaces in a rectangle around the board edge.  But, instead of having ten spaces on each side, there are eight spaces on two opposing sides and twelve on the others.  In addition to the name of a local business, the spaces for which a player can obtain "Property Title Cards" have addresses between 102 and 415.  (Many numbers are not used.)  The center of the board has a map of the community as well as sketches of a local school and some of the churches in the community, and additionally a list of nearly 250 patrons for the production of the game.  None of the artwork or text in the center of the board is relevant to the play of the game.

There are twenty-eight Property Title Cards.  So that they are identical from one community's game to another, they do not have the name of the property on the card.  Instead they just have the address of the corresponding space from the board.

Instead of Chance and Community Chest cards, this game has eight spaces that give "Star" cards.  There are forty-seven of these Star Cards.  Presumably these are identical to the Star cards in other community games published by Unique Promotions Incorporated.

There is one more card, the "Lots'o Rule Card", from one of the three sheets of Star cards.  This is an information card which explains what happens when a player lands on the "Lots'o" space on the opposite corner from the "Start" space.  When a player lands on this space they can gamble any amount of money on one roll of the dice.  On a 3, 5, 9, or doubles the player loses the bet.  On a 4, 6, 8, or 10 they double their bet, and on a 7 or 11 they triple it.  Note that in the game sometimes this is referred to as "Lots'o" and other times as "Lotso".

There are also two red dice, six pawns in different colors, and play money in denominations from $50 to $5000.

There are no houses or hotels in this game, but, for each property in a color group a player owns, they receive progressively more rent when an opponent lands on it.  This is roughly double rent for properties where the player has two of a color group, approximately triple for three of a color group, and approximately quadruple for four of a color group.  Players do not have to own an entire color group to receive a bonus.

Players who go bankrupt are eliminated.

For the most part prices are inflated from Monopoly.  Players start with $27,500.  Properties cost from $650 to $5000.  Basic rent runs from $50 to $1000.  Yet passing "Start" only gets the original $200.

The artwork for this game is similar to that in Unique Promotions's other games:

     The Game of Denver, Pennsylvania
     The Game of Hoboken, New Jersey
     The Game of Midlothian, VA
     The Game of Riverton, N.J.
As with other titles in the toppen! game line, in the card game Legenden toppen! players compete in multiple tricks at once by using cards that have multiple statistics on them.

Taco Takedown Tuesday invites players into the spicy and competitive world of taco truck ownership, where the ultimate goal is to prove that you are the best in the business. Assemble your ingredients, complete customer orders, and strategize to outshine your opponents in this fast-paced card game.

Gameplay revolves around playing Ingredient cards from your hand to complete customer orders. Timing is crucial, as the player who places the final ingredient on a customer's order must collect that customer along with all assigned ingredients. This creates a balance of completing orders efficiently while considering the potential consequences for opponents.

Unique Review cards add an extra layer of strategy. Players can manipulate their reviews from negative to positive and vice versa, influencing the pace of the game. The Achievement Cards further elevate the competition by offering additional points for fulfilling specific conditions. These elements make every decision impactful, ensuring that players remain engaged throughout the entire game.

New to the Game is a quick-paced card game where each player is a parent of four children. Each parent is trying to get their children to bed, while simultaneously waking up the children of their opponents. Players draw a card each turn. Then, they can play a single card, either a snooze card on their own children, an alarm card on an opponent's child or a special card that mixes things up. Once two snooze cards are on a child, that child is in bed. When a player gets all four of their children in bed, that player wins.

'—description from the designer

Six Forms is a “chess style” expandable card game (XCG) crafted for players with a competitive spirit and desire for evocative high-fantasy art.

Collect your deck and go head-to-head with your opponent. Make tactical battlefield card maneuvers on the 4 x 4 grid board, display your power with exciting combinations, and astonish everyone with your perfect positioning on the board.

Each game you play allows you to try a new approach, even if your cards remain the same. Explore a defensive, aggressive, or crafty strategy. 

Defensive? Block and dodge the assault of your opponent until you are ready to hit them where it counts.

Aggressive? Who wants to wait to attack?! There is no "summoning sickness" in Six Forms. Play and attack in the same turn, making your heavy hitters hit harder.

Crafty? Place cards face-down to draw your opponent into a trap or bluff them into giving you the upper hand.

—description from the designer

In the cooperative deck building game Zoic Zoo, time travel is possible. The players choose roles and then set about building a zoo of prehistoric animals and money-making facilities. Players buy cards from the market to better fulfill their roles. The collectors add time machines and collecting gear to their deck so that they can go back in time to collect the animals of their choice. The builders buy habitats, acreage and facility cards that can then be built in the zoo. Each animal and facility brings in visitors and research.  At the end of each round, the revealed animals back in time shift, and some are hidden and new ones are revealed. Once all the animals have been revealed, game play ends. Goals that change according to the number of players and the number of animals collected, are then compared to the number of  visitors and research that was brought in by the players’ zoo. If the players reached or exceeded these goals then they win.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Taking Tiger Mountain by Oblique Strategy

description:
In Brewing Shenanigans, players take the role of a competing barista in Café Glaum’s annual Bean Lien!

As such, they will be investing time into their slow brew Siphon, dirtying the necessary tools to create vital ingredients, combining those ingredients to craft Espresso based drinks, all while brewing shenanigans in an attempt to foil their opponent’s progress!
Participating baristas will attempt to utilize wit and cunning to accrue the most points by the end of the last round to be declared the victor!

Pozitivity guides you step by step, playfully, and entertainingly introduces practical techniques to transform a pessimistic mindset into optimism. Players, regardless of age, can sow the seeds of optimism within themselves based on these methods.

Because cultivating an optimistic mindset not only brings better mood, problem-solving, and performance but also contributes to overall physical well-being.

Our goal is to collect as many hearts as possible to reach the Castle of Happiness. Each color on the path corresponds to a deck of cards or a so-called self-experience field. In the former, you have to answer questions, while in the latter, you need to complete tasks related to happiness-boosting techniques.

The very first expansions of Gnomistic adds new challenges and layers for strategy and fun.

This extension adds a new module of cards called "The Pot Cards" that awards coin bonuses at the end of the game. It also adds new special cards with power immediate and permanent effects, minigames and a new gnomeeple to control powerful habilities during the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Bargain Quest: Brick & Mortar Promo Pack
description:
This Bargain Quest: Brick & Mortar Promo Pack includes:


     1 Convention Season event card
     4 alternate art heroes


Convention Season:

During the Shopping Step, each time a hero enters a shop, draw a new hero to replace them with tokens equal to their purse value. Once all shops are occupied, shuffle the remaining heroes back into the hero deck.

Introduction:Vaxandis (from Old Norse: vaxandi íss, meaning "growing ice") is a drawless stalemate game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 5 cells per side, but boards of 4 or 6 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces in their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A stack is a set of pieces of the same color placed on the same cell. A single piece is also a stack.
     The height of a stack is the number of pieces it contains.
     A group is a maximal set of stacks of the same height connected to each other. A single stack is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stacks it contains.
     A stack is frozen, along with the others in its group, if at least one of them is adjacent to an enemy group of greater height.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stack of height 1 of your color on an empty cell not adjacent to any stack.
     Move a stack of your color in a straight line, crossing empty cells or jumping over stacks of any color. The stack must begin unfrozen and may only become frozen at the end of its path.


During the move, in sequential order:


     For each new appearance of friendly groups (not adjacent at the start) while crossing empty cells, resolve growth only on the first cell that touches those groups.
     Growth is resolved in order: first, the moved stack gains +1 in height, then all stacks in the newly touched friendly groups gain +1.
     Upon landing, the stack must be adjacent to at least one friendly stack of lower height than the lowest friendly stack it was adjacent to at the start. If it wasn’t adjacent to any friendly stack at the start, it must end adjacent to at least one friendly stack of equal or lesser height than itself.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player cannot place or move stacks, in which case his opponent is the winner.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stack on an empty point, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Introduction:Frasvel (from Proto-Germanic: Fraswelganą, meaning "to absorb" or "devour") is a stalemate game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 5 cells per side, but boards of 4 or 6 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A stack is a set of pieces located in the same cell. A single piece is also a stack.
     The color of a stack is the color of its top piece, indicating its owner.
     The height of a stack is the number of pieces it contains.
     A group is a set of stacks of the same color and height that are connected to each other. A single stack is also a group.
     To absorb is the process by which a friendly stack removes one piece from each enemy group touched during its move, adding those pieces to its own color.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in order:


     Optionally move a stack of your color in a straight line a distance equal to its height, either through empty cells or by jumping over friendly stacks. The stack may not touch any enemy group of greater height. During the move, the moved stack must absorb all stacks of enemy groups it touches through empty cells, starting from the first empty cell it moves to. Each enemy stack may lose only one piece, even if its group is touched more than once in a single move. The moved stack and all stacks of friendly groups of equal height it touches during its move increase in height by as many pieces as enemy stacks are absorbed. If your stack cannot absorb, it cannot move.
     Place a stack of your color with height 1 in an empty cell not adjacent to more friendly stacks than enemy stacks.


At the end of your turn, the moved stack must absorb at least one enemy group and if you cannot place, you must move.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when, after optionally moving and absorbing, a player cannot place a stack of their color; in this case, that player is the winner.

To ensure the game is fair, before the game starts, the first player places a black stack in an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Introduction:Herfor (from Old Norse: herfǫr, meaning "war expedition") is a drawless territorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board with triangles. The recommended size is 6 cells per side, although boards of 5 or 7 are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of identical stones, each with one color on the front and the other on the back.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A phalanx is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected in a straight line. A single stone is also a phalanx.
     An invading phalanx is one that, when formed or extended by the placement of a stone, ends up positioned between two enemy phalanxes located on the same straight line, occupying all empty points between them. The affected enemy phalanxes are invaded phalanxes.
     To flip is to replace an enemy stone with a friendly stone.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, do the following actions in order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty spot not adjacent to friendly stones, unless you form an invading phalanx with it.
     Flip pairs of enemy phalanxes for each corresponding friendly phalanx you have formed or extended after your placement.


If you cannot place stones, pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass on consecutive turns. The player with the most stones of their color on the board wins. To avoid ties, the player who passes first awards half an additional point to their opponent.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player chooses an integer as the komi value, and then the second player chooses a side. During the game, White may choose to spend one komi point instead of placing a stone. This balancing method is called the komi pie rule.

—description from the designer

The official family party game for people who can't keep a straight face
The funniest person goes first. They are the Joker. Jokers pick a card and have 30 seconds to make others in the group laugh by doing what the card says. If anyone laughs, they lose and must give up a laugh token to the Joker.
When a player runs out of Laugh Tokens, the game ends. And the player with the most Laugh Tokens wins!

—description from the publisher

In Cinema players put together movie deals; players have hands of script and talent cards and take turns producing movies.

A movie requires a script card and three talent cards. However, a producer can't use their own script - they must choose one that is pitched by another player, or forfeit their turn. After choosing a script, the talent must be selected - the producer can make any kind of mutually-acceptable deals with other players for their talent. They can also use their own, but at a steep cost, or use random cards from the deck for a lesser but still high cost.

Once a movie is made it receives box office, prestige, and miscast cards based on how well the talent cards match the script's requirements; box office cards provide money immediately while other cards are kept hidden until the end of the game.

Once all players have had a set number of turns awards are given out for best movie overall, best movie in each genre, and worst movie, with the quality being determined by the cards won by each movie. The awards are worth money and the player with the most money wins.

A suitless trick-taking game consisting of animal cards numbered 1-16. Four to six players divide themselves into the blue and red team and each is dealt three cards. The team that plays the highest value of cards wins the trick, but can only score if they win two of three rounds. Scoring begins at 1, but players can decide to raise the point value by playing a blue jay card or raising the stakes at the beginning of the round. For a short game, first team to reach 12 points wins, in the long game, team that wins the most of 5 games wins.

Scope is a flip-and-write game played on a 6x6 grid of numbers. On each turn a card is turned over and each player may cross out one or more numbers than sum to exactly the number on the card; if multiple numbers are crossed out they must be adjacent. A player may skip a card if they wish.

The cards are 1-20 but five are removed before the game. Once all fifteen cards have been revealed the player with the fewest numbers remaining on their grid is the winner.

Two different grids are provided with the game and five more are available on the publisher's site.

Banana Blitz is a trick-taking game similar to Hearts. There are four fruit-based suits numbered 1-12 plus four Fruit Salad cards. The deck is dealt out as evenly as possible and remaining cards are added to the first trick.

Players must generally follow suit and the high card of the suit led wins the trick. However, the Fruit Salad cards are numbered 13-16 and can always be played, even when a player could follow suit. They always count as the led suit.

Players start with 25 points and gain points after each hand for the banana and Fruit Salad cards. However, a player winning at least 12 of the possible 16 cards instead loses points for them. There are two special cards which double and halve the banana scoring if they are won.

The game continues until a player has either 0 or 50 points, at which point the player with the lowest score wins.

There are special rules for two or three players.

boardgame: I'm the Boss: The Dice Game
description:
In I'm the Boss: The Dice Game players compete to form deals. Each player starts as the expert in one or more of the six fields and rolls dice and plays cards in order to become expert in more fields and to initiate deals.

When a deal is initiated players can freely negotiate in order to assemble the required set of experts. Deals always have some flexibility in which experts they require and cards can be played to temporarily become expert in a field. Cards can also be played for other effects, such as "I'm the Boss!" which lets a player take over control of the current deal.

After every two deals the payouts increase. After eight deals the game is over and the player with the most money wins.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Murders at Karlov Manor
description: A series of shocking murders involving some of Ravnica’s most powerful citizens has the city on the verge of chaos. Now detectives are on the case . . . and you’re one of them. Return to the fan-favorite plane of Ravnica in Murders at Karlov Manor, Magic’s unique take on the murder mystery genre.
