description:
The objective of the game is for your family to enter the Castle of Happiness, collecting reward hearts along the way and getting to know each other's stories better. As you progress toward the Castle of Happiness, monsters appear, presenting new challenges. Defeat them and continue your journey with even greater unity!

The game of family togetherness that builds self-esteem and confidence through play!

Pozitivity Family is specifically designed for families, offering a chance to play, grow, and laugh together with those you love most. It strengthens self-esteem, positive focus, and unity, enhancing psychological and emotional well-being. Promoting optimistic thinking, it provides playful bonding, quality time, and rejuvenation, contributing to the experience of positive moments.

Arthropods: Ants & Bees : build your colony & bug your friends

Arthropods consists of four interactive tile placement games in one box. Depending on which game you choose, you play either as an ant or as a bee colony. Both versions have two difficulty modes. Families with children (8+) should definitely play the basic game(s) for a maximum of fun, while more experienced players are better challenged playing the full game(s). In all cases should you aim to score the highest amount of points while preventing others from scoring or even forcing them to take minus points. 
Game mechanics used to achieve this are:

     Tile placement
     Worker Placement
     Area Control
     Resource Management
     Blocking and/or attacking


Other mechanics used for a ballenced play are:

     Drafting
     Open market
     Hand building


The Ants


     Antropolis - basic game playing as ants
     Supersize Colony - strategically challenging game playing as ants


Ants are hard workers who build nests and tunnels (hexagon tiles). They also gather food (resource) in the forest, which allows them to perform more actions. Each type of ant (queen, worker, soldier and larva) can perform specific actions and is needed to (f.e.) score points, gather resources, get more ants or defend against other players. Points are scored for completed nests, tunnels and achieving bonuses.

The Bees


     Happy Honey Beez - basic game playing as bees
     Knights in Buzzing Armor - highly interactive game playing as bees



Bees are buzzy building hives (hexagon tiles) and collecting pollen or honey (resources). They too have different tasks per type (queen, worker, drone and egg) and interact a lot with other colonies. Points are scored by building hives, producing honey and the amount of bees on the board.

Load your truck bed with the latest floral requests. can you complete more deliveries then the competition? Find out in this dice drafting, pattern matching game of Florist Tourist!

Compete against a fellow florist runner to complete the most daily deliveries. Players draft trucks to determine flower color and dice for locations. By arranging petals on one of a players' three mats and completing the pattern of a delivery goal, they can score 1-3 points. Two of the three levels of goals reward temporary or permanent placement petals. When two of the three goal draw decks are depleted, points are tallied and onenplayer will be the master Florist Tourist.

—description from the designer

Two jealous Jellies, green with envy, compete for the most consumed objects. Objects in the dungeon labyrinths are located using Gem Positioning Spells. This is because the Jellies are too slow to hunt and Magenta Minotaur energy elixirs are located in another kingdom.

Players are competing Jellies who take turns combining four secretly selected gems with four of their opponents' gems.

When gem colors are mixed with each other, different objects are gained. This is known as the Finding Phase. In the second Phase, the Feeding Phase, players spend their objects for valuable rewards worth Victory Cubes. The players with the most Victory Cubes at the end of the sixth round is the victor with the fullest Jelly belly!

—description from the designer

One player plays with the yellow pawns, the other one with the black pawns. The players place at turns one pawn on the table game. The player who puts first five pawns of his colour on line is the winner. This line may be horizontal vertical or diagonal. If the player manages to surround two pawns of the adversary (only two) when he may move them off the game. These pawns may be used again at the game. The player with the black pawns starts the game

—user summary

Agents of Timekeepers Incorporated, we need you! In trying to uncover the location of Cleopatra’s tomb, we seem to have accidentally unleashed a monumental curse upon the world that is ripping the space-time continuum apart. Nothing is where it should be—one agent has sighted a caveman eating a hot dog in Times Square! You must travel back and break the curse to restore the timeline to its proper order.

Embark on a thrilling journey through Ancient Egypt in this captivating escape-room experience. Solve challenging puzzles, break centuries-old curses, and discover hidden treasures as you delve into the mysteries of Cleopatra’s tomb. Use your powers of logic, cunning, and courage to solve the puzzles and save the future. The fate of time itself rests in your hands.

Perfect for friends, families, puzzle solvers, and Escape Room fanatics, it's suitable for ages 12+, 1 or more players, and has an average 90-minute gameplay. Plus, unlike most escape puzzle games, Timescape: Cleopatra’s Curse is entirely reusable and re-giftable as the content is not damaged during play if an erasable pencil is used for note-taking.

—description from publisher

Each player, in turn, takes the role of the emotion manipulator, while the others play as emotion investigators.
The manipulator's task is to draw 4 different situation cards and associate secretly the emotion reaction to them, by using the emoji tokens.

The investigators' role is to guess the emotion reaction of the manipulator. Every investigator scores 1 point per each correct guess, the manipulator scores the same point as the highest-scoring investigator.
If a player scores 0 point in a round, an emoji token is selected by the losing investigator, and then all players discard the that token.

The victory is awarded to the player with the highest score when the set of emoji token of each player is made of 3 or less elements.

—user summary

A quick and fun party game, in which you will have to test your ability to empathize with other people's thoughts!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un party game rapido e divertente, in cui dovrai mettere alla prova le tue capacità di immedesimarti nei pensieri degli altri!

—description from the publisher

Who knows their television and films?

This is a trivia game themed around TV and movies, featuring 100 cards, each with a question and 4 possible answers to pick from.

Play against friends and family, alone or in teams.

A team trivia game featuring 450 cards split between Music, Sport, TV & Film, and General Knowledge.

Cards are double sided - each side has 5 questions in the relevant category with a title relating to those specific questions.

Teams take it in turns choosing a category, then choosing a side from the two titles available on the chosen cards. They then work through the 5 questions on that side, scoring points for correct answers. Bonus points available if the question is passed, with the reader obviously not allowed to participate in answering.

After each team has had 20 cards the game is over, and scores are compared to find the winning team.

Players in the role of brave journalists are sent on a mission to collect unique pictures of incredible animals. You will have to drive cars around the Dinopark, and its inhabitants will certainly run away from you!
Plan your movement on the map correctly to get next to the right dinosaur, take photos and get victory points for completed editorial tasks.

A simple family-format game in which players roll dice, choose from the dropped values how much to move their car, and then, by throwing the same cube, how much the dinosaur will move. The task is to be in the same square with the dinosaur or in the next one, take a photo by pulling out a card and, collecting sets, complete tasks that bring victory points.

Welcome to Kittycombs, a co-op dungeon crawler for 2-4 players where you and your friends play as a brave team of Kitties trying to loot a dungeon.

Your team's goal is to cooperatively defeat the four Bosses hiding in the dungeon and escape back to the Entrance. As a Kitty, you can gain power by acquiring Item Cards or Monsters when you defeat them.

If the snake (which moves every turn unless a card specifies otherwise) reaches the Entrance before the Kitties have defeated the four Bosses and also made it back to the Entrance, the game is lost.

So be fast or your team and your treasures may get lost in the Kittycombs!

Rifles in the 'Nam, the next installment in Gottardo Zancani’s popular solitaire RIFLES series, this time set in the intense battlegrounds of the Vietnam War. Experience the gritty action of jungle warfare in a uniquely immersive single-player format. Say goodbye to traditional hex-maps; instead, dive into three simplified yet engaging maps (Tunnel, Jungle, City) that capture the essence of the Vietnam Theater.

Engage in thrilling firefights, with five to ten units per side, across various scenarios, including a convoy rescue, tense tunnel searches and fights, city battles, jungle ambushes, and a linked campaign. Utilizing blind chit draws and strategic die rolls, alongside carefully crafted charts, the game offers an authentic taste of the unpredictable nature of war.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Sunday Bazaar is a board game developed by a team of board game enthusiasts that brings the experience of a Pakistani flea market to every household in form of a board game.

It is a game of negotiation where players get to role play as buyers and sellers from the famous Sunday Bazaar. It’s a fun and exciting experience to challenge your bargaining skills amongst family and friends.

In Sunday Bazaar, players try to buy most items from the market to get most Victory Points. The active player tries to lower items’ price to get the best possible price, meanwhile other players try to counter the negotiations to not let the active player get away with a great bargain. 

All Items in the market are worth Victory Points and each item belongs to a set. Bonus Victory Points are rewarded at the end of the game if Players collect more items from sets as defined in the “End Game Bonus” Card. 

Game ends when either any player buys 5 items from the market, or when all the items from the market are sold out.

Enhance your Bargaining skills and enjoy the shopping in Sunday Bazaar.

—description from the publisher

Vil et un conte is a narrative game cards.

Each player plays a villain and must tell their version of the facts of the chosen tale while avoiding the trap cards placed by their companions within a time limit. The hourglass is turned over at each stage and the storyteller must say or mime the trap imposed. The storyteller obtains tokens if he passes the tests.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Vil et un conte est un jeu narratif d'ambiance et de cartes.

Chaque joueur incarne un vilain et doit raconter sa version des faits du conte choisi tout en évitant les cartes pièges posées par ses comparses dans un temps imparti. Le sablier est retourné à chaque étape et le conteur doit impérativement dire ou mimer le piège imposé. Le conteur obtient des jetons s'il réussit les épreuves.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tales from the Adventurers' Guild: A Supplement for Four Against Darkness
description:
Adventure Calls. This supplement for Four Against Darkness adds plenty of optional rules:

     Guidelines for Troupe play
     Rules for stealth, streetwise, banking, magic lockers, gambling and hidden treasure troves
     New equipment and spells
     New monster tables (riff-raff, ambushers, weird monsters, chaos cultists)
     Rumors, Scenes and Red Herrings
     Nine new thematic dungeons
     Rules for joining an Adventurers Guild
     Tables for Guild Jobs and unique minor quests
     Rules allowing you to play with 1 to 3 characters
     Trinkets – six new minor magic items
     A ready-to-Run hex-crawl campaign


This book requires only Four Against Darkness but also provides many interactions with other titles.
D6, d10 and d12 are required to play.

Long-long ago in the kingdom of Fairytales for princesses were Born. Lots of guests came for the celebration and the magic firebird flew along. It made a special present for the princesses: awarded them with the Talent of ruling the seasons. Since then on they were called - Spring, Summer, Autumn and Winter.

Time passed, princesses grew up and turned into young beauties. Princesses started ruling the kingdom in turns, each in her season. But once a prince from the far away kingdom caught the firebird and put it into cage. Day by day magnificent plumage of the firebird started disappearing and golden feathers stopped shining with radiance...

In a short while a great misfortune came to the kingdom of the four princesses: All the four seasons got mixed up. It started snowing in summer, and flowers bloomed in winter. So one day princesses went to the firebird to seek for help in restoring the order, but could not find the firebird anywhere. Then princesses Set off for a dangerous journey in search for the magical bird.

—description from the back of the box

Gameplay: spin the arrow. When the rotating arrow stops move the princess on which the arrow points one step forward. The first princess that reaches the firebird can make a wish to all other princesses and all together win as everything is back to normal.

Delve into the bowels of Norindaal and level up your heroes! This book has all the rules for the Heroic and Legendary adventuring Tiers, and describes the lethal dangers and limitless riches of the Forsaken Depths.


     Heroic skills, including Heroic spells.
     Legendary Skills,including Legendary spells.
     Heroic and Legendary magic treasures.
     Full profiles for all the monsters that dwell in the bowels of Norindaal.
     Map and content generators for the Six Rivers of the Forsaken Depths, the Citadels and the fearsome Cyclopean Idols.
    New tiles for underground dungeons and for the Six Rivers.


This book requires a copy of Four Against Darkness and Four Against the Abyss.
A D10 and a D12 are required in addition to the standard D6s.

boardgame: Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Colossal
description:
Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Colossal contains enlarged room tiles, tile trays with lids doubling as within-game tile holders, and a score track adjusted to the setup with the larger pieces (as they are not stored on the track in contrast to the standard game). The tiles are twice as big (i.e. four times bigger in area) compared to the standard tiles.

The expansion allows the Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Collector's edition to be played with larger pieces, especially convenient for solo play and conventions. For playing the game, the expansion requires the cards, card trays, and game pieces (player markers, coins, towers, tokens, etc.) of the base game.

The expansion contains a gameplay differences compared to the base game: The Foyer tiles (i.e. the starting tile for each player) of the base game have three entrances at both sides of the tiles. The Foyer tiles of the "Colossal" expansion have three entrances on one side and four entrances on the other side, thus allowing more variation in the starting position.

This expansion was available as a bundle with Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Royal Collector's Edition in the Kickstarter campaign.
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

description:
Pocket Ultimate is a card for two players. Every players get offense and defense card, and using them they try to advance the disk to the opponent's end zone and score a point. In each round the attacking player plays an offense card and the defending player reacts to it by playing a defense card. Each card has its unique ability. The attacking player tries to complete passes until they reach the opponent's end zone, the defending player tries to prevent the opponent's passes.
The player who scores the most point wins the game.
This game was released at the Paganello Beach Ultimate tournament in Rimini, Italy.

Up! is a Hungarian Ultimate Frisbee card game for 2-7 players. The player who scores the most Up! points or successfully call a Stall Out.
In each round a player rolls a dice for the deciding aspect of the players. The aspects are: Up!, Team Play (Cs), Speed (Gy), Routine (R), Height (M) amd dive (V). They play a card from their hand and the other players do so. The round is won by the player with the card of the highest value of the given aspect.
Any player can call a Stall Out at any time. In this case the player who called the Stall Out, playe all three cards in their hand and has to win all three rounds in order to win the whole game. Otherwise the game continues on.

A dice game similar to Yahtzee and 421, played with three dice.

The goal of the game is to gain the most points by rolling the best combinations. The starting player decides how many dice they will roll; the other players can roll the same number or less. Only the player that rolls the most valuable combination scores.

A combination of three times the same number (666, 555, etc.) is called a Zanzi. It is worth 300 points (but 666 beats 555, etc.) If not Zanzi is rolled, the value of the dice is rolled togather. An aces is worth 100, a 6 is worth 60, and the 5,4,3,and 2 are worth 5,4,3,and 2, respectively. So, 643 is worth 60 + 4 + 3 = 67 points.

Fight the air war over WW2 Europe in this 1-2 player deck building card game. Like our popular Airborne Commander, you start with a few cards and choose to use them to either attack enemy targets or to add cards to your deck.
Pick when to launch a mission and the target area but beware, you have limited time and air defense cards are added each turn. The strength of the bomber formations that survive the run in to the target areas determine how many cards are drawn to get hits. Each Target Area has a minimum number of hits required to win the game.
Do you execute pre-war doctrine and send unescorted bombers deep into enemy territory? Use your fighters for escort duty or send them on fighter sweeps? 
Assemble B-17 Flying Fortresses, B-24 Liberators,
P-47 Thunderbolts and P-51 Mustangs. Assign Lead Bombardiers and Ace Gunners.  Will they encounter FLAK filled skies, flocks of ME 109s and FW 190's, or a dreaded Me 262? Be picked up by radar that steers more fighters to them?

Send the boys up. Hope they hit the target. 
See how many make it back.
Then send them up again.

145 cards which also includes P-38 Photo Recon, Me 410, and more.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 7: Fight against Evil!
description:
Etheria needs help! What, on the surface, looks like a breathtaking world, is in reality a dystopia. It's gripped by the iron fist of the Evil Horde, which seized control over that planet a long time ago. Yet, hope flickers on the horizon. The Great Rebellion resist the oppressor's rule abd tries every day to end their tyranny. There's no time to spare - She-Ra and her friends require your assistance!

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Playing Jökö requires a sharp eye, quick reflexes and an ability to play faster than your opponent. The aim of the game is to be the first to get rid of all your cards by placing them onto the matching symbols.

PREPARATION

Shuffle the cards and deal out the entire deck to all players. Each player must hold their cards face-down in one hand. The other hand is used to play.

PLAYING THE GAME

For 2 players

Each player, one at a time, flips over the top card of their pile and places it face-up in a row in front of them. Each player has their own row, and they take turns to play up to 5 cards in each row.

When there are two matching cards on the table-identical symbols-, players must place one of their cards onto the matching cards before their opponent, irrespective of whether the cards are in the row in front of them or in the opposite row. This can happen before these rows are completed with 5 cards each. If so, after covering the matching cards on the table, players continue taking tums to complete their rows.

If each player has already placed 5 cards in their row and there are no identical symbols on display, each player collects the cards of their own row and places them face-down at the bottom of their pile of cards. The game proceeds the same way until one of the players gets rid of all their cards.

—description from the rulebook

At a glance

     Two books, two players, 50 enigmas: you will only reach the keys if you always work as a team
     There is only one rule: never show your book to another! You will have to communicate very well
     Linguistic, logical, mathematical, visual enigmas... keep your five senses alert
     When you find the key you will always be able to say... Efectiviwonder!
     The perfect original gift for those who have a Sherlock Holmes soul


What does it consist of?

     Are you and your best friend the queens of puzzles? Do you know a couple of geeks who are professionals at solving puzzles? If you are passionate about challenges, you can't miss ¡Efectiviwonder! * In the pair of books it contains, a couple of researchers will be able to find fifty enigmas organized into three levels of difficulty: elementary, advanced and expert. This book is not like the others: in each one you will find only half of the enigma that you will have to solve and there is only one rule: Do not show your book to your partner. They will be able to talk as much as they need, draw, mime, whatever they want except see both books at the same time. All the puzzles are totally exclusive, they are designed by Regalador's creative team. The biggest challenges to squeeze your brain, train your communication, develop your lateral thinking and exercise your imagination are here. When they solve them all they will only be able to shout... EFFECTIVIWONDER!


—description from the publisher (translated)

A game with almost 200 very own tests to organize a very Typical Spanish party
     Let the fun begin... roll the dice and read the card from one of its six different categories!
     For those fun people who are nicer than pesetas, this game will be, in two words: impressive
     This original gift will bring out the best in a dinner, a few drinks or any celebration and will disconnect you from your cell phone for a while.
     If you are looking for a different type of board game, check out all our games here


What does it consist of?

     If you have heard someone on the beach shouting "There are beers, potatoes...!", you remember who said "How are you?", you take a nap, you have ever sent someone to fry asparagus and your hair stands on end when The jackpot comes out in the Christmas lottery... You are Typical Spanish and this is your game. Do you want to know what challenges you will have to face? These are the six categories:
     How is the people: if you imitate Made in Spain characters like a flamenco dancer, do you think anyone will guess it?
     Become a Picasso: like Picasso, paint Spanish concepts like Naranjito, the rest will say what it is about
     Here there is tomato: there are mythical Spanish phrases that we all know. Who said them for the first time?
     Spanish Trivial: a question about Spanish popular culture and three possible answers
     White and in a bottle: say Spanish expressions moving your lips and gesturing trying to get the team to guess it
     Ask Reverte: the card proposes a word, the team will have to guess whether or not it is accepted by the RAE


The first team to get a card from each category will be the winner of Typical Spanish, a game that makes you laugh!

—description from the publisher (translated)

You will speak with your tongue hanging out, adding stupid or "otolozondo solo lo o" to the final words.

Roll the dice and find out how you will have to read the cards during the next round, there are 6 different categories!

Each round will last 30 seconds, match as many cards as you can in that time! You will keep each correct card. Whoever accumulates the most cards in each of the ten rounds will win a "Güiner" card.

—user summary

At a glance

     A game of almost 200 series tests to dance, guess and of course... Act! The ideal game to have a great time.
     You don't have to be a “serial buff”, just wanting to have a good time is enough!
     Today the protagonists are you: roll the dice and find out which category you get!
     Enjoy humming your favorite intros, remembering the iconic phrases of your favorite characters or acting out the scenes that have stuck in our minds.
     An original gift that will bring out the best in a dinner, drinks or any celebration.
     If you are looking for a different type of board game, check out all our games here


What does it consist of?

     The laughter marathon is guaranteed! From the frenetic fight for the Iron Throne in Westeros, through the mysterious investigations of Sherlock Holmes to Carrie's heels wandering the streets of New York, all the series in our life are here to make you have an unforgettable time. And if you don't have much of a habit with Netflix or HBO, don't worry, this game is not going to be an encyclopedia about series. How I Met Your Series is a party in the form of a board game. Its mechanics are very simple: one dice, six categories, two teams eager to enjoy and a single winner. These are the six categories, which team will win this season?:
     Trivial: One question and four possible answers. Choose the right one and show that you know more about series than Sheldon does about comics.
     Script: “Hello hello, neighbor!” What series does the phrase on the card belong to?
     Spoiler: Describe a series with only 5 words without using those that are prohibited. The series rippers have arrived!


Intros: Humming the intro to your favorite series seemed easy... until your team has to guess it.

     Outtakes: Action! It's time to bring out your acting skills: play the proposed scene and the rest will have to guess.


Series marathon: You will have to say all the series that come to mind following the proposed category, for example, Disney series!
Ready to play? Every time you manage to win a test, your prize will be the game card itself. The first team to get a card from each category will be the winner.

—description from the publisher (translated)

At a glance

     Do you know yours? Be the fastest to prove it!
     Read a question, choose your answer and place it face down without anyone seeing it. The others will have to quickly go for the option they think you have chosen to win the point, are you ready?
     280 cards and more than 500 questions to have a great time.


The game that will succeed at your next family gatherings and at any drink, picnic or get-together you have with friends.
If you are looking for a different type of board game, check out all our games here

     The ideal original gift for others to show how much they know you and vice versa. And remember! Keep your friends close and your enemies even closer ;)


What does it consist of?

     Have you ever had a soul mate, an inseparable cousin or that special person about whom you know every detail of their life: their fifth surname, their craziest hobbies, their ID number, their strangest tastes...? The definitive game has arrived to prove that this is the case. Playing is very simple, but be careful, because you will have to put all your senses alert and be the fastest. There are three types of cards: Game cards, Response cards and Voting cards. The player on his turn reads the question on the Playing Card and secretly chooses the number among the Answer Cards that corresponds to his decision and places it face down on the table. This is the starting signal for the rest of the players to quickly rush for the Voting Card with the number they think will be the correct answer. The player on his turn reveals his answer by turning over his card and whoever got it right gets the point. There are three different rounds where the number of answer options increases, therefore, the level of difficulty in getting it right also increases.


Round 1: Pink Playing Cards and Voting Cards 1 and 2.
Round 2: Blue Playing Cards and Voting Cards from 1 to 3.
Round 3: Orange Playing Cards and Voting Cards from 1 to 5.
Get all the points, fight for the correct answer and become the winner. Ready, set… GOTYA!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Warlord Ascendant is a wargame focused on small-scale battles, set in the world of Sacrifice: An Incense & Iron RPG. In it, you take control of a small warband; this can be anything, from a group of mercenary soldiers, to a horde of desperate peasants, or even an inquisitor with their retinue. Your goal is to grow more powerful and extend your influence over as much of No Man’s Land as possible, potentially turning you into one of the big names in the region.

boardgame: Bear Mountain Camping Adventure: Extreme Weather
description:
Don't forget your umbrella and rain boots to combat the extreme weather introduced in this expansion. Earn a lantern to protect your hand and experience even more fun activities.

With this expansion, weather will affect how the game is played through Weather Events. All of the possible Weather Events are shown across the Weather Board.

The game is played in rounds. The first round starts before the first player takes their turn, and ends when the gameplay gets back around to the first player (just before they take their second turn). The purpose of the Weather Event Tracker matching the color of the first player's Player Mover (camper) is so that all players know exactly when each round begins and ends.

At the beginning of each round, players decide who will roll the Weather Die for the round (it doesn't matter who rules the dice each round), to see which Weather Event will take effect.

To know which die to roll each round, you will look at the forecast. If the Forecast Token is sitting on a sunny space then you'll rule the white Weather Die. If it's sitting on a stormy space, you'll roll the black Weather Die. Since the Forecast Token starts on his sunny space, you'll roll the white Weather Die for the first round.

After rolling the die, move the Weather Event Tracker to the Weather Event for that round. This way, all players will know what the Event is throughout the round. You can then put the Weather Die back in its space on the Weather Board.

Paperi scatenati ("Ducks Wild") is a game inspired by the cartoon container called Go cart. This program was broadcast in the 90s in the evening on Italian TV. All the squares on the board, except the starting ones, have face down tiles. At each turn you move one space and turn over the cards face down. These can have positive and negative points that must be added or subtracted from your score. At the end of the turn you have to redistribute the tiles on other spaces on the board. The game ends when a marker reaches another spot space. Whoever has the most points will be the winner.

Critter Kitchen: À La Cart adds fun twists to locations, while additional Zous Chefs, critics, restaurateurs and rumors increase replayability. The 6/7 player mode allows you to bring everyone to the table on game night.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
This Critter Kitchen promo pack features additional fabulous critters with game changing powers.


     Critic - Chimera
     Critic - Cockatoo
     Restaurateur - Swedish Heff
     Restaurateur - Kit-Joy
     Mini Expansion - Moar Monkey
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

boardgame: Critter Kitchen: Lucky Duck Promo Pack
description:
This Critter Kitchen promo pack features 2 additional special guest critters.


     Restaurateur - Wolfgang Duck
     Zous Chef - Cosmo Octopus
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description: This Critter Kitchen mini expansion introduces 7 secret menu challenges that add a twist in gameplay.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
This Whispering Woods micro expansion adds new goal cards and mushroom tiles to Undergrove.


     2 Goal Cards
     4 Mushroom Tiles



Note: Whispering Woods is included in the deluxe edition.

boardgame: Team Quiz Challenge: Cats vs Dogs
description:
Dog or cat - which side are you on? 222 quiz cards with exciting, informative questions and quirky tasks about the furry four-legged friends will challenge your team's knowledge. And in the end, it may not be so much a question of dog OR cat, but simply a lot of fun! Party on: Which team will win the challenge?

—description from the publisher

Four mini expansions for the Carcassonne base game combined in one bundle: Score points in a renewed way with well-known German monasteries, or build famous cathedrals and appoint your meeple as archbishops. Visit the markets of Leipzig and use different districts to your advantage. If you've always wanted to collect tolls, place your meeple as tollkeepers on the roads around Carcassonne.
This micro expansion introduces more player interaction by adding 20 brand-new ability cards with wild effects into the base game's ability deck.

boardgame: Team Quiz Challenge: Natur vs Technik
description:
Nature or technology - which side are you on? 222 quiz cards with exciting, informative questions and quirky tasks relating to both topics will challenge your team's knowledge. And in the end, it may not be so much a question of nature OR technology, but simply a lot of fun! Party on: Which team will win the challenge?

—description from he publisher

As raccoons, you try to crack the code of the large safe by skillfully rolling the dice. The first person to enter all their numbers into the safe wins.

On your turn, you try to roll the numerical value of any tile in front of you. he dice eyes of the white dice are added (plus calculation). the dice eyes of the red dice are subtracted (minus calculation). Is the result a number that is displayed in front of you as a tile? Take the tile and throw it in the safe. No. What the heck: You can roll more dice one after the other.

—description from the publisher

Uninhabited islands with plenty of nesting sites next to rich fishing grounds: What more could a gannet's heart desire? But watch out: Other gannets have also discovered the paradise of Insularo. Who will manage to occupy the best nesting sites and conquer the most islands for themselves? Whoever has the most points at the end wins the exciting game!

Each player receives 12 identical cards, each of which may only be played once. The cards are used to build nests, fly or plant bushes

"Piks, bams and bangs! The porcupines love nothing more than popping balloons. But the competition is fierce because everyone jumps into the fray at once. Whoever gets rid of all their spade cards first wins the round. And whoever wins two or three rounds first wins the game. 
A spiky card game"

You have no cards of your own and do not have to get rid of them. The cards are valid for both teams - and the team that has the corresponding colored chip in its half of the field always reads out.

—description from the publisher

Melrose Place is a game inspired by the 90s TV series of the same name. By moving around the board you have to try to get all the photos of the characters from the show by passing some tests, answering questions not related to the series, using appointment cards, etc.

Beverly hills 90210 game inspired by the TV series of the same name. By going around the board you need to overcome different tests to be able to reach a certain number of points, based on the number of players, in the 6 school subjects. With audio cassette in which the voices of the protagonists of the show describe different tests to be faced.

In this survival game, face a unique challenge at a camp surrounded by wild animals. Employ strategy and cunning to outlast other and be the last one standing. Toss cards, accumulate effects, and brace for action as you fight for survival.
Get ready for an exciting gameplay filled whit unexpected twists!
Do you have what it takes to conquer the forest and be the ultimate survivor?

HyggeSNAK is a conversation game. For those who want to have a pleasant evening, with a little extra activity in the conversation.

In HyggeSNAK you have to guess, laugh and talk! You must try to guess which questions other players would prefer to answer, based on a few clues. Should we talk about favorite movies or food? Are secret hobbies revealed or maybe you will be entertained with the crazy experiences?

HOW TO PLAY
You take turns to draw two cards and choose a question that you would like to answer. The rest of the table must now try to guess which of the questions the player has chosen. You choose whether you want to play with points.

This game can be used alone, or expanded with the conversation cards from e.g. FriendSNAK and other Either/Or games from SNAK.

The game consists of 110 cards with 200 questions and rules.

boardgame: Sky Team: BUD Budapest Ferenc Liszt
description:
Flying over Lake Balaton, they arrived in the Hungarian capital from the southwest. The tailwinds are strong, so it is especially important to steer the aircraft with a steady hand. Approach the runway with caution.

Note: Available as a Print and Play

At a glance

     The game for couples with which you will surely end up upset


Concentration and speed will be the keys to getting your partner to take everything away

     Its travel size makes it your perfect companion
     The perfect original gift to give a touch of fun to the hottest nights


What does it consist of?

     Within this game are the most fun and different moments of passion as a couple. Despelotados is a game of concentration, observation and speed in which, of course, like any good couples game worth its salt, whoever loses takes off their clothes. Let your partner surprise you with this game in which the balls will make the temperature rise as the clothes disappear. Do you want to know how to play?
     Shuffle the cards and divide them into two piles that will be placed face down in front of each one.


In turns you must reveal the top card of your deck in a shared central pile.

     When the last two balls match, the first to hit the central pile will get his partner to take off a piece of clothing
     There are some special cards that do not need a partner... the hot balls: the last one to hit them will perform a special task ;)
     When you take off a piece of clothing you will take the cards from the central pile, whoever runs out of cards wins... and will be able to completely undress their partner!


—description from the publisher (translated)

At a glance

     The first intimate Escape Room on the market, a very original board game for spicy couples
     Solve the puzzles and indulge yourself in the racy scenes, thinking has never been so exciting!
     Its five different scenarios will take you to live five of the hottest stories. 5 games in one... and always with a happy ending
     Do not panic! If at any time you don't know how to continue, there are clues that will help you continue.


You can only complete the mission... by raising the temperature of the room!

What does it consist of?
The mechanics are simple: choose one of the five Hot Room stories and solve the riddles, puzzles, games and enigmas that each adventure proposes to know which card to continue with. Furthermore, to get to the end you will also have to recreate the spiciest scenes with your partner. Will you dare? Here is a small preview of each of the stories:

     The wedding of your life: The big day has arrived, your brother has decided to say I do! Between drinks, laughter and dancing you meet someone very very interesting. How and where will the party end?
     Conference room: A boring work conference becomes a very interesting place thanks to someone's mischievous gaze. Can you overcome your bosses and make the fantasy come true?


Romantic getaway: It has been many days without seeing each other and, finally, you will be able to spend a few days together in a rural house on the outskirts. Will you be able to find the right room and prepare the right environment to unleash your passion?

     Forbidden massage: Work, home, everyday worries... the time has come to pamper yourself and put yourself in the hands of someone professional, but you didn't even remotely expect them to be so attractive. Who was going to tell you that a simple massage could raise your temperature so much?
     Reunion of neighbors: You have just returned to your city to visit family after a time working abroad and you meet your next-door neighbor by chance. You realize that the years have treated him very, very well. Will you take advantage of the lost time?


Set the mood, overcome the most insightful mental puzzles and enjoy the most physical challenges. If you do everything right, every story will have its... happy ending ;) The original gift of the moment for the spiciest couples

—description from the publisher (translted)

Welcome to GRAPH-T.
You have been selected as testers of the GRAPH-T program.
GRAPH-T brings together the best talent from all over the world.
It is an organization that aims to create a better and more enjoyable world.

The purpose of this test is not disclosed to the tester.
GRAPH-T will contact the tester with excellent results later.

"GRAPH-T" is a game using cards whose strength is determined by the height of the graph.

—description from publisher

Graph-T is a must follow trick-taking game, where the cards have no number but a height to determine how strong it is. In a round, you must follow the lead suit or, if the player before you played a gray card or a card containing gray, you may play your gray card instead. If you have no card of the lead suit, you may play any card containing gray, the gray side facing you.

At the end of the trick, the player having the highest card of the lead suit or gray wins the trick, keeps every card from this suit, discards the others, before leading the next trick.

The game ends when players have emptied their hand, and compare their tallest suit from the trick they've won, placing cards from a suit one above the other. The player with the tallest stack wins the game.

—user summary

Like Cain and Abel is an 18-card trick-taking game with light bluffing for two players. The game comes in an easy-to-carry mint tin and plays in under 15 minutes.

Like the biblical siblings, you are making offerings of harvested grains and livestock to God. However, you only score points amassed during the rounds that you have God's favor--whether or not you do, each round, is a fact hidden from your opponent.

Make boasts about the offering you'll give each round, bluff about whether or not you were telling the truth, make them think you have favor when you don't, and play to tricks with a unique, three-card cadence.

At the end of five rounds, the holder of the Last Act token can strike down one of their opponent's scoring cards, but they must choose wisely. It's only then that players reveal which rounds they were actually in God's favor, and the final, real scores are tallied.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Skier's Paradise (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
This semi-modular expansion map has a Ski Resort theme. The main cities (chair lifts) appear on the base map.

The board grows organically outward by using the Urbanization Action which adds map tiles.

The map tiles have new cities (gondolas) on them as well as a variety of features such as towns (warming huts) and grass (non-skiable terrain) which provide strategic options when placed.

The goods are themed as skiers and the new Heli-Ski Transport Action allows one to drop off skiers at the peak.

—description from the designer

Retro Racer is an exciting dice-driven, card-based racing game for 1 player.

It is inspired by the old video game racers of the past: Pole Position, RC Pro Am, Rad Racer, F-Zero, and more.

Race your car on 11 different courses, encountering random obstacles and trying to beat the Pace Car. But be careful, don’t blow up! Speed is dangerous and the faster you go, the more perilous the drive! Dice determine the obstacles you face, and how well you control the race car! Roll different dice as you go faster.

Retro Racer does not use a board; it is totally card-based and dice-powered. It is a small-footprint game that gives the full racing experience. Your player dashboard, course layout, and track segments are all cards. Your car and obstacles are small chits.

Each course is composed of modular track cards. You will reveal new segments of track as you speed off your current track card.

It's Retro Racer!

For use with the Home Tabletop Entertainment System.

—description from the designer

Exclusive Status Cards that were available only during Kickstarter campaign and Russian crowdfunding campaign. 
These cards add more variety by granting special abilities to players. During the game each player can buy only one status card to add to his/her deck.

boardgame: Primacy: A Coyote & Crow Card Game
description:
In Primacy: A Coyote & Crow Card Game, two players take on the role of one of the five factions of Makasing, the nations first laid out in the award-winning roleplaying game, Coyote & Crow. Players will construct a deck of 50 cards from a pool of 250 that allow them to gain status and prestige against their opponents by completing daunting mutual challenges.

The world of Coyote & Crow is an alternate future where the Americas were never colonized. Instead, powerful Native nations rose in their stead. The world is a mix of science and fantasy. Players will have advanced technology, powerful ceremonies, and epic heroes at their disposal. Each of the five nations available to play have unique asymmetric advantages and disadvantages to create distinct play styles for every kind of gamer.

—description from the publisher

First released to the public in 1986, BARE FOOT-N-WILD is the newest, wildest, most exciting, card game ever! Combining strategy with the luck of the draw, this fun and easy card game is great for bringing together family and friends. Carefully designed cards make playing the game easy to learn. Using strategy, math skills, communication with partners, and learning new card terms, this game appeals to the fun side of kids of all ages!

BARE FOOT-N-WILD is a canasta/rummy style card game for two to six players. When playing with two, three or four players, you will use the cards with blue backs. With five or six players, you will use the blue-backed cards plus the cards with red backs. Playing with partners is recommended when four or six people are playing the game.

Playing BARE FOOT-N-WILD can be exciting for both singles and doubles. A family friendly card game, BARE FOOT-N-WILD is good for ages from 13-113. Take your family back to the days when families shared time at the table and games were happy, good, clean fun. Get BARE FOOT-N-WILD today!

—description from the publisher

MEMO GAMES Washington, D.C., is a family-friendly card game with hand-crafted illustrations. The game suits family nights, parties, and traveling. The card game is based on the classic memory-matching game, supplemented with special rules and cards with special action powers.
 Perfect for 2-5 players aged seven and up. The game's got simple rules jazzed up with snazzy twists. Bamboozle your pals with special power-up cards. Keep things spicy and shuffle those last ten cards in the grid.

Embark on a fantastic journey through Washington, D.C., right from your chair!
Revel in 25 hand-crafted illustrations designed to tickle your fancy. Warning: uncontrollable drooling over our eye-candy artwork may occur! Develop and enhance your memory, cognitive, and visual perceptual skills.

The streets of Washington, D.C., are teeming with tales. Dive into these stories! Delightful details of city landmarks await in the MEMO-GUIDE, available as a handy booklet or a quick QR scan right off the cards. No heavy encyclopedias or endless online searches!

—description from the publisher

Louisbourg 1758 is a two-player Operational level block board-wargame on the campaign to take the Louisbourg fortress. One side command the British while the other commands the French. The object of the game for the British player is to occupy the fortress of Louisbourg with their army or occupy Louisbourg harbor with their ships. The French goal is to prevent the British from achieving their objectives by the end of the game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Barnyard Zombies is a rummy style board game about herding zombie livestock.
When mad cow mutated into mad sheep, mad pig and eventually the dreaded mad chicken, humanity was completely unprepared. City slickers, unaccustomed to dealing with livestock, were gonners. But country folk survived by learning to herd the monstrous beasts. In the post apocalyptic war for limited resources, barnyard zombies have become the new weapon of choice.
Each player commands a rival survivor faction and shares two objectives, herd zombie livestock to enemy barnyards and recruit survivors. Each zombie has two traits, animal and color. Players manipulate these traits to Attach, Spawn, Link and Split zombie hordes. The Spawn and Link actions recruit survivors. When a survivor pool is depleted, all barn bunkers are breached. The more zombies in a player's barnyard, the more casualties they're likely to suffer.
To help them in their quest for dominance, players employ scavenged supplies like ammo, grenades, brains, barbed wire, radioactive waste and more.
At games end the player with the most survivors wins.

Dragons of Arcania is a competitive tile-laying game in which players take on the roles of draconologists and explore mythical dragon lairs.

Gameplay is divided into 2 phases: turn-based Tile Draft and simultaneous Tile Placement. Placement is NOT final until the end of the 2nd phase. Players use spatial reasoning to puzzle together dragons parts and form colorful specimens on 5x5 grids.

Completed dragons are worth varying amounts of points depending on their color combinations and chains. Color sequencing mechanics follow the natural order of the rainbow, so the more visually appealing the dragon, the higher it scores! Players can also earn additional points by completing secret & public objectives, turning sheep into tasty Snacks or grabbing the 1st place on the Speed Bonus track. The player with the most gold wins the game!

The party game of impossible choices!

We've all had to choose between 2 terrible things: "Would you rather...?". But this one is full of all GREAT things. And you and your group have to vote on what you can't live without.

Puppies or Pizza can be played multiple ways, but the easiest is to draw cards, set them up on the board in a tournament-style bracket, and vote on each set until you only have 1 card left.

Simple, right? Uh...have you ever tried to decide where to eat with more than 3 people? Impossible. It's like that, only with higher stakes...

The perfect game to get to know people better, play in larger groups, or for a casual conversation night. Though we should warn you that discussions can get heated.

boardgame: Unstable Unicorns: Unicorn of Illusions Promo Card
description:
Convention promo card for 2024
This has the "CON" convention promo logo for an expansion symbol.

Card Text
Unicorn of Illusions
Magical Unicorn Card
If this card would be sacrificed or destroyed, you may return another Unicorn card in your Stable to your hand instead.

boardgame: Happy Little Dinosaurs: Smell the Flowers Promo Card
description:
2024 Convention Promo card
Convention promo card for 2024
This has the "CON" convention promo logo for an expansion symbol.

Card text:
SMELL THE FLOWERS
If you would add this card to your Disaster Area, move it to the bottom of the Disaster deck instead, then search the discard pile for an Instant card and add it to your hand.

When the explorers discovered Sigma-24, the settlers celebrated the discovery of a seemingly perfect haven. The colony swiftly took shape, with the colony ship repurposed to construct the inaugural city on this promising new world. For decades, the colonies thrived in harmony.

However, the idyllic scene shattered during the 30th year, precisely at the arrival solstice. An unseen, unknown gravitational force emerged, tearing the planet asunder beneath their once-stable homes. In the ensuing month, violent earthquakes and tremors ravaged their once-thriving cities and vital infrastructure. Faced with impending doom, the clans rallied for a final, desperate effort to escape.

In a race against time, they erected a last outpost within the dwindling stable region of the planet. The colonists feverishly worked to construct a generational colony ship, their lifeline to a new world and a chance at survival.

Last Outpost is a simultaneous-play roll-and-write game that combines polyomino colony building, engine building, and tech tree mechanics. 
Players will permanently mark their player boards as they construct buildings, load evacuation shuttles, progress through tech trees, and manage their in-game economy.

—description from the designer

Truck-kun is a game in which players try claiming the best Isekai they can! Isekai are randomly generated by two cards: Subject card + World card and are assigned a random value determined by a six-sided die rolled by the player currently with the Truck-kun meeple.

Play continues until a player reaches 20 or more points or when a player can no longer play a character from their hand.

Let's see how you play the hero or villain, and which Isekai you can claim!

—description from the designer

Each player picks a faction to play (Mafia, Cartel, Yakuza, or Triad), and which scenario to use (Riot or Bank). An Ally and Action deck are created and the Scenario deck is placed next to the four-spaced Victory Track. Players are dealt a hand of four action cards and takes turns drawing and action card, an ally card, and placing a scenario card on the track, then using their action cards or ally's special abilities to impede other players by stealing or destroying their cards, preventing them from using abilities, etc. The goal of the game is to collect scenario cards for their victory points by using allies' power values to defeat them. When the "final job" card is placed on the victory track the game ends and players count up their victory points, with the highest score winning.
Replace the base game tiles with these eight new river tiles to adjust the balances based on your company's preferences and for more variability!

Welcome to the Old Wood, an ideal place for a growing warren of rabbits to call home. You are not the only one to think so though, so you will need to compete to forage the most carrots and grow your warren the largest! Be careful foraging too deep into the Old Woods as a hungry skulk of foxes prowl these trees, always on the lookout for their next meal!

Feed & Breed is a competitive push your luck card game where 1-4 players will compete to grow the largest warren of rabbit by gathering food and trying to avoid foxes!

—description from the publisher

Four cats live in a town protecting the cheese with their mother. To prevent them from stealing the cheeses, they have to hunt the mice that live on the outskirts of the town. Since they were born, cats have learned skills to hunt mice. However, they have never tried it. One day, the cats decided to hunt the mice while their mother was away. But it is not an easy task because mice are smart and hide in the garden. Now it's time to test your skills. Who will catch the most mice?

- Game instructions:

The cats must be guided by a colored die to cross the bushes and take the different paths to hunt the mice. But the mice can accumulate. Spin the wheel to see how many mice you have to place. The cat that has managed to catch the most mice at the end of the game is the winner.

Cuatro gatos viven en un pueblo protegiendo el queso junto a su madre, Para evitar que roben los quesos, tienen que cazar a los ratones que viven en las afueras del pueblo. Desde que nacieron, los gatos han aprendido habilidades para cazar a los ratones. Sin embargo, nunca lo han intentado. Un día, los gatos decidieron cazar los ratones mientras su madre estaba fuera. Pero no es una tarea fácil porque los ratones son listos y se esconden por el jardín. Ahora es hora de probar sus habilidades. ¿Quién cazará más ratones?

- Instrucciones de juego:

Los gatos deben guiarse mediante un dado de color para cruzar por los arbustos y tomar los diferentes caminos para cazar los ratones. Pero los ratones se pueden ir acumulando. Gira la ruleta para ver cuántos ratones te toca colocar. El gato que haya conseguido cazar más ratones al final del juego es el ganador.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Dream-Eaters: Campaign Expansion
description:
There is a hidden realm outside the world of the waking: a realm of whimsy, imagination, and nightmares. After occult author Virgil Gray writes about his adventures in these “Dreamlands”, you decide to set forth and learn the truth for yourself. Nothing could have prepared you for the terrible wonders that dwell on the other side…

In The Dream-Eaters Campaign Expansion, one to four investigators take on the roles of either a group venturing into the Dreamlands or their companions who have been left in the waking world. Together, these divided groups must confront not one, but two ancient terrors vying for control over both worlds. What is real, and what is fiction? And what secrets await in the dark, forbidden places beyond the gates of sleep?

This expansion combines all of the campaign and scenario content from the original The Dream-Eaters cycle, which was the fifth cycle of expansions for Arkham Horror: The Card Game. Players only need a copy of the Arkham Horror: The Card Game Revised Core Set to dive into the story.

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – The Dream-Eaters
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)
Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description:
Wield the power of Electricity, featuring lightning-fast spells and  deadly combos. Strike your opponents when they least expect it by chaining electric spells during their turns.

Part of Season 2 Kickstarter.

—description from the designer

Harness the warm light of the sun and cast spells at its zenith to shine the brightest. Perfect your timing and cast your spells as the sun reaches its highest point to make full use of their effects.

Part of Season 2 Kickstarter.

—description from the designer

Freeze your opponents and send chills down their spine with ice magic. Bend the cold to your will and set the pace to your advantage.

Part of Season 2 Kickstarter

—description from the designer

Swarm your opponent with a myriad of flowers. Resilient and able to bloom anywhere, flower magic will suffocate your opponent before letting you deal the finishing blow.

Part of Season 2 Kickstarter

—description from the designer

Manipulate sand and master its ever changing flow. Allocate your sand wisely between offensive and defensive capabilities to adapt to all situations and bury your opponent's chances of victory.

Part of Season 2 Kickstarter

—description from the designer

Use mind-bending spells to seize and alter the future thoughts of your opponents. Discard their deck before they even have a chance to use it and deplete their options until defeat becomes their only choice.

part of Season 2 Kickstarter.

—description from the designer

This Great Old one for the Great Cthulhu faction was released as part of Cthulhu - The Art of Richard Luong.

Rules:
How to Awaken Buffthulhu:

There must be a gate in Great Cthulhu's starting Area (can be abandoned or enemy-controlled).
    If this is first Awakening, pay 10 Power. Otherwise, pay 4 Power.
    Cthulhu appears in its starting Area. (Remember to gain 1 Elder Sign).

Cost: 10/4
Combat: 9
Ability: None

This unit can be awakened instead of the standard Cthulhu unit whenever Cthulhu is awakened, and was originally developed for Cthulhu - The Art of Richard Luong.

There's not much to say:

It's Football.

Everyone wants to play football, but this board game is the first true simulation of American Football.

You can run or pass the ball or make any play that you see on a standard American football game on television.

Every play develops naturally. You can run the ball or pass the ball on any play.  It is like playing at the speed of a slow motion replay where you control where the players move next.

One play is divided into small time segments separated by a dice roll. Each roll of the dice represents a few seconds of play time.  Each team rolls 2 dice to determine how much energy (energy units) it has for that roll.

The game is played on a light and dark green checkered field, and the scoreboard and down markers are built in. The offensive and defensive player pieces are setup on the field.  There are two main types of player pieces, Backs and Linemen.

To start a play the offense rolls 2 dice using the dice shaker.  The game can be played between two players like checkers or chess, or it can be played in groups of 2 to 4 people.

There are two teams, Home and Visitors. Each team takes turns rolling the dice and moving their pieces a little further until the play is over which can be a pass completion, incompletion, interception or tackle. The field represents 10 yards so each touchdown on the field scores 10 points (or yards) on the scoreboard. The team that reaches 100 yards first wins the game!

Note: Basic knowledge of Football is a prerequisite for the rules.

Recommended for ages 8 and up depending on brain size.  The game is not as difficult as Chess or Go, but more challenging than Checkers, Reversi or Backgammon.

There are 3 levels of play: Beginner (Amateur), College, or Pro-level. At the pro-level the game can be as challenging as Chess or even Go.  At the Beginner-level anyone can learn to play most of the basic moves in less than 10 minutes.

There are no cards or guessing.  This is real football, where you control the OFFENSE and the DEFENSE.

ARE YOU READY TO PLAY SOME FOOTBALL ???

—description from the publisher

Escape from the Tinker's Trap is a cooperative puzzle game. It can be played alone or with up to 4 people.  Players journey into the mysterious tinker's workshop working together to repair his various gadgets while searching for hidden puzzles and finally uncovering the truth behind the Tinker's trap.
Typical duration is 60-90 minutes.  This is a paper puzzle solving game and is really designed to be a one-time experience.  It is published by the Mysterious Package Company

Welcome to the uncharted depths of space! Your mission is to settle and thrive in this newly discovered solar system. But beware, for the journey ahead is filled with peril and competition. To emerge victorious, you must master the art of trading, resource management, and strategic development.
There are many things to keep in mind as you embark on your quest.
Use the unique skills and perks of your captain and ship to gain an edge over your adversaries. Water, metal, and gas are the lifeblood of your victory, efficiently gather and utilize these resources to advance put yourself ahead of other players. Trade with fellow players; be crafty in your negotiations, seeking to acquire scarce resources while offloading surplus goods. Build settlements and cities to establish a strong foothold in the solar system -- upgrade your infrastructure to enhance resource production. Invest in Progress cards to gain strategic advantages and stay ahead of your rivals. Enhance your ship’s and buildings’ capabilities for greater attack, defense, and range.

Compete against others to score the winning amount of points to become the most dominant force in the solar system.

Designed for 2-5 players, this game features (not including add-ons) 8 captain cards, 8 ship cards, 85 Progress cards, 30 Hazard cards, 60 resource cards (20 of each), 6 dice, 5 sets of player tokens and markers (1 player token, 5 settlements and cities, and 4 player card markers), 16 Habitable planet markers, 6 Hazard planet markers, 2 special planet markers, 4 wormhole markers, 5 player cards, and an instructions / rules pamphlet.

—description from the publisher

A new way to play your favorite pro football board game: play a complete pro football game in less than 30 minutes, with SECOND SEASON Express! The realism, excitement and drama of the full-fledged Second Season board game, distilled into a half-hour card-and-dice experience!

SECOND SEASON Express presents a game of football in "minute-sized" chunks of action, similar to our hockey and soccer games. The field is broken down into zones rather than specific yard lines. You choose a basic scheme for both the offense and defense, which represents a sequence of two to four downs. The offense chooses PASS/RUN, defense chooses from among the four base SS defenses, PASS-SAFE-RUN-BLITZ. Roll the dice, check the team cards, register the result. That's it!

It's important to note that SECOND SEASON Express is NOT a score generator or drive simulator. It's based on the original game, and uses the SECOND SEASON framework for play results. Play sequences succeed or fail largely on the abilities of the players, aided by smart coaching calls. You'll experience the same highs and lows of real football, with games decided by players making big plays--or NOT making them!

—description from the publisher

A new way to enjoy your favorite Canadian pro football board game: play a complete pro game in less than 30 minutes! The realism, excitement and drama of the full-fledged COLD SNAP board game, distilled into a half-hour card-and-dice experience!

Using COLD SNAP's established ratings, terminology and play mechanics, the express version breaks the game's play down into sequences or "chunks" of plays, rather than individual play calls. The offense chooses pass or run as the primary play-call mode, and the defense calls one of the four classic COLD SNAP defense settings (deep, up, safe or blitz) as its primary defense mode. The dice are rolled and the result is generated, same as with the original game. The difference is, the result will represent what happened over a sequence of plays, not just one play.

Total game-play time is condensed by about two thirds so a full game takes around 30 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Do you know the famous german illustrator and author Janosch? This game is about the characters from his most famous children's books: the tiger, the rabbits and all of their friends. 
In this game you play one out of 6 bunnys (the postmen) who has to collect 4 specific letters out of letterboxes to bring them to the tiger. You can also try to "phone" your fellow bunnys to jump to one of the letterboxes and get a letter when its not your turn. 
This tabletop game for children is all about collecting and memorizing and the world of Janosch.

—user summary

This is the ideal game to test the knowledge and creativity of all those who enjoy the best in entertainment.

Talent for mime or real actors on the rise? Encyclopedic knowledge about films and series? Voice always in tune and ready to sing any song or soundtrack? Perfect stroke to draw any 7th Art theme? Then it's time to shine!

Go play and bet your popcorn, answering questions about films and series, singing soundtracks, drawing and representing through gestures the unforgettable scenes from the most epic content ever.

Who has never invented a lame excuse to get out of an inconvenient situation? In this party game, messages are presented with embarrassing situations that we have to overcome. To make the game even more challenging, each player will have to invent an excuse based on the keywords presented. The best and most hilarious excuse wins!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Traitor’s Tide is a fast-paced, hidden-role dice game of suspicion, sabotage, and swashbuckling bluffery for 3–6 players. Set aboard a perilous pirate ship, players assume secret identities as either loyal Shipmates or treacherous Traitors, working together (or not) to fend off attacks from a rival ship while hoarding treasure for their side.

At the start of the game, hidden role cards are dealt, and traitors may secretly identify each other. Players take turns rolling five custom dice (with up to two re-rolls), hoping for favorable results—but danger lurks on every face. Waves cause ship damage, pirate flags trigger enemy attacks, and swords provide defense. Roll too many waves? Your ship takes a hit. Lose to the pirates? Treasure vanishes from your chest. Roll some coins? You might keep the loot… or steal from your fellow crewmates.

As in BANG! The Dice Game, luck is tempered by strategy and push-your-luck risk. But unlike many classic social deduction games—such as Werewolf, Secret Hitler, or Feed the Kraken—Traitor’s Tide features no player elimination, no variable powers, and no complex setup, making it an accessible entry point into the hidden-role genre, perfect for families, casual gamers, or fans of The Traitors TV series.

The game ends when either the treasure chest is empty or the ship is fully damaged. A final vote reveals who the traitors are, and teams compare loot totals. The side with the most gold wins—but glory goes to those who outwitted the crew and walked away with the treasure.

In A Peaceful Garden players place flowers to gain control of the positions near the butterflies and attract the most valuable ones.

It’s a 2 players game of strategy, long term planning and timing: when you attract a butterfly you also have to remove your flowers near it, so you need to be very careful not only on where to place them, but also on when to take them to gain points. Sometimes forcing the opponent to take a butterfly will result in a bigger gain for your garden!

The game is completely made of 3D cardboard figures, with card stickers to keep it close, so it’s completely plastic free!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the fast-paced city of trash and treasure where the bins are always full and the back-alley animals run free!

Jump into Trash Dash, the chaotic critter racing board game for 1-6 players by designer and illustrator Parker Simpson. Players will complete laps around a cluttered alleyway while scrapping with each other and grabbing special power-ups out of trash cans. The first player to finish the final lap claims the title of quickest critter in the city!

—description from the designer

Sandcastle Solitaire is a quick, breezy game for one player, that can be played with a standard deck of cards. Your goal is to build your sandcastle as large as possible, so it can withstand the waves when high tide comes.

Basic Turn Structure: At the start of your turn, draw anywhere from 1 to 7 cards from the deck (gather sand). After drawing cards, you must play all the cards in your hand to your castle, one at a time (improve castle). The first card must be played to a non-empty column in your castle, if one exists. Subsequent cards must played to an adjacent column to the previous card played, i.e. the column immediately to the left or right. After playing all cards in your hand, count how many columns are standing in your castle, and discard half that many cards from the deck, rounded up (tide comes in).

Trick Builders is a combination trick-taking / deck-building / drafting game where 2-4 players (5-6 with additional cards) compete to win tricks and score points. Between hands players acquire cards for the next hand. These cards give them a benefit for the round they are acquired, but then go into the common deck for future rounds.

There are two ways to play Trick Builders. The Standard Version is recommended for new players and players wanting faster, more streamlined play. Between trick-taking rounds, cards are drafted to add two cards to each player’s hand as the deck grows.

The Advanced Version adds a layer of complexity and depth. Each card has both a cost and an income. Winning tricks will give players currency to purchase stronger cards that will go into their hands for the next round and then into the common deck for subsequent rounds.

—description from the designer

A must-follow trick taking game where trump changes each trick.  Players vie for ingredients used to make potions which they will then sell to customers.

The 0 card of each suit is the debtor and can be played at any time, even if the player has a card of the led suit.  It allows subsequent players to also not have to follow lead.

Winner of a trick wins the ingredient card that is up for grabs and then can either claim a potion (which shows three required ingredients), brew a potion they've claimed, or sell a potion they've brewed to a customer looking for an ingredient that matches what is in that potion.

After all 12 customers are claimed, players get a point for each customer they've satisfied and each potion they've brewed.

—user summary

Phoenix Farm is an adorable Dice-Rolling Euro-Style game of Competitive Nurturing for 2-6 players! Like the age old fable of Rumpelstiltskin, players are given a shanty farmstead, a broom, and a box of glittering ashes, then given the daunting task of turning these materials into a wealth of gold! Each turn, you'll have opportunities to: improve your farmstead, shop for farming supplies, or tend to your firebirds. By developing as a Phoenix Farmer, you will earn more wealth each turn until you can afford to purchase luxurious gold coins with your excess profits!

The catch to this game is that there's only x21 gold coins available to buy. When the last coin is purchased the game ends immediately! Players are then judged on: How much wealth they accumulated in gold coins and how well they tended to their flock of phoenixes. Only the most nurturing AND profitable farmer will win the game! Do you have the passion and the strategy necessary to rise above the ashes of your competitors? Try your hand in this year's Phoenix Farming competition to find out!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Barefoot to Glory!: Estigarribia's Western Chaco Campaign in 1934
description:
Barefoot to Glory is a free print & play operational simulation of the battles for the control of the Western Chaco in 1934 (when the Battles of El Carmen, Yrendagüé and Ibibobó took place); unit scale is regiment, each hex spans 10Kms from side to side and every turn covers three days.

During the 1932-35 war between Bolivia and Paraguay, Pata Pila (barefoot) was the nickname the Bolivian soldiers gave to the Paraguayan counterparts due to the fact that they made war barefoot. During the second half of 1934, the Paraguayan barefoot troops achieved one of the greatest military victories of the 20th Century in an intense highly mobile (and under severe environmental conditions) campaign under the leadership of General Felix Estigarribia.

Although the Chaco War (1932-35) is mostly known for its WWI like fighting, as the one that took place during the siege of Boquerón or the battle of Nanawa; the Paraguayan commander in chief, Gen. Félix Estigarribia, lacking the resources to beat the Bolivians through pitched battle, developed a bold approach based in wide deep sweeps, aimed at the Bolivian rearguard, isolating his foe so that he either surrendered or died of thirst.

This simulation has its focus on this particular type of warfare. Game rules implement rational mechanics for simulating initiative, intelligence and command and control in battle.

—description from the designer

Introduction:Vadus (from Gothic: Waddjus, meaning "wall") is a board game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections The recommended size is 9 points per side, but boards of 7 or 11 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones in their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The connection part of a group is an arbitrarily chosen minimal connected subgroup containing two edge stones. If the group does not contain two edge stones, its connection part is empty.
     The surplus part of a group is the stones not in its connection part. This part can also be empty.
     The value of a group is the difference between (i) the number of stones in its connection part and (ii) the number of stones in its surplus part (the former minus the latter).


				
				
					Turns:
     On the very first turn, Black places one stone on any point.
     From then on, starting with White, the players take turns placing exactly two stones each on empty points.
     A player is allowed to pass instead of placing their two stones.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass their turn consecutively, and the player with the group of higher value wins. In the case of a tie, their second-largest groups are compared, and so on until reaching a pair that doesn't have the same value.

				
				
					Notes:This is the improved version of the previous Vadus, being much more organic, which was designed by Saïd Galdseid and Michael Amundsen in February 2024.

—description from the designer
