Clash Masters, is an engaging card game that offers a refreshing blend of simplicity and strategic depth. The game kicks off as players construct their defenses, paving the way for an exciting journey of destroying each other's fortifications and engaging in intense duels. The ultimate triumph awaits the last player standing.

Clash Masters is a card game for 2-4 players and the age target is 7 and above. Combine more decks to increase the player limit.
The game features a range of attack cards and a set of five versatile action cards. Utilize attack cards to engage opponents in strategic confrontations, while the action cards offer a spectrum of unique functions.

Game content:
- Game box
- 51 playing cards
- Rule booklet

How to play (in brief):

     Shuffle the deck
     Deal 8 random cards to each player.
     All players set up 3 defense cards, which leaves 5 cards in hand.
     In turn, use the attack cards to destroy enemy defenses and use the action cards to turn the tide of the battle to your advantage.
     The game is played in a clockwise direction and the person to the left of the dealer starts the game.
     All players will in turn try to destroy each other’s defenses.
     When a player loses all 3 defense cards, the player may be challenged to a duel. If the duel is lost, the player is eliminated from the game. If the duel is won, the player is rewarded with +1 new defense card and gets to continue the game.
     The last player remaining wins the game.


—description from the publisher

Rescue him from the dungeon with the two keys that we will obtain during the game. Likewise, we will steal a player's dragon and fortify it with defense so that another player does not win. The game has an upgrade store and has special skill cards. To carry out attack duels, 2 dice are used and 3 for damage. Whoever manages to reach their own dragon with the 2 keys WINS!


Reino Dragón es un juego de duelo y estrategia condimentado con un poco de azar. El objetivo es conseguir recuperar el dragón secuestrado por otro jugador utilizando cartas de Hechizos y habilidad, mientras fortificas y defendes tu reino. Se puede jugar de 2 a 4 jugadores y las partidas duran aprox 45 min. Mezclando lo mejor de los TCG, LCG y juegos de mesa.

Es un juego de duelo, se juega con 5 cartas en mano y se roba al comenzar el turno siempre hasta llegar a 5. El objetivo es conseguir utilizando ataques y diferentes habilidades (combos) para llegar a recuperar nuestro propio dragón que es robado por otros héroes o heroínas (otros jugadores). Rescatarlo del calabozo con las dos llaves que conseguiremos durante el juego. Así también nosotros le robamos a un jugador el dragón y lo fortificaremos con defensa para que no gane otro jugador. El juego cuenta con una tienda de mejoras y tiene cartas de habilidades especiales, para realizar los duelos de ataque se utilizan 2 dados y para el daño 3. Quien consiga llegar a su propio dragon con las 2 llaves GANA!
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

boardgame: Reino Dragón: Misión por la Reina – Campaña en Solitario
description: With this expansion for Reino Dragón you can play alone and practice different games and it is important to know that YOU WILL NOT ALWAYS WIN. Find a strategic way to beat him with the sequences of cards you play. Defend your dragon from Morgon.

boardgame: Les batailles de Louis XIV, 1703 l'année des victoires: Höchstädt, Speyerbach
description:
In September 1703, the Franco-Bavarian army of the Danube clashes with two enemy armies. One is under the command of Count Styrum and the other of the Margrave of Baden. On 17 September, Villars is informed that Styrum’s army is marching on Höchstädt, ready to establish a bridge at Grenheim to launch a combined attack against the French Army at Nodendorf on the other bank of the Danube.

In November 1703 , the latter’s troops cross the Rhine and join forces with the Prince.  The troops camp between Heilingenstein and Dudenhofen in front of Spire (Speyer). The attack against the French forces besieging Landau is planned for the 16th. In the morning of the 15th, he reaches Pracontal at the appointed time and, now with 18 000 men, prepares to face off against 20 000 men from the imperial army. At 13.00 the French Army in battle order engages the combat against the enemy’s left wing which is only barely deployed and is disorganised!

The game proposes two scenarios for each of the battles.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Become one of the Dreamscape Weavers, mighty overlords who seeks to expand their influence over the eternal realm of Dreamland. Deploy your loyal minions to the Dreamland where they will scatter and mark your territory according to orders you gave to them. Only the most influential Dreamscape Weavers will grant the power to rule the dreamlands. There are many Dreamscape Kingdoms to fight for. Would you prefer to deploy your minions to bigger terrain of Lumina or Zephyra or would you like to send them to safer but smaller ones like Nixia or Ventia? It is your decision to make, in every round unexpected things can happen leading to your enemies taking over your lands! Also don't forget about the Nightmare Machine that can get in your way. Will you master the challenge and become the most powerful Weaver at the end of the game?

Dreamscape Kingdoms is a roll and write print and play game prepared for 1 to 4 players, age 8 and up in which you will become Dreamscape Waver and fight for the most influence over various kingdoms. You will Roll the Dice, draft one of them and use it to send your minions to various positions gaining influence over the map. Places you captured will give you power of influence depending on how many terrains you got and how many minions and terrain you have captured in one of many Dreamscape Kingdoms. The game takes 30-60 minutes to play, but it is full of satisfying decisions to make! If you like Territory Control Games you should try this one for sure!

—description from the publisher

Beneath The Sewers is a solo print-and-play tri-fold game. Recruit a team of mice, clear a path, and lead the rest of your colony to safety.

Humans have created a machine. A machine so terrifying that it forces the mice to evacuate their homes. The sewer pipe, your only escape route, has been invaded with the machine’s tentacles. In Beneath The Sewers, you must recruit a team of mice, clear a path, and lead the rest of your colony to safety.

Each sewer pipe is segregated into 6 levels, consisting of 3 parts and 6 segments. You must climb up segment by segment to move on to the next level till you reach the exit and escape. Each level will have a starting number of machine tentacles. Roll a d6 to determine the part and segment where the tentacles will emerge. More tentacles might also emerge after the end of each turn.

You can perform several actions per turn depending on the number of recruits you have. Your move count is also dependent on your endurance. The higher you climb, the lesser endurance you will have. However, this can be mitigated by purchasing upgrades using experience points earned by eliminating the tentacles.

You and your colony will be safe once you have climbed all 6 levels.

In a relaxed and challenging way, #Fica a Dica will test your skills in giving tips, miming and guessing the names of people or things during three exciting rounds.

With up to 16 players divided into two teams, everyone will try to get the most words right while racing against time! The team with the highest score wins!

But stay tuned! The same words that fall to the opposing team may, in subsequent rounds, fall to your team as well. Memory and attention can help.

And, to get even more excited, teams will be able to write the names of people who are part of their circle of friends on the blank pieces of paper, personalizing the game in a very fun way.

boardgame: LEADERS: The Combined Strategy Game – Soul of Africa
description: One of the nation expansions for the main game. This set gives a new possible leader with special characteristics (non attacking region decree).

Simulate the evolution of plants and animals over roughly 500 million years during the Phanerozoic period. Each player has a unique goal to score points, so they will try to influence the ecosystem in different ways.

During the action phase, players will draft cards and play mutations, evolve species or use events to their advantage.
Each round ends with a nature phase, where the ecosystem finds its balance as plants grow and animals feed.

Will you cause the extinction of the dinosaurs or will you rejoice when inventive animals thrive?

—description from the designer

Big Time Boxing simulates the sport of professional boxing like no other game before it. You'll move your boxer inside and outside, throwing a variety of punches and combinations. Learn your opponent's style, then wait for the best time to go for the knockout or trap him in a corner. You might get hurt or warned by the referee for hitting below the belt. Worse, you could get knocked down (and out!) if careless. Your boxer can win by KO, TKO, or decision if the fight "goes the distance" – and remember, the ringside judges are always watching (and sometimes mistaken).

—description from the designer

Hydrologic Cycle is a fast-paced, 1v1 or 2v2 player, area-control card game where players fight for control of the global water cycle. To win, players must use their knowledge and memorization skills to connect water cycle reservoirs and transport processes and end the game with more of their cards covering the game board.

Hydrologic Cycle uses simultaneous play to keep the game flowing (like water in the global water cycle!), which means players are drawing, playing, and discarding cards in real time as they attempt to match reservoirs and transport processes to claim areas of the board. Cards can be played on top of one another as play progresses, so who controls a particular location on the board will change rapidly during the game. A player can also play a card on the board more than once, so long as it is played with a directly connecting card.

The game ends immediately when every space on the board is covered with a card. The winner is determined by noting the player whose cards cover the top of the most spaces on the board.

For science educators and home schoolers, Hydrologic Cycle meets a number of Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) and Common Core Math standards for learning the global water cycle for grades 3-12 using the introductory side of the game board, while the advanced side of the game board is appropriate for use at the university level. A curriculum guide containing suggestions, lesson plans, optional rules, and other resources for using Hydrologic Cycle in your high school or college classroom can be purchased at the Central Michigan University Press website.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
publishers: Center for Learning Through Games and Simulations

description:
Expansion and Design Kit for basic fighter combat and advanced fighter combat.

Includes counters for:

SBD-5 Dauntless dive bombers
Ju87B-1 Stuka dive bombers
Il-2 Sturmovik dive bombers
D-3A Val dive bombers 
He 111P-2 Heinkle medium bomber
G4M2 Betty medium bomber
B-25C Mitchell medium bomber
Bristol Beaufort medium bomber
Avro Lancaster heavy bomber
B-17G Flying Fortress heavy bomber
B-24J Liberator heavy bomber
B-29A Superfortress heavy bomber
& blank fighter size compass circles
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
www.knowthyself.space

Know Thyself is a bold, original, and deeply human 60-minute transformative experience disguised as a game.

Designed for 2–4 players, Know Thyself uses universal personality archetypes to spark introspection, emotional honesty, and some of the most meaningful conversations players will ever have around a table. It is not about winning — it is about seeing and being seen.

Each round, players draw an archetype card representing a core human trait — such as Idealist, Provocateur, Caregiver, Rebel, or Visionary. Players then cast secret votes from 1 to 5, rating how strongly they believe themselves and each other embody that trait.

Votes are revealed one by one.

What follows is the heart of the game: players explain why they voted as they did — sharing stories, observations, blind spots, and moments of unexpected insight. Conversations naturally deepen. Assumptions are challenged. Laughter, vulnerability, and powerful emotional shifts emerge organically.

100% five-star reviews. Backed by nearly 2,000 supporters on Kickstarter. The game has been described by players as “a masterpiece," “unlike anything else on the market” and “the most meaningful game experience we’ve ever had.”

Beyond the tabletop, Know Thyself has attracted interest for use in therapy, marriage counselling, group facilitation, rehabilitation and prison reform, and other social and reflective settings.

Know Thyself is an entirely original concept, never previously attempted in modern board gaming:a game that replaces competition with reflection, strategy with empathy, and turns play into a mirror.

Pick up the game at www.knowthyself.space

boardgame: PNW: Card Games Inspired by the Pacific Northwest
description:
PNW: Card Games Inspired by the Pacific Northwest is a beautifully illustrated book of six modern games for fans of card games, board games, and the Pacific Northwest.

All you need is a deck of cards to hunt with a pod of orcas, forage for tasty mushrooms, brave the swift rivers of the salmon run, and more. Created and illustrated by Pacific Northwest locals as an ode to card games and the beauty of the PNW, these six games are a great way to dive into a world of fast and fun games with a familiar set of components. Free official downloads of the games are available on our itch page.

Forage | Players: 2-8 — Complexity: 1
Explore a forest and gather baskets of mushrooms faster than your opponents in this set-collection game.

Trail | Players: 3-4 — Complexity: 2
Pack supplies for your journey, then hike past landmarks in this drafting and resource-management game.

Orca Isles | Players: 3-6 — Complexity: 2
Place orcas from your pod to gather prey, and collect poker hands to hunt at wooded isles in this worker-placement game.

Cabin by the Sea | Players: 1 — Complexity: 3
Build stacks of cards from the sea, the beach, and your supply. Each turn, the sea gives and the sea takes in this solitaire game.

Salmon Run | Players: 2 — Complexity: 4
Brave the chilly waters and predators along the salmon run, acclimating to changing conditions of the sea, the river, and the stream in this deckbuilding game.

Signal Fire | Players: 4 — Complexity: 5
Communicate to your partner with smoke and fire signals, moving around the ridge and valley strategically in this partner trick-taking game.

—description from the designer

Take turns to describe and guess words, but make sure you don't give it away.

The describing player must choose a word on a card and describe it, aiming for half the players to guess the word correctly. If everyone or no-one guesses correctly then you are awarded no points and must wear the hat of shame.

Earn points by guessing correctly or by getting as close to half the other players to guess your word. Points awarded changes depending on how many players there are.

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

boardgame: Bop It!: Star Wars – The Mandalorian: The Child
description:
This small Star Wars edition of the classic Bop It! game features the franchise's beloved character The Mandalorian The Child.

The game commands you to Bop it, Twist it, Pull it, in the voice of The Mandalorian and you have to respond as fast as you can. If you get it right, you get another chance, but if you don't, you're out! The commands get faster the longer you go on, and you'll hear the sounds of The Child as you play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Firefly Adventures: Brigands and Browncoats – Escape the Dragon's Lair
description:
Steal important information from the home of the planet's most notorious crime boss, who happens to be nicknamed, The Dragon? No problem, In and out. Easy money. Well... things didn't go smooth and now you and your crew have to hightail it out of there, doubletime!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Firefly Adventures: Brigands and Browncoats – Joyride
description:
You touched down planetside ahead of schedule. Rather than twiddle yer thumbs waitin' for your contact, you stopped by the bar to celebrate a job well done. Your celebrations turned out to be premature however, when some hoodlums hotwired your M.U.L.E. and drove off with your contraband in the back seat!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Firefly Adventures: Brigands and Browncoats – Part Problem, Part Success
description:
The Client wants to deliver some Delicate materials to a contact outside Town. In and out with no fuss. Pity the M.U.L.E. has broken down and needs a part. The locals can help provided you don't snoop where you ain't wanted. There is another issue, a band of cowboys have caught wind of the Job and want the goods for themselves, or destroyed. They will reach Town at any moment.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Firefly Adventures: Brigands and Browncoats – Derelict
description:
Your employer had some sensitive data he sent around the normal Alliance routes—that meant traveling through Reaver space. The transport had a full complement of passengers hopeful for a new life on the rim worlds at the fringes of Alliance control. They didn’t get what was expected. The craft is a total loss. It remains structurally intact, but has been dead in the water for weeks until its discovery and it bears the distinct marks of Reaver boarding. The find came across the Cortex and your employer needs you to collect his data before the Alliance arrives to claim it—and he’s willing to pay you well for your efforts.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Firefly Adventures: Brigands and Browncoats – Unification War
description:
As sergeant of the 57th Independent Ground Brigade you’re defending the Serenity Valley. An airborne Alliance Skiff threatens your men and to take it out you’ll need to reach a nearby UA 571-D, and anti-aircraft gun. Unfortunately it lies on the other side of a squad of Alliance soldiers hidden in trenches. No one leaves Serenity, you just learn to live there. Let’s go duck hunting!

—description from the publisher

"D-Enhancement", a genetic therapy that improves human abilities by using an unknown mineral called "flowshell", has advanced further. DNA Inc. has found a new method of utilizing D-Enhancement to improve Noman's abilities. By injecting the processed flowshell into a Noman through a special machine, powerful abilities will manifest.

In Biohack: Flowshell Expansion, one of the Noman tokens can be replaced with an "enhanced Noman" with new abilities. You will need to acquire flowshell crystals through biohack research for utilizing these abilities.

Collect flowshell and utilize your new enhanced Noman's ability to be a better scientist!

—description from the designer

The news of the race to bring Dr. Maischberger's research back to life has reached the other scientists!

In Biohack: Scientist Expansion, more scientists with different capabilities are joining the biohack research.

—description from the designer

The First Great War ended in December 1916 with Wilson’s Peace, a negotiated settlement that left all parties vaguely dissatisfied (except those no longer called on to die by the millions). The Second Great War erupted in August 1940, with Serbian, Russian, French and Italian armies attacking the Central Powers in a naked grab for territory and power.

The Imperial Russian Army, equipped and trained for new tactics known as molniyenosnaya voyna, launched an unprovoked invasion of Central Powers allies Poland and Lithuania. The defenders had no answer for the masses of Russian tanks supported by helicopters and rockets. But they hung on grimly nonetheless, waiting for help to arrive from their Imperial German allies.

The Germans dispatched their most secret weapon to blunt the Russian advance: the massive mobile fortresses known as Land Cruisers. These huge, crawling machines bristled with heavy cannon and automatic weapons, and even carried their own dedicated troops.

Land Cruisers: The Lithuanian Campaign is an expansion set for Panzer Grenadier: Land Cruisers (which is itself an expansion, but that’s okay). It comes with 88 new playing pieces plus a set of 10 scenarios so you can play with them.

—description from the publisher

In May 1940, German panzer divisions attacked the Soviet Union, beginning a two-year campaign that finally ended in the summer of 1942 with the defeat of the Stalinist regime. Immediately afterwards, Germany’s supreme leader turned his attention to the Western allies, Britain and France, beginning the lengthy conflict that would become known as the Long War.

1942: Moroccan Division is a Panzer Grenadier expansion, set in this alternative history (it’s a sequel to 1942: The Defense of France). The Moroccans, seen in our 1940: Swallows of Death book (which covers their actual battles in 30 scenarios), have received the weapons the French Army hoped to soon issue to its troops. The French defeat in 1940 precluded that, but that’s not the case here: the Moroccans (and the French, too) have the MAS40 semi-automatic battle rifle, the AMX38 light tank, the 75mm TAZ 39 anti-tank gun, and better artillery, too. They can stand toe-to-toe with the Germans, and in these scenarios, that’s exactly what they proceed to do.

To play those scenarios, you’ll need a few other items:

1940: The Fall of France. This is a large, complete Panzer Grenadier game with 40 scenarios of its own plus eight maps and lots of pieces. This is where you’ll find the game rules and the maps you’ll need, plus a lot of the pieces.

Kursk: Burning Tigers. This is also a large, complete Panzer Grenadier game with 40 scenarios from the “northern flank” of the June 1943 Battle of Kursk; you’ll need this game for some German pieces.

Elsenborn Ridge. This is another large, complete Panzer Grenadier game with 40 scenarios from the “northern flank” of the Battle of the Bulge; you’ll need this game for some German pieces.

1940: Swallows of Death. This is an expansion for 1940: The Fall of France, a book with pieces for Moroccan troops fighting for France and 30 more historical scenarios.

1942: The Defense of France. This is a downloadable expansion for 1940: The Fall of France, with 88 pieces that you’ll have to assemble yourself (just like the pieces that come with this expansion), and ten more scenarios.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Alice in Wonderland: Wet Girls and the Caucus Race
description: As Alice characters in the caucus race drenched in Alice's tears, you will play 1-2 cards from a hand of 2, drawing tokens to reduce your wet rating and performing card effects. Afterwards, draw back up to 2. At the end of a round, points are awarded based on how dry players are. Most points wins after a few rounds.

The World Tourism Organization organizes the "Around the World" competition, calling on people who like to travel to compete to visit tourist attractions using limited air tickets and numbers on them. On each journey, the more attractions you visit on different continents, the higher your score. If you visit a continent repeatedly on multiple trips, try becoming a "travel expert" on that continent to get extra points. Can you win among the many travel experts?

What's the best way to break out of prison? You could smuggle some tools, dig a hole, and crawl out. Or you could horde ingredients from the bakery and make a pineapple cake to bribe the guards to look the other way while you grab their keys.

In Pineapple Cake Prison Break, players race to be the first to escape jail.

The core rule set involves bluffing. On your turn, offer another player a card face down and say what one of the objects on it could be. They say if they believe you or not. Reveal. If they were correct, they choose to keep it or not. If they were wrong, you choose. Both of you want to finish a set of 5 tools or 5 ingredients. But you also want to avoid 3 matching items, which will get you caught and reward the other player.

You may also play without bluffing rules, either half-truths or open information. All three rulesets emphasize strategically choosing which player to target and which card to offer.

The game is enriched with optional action cards that can dramatically change the game. They are still offered to another player and may backfire if they catch you bluffing about them.

Includes rules for solo and co-op play.

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. Each player starts with 300 DM and has to make deliveries to 10 customers. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. On arriving on space x the players get bonuses or penalties according to the sum of money they own.

The players with the most money, after having completed his deliveries, wins the game.

—user summary

The players take turns to roll 3 dies and tries to get a triple. After choosing a number, they may roll again with 2 dies and a third time with 1 die. If they get a triple, they get get the tokens from the corresponding space on the game board. If not the have to pay a token for each number different from their chosen one to the space of this number (double if it is a "1")

If 3 following numbers are rolled, the player may change one number and roll the third die two times.

On the third roll a "6" is a joker.

If a player is out of tokens, he is out of the game. The player with the most tokens at the end of the game (after an agreed on time) is the winner.

—user summary

Perfect Diet aims to be a simple and fun support to make people understand how important daily nutrition is when it comes to taking care of your body. In this case "diet" is not intended as a diet with the sole purpose of making you lose weight, but as a tool for health. By "perfect diet" we mean the one identified by the WHO, which has published a list of advice to follow for a healthy and balanced diet.

In the game, these tips become the objectives to be achieved to collect points and win the game. The game is divided into 5 days and each day consists of 4 meals. Draw cards and create the most balanced meals; calculate daily and weekly points.
Respect the goal cards and discover how to eat best for your health.

A board game designed for the many manga and anime fans of all ages, a particularly fashionable topic in recent years.
400 questions divided into the four categories manga, anime, manga culture and genius. 60 additional challenges divided into three categories: forbidden words, draw or mime, say it with a word or hum it.
A game for two to eight players to challenge friends, enthusiasts or not and of all ages, given that the game includes both the great classics of the past and the latest productions.
Whoever answers the most questions and wins the most challenges, thus demonstrating the most culture and intuition, will be the first to reach the dragon's head at the end of the game and win the game.
It contains a 96-page book which, in addition to summarizing the rules of the game, presents the reader with an overview of the most well-known characters and series of all time.

Find the Knights of the Zodiac on your Monopoly board. In this special Saint Seiya edition, inspired by the anime, rally as many Knights as possible to Athena's cause. Gain power by crossing the sanctuary to reach the cosmos before the last flame of the clock seals Athena's fate.
Draw Knights and Cosmos cards, develop new techniques through training and meet your favorite Knights.

In this Roll & Write game, players will try to gain as much profit as possible with their business of lodging renters.

Each turn we will have 3 cards of renters that can range from 1 to 5 people. These are willing to pay an amount of coins for an apartment and even pay extra if the apartment has certain services. We can host up to 5 people per turn (from one or several cards).

At the beginning of the turn we will roll 1 black die and 2 yellow dice, the black die is common for all players, simultaneously each player chooses one of the yellow dice. With the combination of these 2 dice we form the position coordinate where to place a tenant card in our apartment block. We can place adjacent to this coordinate another tenant card if we do not exceed 5 people staying per turn.

The building has broken elevator, community air conditioners and wifi on the top floors, in addition to the apartments on the sides require soundproofing. Throughout the game we will be able to acquire repairs and soundproofing that we will assign to our choice in order not to suffer penalties and to make our rents profitable.

At the end of the turn we draw a card until we have 3 tenant cards again. The game ends when a collection goal is reached.

It includes an advanced mode where you can score by rows and columns completed and suffer penalties for not taking advantage of the space in the apartments by including all the tenants it can accommodate.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Captain America and the Original Human Torch
description:
After receiving the Super-Soldier serum, the once sickly Steve Rogers fought for freedom on the front lines of World War II. The serum grants him superhuman strength, stamina, and agility, making him one of the strongest heroes in the world. Along with his signature vibranium-infused shield, Captain America contends with any foe who threatens the innocent.

The Original Human Torch was not born but built. Created by the brilliant Professor Phineas T. Horton, the android burst into flames when first exposed to oxygen. Eventually, the synthetic man learned to control the flames that wreathed his body and vowed to help humanity. From the battlefields of the Second World War to the streets of modern cities, the Original Human Torch burns brightly to banish the darkness of the world.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Baron Strucker and Arnim Zola
description:
Baron Wolfgang von Strucker may have been born into the ranks of the nobility, but he has spent his prolonged life in pursuit of nefarious goals. As an ally of the Red Skull and wing commander of the Death’s Head Squadron, Strucker waged campaigns of invasion and oppression. As the founder of HYDRA, Strucker’s villainy has spread deeply across the world. After becoming infected with the deathspore virus, Strucker gained an array of superhuman abilities, including suppressed aging that keeps this notorious villain alive to this day.

Doctor Arnim Zola was a scientist who pursued the creation of super-soldiers for the purpose of world domination. Performing cruel experiments during World War II, Zola worked alongside villains like Baron Zemo and the Red Skull. The mad doctor transferred his consciousness into a synthetic body, allowing him to continue his nefarious work well into the future, using his genius intellect and scientific knowledge as weapons against all who would stop him.

—description from the publisher

Since the statue was stolen from the temple, the water flowing through your village has turned brown and rotten. Could you help the temple servant and replace the statue of the god?

This demo adventure will introduce you to the basic principles of the game and give you a taste of the Magnificum adventure games.

boardgame: Forsaken: The Shaman and The Stray
description:
Two new characters are included in this expansion - Stray and Ghixu-Ghexu, the Shaman.

This expansion includes:

     2 new characters (each with a miniature, character board, 3 story cards, 10 feats and 1 starter gear)
     18 encounter cards
     1 Offworld Salvager Class Module (4 story cards and 4 feats)
     24+ reserve cards

boardgame: Forsaken: The Prisoner and the Peon
description:
Two new characters join forsaken in this expansion: The prisoner O'phyddia Cold and the Peon Killquick.

This expansion contains:
- 2 new playable characters (each with miniature, character board, 10 feats, 3 story cards and 1 starter gear)
- 1 warden pod miniature
- 18 encounter cards
- The Empyriad Exile class module (4 story cards and 4 feats)
- 24+ reserve cards

All of the Kickstarter stretch goals and bonus content in one box!

Contains:
2 Character miniatures
2 Character boards
28 Feat cards
22 Story cards
4 Ruins cards
9 Persona cards
17 Asset cards (gear, weapons, and data)
2 Bounty cards
1 Wondrous Sundry
18 Encounters
1 Observer reference card
1 Observer dice tower replacement tile
3 Acrylic persona standees
2 Cub Cards (Lil Watta and Femryk)
42 Reserve cards

boardgame: Potion Explosion: Tonic of Glacial Preservation – Expansion Pack
description:
Keep your Ingredients cool and safe with the refreshing power of ice! The Tonic of Glacial Preservation will allow you to place up to 2 marbles from your hand on any holes of its tile as temporary storage, for a later use!

Mix the eight bottles set to the components of the Potion Explosion base game and take on a new challenge!

—description from the publisher

The Ulti-mutt Board Game!

Jump on board with Scallywags, where ruff around the edges canines compete for land and loot! These tough pups are out to seek adventure, plunder hard fought treats, and seek fame as the top dog of the high seas.

2 to 4 players take turns working through explore, buy, battle, and loot phases to consolidate a cat free empire. Claim unexplored islands, build & battle yer fleet on the high seas, and bury the most plunder to win!

Watch out for event cards that will change the tide of the game and enjoy plenty of well-placed dad jokes along the way.

—description from the designer

Black Spirits: Wayward and Weathered is an expansion for the popular Black Spirits: Combat Cards game system, a supplement that replaces dice components in board games based on extremely popular dark fantasy action RPG video games to use cards for their combat systems rather than dice. Expand on the tactics established in that core set to enrich virtually every aspect of the board game. Support and customize your specific build with specialized stat cards, use completely new and intricate playstyles with brand new character class cards, dive into exploration and adventure with discovery cards, or accept a new level of challenge with affliction cards. Or include them all for a rich and tactical experience full of hard decisions and fraught with peril.

As with the Black Spirits: Combat Cards core set, Black Spirits: Wayward and Weathered is drop-in with no modifications, house rules, or tweaks necessary to the board game played. It's built with the latest sets of those board games in mind, but is also compatible with earlier versions of those games, so you can inject new life (or of course, death) into your already-owned games regardless of what your collection looks like.

Welcome to FARMily Feud, where farmers must grow and protect the crop they are championing until one comes out as the ultimate winner. FARMily Feud provides players with the choice of 14 identical actions on seven farm cards, so the playing field is level and each farmer has an equal shot at victory.

Everyone has an identical starting card showing three of their crop--tomato, aubergine (eggplant), carrot, or corn--which is placed in a set formation with the other players' starting cards.

From here on out, the game is played in rounds, with players picking and simultaneously announcing the number of the action they'd like to take. The numbers determine player order. The action is executed by paying its cost in seed, then placing the card back side up, generally in order to either increase the player's own crop or decrease another's.

In addition to outnumbering rivals' crops, players can also score points if they are able to finish with their crop in certain formations. The game plays quickly so you can challenge anyone at any time, so let's settle the sibling rivalry once and for all and kick off this FARMily Feud!

—description from the publisher

WOSDEC is a fan created campaign rule set to breathe new life into your old Super Dungeon Explore collection.  It brings more to the game as you can level up your heroes, form guilds, quest, craft, and much more.  The WOSDEC game besides being an expansion is also a stand alone starter.  It comes with five new heroes, new spawning points and dungeon tiles.

WOSDEC also adds “end game” content such as crafting specialties, and end game raids.   Play through the story of Lady of Plagues, be a witness to her return to power and an instrument in her fall! WOSDEC offers narrative dungeons, a town stay phase, as well as mega dungeons.   This allows you to take larger adventuring parties, contend with unique dungeon rules and encounters.  The raid boss fights introduce new tactics that will make you feel like your battling epic bosses from an online MMO style.

This game allows you to play by yourself, with others, or build your world experience with an entire store or community. WOSDEC makes use of your entire collection, the items in this box just expand your collection.  How you explore and fight the dungeons is up to you.

This game is NOT a Ninja Division product and has been created by Team Drew’s Basement under a special license where it is completely on its own and has no affiliation.  This game was a passion project that has grown and can now actually be made.  So hop back into Crystalia with a Fan made ruleset, made by a fan of the original game, for fans of the original game.

—description from the designer

Updated: 2.10.26:  The Full Rules will be free via PDF download on 2.24.26, hardcover books are in production for those that wanted them, and miniatures are already actively shipping.  Despite 2 failed crowdfund attempts, it is out there and available.

As the company grows more industrious and innovative, life gets more interesting. Well-developed departments grow more prosperous, and oft-used technologies become increasingly efficient. Welcome to the age of Advancements!

The Advancements expansion for Galactic Cruise includes several modules, which may be played individually or in any combination with one another.

The Cabin-Matching module includes 48 blueprints, which will replace the ones included in the base game. Instead of having half-cabins in the middle of each segment, these blueprints have half-cabins in one of three places–left, center, or right–on their tops and bottoms. In order to build a segment into a shuttle, it must make a full cabin with other previously-installed segments.

The Advanced Technologies module introduces upgradeable versions of the technology tiles from the base game; each Technology now has a technology track along the bottom that determines the level of its ability. Each Technology starts with its ability being the same as in the base game, but can eventually become better.

The Advanced Locations module upgrades the locations throughout the game when a certain number of Developments have been built. When a location is upgraded, it grants bonuses to players based on how many Developments they've built adjacent to that location.

There are also new Agenda Cards, new Upgrade Tokens, a new Cockpit and Engine tile, and and 2 new Expert Worker Tiles. Go forth, you innovators, and advance!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Panzer Grenadier: The Book of Armaments Eastern Front Artillery
description:
On the combined-arms battlefield, it’s the armor that slashes through and the infantry that takes and holds ground. But it’s the artillery that destroys, that creates the breakthroughs and crushes attacks before they even begin.

The Book of Armaments is all about artillery in Panzer Grenadier, focused on the Eastern Front games (Fire in the Steppe, South Flank, Burning Tigers, Broken Axis, Gates of Leningrad, The Deluge, Slovakia’s War, Armata Romana) and thus on the Soviet, German and Romanian armies (including related forces like Soviet Guards or German Waffen SS).

It comes with 88 new die-cut and silky-smooth pieces: additional artillery units, plus new units for the heavy and medium batteries of corps- and army-level battalions. There are German 170mm and 210mm guns, Soviet 152mm and 203mm batteries, Romanian 155mm guns and so on. And there are more of the artillery batteries already present in some (though not all) of those games, like the German 105mm howitzer.

In Panzer Grenadier, each off-board artillery increment corresponds to an artillery battery and has the same strength as the battery’s indirect fire rating (so most German increments have a strength of 16 just like the 105mm howitzer). The Book of Armaments includes Artillery Display Cards for each player, and you’ll put your off-board batteries right there on the chart and change their status as you fire them. While it helps keep track of which ones have fired, more important is the coolness factor of playing with artillery batteries on the game board instead of numbers that you write down on paper.

There book also includes some new artillery-centric optional rules to let you do more stuff with the big guns. And of course there’s historical background about the guns and their users.

—description from the publisher

All we do, we do for the guests. The happiness of our guests is the lifeblood of our company, and we refuse to be outdone! By jump-starting our team’s work ethic and further innovating the aerial cruise ships, we will remind the world that Galactic Cruise is synonymous with Luxury-in-Space.

The Accommodations expansion for Galactic Cruise includes several modules, which may be played individually or in any combination with one another.

The Specialized Blueprint module includes 48 new segments that replace those found in the base game. These segments all feature a unique action or bonus. During a Day in Space, instead of gaining the Guest Bonuses like usual, you will instead send Guests to different segments on the ship that include their matching icon, and then carry out the action (and/or gain the bonus) listed on that segment.

The Starting Upgrades module includes 36 cards, all of which give players a starting upgrade and a token removed from their launch tower, jump-starting their game and providing asymmetry among the players from the very beginning.

The game also includes new Agenda Cards, new Upgrade Tokens, 2 New Expert Worker Tiles, and 2 new Technologies. Well, what are you waiting for? Let's go give some guests the best trip of their lives!

—description from the publisher

King John’s Tournament is a 1 to 2 player card/dice game that plays in 20 to 30 minutes.  Your goal is to obtain the highest score possible by shooting three arrows at a target.  To do this you will be rolling dice, adjusting the dice and placing the dice to match other required dice results.  There are six cards representing the travel of the arrow before it hits the target.  The game can be played using multiple levels of difficulty.

Goal of the game
The goal of the game is to get the highest score possible.  To do this, you need to fire all three of your arrows into the direct center yellow section of the target which will earn you a max total of 30 points.  The farther you get from the center, the lower your score will be for that arrow shot.  Player with the highest score wins.

—description from the designer

Who hasn't dreamed of participating in the Olympic Games? Through an original game of idea association, discover the various Olympic and Paralympic sports. Imagination, deduction, and risk-taking will be the three necessary ingredients to shine in this game!

Each round, 5 sports are revealed. A Coach draws 6 Clue cards. Using these cards, they must guess 3 sports among the 5 visible ones.
For each sport, the Coach plays a Clue card, which can be placed in two different ways: vertically for a very good clue, horizontally for an average clue.
Once the three Clue cards are played, the other players will then try to figure out which sport corresponds to each clue. But be careful not to rush!

Olympikos
• Family game
• +8 years
• 2-6 players
• Approximately 20 minutes
• Difficulty: Easy
Description
• Association of ideas and taking risks
• Game that suits both athletes and non-athletes
• Game changes linked to anti-doping control
• A booklet of anecdotes about the sports present in the game, also, interviews of Olympic athletes.
• 28 Sports cards (double-sided)
• 71 Clue cards
• 6 Scoreboard cards
• 6 Flag Bearer cards
• 30 Delegation cards
• 5 Ring cards
• 1 Anti-Doping Control card
• 3 Medal cards (bronze, silver, and gold)

The popular party game Party Alias in the latest version. As the name suggests, the game is a battle of generations - parents and children. Or children and parents?

Unlike Party Alias and Party Alias Big Bet, this version is all talking, no pantomime or drawing - just like Party Alias Women vs Men.

One of the players explains a word or phrase to their team so that they can guess it and thus come closer to winning.

Does that seem too easy? Well, watch out, there's a catch: kids explain words from their parents' world and parents explain words from today's teenagers' world.

Can you express yourself succinctly and clearly in a limited amount of time? See if you can be equal competitors!

Party Alias: Souboj generací ("The Party Alias Clash of Generations") game includes: game plan, 400 cards containing 2,400 words, hourglass, 2 pieces and rules.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Heroes of Timeline is a board game that brings you into a captivating fantasy world and challenges your strategic skills in quick and dynamic battles.
- Zero randomness
Heroes of Timeline is a board game that will never let a victory to be stolen from you by an unlucky dice throw. Only your strategy and choices lead the Heroes to a victory or a defeat.
- Unique heroes
Unique abilities of each Hero and each Team mean that every move you make is crucial, and every match you play is unique.
- Painless rules
The game is designed to start playing quickly. This video will prepare you for your first Heroes of Timeline game in roughly 10 minutes.
- Deep strategy
You lead two Teams of mightiest Heroes, and you direct their maneuvers, attacks, and abilities in a turn-based combat. The stakes in this game are high: any attack on an unprotected Hero means elimination.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Playing Pieces: Five Spotzee-Dotzee dice, with different colors on each side.  Scoring sheets included.

Your Goal: To have the highest total score after each player has scored all 13 turns.

To Start: Choose a player to go first. Play moves to the left.

On Your Turn: Roll all five dice. Then, you may make up to two re-rolls of any dice you wish, trying to create the best possible score. You do not have to use all three rolls of the dice, and can score at any time.

Scoring: You must record a score on one of the rows of the score sheet each time you take a turn, even if it means scoring zero in an empty row that your dice do not match. You cannot move or change any score after it has been recorded on the score sheet.

A "Super Spotzee" (five of a kind) is the highest score you can get, and is worth 10 points. If you roll additional "Super Spotzees," you can score them in another row (such as "Count the Red Dots") and mark a small box in the Bonus line to add 10 more points to your final score!

24 pairs of cards and 1 Grinch card - avoid the Grinch to win.

Cards are shuffled and all dealt out. Players remove their pairs before play proceeds. Players take it in turns to take a card from the previous player without looking and remove a pair of it makes one.

The player at the end of the game with the Grinch loses.
family: Books: How the Grinch Stole Christmas

description:
Velociraptores The Board Game is a board game for children 5+ years old.

Objective
Be first velociraptor cross the finish line on board.

Gameplay
The game is played in turns. Every turn, players play color cards to move your velociraptor, discard ALL cards to move 1 space in front or pass and do not move. After play any card, draw cards, hand limit 3 cards.

Velociraptors: The Board Game is a 100% national production, totally independent, aimed at children from 5 years old. 
This game was specially developed to be accessible to all children, regardless of their reading or numbering skills, promoting inclusion and fun.

Developed in a school environment, this game had the active participation of students from 4 to 5 years old in its creation, ensuring that every detail was thought out to meet the needs and interests of our little players.

The game was meticulously prepared in line with the competencies of the National Common Curricular Base (BNCC) in the subjects of Physical Education, Arts and Mathematics, providing an integrated and fun learning experience.

The Velociraptors game is an exceptional tool for educators and parents looking for quality play resources. It is highly recommended for children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), offering significant benefits for cognitive and social development.

The game encourages collaboration, problem-solving, and creativity, all while having fun.

The proposal of HB Boardgames is to bring education and entertainment to children in an inclusive and engaging way, making learning an unforgettable adventure.

Velociraptores o Jogo de Tabuleiro.

Velociraptores: O Jogo de Tabuleiro é uma produção 100% nacional, totalmente independente, destinada a crianças a partir de 5 anos. 
Este jogo foi desenvolvido especialmente para ser acessível a todas as crianças, independente de suas habilidades de leitura ou numeração, promovendo inclusão e diversão.

Desenvolvido em ambiente escolar, este jogo contou com a participação ativa de alunos de 4 a 5 anos na sua criação, garantindo que cada detalhe fosse pensado para atender às necessidades e interesses dos nossos pequenos jogadores.

O jogo foi meticulosamente elaborado em consonância com as competências da Base Nacional Comum Curricular (BNCC) nas disciplinas de Educação Física, Artes e Matemática, proporcionando uma experiência de aprendizagem integrada e divertida.

O jogo Velociraptores é uma ferramenta excepcional para educadores e pais que buscam recursos lúdicos de qualidade. Ele é altamente recomendado para crianças com Transtorno do Espectro Autista (TEA) e Transtorno de Déficit de Atenção com Hiperatividade (TDAH), oferecendo benefícios significativos para o desenvolvimento cognitivo e social.

O jogo incentiva a colaboração, a resolução de problemas e a criatividade, tudo enquanto as crianças se divertem.

A proposta do HB Boardgames é levar educação e entretenimento para as crianças de forma inclusiva e envolvente, tornando o aprendizado uma aventura inesquecível.

Objetivo:
Cruzar a linha de chegada em primeiro.

Gameplay:
O jogo é jogado em turnos. No seu turno você deve jogar cartas de cores para mover-se, descartar TODAS as cartas da sua mão para andar 1 casa para frente ou passar a vez e não se mover.
Após jogar cartas, você deve comprar novas cartas até o limite de 3 cartas na mão.

Duelo de Reinos is a game for two players.

Each player have cards from 3 collors represent 3 Kingdoms: Ice (Blue), Fire (Orange) and Nature (Green).

Objective:
Win five duels to win the game.

Gameplay:
Each children play a card face down in the table. Simultaneously turn your cards to face up and compare the collors: Blue beats Orange, Orange beats Green and green beats Blue. Same collor is a tie and play again new cards.

If nobody wins a 5 duels, the game end with a tie.

The streets of Hong Kong are brimming with exceptional restaurants, showcasing the city's rich culinary heritage. But a major political crisis has shaken the archipelago, dividing society into two polarized camps. Among the city's businesses, some are quietly committed to support the pro-democracy camp (the yellow ones). Will you be able to share the ones that does support the movement without risking exposing them to the authorities?

Les adresses jaunes is a cooperative game in which players are trying to give clues about the tiles they got in their hands by cleverly positioning their pieces on the board. The board represents a district of Hong Kong with 27 businesses. Players win the game together if they all manage to get people guess their tiles.

—description from the designer

Postcards from Canada is a children's/family game about collecting stamps of Canadian animals to complete postcards.

The game comprises six dry erase postcards, 150 stamps and 54 attribute cards. These cards have attributes that are used to collect stamps and manage destinations. The dry erase postcards have spaces to collect five stamps and two boxes to write and manage their destination.

Also available: Postcards from America.

GOAL:

Complete your postcard by collecting five stamps and all the letters in your destination.

SETUP:

Each player gets a postcard on which they write a destination. The destination must have at least five unique letters. For example PARIS or CHICAGO, but not TORONTO. Each player also gets at least five stamps, more with longer destination words.
Using the destination, players randomly draw stamps equal to the number of unique letters in their destination.

GAME PLAY:

Game play is simultaneous with a player drawing the next attribute card. All players act upon the attribute revealed to either mark a letter from their destination or add a stamp to their postcard.

To collect a stamp, the animal must match the attribute. For example, the attribute "fins" matches a salmon, a turtle, a manatee and a dolphin.

To collect a letter, the destination's word must have that letter. All matching letters are recorded in the collection box. For example, with "SEATTLE" (with five unique letters), a match with "E" will collect two E's.

WINNING:

To win, a player must collect five stamps and all the letters in their destination.

STRATEGY:

The destination a player selects may have advantages or disadvantages compared to the destinations of other players.
For example, a longer destination gives a the player more stamps to use, but also may take longer to complete their postcard. Effective selection of the destination is key to winning.

—description from the designer

El Roscón es un juego de mesa con la temática principal del clásico roscón de reyes de navidad. Los jugadores irán cogiendo trozos de roscón y acumulando puntos hasta que alguien coja la desdichada haba. Hay cartas de acción y de puntos. Al final de 3 roscones, el jugador que más puntos tenga será el ganador. Se podría clasificar como filler y Party, ya que es un juego sencillo, rápido, para muchos jugadores y divertidísimo. Se cogen todos los trozos y se colocan en formas de roscón, si no te sale un círculo muy definido no importa, eso quiere decir que el roscón es más artesano. Cada jugador en su turno coge un trozo de donde quiera, aunque esté en la otra punta (Es que era el más grande, con más almendras y el que mejor pinta tenía, punto). Si es una acción, la hace, teniendo en cuenta un máximo de una acción por jugador. Por lo que si una acción te lleva a otra acción, pues se acaba tu turno y no se hace. Y si son puntos, te lo quedas boca arriba enfrente tuya. Así hasta que alguien coja el haba o te hagan cogerla.

El Roscón is a board game with the main theme of the classic Christmas roscón. Players will collect pieces of roscón and accumulate points until someone takes the unfortunate bean. There are action cards and points cards. At the end of 3 roscones, the player with the most points will be the winner. It could be classified as filler and Party, since it is a simple, fast game, for many players and a lot of fun. All the pieces are taken and placed in roscón shapes, if you don't get a very defined circle it doesn't matter, that means that the roscón is more artisanal. Each player in turn takes a piece from wherever they want, even if it is at the other end (It was the biggest, with the most almonds and the best looking, period). If it is an action, he does it, taking into account a maximum of one action per player. So if an action leads you to another action, then your turn is over and it is not done. And if they are points, you keep them face up in front of you. That is until someone picks the bean or they make you pick it.

Viking, Japanese, Roman, and Egyptian armies set out to conquer the land! They gain ground by building fortresses. They must use strategy to bypass or hinder rivals they meet along the way and to return quickly to their castles.

Les conquérants is designed to help children aged 7 to 14 develop their planning skills and mental flexibility. The game encourages them to plan their movements and those of others, while adjusting to each turn. The various ways in which the game can be made more complex make it a great value, even for adults!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Thwarted! A Game of Super-Villainy Power Pack
description: This 22-card expansion to Thwarted! A Game of Super-Villainy contains new Villain Persona cards, Hero buff and debuff cards, and updated reference cards.

According to the rules Bomber Command is "a simulation loosely based on the RAF's night bomber offensive against the Third Reich."

In the standard game the RAF player launches ten bombing raids. Before each raid the German player receives six random units and deploys them on the map face-down. The British player then receives three units and random and programs the raid they will carry out using a copy of the map and the Raid Details Chart.

Once the raid begins the British units carry out their program and the German units may react. However, each German unit has a limited number of moves it can make during the raid.

The German player knows how much damage the bombers do to the cities, but they do not know how much damage the bombers take from either flak or nighfighter attacks; both players see the die rolls but the British player chooses one of two combat results tables to use for the raid and the German player does not know which one it is.

Victory points are awarded to the British for damaging cities and to the Germans for damaging plans and for British raids in  which fewer than two planes are used. Since the German player does not know how much damage they are doing (and may not find out how many planes are used in a raid) their points are hidden from them until the end of the game.

It’s the future, but don’t think about it too hard.  It’s just ducks strapped to engines... or maybe the ducks are piloting them from a cockpit... or maybe visualization is the enemy of suspended disbelief.

This game is meant to contemplate the difficulties that arise within a tight pack of racers who are propelled at breakneck speeds by engines that contain radioactive fuel and that heat up uncomfortably quickly to meltdown temperatures in environments that hate you.

Being in the lead is bad:  you must smash through an everlasting air barrier that builds resistance heat.  Being in the middle of the pack is bad:  you will absorb extra exhaust radiation from ducks ahead of you.  Being in the rear is bad:  if you don’t floor it to the brink of explosion, you’ll never catch the leaders.  Hitting obstacles at these speeds is very bad.

Which equipment should you bring?  Will it be stuff that staves off dangerous meltdown and blackout conditions?  ..or perhaps performance-pushing engine boosts and aerodynamic modifications? ..or tasty duck snacks that have an assortment of effects?

Each race is abstracted via relative movement on a series of 3-laned boards that cycle in the style of Thunder Road.  Turns are simulated by moving forward or backward in the inside or outside lanes, respectively.  Hazards, turns, and environmental effects are dictated by a 6-stage course card.

Aggression and thrillseekery are provided by you.

—description from the designer

Race steam locomotives across the United States, delivering cargo to earn money.

Spend your earnings to buy bigger locomotives and pull longer trains, or buy the railroads themselves, and charge other players for using them.

Only watch out for cows on the tracks ….

—description from the designer
An abstract game similar to chess and checkers, played by 2-4 players. The objective is to capture the crown squares of other players, the last remaining player is declared the winner.

In Without our King: 2-4 rulers compete in a game of a strategic conquest and political maneuvering to seize control of The Last Kingdom, a once mighty realm on the island of Luminar. The Last Kingdom is thrown into chaos following the slaughter of the God King. The island of Luminar now witnesses the rise of warlords and zealots challenging established treaties and faiths. March your legions of archers, swordsmen, and deadly shock cavalry to dominate enemy lands. Construct walls and castles to defend your territory. Ensure your councilors are elected to the Council and deceive your enemies to win at all costs.

—description from the publisher

Accept the sinful nature within you and embrace the aura of fallen Champions, of men and women who believe that the ends justify the means and that the first victim of war is the truth.

Each Champion Aura comes with a new set of: Champion, Maneuver, and Starting Gear cards.This ensures that the experience of a Champion’s Aura is truly different from playing the standard version.

Includes: +4 champion miniatures, +4 champion standees, 16 champion cards, 76 unique maneuver cards, 4 starting gear

Accept the path of virtue and embrace the aura of immaculate Champions, of men and women who believe that the path to salvation is the path to Holiness.

Stand your ground, for a chain is only as strong as its weakest link. Each Champion Aura comes with a new set of: Champion, Maneuver, and Starting Gear cards.

This ensures that the experience of a Champion’s Aura is truly different from playing the standard version.

Includes: +4 champion miniatures, +4 champion standees, 16 champion cards, 76 unique maneuver cards, 4 starting gear

Author of the Divine Comedy and inventor of Terza Rima, Dante is a perfectionist and abuses of details to achieve the greatest feats.

Includes: 4 champion cards, 20 unique maneuver cards, 1 starting gear card, 1 threat marker, 1 champion standee, 1 champion miniature

The new generation quiz game, which was created in cooperation with the documentary TV Prima Zoom.

You can look forward to 4 different categories - nature, interesting places, science and technology, history - which will check your knowledge. Game cards contain a number of beautiful photos and 3 different types of questions. Players collect points for the correct answers and the one who gets the most wins the game.


Kvízová hra novej generácie, ktorá vznikla v spolupráci s dokumentárnym televíznym kanálom Prima Zoom.

Tešiť sa môžete na 4 rôzne kategórie – príroda, zaujímavé miesta, veda a technika, história – ktoré preveria vaše znalosti. Herné karty obsahujú množstvo krásnych fotografií a 3 rôzne typy otázok. Hráči zbierajú body za správne odpovede a ten, kto ich získa najviac, vyhráva.

Each player receives 5 slices. The remaining slices form a hidden stack, The youngest player starts by placing one of his slices on the table. The other players proceed clockwise and try to complete one of the two figures by placing a matching slice next to it. If a player does not have a matching slice they draw a new one from the stack. If the new slice does not match either, it's the next player's turn. The first player to place all their slices wins the game.

Play as France!

This expansion includes a complete deck of the nation of France and the units that participated in World War II.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Add allies to your 2GM TWG game.

This expansion includes unit cards from various other nations that actively participated in World War II, to join the Allied or Axis armies
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
You are a rookie hacker and want to be admitted into the inner circle of the hacking fraternity. Prove your worth by stealing as much personal data from the marketplace and prevent your competitors from stealing too!

The game's objective is to score as many points as possible by successfully performing attacks on victims in the marketplace.

Players can attack by choosing a target from the marketplace, playing Praesidium cards from their hand (to determine the type of attack: DDOS, phishing or social engineering) and rolling the corresponding number of dice to find out the strength of the attack. If the strength matches or overcomes the resistance value of the target, the attack is successful and the player scores the number of points depicted on the target card.

In the more advanced variant of the game, players may obtain special attacks and use protection measures to thwart attacks of other players, so that they themselves can win the game.

—description from the publisher

Rêvelune is a co-operative game of storytelling and deduction. As guides, you take turns imagining the adventures of your teammate, trapped in their dream. Turn by turn, they must deduce the hidden meaning behind each intervention. Will you succeed in freeing your teammate by guiding them wisely to the end of their dream?

In more detail, the game is played over several chapters. During a chapter, the guides take turns inventing the adventures encountered by the Dreamer, respecting the narrative constraints of their guide card, e.g., a simple description, an obstacle, a cute moment, etc. The Dreamer, meanwhile, listens attentively to the story, then — once all the guides have spoken — must find the hidden meaning behind each intervention, with the help of their Dreamer cards, which are identical to the guide cards in play. A certain number of successful associations enables the Dreamer to move on to a new chapter, with changing constraints. Conversely, each failure draws the Dreamer deeper into their dream at the risk of not being able to get out...

If the players all reach the end of the dream together, the Dreamer is freed and they win the game.

—description from the publisher

Rêvelune est un jeu coopératif de narration et de déduction. En tant que Guides, vous devez imaginer à tour de rôle les péripéties vécues par votre coéquipier, prisonnier de son rêve. Celui-ci doit en retour réussir à déduire le sens caché derrière chaque intervention. Parviendrez-vous à libérer votre coéquipier en le guidant judicieusement jusqu’au bout de son rêve ?

Résumé d’une partie :
Le jeu se joue en plusieurs chapitres. Au cours d’un chapitre, les Guides inventent tour à tour les péripéties rencontrées par le Rêveur, en respectant la contrainte narrative de leur carte Guide (exemples : simple description, obstacle, moment mignon, etc.). Pour cela, ils peuvent s’aider de leur carte Inspiration qui leur suggère des mots-clé.⁠ Le Rêveur, quand à lui, écoute attentivement le récit puis – une fois que tous les Guides se sont exprimés – doit retrouver le sens caché derrière chaque intervention, à l’aide de ses cartes Rêveur (identiques aux cartes Guide en jeu).⁠ Un certain nombre d’associations réussies permet de passer à un nouveau chapitre, avec des contraintes qui évoluent. A l’inverse, chaque échec entraîne le Rêveur plus profondément dans son rêve, au risque de ne plus pouvoir en sortir…⁠ Si les joueurs arrivent tous ensemble au bout du rêve, le Rêveur est libéré et la partie est gagnée.⁠

—description from the publisher (French)

A 2-player board game for friends, family, and especially couples (even on dates) to have a fun challenge in getting to know one another better through questions about preferences and little things in life. How much you really know about them?:“Do you prefer eastern or western food ?”, “Can you swim well?”, “Do you prefer a good view or a good food restaurant ?” and many other questions. Who can give a more accurate answer about the other person will be the winner in this game of authentic communication.

—description from the designer

A one vs one deep space starship battles card game that is quick to learn and fast to play. It takes the WAR card game of old and adds a bit of flare by adding a way to track your progress towards victory with a first time ever seen card mechanic that delivers visual elements that is sure to please any science fiction fan.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: What Do You Meme?: Family Edition Expansion Pack #1
description:
More Cards, More Memes, More Fun: This is the expansion to the fan favorite (and family-friendly) meme-matching game you know and love.

Contents
100 caption cards
25 photo cards

boardgame: RuneScape Kingdoms: King Black Dragon Expansion
description:
RuneScape Kingdoms: King Black Dragon Expansion enhances your RuneScape Kingdoms experiences with a new skill, campaign, area and boss. Supplement your abilities with the unique Prayer skill, work with Bob the Cat to traverse the Wilderness and face down the mighty King Black Dragon.

—description from the publisher

RuneScape Kingdoms: Kalphite Queen Expansion enhances your RuneScape Kingdoms: Shadow of Elvarg experience with a new skill, campaign, area and boss. Supplement your abilities with the unique Summoning skill, track down the elusive Osman, explore the desert city Al Kharid and face down the Kalhpite Swarm, directed by their venomous Queen.

—description from the publisher

RuneScape Kingdoms: TzKal-Zuk Expansion enhances your RuneScape Kingdoms: Shadow of Elvarg experience with a new skill, campaign, area and boss. Supplement your abilities with the unique Runecrafting skill, work with the Wise Old Man to delve into TzHaar City and face down the demi-god TzKal-Zuk.

—description from the publisher

RuneScape Kingdoms: Culinaromancer Expansion enhances your RuneScape Kingdoms: Shadow of Elvarg experience with new side quests, events, unique equipment and a confrontation with the nefarious Culinaromancer.

—description from the publisher

Published by A. Williams & Co. of Boston and copyrighted by Mudge & Spooner in 1857. Thought to be the predecessor game to the Peter Coddle games (Trip to New York, Trip to Chicago) as it was published a year prior to Gould & Lincoln‘s Peter Coddle’s Trip to New York game in 1858.

One person is selected to read the story which is accompanied by a set of cards in the box. The cards, containing the names of various objects, are distributed equally to all participants. When the reader encounters a blank space in the story, that person looks at one of the participants who then must read one of their cards to complete that missing part of the story.

Embark on a harrowing journey to Illnue in the first captivating installment of our Harbinger Series. Uncover the dark secrets that lie beneath the mysterious affliction that have befallen the Toreln. Whispers of kidnapped children echo through the Storm Watcher’s Forest. As the main hero, you catch wind of these unsettling rumors and immerse yourself in not only a search for the missing children, but for answers.

Your mission begins with tracking down the sole survivor of these kidnappings, leading you into a quest to unravel the mysteries behind the nocturnal abductions by enigmatic creatures. Face unseen dangers and forge alliances. Your new ally is the key to unlocking the secrets of these mysterious kidnappings as you navigate treacherous paths and encounter dangers that will put your skills to the test. Illnue conceals not only its kidnapped children, but an unforeseen force working against them. Brace yourself for an immersive narrative that portends evil at every turn.  Are you ready to face the shadows, rescue the kidnapped children, and unveil the mysteries of Illnue? In Under the Cursed Fells, the fate of Illnue’s children rests in your hands!

This first set allows players and content creators to add dimensional dungeon structures to their adventuring! This set comes with a campaign compatible with Vrahode – The Calteeryn Ascension, three exclusive minion miniatures, one Legendary Creature miniature and cards for each.

—description from the publisher

"The Filipino Game of Life"

Demopoly is a game with obvious similarities in appearance to Monopoly. Players roll dice and move along spaces around the board.  When they land on a space they take the action listed.  They may buy a company share, draw a cash prize card, pay a fine or be arrested. Players who are arrested then draw a sentence card to determine the penalty for their particular crime. Shares pay preset dividends.

Demopoly is almost entirely a luck dependent game.  The only player decision is whether or not to buy a share when you land on a share space.  All other outcomes in the game are decided by the dice and the cards. There is no player interaction such as trading or negotiation.

The winner of the game is the last player left.  Players may be eliminated from the game by going bankrupt or, less likely, being sentenced to death for a capital crime.

boardgame: Don't Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus Game!
description:
A simple roll-and-move game included in 20th Anniversary Edition of the Mo Willems picture book Don't Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus.

Players roll a die to move their buses through town, trying to reach the finish while doing silly tasks along the way.  First player to the finish wins.

Note: this game is a similar but simpler game to the 2010 game of the same name.

The tranquility of the seas has been shattered by the emergence of an ancient ocean goddess. With vengeful fury, she vows to annihilate any of the crafted races daring to navigate her domains. As the hero, your mission is nothing short of colossal—gather the concealed fragments of a powerful device to thwart the wrath of the ocean goddess in this deadly second installment in our Harbinger Series.

Scour treacherous waters, haunted islands, and deep sea-caverns across the expanse of the Gulf of Duwora to the Kharmandryn Ocean for the pieces you need to defeat your legendary enemy. Unveil the legends of the deep waters in this nautical quest that will challenge your seafaring and problem-solving skills.

Shadowed Seas beckons you to immerse yourself in this adventure of the tides. Will you emerge victorious, or will the goddess’ wrath prevail before you can gather the device you need to destroy her?

This second set allows players and content creators to increase the depth and complexity of dimensional dungeon structures to their adventuring! www.harbingersystem.com

This set comes with a campaign compatible with Vrahode – The Enlightened & The Enslaved(exp 1), three exclusive minion miniatures, one Legendary Creature miniature and cards for each as well as five rooms, five hallways, thirty 2” supports, thirty 4” supports,  two stair units, two stair risers, two spiral stair units, fifteen M/M connectors, twelve stair treads, twenty-four walls clips, one 2-sided 2” wall, one 3” 2-sided wall, two 2-sided 4” walls, two 2-sided 5” walls and two 2-sided 6” walls.

All full color vinyl artwork for the rooms, hallways and walls in this set were all created by Léni Scheider of Darkest Dungeon the board game fame! Time to go vertical!

—description from the publisher

With the dangers of the shadowed seas behind you and the fate of Vrahode sprawled before you, meet your next enemy in the depths of the Forgotten Mountains.
Dive into the labyrinthian complex of Warren’s of the Heretic, spanning four levels of unparalleled challenge. Encounter the perilous Priul Guardians, meticulously crafted to test the mettle of even the most seasoned of heroes. Your Enemy, the wielder of a mysterious cloak that renders its wearer invisible to the true creator’s of Vrahode, proves to be one of your most treacherous foes yet.
 Continue to expand your horizons with new races and rewards, facing the dangers of the unknown, and a fated battle with the Priul Engineer, who wields more than just the power of invisibility, but the ability to construct monstrosities that will put your courage to the test. Are you ready to face the challenges that lie within the labyrinths? www.harbingersystem.com

This third set allows players and content creators to further increase the size, depth and complexity of dimensional dungeon structures to their adventuring! This set comes with a campaign compatible with Vrahode – Facing The Storm (Warrens of the Heretic), three exclusive minion miniatures, one Legendary Creature miniature and cards for each as well as seven rooms, eight hallways, thirty 2” supports, thirty 4” supports,  two stair units, two stair risers, one 4-piece dungeon riser, four 2” balustrades, fifteen M/M connectors, four stair treads, twenty-four walls clips, one 2-sided 2” wall, one 3” 2-sided wall, two 2-sided 4” walls, two 2-sided 5” walls and two 2-sided 6” walls.

All full color vinyl artwork for the rooms, hallways and walls in this set were all created by Léni Scheider of Darkest Dungeon the board game fame! Time to go vertical!
