Legends speak of the Dead Keep. Travelers and locals alike spin tales of this accursed locale 'round hearths every evenings. Public houses are always filled with whispers and rumours about what lies within. And yet, you'll probably never meet a single soul who has seen the Keep and returned. The many faded names carved into the table you are at is the only reminder of those who dared venture into the Keep. But you? You might be different.

Embark on an adventure with The Dead Keep, a cooperative dungeon diving campaign game designed for 1-6 players. Immerse yourself in the fantastical artwork, crafted from the original designs of renowned artist Paul Bonner. As fearless adventurers, you'll plunge into the depths of The Dead Keep, driven by the promise of untold riches from the enigmatic Patron. Will you unravel the secrets lurking within, or will the mist claim you?
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Baba Yaga

description:
Race to Tokio is an Australian wartime spin and move game where two players follow a winding track with 83 squares to reach Tokio. Each player has 3 military pieces including a tank and infantry. They advance their pieces along the board and can be delayed by mines, enemy tanks and major battles. First to Tokio is the winner.

—user summary

A single player, rogue-lite, dungeon crawling, adventure card game.

As the player, the number of adventurers you have are represented by ten-sided dice (d10) while a standee represents your location in the dungeon. Your dice pool determines your adventurers health and attack skill, meaning the more dice you have the more people in your party and thus your party has more health and a greater attack skill.

The basics of playing: shuffle the room cards (which include halls, rooms, monsters, bosses, and traps) and draw a card. Your standee enters that room and resolves any actions needed (fighting or disarming/bypassing a trap). Next, draw a card for the Final Boss, attempting to create the shortest path to the Entrance card; the boss does not begin moving until a path has been created from the Final Boss to the Entrance. This continues until you have defeated the Final Boss, you've been defeated by monsters/bosses, lost your dice to traps, or you choose to leave the maze; however, if you are ever defeated by the Final Boss you lose the game or if you don't defeat him in a limited amount of time.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Thunderchief: The F-105D "Thud" over North Vietnam, 1965-1968
description:
Thunderchief expands the Player's ability to fight a detailed air-to-air, one-on-one Dog Fight against a Vietnamese Air Force MiG Fighter. A New Mission has been created - Close Escort MiGCAP , where you are hunting MiG Fighters and protecting the vulnerable bombers. This NEW Combat System takes the basic abstracted "MiG encounter" found in the original Skyhawk game and adds a new dimension to it. Aerial Engagements are NOW fought in Combat Rounds. During each Combat Round only the Advantaged Fighter can fire its guns or missiles at their opponent. Your Fighter can end the combat round in one of three conditions; disadvantaged, advantaged or in a neutral position to begin the next round of combat. You must be advantaged to engage in combat!

Thunderchief also expands on the generic skill levels for your pilots that is found in the Original Skyhawk Rolling Thunder game. A NEW, more immersive Character generation and "Pilot School" module has been added to the game.  There are new skill levels and pilot characteristics that give each pilot in your squadron their own individual abilities and skills.

The New Combat System and Pilot School modules give both Skyhawk and the new Thunderchief Expansion Pack a whole new feel. You can now equip your Skyhawk with the Sidewinder or your Thunderchief with heat seeking Sidewinder or radar guided Sparrow air-to-air missiles, or use your guns on both aircraft for close in combat. Can you shoot down a MiG and earn a Silver Star for your victory? Find out in THUNDERCHIEF.

'Thunderchief also contains a NEW US Air Force Campaign Game that shows the USAF's involvement in the Rolling Thunder Bombing Campaign. The expansion contains a new USAF aircraft, the F-105 Thunderchief that can be flown in either the NEW fighter vs fighter role or in the traditional bomber role.

—description from the publihser

boardgame: Dungeon Degenerates: Blasphemous Borborygmi of the Badlands
description:
The third book in the series of Choose or Lose Adventures. This book also came with a promo epic monster card: Jibbles.

Jibbles
Epic Monster. 
Class: Ooze
Val: 4xp / 3 gold
Target: lowest MAG
Attack: 4
Armor: 0 
HP: 11
Abilities: Acidic, Endless Regenerate, Slime

A classic board game is coming back in a unique Special Edition.

Puerto Rico is one of the most iconic strategy games in the world. In this game, you take different roles, including industrious builder, clever trader, stalwart captain, opportunistic prospector, and more. Bolster your wealth and reputation by carefully building your city, harvesting goods, and strategically governing the island of Puerto Rico. The player with the most prosperous island wins!

The Special Edition offers enhanced components and artwork, with an improved layout and general game feel. It includes all the previous expansions, a new expansion, and a solo mode! Some versions of this edition will include playmat, deluxe components, and/or 3D terrain components.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description: The Röstigraben is the connecting chasm of the Helvetians, bridging German-speaking Switzerland with French-speaking Switzerland. This division dates back to the 4th century BC, separating Eastern Switzerland from Western Switzerland. Today, the difference is mainly evident in language, voting behavior, social behavior, and openness to foreign influences. Risk: Röstigraben depicts the 6 Swiss major regions. These regions are further divided into partly fictional territories that must be conquered. A thrilling game for the whole family.

boardgame: Lone Sherman: The Pacific – A Solitaire Wargame
description:
Lone Sherman: The Pacific is a standalone sequel to Lone Sherman. It puts the Player in charge of a single Sherman Tank and its crew in various locations on the Pacific Islands during World War II, and tasks them with navigating their Sherman through varied and challenging Scenarios. Whilst this game is not a ‘narrative driven’ game as such, with a little imagination it will provide memorable stories. Some events are randomly generated, but the Player always has at least some degree of control over the level of risk they wish to take and how vulnerable they leave their Tank to these potential Events.

Whilst the Player will be controlling just one US unit (the Sherman) there were of course many other types of US units present in these battles. Those units are not depicted on the game Maps, but US casualties can be suffered and the game includes a mechanism to reflect this. The longer Scenarios take to complete and the more Japanese units there are on the Map, the more general US casualties will be inflicted and this can result in defeat for the Player should those casualties get too high.

The aim of this book game is to present a clear, accessible, and quick to learn game, so that players can get into the action quickly. Each of the 12 Scenarios can be played in around an hour, although sometimes a Scenario will last a much shorter time if your Tank is destroyed! Each Scenario provides plenty of replayability due to the random set up of the initial forces and the many Events and decisions in the game.

This book is a solitaire hex and counter style wargame. You play the game, and the enemy is controlled by the game book (or the Artificial Intelligence or ‘AI’). You will need to bring at least 5, but preferably 7, standard six-sided dice. Full colour 'Mission Maps' are included, as are suggested counters which can be removed and mounted on card.

—description from the designer

Marañon has left us in charge of their pet, a chick whose body is made of sugar and we have to feed it. Let's compete to see who prepares 3 desserts first to feed the chick.

In Feed the Chick we will play ingredient cards in pairs to complete our recipes or yellow cards to gain advantages.

Your goal is to cook the 3 desserts. If you do it first, you will have won.

Grass. Who needs it? Grow all the things!

Garden Builder is a grid coverage pattern building game. The goal is to accrue the most points by optimally arranging flowers, vegetables, and lawn ornaments.

Draw Garden Cards to plant seeds. Once the seeds match the pattern on any of the Plant Cards, the seeds are removed and replaced by a plant. Increase your score by watering plants, growing more of the same type, and safeguarding against pests. Earn bonuses by planting the Three Sisters and/or Salsa Garden plants.

—description from the designer

Second in the "Builder" series, after "Village Builder".

boardgame: El gran juego de las sumas
description:
Old educative boardgame to help improve students to do sum operations.

It is kind of a race, from 2 to 6 players or teams. It is played with four dices. All players throw them and the one with the highest sum starts playing.

During the game a player will throw all four dices and remain with three of them. He or She will then calculate the "four" possible scores (the title refers to that) and use it to advance on the board.

boardgame: El gran juego de las restas
description:
Old educative boardgame to help improve students to do subtraction operations.

It is kind of a race, from 2 to 6 players or teams. It is played with four dices. All players throw them and the one with the highest sum starts playing.

During the game a player will throw all four dices and remain with three of them. He or She will then calculate the "four" possible scores (the title refers to that) and use it to advance on the board.

boardgame: El gran juego de las divisiones
description:
Old boardgame by Distein meant to help improving students their divisions skills.

It is kind of a race, from 2 to 6 players or teams. It is played with four dices. All players throw them and the one with the highest sum starts playing.

During the game a player will throw all four dices and remain with three of them. He or She will then calculate the "four" possible scores (the title refers to that) and use it to advance on the board.

boardgame: El gran juego de las multiplicaciones
description:
Old boardgame by Distein meant to help improving students their multiplication skills.

It is kind of a race, from 2 to 6 players or teams. It is played with four dices. All players throw them and the one with the highest sum starts playing.

During the game a player will throw all four dices and remain with three of them. He or She will then calculate the "four" possible scores (the title refers to that) and use it to advance on the board.

boardgame: Marvel HeroClix: X-Men X of Swords Miniatures Game
description:
Based on the critically acclaimed crossover epic X OF SWORDS, this HeroClix miniatures game features iconic X-Men characters like Wolverine, Magik and Storm as they stand together to protect the mutant nation of Krakoa from the Swordbearers of Arakko!

Engage in up to 8 exciting campaign scenarios for 2 players where the results matter from session to session, or sit down for a head-to-head single match of raw power! Each of the beautiful pre-painted figures comes ready to play with two different modes making this box incredible for both first-time miniatures players and HeroClix veterans!

Included are also five of the all-new Tarot Cards that bring a new level of strategy to HeroClix and create even more new ways to play for hundreds of different combinations! Complete with thick, double-sided map components and unique X OF SWORDS themed dice, this set contains everything 2 people need to play Heroclix.

—description from the publisher

You Got Schooled is a fun, fast-paced strategy battle game. The objective is to outwit your rivals by assembling real groups of historical figures and earning points for yourself. Battle your way to the top using legendary people from history!

Imagine Einstein and his team of physicists facing off against Martin Luther King and the Freedom Fighters of the Civil Rights movement. In this epic clash, physics luminaries like Einstein, Marie and Pierre Curie, Niels Bohr, and Schrödinger engage in the Manhattan Project and strive to win Nobel Prizes to gain health to clench victory.

Here are the game mechanics:

Goal: Each player aims to score 10 points per player playing.
Unique Strategies: Utilize individual historical figures’ unique abilities and devise overall strategies within your deck.
Brain Cards: Deploy your chosen historical figures onto the battlefield using Brain Cards.
Money Management: Spend resources wisely to leverage their accomplishments.
All of human history has led to this moment!
We Got Schooled, Did You?

—description from the designer

This wedding-themed card game is the perfect gift!  Each card represents a step closer to the big day, from Dress Shopping to Cake Tasting.  Starting with 9 face down cards, each player replaces one card per turn trying to get sets of same cards next to one another. Each pair is cleared from your score.  The lowest total score wins!

—description from the publisher

Maybe some monsters are better left on the page. Play out favorite Goosebumps books with your horror-loving friends in this board game based on the spooky children's books series. Fans of Halloween and scary stories will love playing out scenarios based on their favorite fantasy books.

Players work together investigating to find items and Scare Score to rid the neighborhood of monsters to win, but don’t get caught! If even one monster achieves their win condition, you lose! 
 —description from the publisher

Our Holy People of Outrageous Misfortune Memorial Hospital is said to be the worst hospital in the country. Many believe it to be cursed. You, unfortunately, are a doctor at Holy People Hospital. Due to the recent shortage of patients (and capable physicians), there have been a lot of layoffs recently. You must protect your patients from awful events, themselves, and your fellow MDs. Be the last doctor employed to win the game.

Hex-Ray is a draw-one-play-one card game set in a horrible hospital. You are all doctors, trying to be the last one to watch your patients expire, all the while wreaking havoc on your fellow doctors' patients. Heal your patients, get new ones, and inflict horrible afflictions on those around you. If you're the last doctor with patients left, you win!

Jane may have come in for a simple headache...then the x-ray machine fell on her. Brian and Larry were on the mend, until two orderlies smashed their wheelchairs into each other. Lillian has some kind of horrible growth -- what IS that thing? Oh, and don't try to save this one, he has a Do Not Resuscitate order. These are just a few things you'll find yourself saying in a game of Hex-Ray!

boardgame: Thunder Road: Vendetta – Carnival of Chaos
description:
Carnival of Chaos is an expansion for Thunder Road: Vendetta (or Thunder Road: Vendetta – Maximum Chrome) that offers a new way to play. Players race their cars into the arena, collecting wild party favors, powerful super-weapons, and precious scrap along the way. This expansion also includes a full set of vehicles and a dashboard, so you can play with a fifth player using this expansion or in the regular game.

Unlike a regular game of Thunder Road: Vendetta, the winner of the Carnival of Chaos is the surviving player who has the most scrap at the end of the game. There is no finish line.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Napoleon's Counterstrike: The War of Liberation – Part II
description:
Schwarzenberg with 200,000 men against Napoleon with 155,000, accepted the 4:3 odds and a chance to capture Napoleon's main base, breaking the agreed-upon Fabian strategy to avoid the battle with the Emperor in person, known as the Trachtenberg plan.

The first of three battles, Löwenberg, took place two days before the Battle of Grossbeeren, covered in "Four Lost Battles." During the pursuit after Dresden followed the Battle of Kulm. In early October, Wartenburg led directly to the epic "Napoleon at Leipzig." With all three games you can contemplate a ten-battle Grand Campaign.
family: Series: The Library of Napoleonic Battles (OSG)

description:
The Lucky Garden is a fun tile-laying game for 1-4 players in which you'll have to find the gnomes hidden in a garden. The game is divided into 2 phases: in the first phase, each player on their turn will place a tile face up adjacent to one of the previous ones, until a 5x5 garden is created. Once the garden is created, we'll flip all the tiles over. In the second phase, each player, taking turns, will pick one of the mission tiles that will indicate how many gnomes, of what colors, and the maximum number of tiles you can flip to find them.

—description from the publisher

The Lucky Garden es un divertido juego de losetas para 1-4 jugadores en el que tendremos que encontrar a los gnomos escondidos en un jardín. La partida se divide en 2 fases: en la primera cada jugador en su turno colocará una loseta boca arriba adyacente a una de las anteriores, hasta que se crea un jardín 5x5 . Una vez creado el jardín, daremos la vuelta a todas las losetas. En la segunda fase, por turnos, cada jugador cogerá una de las losetas de misión que te indicará cuantos gnomos, de qué colores y el máximo de losetas que puedes girar para encontrarlos.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Verrotwood: The Old West is a standalone expansion for Verrotwood that takes the folk horror into the American southwest.

Verrotwood is not required to play this standalone expansion but you can easily add to your games by combining elements from both games.


     A simple yet robust D10 dice pool system that grows the more strategically you play. 
     A very low model count. Each cult is made up of only four models.
     A flexible cult creation system that lets you make the cult you want.
     A focus on kitbashing.
     AI creatures and special terrain to make every game you play unique.
     A fully solo mode.
     A versatile campaign where your cults will battle for power to please their sleeping gods. 


—description from the designer

Tonight we're going out. Ready to stir things up? Open this box and turn any ordinary party night into a legendary one.

Take a card from the main deck and follow its instructions. If you fulfill it, you keep it; if not, you drink and discard it.

The winner is the one with the most cards at the end of the game.

Answer compromising questions, overcome challenges, uncover suspicious behaviors, and compete face-to-face with your friends in duels while trying not to end up being the drunkest one. Watch out for the ultraviolet marker... this challenge will get people talking...

Break the ice and heat up the atmosphere with the ultimate drinking game.

—description from the designer

Family game in which you become couriers who are trying to deliver as many packages as possible. It's a game in which you try to be better than your opponents, but you have to help them if you don't want to lose valuable points. 
 Each round, players choose from several actions to evaluate. They evaluate the actions on their erasable city boards - they draw delivery routes with a marker between each city block, which then allow them to pick up and deliver packages.  Players distribute the packages around their city, collect them at the depot, and then send them to a set of "belts" on a special transshipment board. There, all players' packages are collected and then sorted. Players must import other players' packages or they will receive penalty points.
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Introduction:Gardaz (from Proto-Germanic: Gardaz, meaning "enclosure") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played at the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 9 points per side, but boards of 7 or 11 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a group.
     An enclosure is a set of empty points or enemy stones bounded by stones of any color without including the empty points on the edge for bounding. You control an enclosure if the majority of the stones that bound it are your color.
     A shared enclosure is an enclosure bounded by an equal number of friendly and enemy stones.
     A loop is a set of stones of the same color around an enclosure, such that no additional stone can be removed without breaking the closed circuit of the loop.
     The perimeter loop of a group is a loop located on the boundary of a group. The perimeter loop may contain one or more enclosures.
     A surplus stone is a stone that, when removed, does not break the perimeter loop of any group.
     The value of a group is the number of stones that form its perimeter loop, excluding surplus stones.
     A dead stone is a stone adjacent to an enemy enclosure, unless it is adjacent to a friendly or shared enclosure. A set of stones that are bounded by more enemy stones than friendly ones are also dead, empty points on the edge are not included for the bounding.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, take one of the following actions:

     Place a stone of your color on an empty point, then remove all dead enemy stones.
     Pass your turn.


At the end of your turn, the board position must be different from the board positions at the end of all your previous turns.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass their turn consecutively. At the end of the game, points are scored based on the number of friendly stones in the perimeter loops adjacent to at least one enclosure within the corresponding group, minus the number of surplus stones on the board. Additionally, half an extra point is awarded to the player who passed their turn first.

To make the game fair, before the game begins, the first player chooses an integer as the komi value, and then the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called pie komi.

—description from the designer

Players lay out a number of cards (4-6 cards, depending on the number of players) face up on the table. One of the players rolls four dice with four symbols on them. The players' task is to take as quickly as possible from the common offer one card whose back side contains as many of the symbols rolled on the dice as possible. Players then check the backs of the cards with the symbols shown and are awarded a winning token for each matching symbol.

This expansion for SAS Rogue Regiment introduces new rules, a new mission and an all-new event deck to your game of Stealth And Shooting. The Heavy Metal is rolling and it’s your team’s job to stop it by any means necessary. Do you dare? Will you win?

Players lay out a number of cards (4-6 cards, depending on the number of players) face up on the table. One of the players rolls four dice with four symbols on them. The players' task is to take as quickly as possible from the common offer one card whose back side contains as many of the symbols rolled on the dice as possible. Players then check the backs of the cards with the symbols shown and are awarded a winning token for each matching symbol.

Towards Gut Alley is a solo board game (not to be confused by the card game of the same name). The box is small enough to fit in a largish pocket.

You are a hero trying to go through as many levels of the dungeon as you can with your the help of your companions.

The game utilizes a mancala mechanism as you and your companions must stave off hunger, traps, monsters and of course the boss! During the day the monsters are not too bad but night time provides new challenges. Complete 6 objectives for each level and the boss.

Crying and screaming, worries and joys. In other words, you just brought home a baby. But are you together enough? Baby in the House is the perfect card game not only for parents-to-be, but especially for parents with a baby or for children with each other.
 Baby in the House is a cooperative game for two players. In the role of mom and dad, you will take care of the baby, fulfill its needs, take care of the running of the household and relax.

A luxury game for couples. In three categories - Tricky Questions, Challenging Lists and Impossible Drawing - partners will discover how well they know each other, reminisce about shared experiences, laugh at their own ineptitude and rejoice in hard-fought rewards.
 The players' goal is to be the first to reach the finish line on the game board. Each task offers the opportunity to move 0, 1 or 2 spaces as it contains a basic and a supplementary task. For tricky questions, the challenge is to answer the basic and supplementary question, for tricky lists, the challenge is to write three things and ideally in the correct order, and for impossible drawings, the challenge is to identify what is in the picture and what the assignment sounds like. The winner of the game gets a scratch card which allows them to choose one of the rewards on the reward sheet, which their partner then has to arrange.

“In Signo Hoc Vinces”. Dive into history and heroism with Crusaders: The Siege of Acre 1291, a Tactical  level Solitaire-Only board-wargame that immerses you and lets you experience the last stand of the Knights Templar and the Crusaders in the Holy Land. Relive the courageous stand of some 15,000 Crusader soldiers, Knights Templars, Teutonic Knights, and Hospitallers as they defend Acre, the critical port city in the Holy Land, against a massive Mamluk army of some 80,000 soldiers armed with siege engines and catapults during the Ninth Crusade.

Crusaders is a dice rolling, tower defense, solitaire game. Players take the role of Crusaders and try to defend Acre from the Mamluk attackers. Players win or lose depending on if the walls are breached and enough defenders survive.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Tír na nÓg: Tower Promo Cards
description:
Four promocards for Tír na nÓg which have been part of the Gamefound campaign:


     Glastonbury Tor (blue, PR4)
     Glastonbury Tor (yellow, PR3)
     Túr Bhalair (green, PR2)
     Túr Bhalair (red, PR1)
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
How did this idea come about?

I have always had a passion for card games. Playing endless hours of various such games made me realize how much laughter they give us and generally how much fun they are for all of us. I like the mystery and suspense that each shuffling of the cards creates.

But I still haven't come across a card game that would make me spend endless hours with my friends and at the same time discover each other's deepest secrets or their way of thinking when faced with a difficult dilemma.

That's why I decided to make a card game so dark and uncomfortable that it will give any group of friends who play it a crazy laughs, but also make them reveal their true way of thinking.

Finally, as I would like to be different from the hundreds of card games on the market I thought I would add a little touch of technology to my board game. What better to hear the question that will be asked to you simply by scanning a QR code?

So the suspense and mystery of what each player is called upon to answer will hang in the air until the question is heard.

Together we can truly discover our friends, let's start this journey.

Have you ever sat around a dinner table with your friends and discussed the weirdest things you could do? This game is made for you and is here to reveal who you really are. It's a game of the weirdest questions anyone can think of. 300 dilemmas waiting for you and your friends to answer. Flip the coin and let it decide your fate. Are you unlucky enough to have to answer yourself, or will you choose someone from the group to reveal their truth?

WHAT IS THIS GAME?

Flip It The Game is a card game that aims to make you and your friends feel as uncomfortable as possible. The questions will put you in real dilemmas that you will have to answer with complete honesty in front of your friends. There is no room for shame, only courage and truth are needed. Find out what your friends would really choose.

Flip It The Game is more than just a card game. It's an experience that will immerse you in exciting moments and twists! Using QR code technology, you can now listen to questions that will challenge you and make you think outside the box.

The rules are simple: just scan the QR code, listen to the question, and enjoy the fun that follows! With questions coming from the darkest and most humorous parts of the mind, every moment with Flip It The Game is filled with surprises and laughter!

Get ready to experience unforgettable moments of fun with your friends!

What types of questions are included in the game?
Would you rather have sex in front of your parents OR have to watch your parents have sex?
Would you rather have to announce to everyone around you whenever you
have to fart or pee your pants daily in secret?
Would you rather watch your parents have sex every day for the rest of your life or join in once to stop it?
All questions have emerged from the deepest and most twisted corners of the mind!

How do I hear the audio?
Scan the QR code found on the card using the free Flip It The Game App. You can download this app from the Google Store or the Apple App Store.

How it works?
Scanning the QR Code: Players use our free QR scanning app Flip It The Game, both for Android and iOS users,  on their mobile phones to scan the QR code located on the card.

Code Recognition: The scanning app recognizes the code and triggers an action, usually by playing an audio file linked to that specific QR code.

Receiving the Question: Once scanned, the QR code plays an audio file containing a question associated with that card, allowing players to engage with the game and answer the question.

Interactive Gameplay: Players listen to the question, contemplate their answers, and continue playing the game accordingly. This interactive element adds depth and excitement to the card game experience.

Setup & Instructions
One player must download the free Flip It The Game APP from the app store or google play.
NOTE: Once the app is downloaded no WI-FI or cellurar data is needed to run it. Just scan and listen.

Place the deck of QR code cards end the coin in the center of the table.

The youngest player starts and the turn goes clockwise. The player draws a card and places it in the middle, without scanning the question to keep it hidden.

The player flips the coin, if it lands on heads they answer the question themselves, if it lands on tails then they choose someone else to answer the question. Then he scans the question with the cell phone and all players hear the question.

Now the question awaits its answer. The winner is the one who answers the post questions. If someone doesn't answer 2 times when selected they will either be out of the game or you will choose what to do with them.

—description from the designer

A quick and dirty 15-30min card game for 2-4 players. Inspired by fantasy adventures, the card game sets players as goblins plundering treasure from a dragon's cave.

Each turn the cave refreshes with new treasure cards. Player goblins can take a treasure from the cave, steal from another player, turn a card in their hand, or stash a card safely in their hoarde.At the end of each round, the dragon hoards the most valuable treasure left in the cave.

When the dragon can't take an action, the last round triggers! Create the best hoard of treasure to score for sets of matching treasure types and value of each card.

Set collecting mechanics, with drafting and stealing, and hand management to create the best treasure sets. Competitive game with players working against each other plus the automated 'Dragon' player - and the Dragon could win!

Competitive with a touch of reluctant cooperation. Two decks can be combined to play with 5+ players.

—description from the publisher

Brothers at War: 1861 is a quick-playing, tactical wargame exploring civil war brigade command. Units are regiments, batteries and companies of skirmishers. This is a quadrigame or set of four games, each featuring a full-size, 22x34" game map and covering battles from 1861:

     Bull Run
     Wilson's Creek
     Carnifex Ferry
     Belmont
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Be the cleverest smuggler in Muamba.
Players have returned from vacation abroad and bought a few things above the customs limit of their country (along with some illegal items), now you need to deceive the customs officials at the airport, passing with these smuggled items hidden in your luggage, hoping it won't be searched. Muamba is a very fun card game with shedding mechanics and a lot of bluffing.

boardgame: KritterForce: A Game of Strategy, Cards & Dice!
description:
Kritterforce is a simple to pick up, fast-paced, battle game of cards and dice!


     Choose your team from a unique roster of characters! 
     Each turn, play cards to Buff your team, Debuff/Damage the enemy team or even alter the rules!
     Roll your attack dice while they roll to defend!


(Think Risk meets Dice Throne with a dash of TCG)


     Build Synergies & Deadly Combos! 
     E-Z deck-building mechanics! 
     Super Fun! 
     Easy to Learn! 
     Depth & Replayability! 


—description from the publisher

Strange Bedfellows - The Card Game of Political Dirty Tricks is a satirical foray into the tumultuous world of Presidential politics full of unexpected twists and turns.

Create your candidate, take positions on controversial issues, compete for endorsements, and get out the vote to strengthen your base and build a winning campaign.  And no presidential run is complete without slinging some mud at your opponents and engaging in a war of political Dirty Tricks to take your seat in the Oval Office.

Strange Bedfellows gives players a chance to create their own political world, and like election cycles no two games are alike! Every Dirty Tricks card has multiple uses allowing the players to decide how vicious or amicable your campaigns will be. With Muckraking Journalism, Media Spin, Fake News, and Digging Dirt, immerse yourself in a game of political humor and unpredictable political campaigns while competing in a run for the White House!

Look for the KickStarter June 2024

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vossa Excelência: O Jogo Político (Second Edition)
description:
This is the Second Edition of Vossa Excelência - O Jogo Político.

In Vossa Excelência (Your Excellency), each player takes on the role of a newly elected congress-person who, in order to stay in power, must use their four-year term to ensure re-election. They must seize every opportunity, utilizing allies, privileges, and all their influence to gain voters and (a lot of) money.

The new base box has several cards replaced, modified, or rebalanced. In addition to the 130 "basic" game cards, the box includes the second edition of the Power Project expansion, completely revamped with 60 cards that bring new possibilities and mechanics. The second edition also features a rewritten, more educational manual and a larger box with more storage space for the game's content and expansions.

It is compatible with the previously released Nova Era (New Era) expansion, as well as the recent Calamidade Pública (Public Calamity) and the mini-expansions Marcação Cerrada (Tight Marking) and Centrão do Brazil (Brazil's Centrists).

—description from the designer

A Strategy based card game that utilizes astrology and tarot cards to create a unique game experience. 2-4 players compete as one of the 12 Astrological signs with the goal of winning by balancing their Energy Centers.

TaroQuest is not like other strategy card games, with gameplay designed to create dynamic twists and turns. No game of TaroQuest is the same, with hours of memorable entertainment that can last a lifetime. The esoteric meanings of tarot cards are incorporated, which leads to some exciting game sessions that players enjoy talking about long after a game has ended.

A truly unique game, TaroQuest will not look or play like other strategy card games. There are no need for expensive booster packs with bulk filler cards or expensive cards to chase. You will need a shrewd mind and a bit of luck to determine when to play your cards to achieve victory. 
Everything you need for this new gaming experience is contained within.... TaroQuest!  Your Journey Awaits!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Gruszka (eng. Pear) - a single promo tile acting as a replacement HQ for any faction. Once per turn a player may choose its special feature out of other available HQs (player places chosen HQ next to the board as an indicator). This includes only universal features such as: additional initiative, higher initiative, increased melee attack, increased ranged attack, additional toughness, mobility, rotation or push back. Originally available at the Neuroshima Hex championship in Poland in January 2020 during PortalCon 12 and presumably up to two years earlier during Pyrkon. It is the other version of The Pear originally released in 2015.

Tricks in My Mind is an expansion for Rewind. In this new way to play, players play a must-follow, no trump, low-luck trick-taking game. In the first round, the winner of each trick gets points, but the players not winning the trick get to make bets for the second round. In the second round, players play the same hand of cards again (just like in Rewind), but this time the goal is to make the first round bets come true. High score wins.

Valiant Wars: Call to Arms is a standalone Valiant Wars game with all-new champions and locations. It can be played by itself and combined with Valiant Wars.

Valiant Wars is an aggressive press-your-luck deck-builder where you must fight to capture lands of the once-great kingdom. Your rivals will resort to trickery and sabotage in their attempts to bring down your forces. Be warned! As you assemble larger armies and conquer more territory, you’ll be haunted by dark visions of a world in ruins; too many of these Dark Omens and withdrawal becomes necessary. Step up and claim the Valiant throne!

—description from the publisher

This is a Five-in-a-row based game, but this is different from the "Five-in-a-row" you are familiar with. 
The game does not always alternate turns, and you have to look at the rows in many directions. 
Place your stone, move your stone, and let's aim to line up 5 stones in a row!

—description from the publisher

This game is the successor to iKNOW and Česko, otázky a odpovědi. As in iKnow, you will find 1600 questions, this time in the categories - Czech personalities, Czech creations, Czech country, miscellaneous.

Inkognito is a party game for 2-5 players, in which you take two roles in turn - the role of the performer and the role of moderator. As a performer, you will guess the profession or identity of a famous celebrity. As the presenter, you will clue the other players into guessing the profession or celebrity.
Your goal is to score as many points as possible for correctly guessed professions and famous personalities.

In this game, each player will create an iQLANDIE exposition using Tetris exposition pieces. However, in order to get the right pieces, you must first solve one of the 4 themed mini-games as quickly as possible.

Optics, in which you use lasers to connect 2 points. Biology, where you try to correctly arrange cells in a grid. Space Cards, which you use to form clusters of planets, and Programming, where you use your robot to follow instructions to the finish line.

The order in which you complete the tasks will determine your chances of getting the pieces you need. You then place the pieces in different exposures, in which they earn you points.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Geonosian Warriors Unit Expansion
description:
Geonosian Warriors join the Separatist cause in this new expansion for Star Wars: Legion! Relying on their sturdy exoskeletons to protect them from harm and their great numbers to swarm their opponents, Geonosian warriors are an imposing new Corps option for the Separatist Alliance. This expansion adds seven Geonosian Warrior miniatures to players’ armies, including a Geonosian Warrior leader and 4 Geonosian Warriors armed with powerful sonic blasters that are devastating at close range. Joining them are a Force Pike Warrior and a Sonic Cannon Warrior who can be added to the unit as heavy weapon options.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Sun Fac and Poggle the Lesser Commander and Operative Expansion
description:
Two high-ranking Geonosians lead the warriors of the hive into battle in this new expansion for Star Wars: Legion! Poggle the Lesser has the vast resources of the Geonosian droid factories at his disposal and this pack brings him to Separatist Alliance armies as a new commander option. He is joined by his loyal lieutenant Sun Fac, who is ready to carry out Poggle the Lesser’s will as a Separatist Operative. In addition to these two miniatures, players will also find the command cards for these two characters in this expansion, allowing them to further shake up their strategies.

—description from the publisher

The board card game Stupid Boys was conceived with the idea of making the best dueling game, with easy entry for beginners and great replay value for advanced players.

The rules of the game are simple: players alternately use attack and defense cards in the amount indicated on the character card, I take into account the sum of the value of the cards, if the sum of the attack exceeds the sum of defense, then the attack passes and the player receives damage equal to 1 heart token, and in addition, players can use cards " prestige" - modifiers, enhancements and extra actions that will add unpredictability to the progress of the battle!

The winner in the game is not only the one who has the coolest cards, but the one who can use them correctly - tactics and strategy will become your allies in battle!

—description from the designer

Настольная карточная игра «Дуболомы» придумывалась с идеей сделать лучшую дуэльную игру, с легким входом для новичков и большой реиграбельностью для продвинутых игроков.

Правила игры простые: игроки поочередно используют карты атаки и защиты в количестве указанном на карточке персонажа, учитываю сумму значения карт, если сумма атаки превышает сумму защиты - то атака проходит и игрок получает урон равный 1 жетону сердец, так же дополнительно игроки могут использовать карты «престижа» - модификаторы, усиления и экстра действия которые будут добавлять непредсказуемости продвижению боя!

В игре выигрывает не только тот у кого самые крутые карты, а тот кто может их правильно использовать – тактика и стратегия станут вашими союзниками в бою!

—description from the designer (Russian)

boardgame: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Sailor, Rich Man, Poor Man, Beggarman, Thief.
description:
Driven by the discovery of an ancient map carved onto a turtle shell and a thirst for exploration, the Northern tribes loaded their ships and sailed South. After months at sea, passage to the distant land is finally discovered and the tribes establish a haven. From here, each Old World tribe must explore and colonize the territories. And quickly!

Players must work with the talents of their group, a Tinker, a Tailor, Soldier, Sailor, Rich Man, Poor Man, Beggar Man….and a Thief. In a luck free game with perfect information, players use a hand management system to trek and sail across the new territory with haste, building outlier forts, strategically placing guards and employing chicanery to outwit opponents throughout the principle regions to claim the land.

The first tribe to successfully navigate the terrain and return to the starting haven is declared the ruling clan …….and the winner.

Did you know that there is only one Czech cheese in the prestigious world cheese encyclopedia, and that is Olomoucké tvarůžky? Do you know how many days it takes in our breweries for the main fermentation of lager in vertical tanks? Did you know that Jamón Ibérico de Bellota is the most expensive and highest quality ham in the world?
 Thanks to this game, every gourmet can become a true connoisseur of good food and drink. You can work your way up to a gourmet who has all the gastronomic terms and concepts down pat, and even in a fancy restaurant, nothing will surprise him. Maybe just the price.

Football on a soft touch mat. In futgoal, you are both the coach and each of your 11 players on the field. A dice game that capture the excitement of playing football for real. You need a mix of strategy and luck to be futgoal champion.

Umbral Knights is a 2-4 player cooperative exploration Board Game in the astral realms of Umbral.
Play as a Knight rescuing lost souls from oblivion, testing yourself during combats and encounters, choosing among the various paths presented, which will lead you to glory.
Choose your blessing, develop your skills, conquer better items, and delve into this incredible and dark adventure.

—description from the designer

This expansion for SAS Rogue Regiment introduces new rules, a new mission and an all-new way to play your game of Stealth And Shooting. Your friend becomes your enemy as they operate the Axis forces you are attacking. Achtung…schnell, Schnell, SCHNELL!

—description from the publisher

There are so many monsters here! Let's sort them by the number of eyes, hands and legs they have. Who will be the fastest to do it?
In Ojo, Mano, Pata, ¡Monstruos! (Eye, Hand, Leg, Monsters!) You will work on your observation, attention and mental arithmetic in the most... monstrously fun way!

Components
20 x Double-sided Monster cards, with the monster's image on one side and the number of eyes, hands and legs on the other.
20 x Challenge cards, showing the body parts that you have to add up. There are 3 difficulty levels indicated with stars on the cards.

Goal of the game
Get the most stars from Challenge cards by being the fastest to properly guess the correct total number of body parts.

The second edition of Dominion: Cornucopia & Guilds contains eight new kingdom cards, and the Dominion: Cornucopia & Guilds – Update Pack features only these new cards for those who own the first edition of this Dominion expansion.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Match the card image by sliding your pucks around in the tray and ring the bell before your opponent. Be the first player to solve 5 puzzles and slide your way to victory! Or challenge yourself in a race against the clock to complete the card patterns faster and faster each time you play. There are two levels of gameplay with Starter Cards for beginners and Tricky Cards for more experienced players.

Time Splicers occurs concurrently with Cities of Venus: Lost Contact and is the story of exactly what the catastrophe was on Earth that caused it to lose contact with Venus. It is 2222, and Earth’s worst-case scenario for The Singularity (when AI becomes self-aware) has happened. The Global Artificial Intelligence has cut off all contact with the solar colonies and has decided that, for humanity’s survival, humans must be enslaved.

Players represent Earth’s last hope in the form of Time Splicers – specialists who will travel back through time to change humanity’s trajectory and keep the The Singularity from occurring. However, the AI detects this threat and splices the timeline into parallel paths, all leading to the same conclusion. As Time Splicers, players must collapse these diverging timelines and collect the necessary time crystals to prevent The Singularity and help humanity prevail.

Players play cards to collapse Timelines in various ways and collect Time Crystals to win. The game ends immediately when a player finds their seventh Time Crystal, indicating they are the winner.

—description from the designer
